It was experimentally demonstrated that pH fell to 5.6 down to 6.7, turbidity rose to 1.6 NTU up to 0.2, and the model had a 96.8% classification accuracy having a 0.3-hour prediction error. The system is inexpensive (approximately 1200 rupees), mobile, and have Internet of Things (IoT) capabilities and thus suit rural and commercial dairy supply chains. It can help in early diagnosis of spoilage, minimize on wastage and increase food safety by means of remote, noninvasive monitoring.

title: Observational Study on Streamer Discharge Characteristics in Long Air Gaps Under High Altitude Conditions
authors: Jun Kang, Jingwen Ku, Xuanrui Zhang, Junhao Li
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Streamer discharge serves as the initial stage of gap breakdown and plays a decisive role in the formation of subsequent leaders. While extensive research has been conducted on short gaps, studies on streamer development in long air gaps of several m remain limited, especially under high-altitude conditions. In this study, we established a multi-dimensional synchronous observation platform to investigate the positive streamer discharge in a 3 m rod-plane gap subjected to lightning impulse voltage. Using an 8-frame ICCD camera with nanosecond-level time resolution, we captured the spatiotemporal evolution of streamer morphology and velocity. The results indicate that the streamer development exhibits three typical stages corresponding to the voltage evolution: rapid initiation, accelerated propagation during voltage saturation, and velocity decay. We observed that electrode geometry significantly influences the initial streamer morphology; specifically, hemispherical electrodes produce needle-like branched structures while conical electrodes result in diffuse patterns. Furthermore, statistical analysis reveals that the inception voltage of the first streamer is largely independent of the applied impulse peak, whereas the peak current shows a strong positive correlation. These findings provide critical data for refining discharge models and optimizing external insulation design in high-altitude power transmission engineering.

title: AI-Driven Task Offloading Prediction Using SAP-DE-Optimized GRU in 6G Edge Computing
authors: Akshat Gaurav, Shin-Hung Pan, Varsha Arya, Ching-Hsien Hsu, Kwok Tai Chui, Brij B. Gupta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Task offloading is a important issue in 6G edge computing, which required intelligent decision making. In this context, this paper proposed an AI-driven task offloading prediction model. The proposed model used differential evolution with self-adaptive Populations (SAP_DE) for feature selection and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) for classification. The proposed model achieves an accuracy of 80.57%, surpassing different baseline models. Compared to baseline models, it reduces FLOPs by 98.82% and total trainable parameters by 97.26%, ensuring lightweight deployment.

title: Optimization of Optical Sensors Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Akash Kumar Pradhan, Mandala Harika, Mursal Ayub Hamdani, M Laxmi Prasanna Rani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: In this work, a Machine Learning technique is employed for the inverse design of a sensor based on a one-dimensional photonic crystal structure. A dataset relating sensor reflectivity to structural parameters—such as the number of layers, refractive indices, and layer widths—is used for model training. The trained model enables rapid prediction of structural parameters without extensive numerical simulations, allowing custom-made sensors to be designed within milliseconds. This approach significantly reduces design complexity, cost, and time, while advancing thin-film optics and photonic device fabrication.

title: Rack-Aware MSR Codes with Linear Field Size and Smaller Sub-Packetization for Tolerating Multiple Erasures
authors: Hengming Zhao, Dianhua Wu, Minquan Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: In an (n, k, d) rack-aware storage model, the system consists of n nodes uniformly distributed across n successive racks, such that each rack contains u nodes of equal capacity and the reconstructive degree satisfies k = ku + v where k = ⌊k/u⌋, 0 ≤ v ≤ u − 1. Suppose there are h ≥ 1 failed nodes in a rack (called the host rack). Then together with its surviving nodes, the host rack downloads recovery data from d helper racks and repairs its failed nodes. In this paper, we focus on the rack-aware minimum storage regenerating (MSR) codes for repairing h failed nodes within the same rack. By using the coupled-layer construction with the alignment technique, we construct a class of rack-aware MSR codes for all k+1 ≤ d ≤ n−1 which achieve the small sub-packetization l = s⌈n/s⌉ where the field size q increases linearly with n and s = d − k + 1. In addition, these codes achieve the optimal repair bandwidth for 1 ≤ h ≤ u−v, and the asymptotically optimal repair bandwidth for u − v + 1 ≤ h ≤ u. In particular, they achieve the optimal access when h = u−v. It is worth noting that the existing rack-aware MSR codes which achieve the same sub-packetization l = s⌈n/s⌉ are only known for the special case of d = n − 1, h = 1, and the field size is much larger than ours.

title: New Associate Editor
authors: Boris Murmann
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits

title: A Comprehensive Federated Framework for Privacy Preserving Techniques in Sensitive Data Applications
authors: Rahul Raj D, G.R Karpagam
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Sensitive data applications such as Healthcare, Finance, Law, Human Resource Management, Real estate, and E-commerce require strong privacy protections due to the exposure of personally identifiable information. Machine learning models pose risks of privacy leakage through attacks like membership inference, Re-identification, deanonymization, Property inference, and Membership memorization etc. To counter such risks, various techniques have been widely explored, including privacy-preserving de-identification, perturbative methods, non-perturbative mechanisms and differential privacy. Recently, model-agnostic machine unlearning has become increasingly popular; this involves the removal of specific user data without retraining from scratch. Methods such as sliced sharded training, knowledge distillation, gradient reversal, and parameter editing are flexible and scaling solutions. The goal of the research is to design an intelligent, adaptive framework capable of evaluating both centralized and federated datasets, learning their statistical and structural characteristics, and recommending the most effective privacy-preserving techniques while guaranteeing the model utility.

title: Emotion Recognition from Electroencephalogram Signals using Hybrid Neural Networks
authors: Arulmozhivarman Velusamy, Harish J, Angeline J, Karthika R, Shreyas P
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Emotion recognition from electroencephalogram (EEG) signals plays a crucial role in advancing Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) systems, with applications in mental health monitoring, adaptive human-computer interaction, and affective computing. In this study, we investigate the performance of recurrent, convolutional, and hybrid deep learning architectures namely Gated Recurrent Units (GRU), Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) networks, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), and their hybrid combinations for classifying human emotions from a publicly available EEG dataset. The proposed hybrid architecture leverages the temporal feature extraction capability of recurrent models and the spatial pattern recognition strength of CNNs. Experimental results demonstrate that the hybrid model achieves a classification accuracy of 96%, significantly outperforming individual model counterparts. PCA has been used to enhance the speed and reduce memory usage of our hybrid system, significantly improving its accuracy to 98% by limiting input feature set size. This is because when using several techniques in conjunction with PCA feature engineering, we can create a system that performs more predictably and dependably for emotion recognition from EEG data in real-world applications of BCI.

title: A Cloud-Fog Digital Twin Framework for Intelligent and Scalable Structural Health Monitoring
authors: Varunkrishna B, J Umamageswaran, M Prabu
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Structural Health Monitoring (SHM) is critical for guaranteeing the safety and long-term operation of civil infrastructure. cDTSHM, Cloud-Fog Digital Twin Framework proposed in this work for real-time monitoring and preventive maintenance, maps physical and virtual structures bidirectionally using fog computing to limit data transfer and computation requirements. Deep learning (DL) models have been applied to the structural damage detection with an accuracy of 92 % for bridge case studies. Still, issues exist, including DL model accuracy, the lack of abundant labeled damage data, and inadequate flexibility to unexpected defects. Statistical models are used as baselines to oppose them, and SHM data are supplemented with 3D numerical simulations of various damage levels. A hybrid data approach is used by integrating FEM-synthetic data with hierarchical feature learning for the purpose of maximizing efficiency. Though promising, the application of pre-defined damage classes and vibration sensing limits localization of localized defects by the system. Future work will explore the implementation of unsupervised learning, incorporate optical fiber sensors to enable enhanced localization, and determine long-term scalability and robustness of cDTSHM for green infrastructure monitoring.

title: Tutor Impact Analysis Using Rough Set Theory in Personalized Learning Platforms
authors: Shoira Usmanova, Shahlo Ahmedova, Jayrona Sultanova, Muhabbat Jumaniyozova, Gulchekhrakhon Mamasoliyeva, Azizbek Sobirov
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Personalized learning platforms have surfaced as a powerful resource for increasing student engagement and personalizing instruction. Tutors' actions are one of many factors that have been recognized as important in the mediation of learning and outcomes. Unfortunately, aforementioned research has struggled to evidently model the impact of the tutor due to overlapping attributes, ambiguous and uncertain learner behaviours, and imprecise evaluative criteria. As such, the reported conclusions and recommendations are restricted from data-driven understanding about the contribution of tutor interventions to learner movement. In response to this problem, the paper presents the Rough Set-Based Tutor Influence Evaluation Model (RS-TIEM) to systematically address uncertainty employing Rough Set Theory (RST) while identifying tutor features that are important in terms of student performance. RS-TIEM has used indiscernibility relations, created lower approximations and upper approximations, and formed decision rules to show certain and possible dependencies between tutor actions and the success of the learner. These tutor actions include factors such as feedback timeliness, explanation clarity, and responsive adjustability, which all evoke a positive contribution to learning outcome success, and thus should contribute to a description and subsequent RS-TIEM evaluation. Empirical evaluation has shown that RS-TIEM outperforms conventional methods in interpretive robustness, accuracy, and transparency, and ensures trustworthiness in monitoring tutor performance. The proposed model has been effectively utilized in personalized learning environments, enhancing tutor assignment and adaptive learning processes.

title: SecureSynth: An Innovative Differential Privacy–Driven Framework Employing DP-AUTO-GAN for Cross-Domain Synthetic Data and Secure Analytics
authors: C. Devipriya, M.L. Valarmathi, S. Divyapriya, B. Suganya
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The growing demand for privacy-preserving data sharing has accelerated research in synthetic data generation, enabling secure access to sensitive datasets without compromising analytical utility. This study proposes SecureSynth, a novel cross-domain synthetic data generation framework that integrates DP-AutoGAN with differential privacy guarantees to produce high-fidelity, privacy-protected datasets. The framework is evaluated on three real-world datasets—Diabetes, Adult, and Phishing—under two experimental settings: (i) models trained on original data and tested on synthetic data, and (ii) models trained on synthetic data and tested on original data. Results show that SecureSynth consistently outperforms baseline approaches, achieving higher accuracy and F1-scores compared to DP-AutoGAN and Multilevel Clustering. The framework yields up to a 1.9% improvement in accuracy and attains an F1-score of 88.2% on the Diabetes dataset. These findings demonstrate that SecureSynth effectively balances differential privacy and utility, making it suitable for privacy-sensitive applications in healthcare, cybersecurity, and socioeconomic analytics.

title: Waste Heat Recovery from Power Electronic Systems Using Thermo Electric Modules
authors: Vivek R S, Divyamol S, Jayan Pp, Aby Joseph
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Waste heat recovery presents a promising avenue for improving the energy efficiency of power electronic systems, where significant thermal energy is often dissipated without utilization. This research paper investigates the application of thermoelectric generator (TEG) modules for harnessing waste heat from the exhaust pipe of such systems. TEG modules, operating on the Seebeck effect, convert thermal gradients directly into electrical energy. In this study, a TEG module is subjected to a controlled temperature difference of 30 °C between an exhaust gas temperature of 60 °C and an ambient temperature of 30 °C. A simulation is performed to evaluate the thermal and electrical behavior of the system, focusing on the performance of a thermocouple embedded within the TEG module. The generated electrical output is assessed for its feasibility in powering a Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA). An analytical model is also developed to complement the numerical simulation, and the results from both methods are compared to validate the approach. The outcomes of this research highlight the potential of TEG-based waste heat recovery for power electronics

title: TCAD-Based Investigation of a Wavelength-Tunable Near-Infrared Photodetector Using Vertical TFET with Optical Gate
authors: Pasumala Vamshidhar Reddy, Raushan Kumar, Kallepelli Sagar, Anil Kumar Pathakamuri
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This paper presents a novel Near-Infrared Gated Vertical Tunnel Field-Effect Transistor (NIRG-VTFET) photodetector designed for high sensitivity and spectral selectivity across varying NIR wavelengths. The proposed device employs a vertically aligned silicon optical gate illuminated by incident NIR light, where photogenerated carriers modulate the tunneling barrier at the source–channel interface, enabling optical control over the tunneling process. Device characteristics are comprehensively analyzed using Sentaurus TCAD simulations by incorporating optical generation, non-local band-to-band tunneling, and quantum confinement models. The simulation results reveal that a decrease in incident wavelength from 1050 nm to 750 nm significantly enhances the photogeneration rate, optical potential, ON-current, sensitivity, and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), with peak sensitivity observed at 750 nm. These characteristics highlight the device’s potential for high-performance, low-power photodetection in NIR imaging, biomedical sensing, and optoelectronic communication.

title: Quantitative Analysis of Operations & Maintenance Outsourcing Challenges in Thermal Power Plants: A Comprehensive Statistical Investigation Based on Industry Survey
authors: Aseemkumar Joshi, Abhishek Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Operations and Maintenance (O&M) outsourcing in thermal power plants presents complex multifaceted challenges affecting operational efficiency, cost optimization, and stakeholder satisfaction. This comprehensive study presents quantitative analysis based on a structured 20-question survey administered to 45 Heads of Departments across 12 thermal power facilities representing 8,400 MW cumulative capacity in India. The research employs advanced statistical methodologies including exploratory factor analysis, multiple regression modeling, cluster analysis, and structural equation modeling to identify critical challenge factors and their interdependencies. Key findings reveal that remote location challenges, as identified through factor analysis, constitute the primary factor explaining 67.3% of variance among geographic and infrastructure-related outsourcing difficulties with statistical significance (p < 0.001). Community relations issues directly influence 58.9% of respondents’ operational decision-making processes, as measured through Likert-scale assessments, and Productivity Linked Performance Based Contracts (PLPBC) implementation demonstrates 43.2% higher implementation complexity scores (M = 4.23, SD = 0.68) compared to traditional manpower contracts (M = 2.94, SD = 0.57), representing a large effect size (Cohen’s d = 2.05). Reliability analysis confirms survey instrument validity (Cronbach’s alpha = 0.847), while principal component analysis identifies four primary challenge clusters explaining 82.4% of total variance. Multiple regression modeling achieves R-square = 0.789 in predicting outsourcing success outcomes. The study provides empirical foundation for evidence-based decision-making in thermal power plant O&M outsourcing through comprehensive quantitative insights derived from industry practitioners’ experiences.

title: An Empirical Study on the Talent Cultivation Mechanism in Higher Education Empowered by the Integration of Virtual and Real Learning Environments
authors: Jun Xiao, Gaocai Fu, Zhangjie Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: This article analyzes the current situation of cultivating innovative practical abilities among college students at home and abroad, and summarizes the problems in cultivating innovative practical abilities among college students under the current trend of engineering education certification. Aiming at the problems of disconnection between practical training courses and the market, outdated teaching resources, insufficient training conditions, and unrealistic teaching situations, a three-dimensional, three-layer, and three objective training model for innovative practical abilities of university talents based on the school-enterprise collaboration model is proposed. Finally, taking the Mechanical Engineering major at W University as an example, a collaborative talent cultivation information platform system between schools and enterprises was constructed and validated in practice, providing reference for the reform of collaborative teaching between schools and enterprises.

title: A Reassessment of the Role of the Human Instructor in a GenAI-Equipped Learning Environment: Quid Nunc de Magistro Fiet?
authors: Abdurrahman Gülbeyaz
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: Education has never been “natural”; it has always been shaped by the dominant media of its time. With generative AI entering the learning space, the order shifts more radically: for the first time, a technology competes not only with the teacher's tools but with the teacher's core function - the production, structuring, and legitimation of knowledge. From a genealogical perspective, the teacher appears less as an anthropological constant than as a function of techno-social conditions. At the same time, error studies show how authority logics and ritualization can turn the teacher into a source of persistent mistakes. In GenAI environments, the classroom becomes co-semiotic; categories such as authorship and originality begin to slide. Quid nunc de magistro fiet? The teacher loses the monopoly yet persists in altered roles framing relevance, curating error ecologies, moderating ethics and citation, and co-creating situational curricula. The proposed transition is from a Decalogue of infallible rules to a “control grammar”: meta-rules of visibility, provenance, transformation, and cross-checking. The teacher “dies” as the old figure and survives as the new - not lord of answers but master of questions.

title: Neuro-Genetic Dynamic Topology Optimization Using Multi-Layer Energy Profiling (NGDTOMLEP) for Next-Generation Sensor Meshes
authors: Sureshkumar G, N. Muthumani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Large-scale, mission-critical Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are subjected to ongoing technical challenges, because these consume minimal energy, establish and maintain dynamic connectivity and deliver data efficiently as power and topology conditions vary. To solve these limitations, this paper proposes a new Neuro-Genetic Dynamic Topology Optimization using Multi-Layer Energy Profiling (NGDTO-MLEP) algorithm. Proposed structure incorporates the multi-layer neural predictive node role design architecture coupled to the genetic founded topology re-shaping scheme that allows the real-time capture of environmental and operational variations. The Multi-Layer Energy Profiling module actively maintains residual energy profiles at multiple tiers of network structure ensuring that the module proactively executes load balancing and hot spot avoidance. As opposed to conventional clustering or genetic algorithms, NGDTO-MLEP includes predictive mobility handling and crossover-mutation energy heuristics that ensure the best routing paths are maintained between set destinations, even in partially mobile or fault-flat sensor configurations. Through comprehensive simulations, the results indicate that NGDTO-MLEP performs better than other current state-of-theart protocols in regard to the network lifetime (↑ 32.7 %), packet delivery ratio (↑ 18.4 %) and average latency (↓ 22.1 %) at different densities of nodes and traffic patterns. The modular implementation of the algorithm allows cross-layer optimization and fits well to the heterogeneous integration of Internet of Things (IoT) and WSNs. Therefore, this algorithm is a scalable solution to the next-generation sensor mesh in industrial, environmental and smart-city use cases. The findings demonstrate the potential of NGDTO-MLEP as a powerful, energy-efficient and self-optimizing topology optimization platform and lay foundations on the way to sustainable and intelligent WSN deployments.

title: Theoretical Modelling of Kretschmann SPR sensor to Analyse Refractive Index Shifts from Healthy to Cancer Cell
authors: Akash Srivastava, Sajal Agarwal, Sandhir Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: The present study investigates a multilayered surface plasmon resonance (SPR) biosensor based on the Kretschmann configuration, aimed at detecting and distinguishing various cancer cell types through changes in refractive index (RI). The analysis covers biological samples ranging from normal cells (RI=1.350) to highly malignant glioblastoma cells (RI = 1.447), evaluating critical performance metrics such as resonance angle, full width at half maximum (FWHM), sensitivity, figure of merit (FoM), and penetration depth (PD). Simulation results demonstrate a consistent increase in the SPR angle with rising RI, yielding an average sensitivity of 86.23°/RIU and peaking at 97.02°/RIU during the transition from glioma to glioblastoma. The incorporation of Tungsten Ditelluride (WTe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) and graphene significantly enhances electric field confinement and analyte interaction, resulting in improved FoM and sensing performance. Additionally, a comparative study between conventional SPR and long-range SPR (LRSPR) configurations indicates that LRSPR offers substantially deeper field penetration (~995.20 nm) and a narrower resonance width (FWHM = 0.43°), making it especially suitable for whole-cell detection.

title: Intelligent Monitoring and Analysis Algorithm for C17ORF49 Gene Expression in ERα-Positive Breast Cancer
authors: Liya Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the dimensional limitations of traditional single-modal analysis in clinical decision-making for ERα-positive breast cancer, a multimodal feature fusion attention algorithm (MFFA) was proposed. This algorithm employs a four-layer progressive architecture. The input layer constructs a multidimensional system by selecting RNA-seq gene data (including C17ORF49), clinical pathology data, and pathology slide imaging features. The feature extraction layer processes gene and time-series data using an improved CNN and bidirectional LSTM, respectively. The attention fusion layer utilizes dynamic weighting to enhance key features. The output layer performs dual-task risk stratification and gene expression prediction in parallel. The experiment utilized dual-source data from the TCGA database (1200 cases) and a local hospital (300 cases). After preprocessing with KNN interpolation and 3σ outlier removal, the dataset was partitioned into a 7:1.5:1.5 split. Using NVIDIA A100 hardware, MFFA was compared with SVM, XGBoost, and a standard attention model. The results showed that MFFA achieved an Accuracy of 94.2% and an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.973 for classification tasks, representing improvements of 7.7% and 8.1% respectively over XGBoost. The RMSE for regression tasks was 0.086, a 43.4% reduction over the standard attention model. In a noise interference experiment, MFFA achieved an Accuracy of 89.5% even with 30% noise, and the AUC variation across datasets was only 1.5%, demonstrating its robustness and generalizability, providing support for precision diagnosis and treatment of breast cancer.

title: Remote-Operated Floating Garbage Collector for Surface Water Cleanup
authors: Prottay Mazumder, Supriya, Anusha, Muskaan Bharadwaj, Swati Swayamsiddha, Manas Ranjan Tripathy
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: The growing problem of surface water pollution, which is caused by industrial pollutants, floating debris, floating waste in rivers and lakes severely impacts aquatic habitats and human activities. This work describes the creation of a remotely operated device designed to gather floating debris from water surfaces. The prototype combines propulsion and conveyor mechanisms with wireless monitoring through ESP32 CAM and Blynk IoT, allowing real-time control and reduced manual effort.The system integrates BO motors for propulsion and conveyor-driven waste collection, efficiently gathering debris into a dedicated storage unit. Remote monitoring and control are facilitated by an ESP32 CAM module and the Blynk application, enabling real-time oversight and reducing the need for manual intervention. The structural framework, constructed from PVC and CPVC pipes, guarantees resilience, buoyancy, and versatility in a range of aquatic conditions. By leveraging automation and remote operation, the proposed system minimizes labor costs, enhances cleaning efficiency, and contributes to sustainable water management efforts. This project aligns with global environmental initiatives, such as supports initiatives focused on river rejuvenation and sustainable water cleanup. Promising avenues to tackle these issues are provided by recent developments in robotics, computer vision, Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, and cloud computing offer promising pathways to address these challenges. Design and implementation of this low cost IoT project implies sustainable use of these resources for real time water surface cleaning.

title: Research on an Automatic Safety Compliance Monitoring System for Construction Sites Based on Computer Vision
authors: Hang Chen, Chunlin Liu, Shengfang Qiao, Junzhi Li, Peitao Gao, Hui Liu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To address the problems of low efficiency, strong subjectivity, and poor real-time performance in traditional construction site safety monitoring, this paper proposes an automatic safety compliance monitoring system for construction sites based on computer vision. The system integrates multiple open-source datasets to construct a standardized Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) dataset, compares the performance of YOLOv8-YOLOv12 series models, selects the optimal lightweight YOLOv12 as the core detection model, and develops a Web-based visual management platform. It realizes real-time detection of safety helmet and reflective vest wearing status, violation record management, and hierarchical decision support. Experimental results show that the system achieves a Precision of 96.8 %, Recall of 95.3 %, mAP50 of 96.1 %, and an inference speed of 58 FPS. The Web interface supports data statistics, violation traceability, and intelligent decision suggestions, which can effectively improve the efficiency and compliance level of construction site safety management.

title: BiYO(5) - A Pioneering Approach for Vision Based Fall Detection Using Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Networks and YOLOv5
authors: Priya S, Amshakala K
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Fall detection systems play a crucial role in monitoring the safety of the people all around. Severe falls may lead to serious physical injuries and if timely assistance is not provided it may even lead to death. Object detection techniques help to precisely identify and locate the falls. YOLO (You Only Look Once) is one such single pass object detection algorithm which works fine when the distance of the person is at the optimal range from the camera. YOLO failed to recognize the falls in situations like poor resolution images, crowded environments and if the person falls far away from the camera and appear very smaller in size. The proposed work aims to handle these setbacks in real time. The proposed methodology incorporates Bi-directional Feature Pyramid Networks (BiFPN) in the YOLO V5 architecture to extract the comprehensive movements at diverse scales. Adaptive Spatial Feature Fusion (ASFF) fuses the relevant features from the distinct layers of the neural networks to capture more intricate patterns even in the low resolution images. Experimental result analysis proved that the model was more robust as it was able to perform well in the crowded environments, in the environments with low resolution and also if the size of the individual is very smaller in the input frame. Additional Cross Stage Partial Networking (CSP) layers and Coordinate Attention (CA) mechanism are also introduced in the YOLO backbone to enhance the feature extraction process.

title: Knowledge-Distillation-Driven Edge Fault Diagnosis for Smart Grid Energy Collection Terminals
authors: Hongxi Wang, Bo Feng, Qilin Han, Chong Sun, Zhengang Shi, Chao Yan
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: With the in-depth development of the Power Internet of Things, electric energy information collection terminals have become indispensable key devices in the new power system. Confronted with a sharp increase in terminal numbers, higher operational reliability requirements, and stricter demands for data accuracy, traditional fault diagnosis methods for such terminals exhibit significant limitations: reliance on manual inspections is inefficient and slow to respond, while cloud-based analysis methods are highly dependent on communication networks and pose risks of user privacy leakage. Against this background, this paper proposes a lightweight fault diagnosis method based on knowledge distillation. This method employs a Transformer model deployed in the cloud as the teacher model and an improved VGG network model embedded at the edge nodes as the student model. Through structural optimization and feature distillation, model lightweighting is achieved. The two models interact via communication networks, enabling timely and dynamic updates of knowledge at the edge nodes. This ensures diagnostic accuracy while reducing the computational and storage overhead of edge devices. Experimental results show that the knowledge-distilled improved VGG network model maintains high accuracy while saving computational resources, demonstrating that the proposed method can effectively improve the efficiency of fault diagnosis, reduce costs for edge nodes, and enhance user privacy protection capabilities.

title: Hybrid Mode of Operation for a Grid-Integrated Dual Voltage Source Inverter
authors: Nimitha Muraleedharan, Mahesh K. Mishra
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: With the growing integration of renewable energy sources (RES)-fed voltage source inverters (VSIs), ensuring stable and reliable operation of the microgrid system, both in grid-connected and islanded modes, is essential to ensure uninterrupted power supply to loads. This necessitates a robust control strategy capable of handling dynamic transitions and grid disturbances. In this work, a hybrid mode of operation is presented for the dual voltage source inverter (DVSI) topology consisting of a parallel structure of two VSIs that provide active and reactive power to the load independently. The RES-fed VSI operates in grid-forming (GFM) mode, and the second inverter acts as a compensator in grid-following (GFL) mode. The GFM inverter regulates the system voltage and frequency, while the GFL VSI operation is dependent on its connection to the GFM inverter. The system also enables low voltage ridethrough (LVRT) by providing reactive power support to the grid. The DVSI system can be operated under different operating conditions, such as grid-connected, islanded, and weak or faulted grids, ensuring voltage and frequency regulation, load power continuity, and power quality of the system. The developed control strategy for the DVSI system is validated through simulation in MATLAB/Simulink under various disturbances.

title: Predicting Maternal Health Risks Using Neural Networks and Feature Engineering
authors: P Loganathan, P Gowsikraja, P A Selvaraj, N D Shrinivassan, V Yashika, K Vijayakumar
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: A total of 1,014 maternal patients from a clinical setting developed a modeling framework for estimating maternal health risk incorporating physiological indicators of age, temperature, blood glucose, heart rate and blood pressure. Clinical indicators were distilled by advanced medical feature engineering to discover meaningful indicators such as blood pressure ratios, pulse pressure, mean arterial pressure, and a risk score for overall assessment. Maternal risk was examined and classified as low, mid, and high risk using ensemble machine learning methods (XGBoost, LightGBM, Random Forest and Gradient Boosting) and a modified neural network. Class imbalance was identified and addressed with the ADASYN approach, while an explainable AI approach using SHAP was applied to facilitate interpretation of the outcomes from both ensemble and deep learning models. The highest-performing method yielded 95% accuracy and F1 score of 0.95, suggesting that combining clinical feature engineering with ensemble and neural network methods of modeling maternal health risk may be effective.

title: Spatio-Temporal Coupled Coordinated Control of Electric Vehicles and Central HVAC Loads in Distribution Networks
authors: Yiming Chang, Qiaochu Zhao, Huan Pang, Yuan Wang, Yi Xu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: With the rapid growth of electric vehicles (EVs) and the increasing share of central air-conditioning (HVAC) demand in distribution systems, peak-load mitigation based on a single resource often triggers adverse effects such as synchronized charging spikes or comfort rebound. This paper addresses the coordinated control of EV charging/discharging and HVAC loads under spatio-temporal coupling constraints. First, we formulate a unified modeling framework in which EV battery dynamics and HVAC thermal inertia are embedded into a distribution-network-aware spatio-temporal coupling model. Second, a quantifiable coordination potential assessment method is proposed to characterize the feasible regulation boundaries and availability across nodes and time intervals. Third, we develop a multi-objective coordinated optimization strategy that jointly considers operational economy, user comfort, and distribution network security, and further present an engineering-oriented rolling control scheme. The proposed framework provides a reproducible modeling and optimization paradigm for coordinated demand-side flexibility at community/campus scales.

title: Gamification in Online Learning: Evaluating Engagement and Classroom Performance in Higher Education
authors: Akanksha Upadhyaya, Deepika, Disha Garg
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Gamification has gained significant traction as an innovative approach to enhance online learning, particularly in higher education. This study investigates the impact of gamification elements—such as points, badges, and leaderboards—on engagement, motivation, and academic performance among online learners. Drawing on data collected from 419 students representing diverse demographics and academic backgrounds, multiple linear regression analysis reveals that perceived enjoyment, motivation, and engagement are the strongest predictors of perceived classroom performance, while factors like satisfaction, ease of use, and self-efficacy also contribute positively. Notably, the sense of competition was not found to have a significant effect. The literature review and theoretical framework further validate the selection of these constructs, referencing established models such as the Gamification Acceptance Model (GAM), Self-Determination Theory (SDT), and the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM). The outcomes suggest that thoughtfully designed gamified elements can enhance both the effectiveness and satisfaction of online learning, with implications for increased motivation, learning achievement, and student satisfaction. However, individual learner differences and novelty effects should be carefully considered for sustained impact.

title: Research and Development of a Fully Automated Testing Assembly Line for Electric Energy Meters and Collection Terminals
authors: Wenjun Zhu, Yuyun Zhang, Jiajun Feng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This article designs and develops a full performance automated testing assembly line for electric energy meters and collection terminals, achieving automated testing and full process management of electric energy meters, intelligent IoT electric energy meters, concentrators, specialized transformer terminals, and secondary circuit inspection instruments for full performance testing. The automated testing assembly line has functions such as automatic sample grabbing, automatic flow, automatic testing, anomaly monitoring, and test management. The main functional modules of the detection system include automatic grasping unit, conveying unit, appearance testing unit, electromagnetic compatibility testing unit, multifunctional testing unit, anomaly monitoring unit, and intelligent control unit. Design and development of automated detection assembly line to enhance the detection capabilities of electric energy meters and collection terminals, ensuring the supply quality of measurement and collection equipment.

title: Secure Human Identification with Multi-Modal Biometrics Using Weighted Jackal Optimization and Transformer-Based Squeezenet
authors: Vijay M, Golla Aravind Kumar, Ganji Umanageswar, Edamakanti Ramanji Reddy, Jangala Gurunadham, N Nandhini
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The restrictions of unimodal systems have resulted in the introduction of multimodal biometric systems. The paper introduces a skeleton based fusion-based multimodal identification system which involves the use of fingerprint, palmprint and iris data. To start with, a guided non-local filter enhances the quality of the image whereas SiaNet is used to extract discriminative features. Parallel maximum covariance fusion then fuses multimodal embeddings, optimizing the best features using a jackal optimization strategy with weights. Lastly, a transformer based SqueezeNet classifier is used to perform recognition. Its system has 99.64 percent accuracy, 93.33 percent and 97.76 percent 5 and 10 fold cross-validation accuracy respectively. It is a computationally simple model which can be applied in real time identification tasks.

title: A Framework for Automated PCB Inspection through Gerber Files Comparison using Computer Vision and LLM
authors: Elton Alencar, Janderson Lira, Pablo H. U. de Pinho, Jair de Almeida, Luziane L. S. Arouca, Daniel Lins Da Silva, Marcel Silva, Agemilson Pimentel
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Printed Circuit Boards (PCBs) play a critical role in the manufacturing of electronic devices, requiring accurate component placement and precise layer connections. Visual inspection ensures the correctness of PCB layouts, but manual comparison between design versions is time-consuming and prone to human error. In this paper, an AI-driven hybrid framework that integrates Computer Vision (CV) techniques with Large Language Model (LLM) is proposed to automate the detection and reporting of design changes across Gerber files. The system converts Gerber data into images, performs pixel-wise comparison using Digital Image Processing (DIP) methods, and classifies differences into additions, removals, or modifications. The extracted discrepancies are then described and summarized through technical text automatically generated by gemma3:4b, a pre-trained LLM. Experimental results with 186 comparisons demonstrated an average total processing time of 52.6 s per analysis, with report generation accounting for most of the execution time (37 s). The approach achieved consistent and accurate detection of geometric variations, reducing inspection time and reliance on multiple third-party tools. This framework provides a reliable and extensible foundation for automated PCB inspection, contributing to smarter, AI-assisted manufacturing workflows in consumer electronics, providing a centralized service that can be integrated into the company’s local infrastructure.

title: Adaptive ML-Driven Detection and Defense Against Side-Channel Attacks in Blockchain Consensus Networks
authors: Love Allen Chijioke Ahakonye, Jae Woo Kim, Hamza Ibrahim, Jae Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Blockchain-based systems, renowned for their de-centralized nature and cryptographic security, are increasingly vulnerable to side-channel attacks in consumer electronics and decentralized networks. Existing defense mechanisms against SCAs, such as constant-time execution and randomization, often fall short at detecting subtle, dynamic attack patterns and at adapting to evolving threat landscapes. This research addresses these gaps, particularly in blockchain consensus mechanisms, where communication patterns and timing sequences are prone to exploitation by adversaries. This study proposes a machine learning-driven framework that not only detects side-channel attacks in real-time but also dynamically adjusts countermeasures, such as masking and jittering, based on attack severity. The framework integrates multi-channel feature extraction, lightweight classifiers, and adaptive feedback systems to improve detection efficiency without compromising system performance. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach, especially the hybrid CNN-LSTM model, outperforms conventional methods, achieving detection rates of up to 91.9% for complex attacks such as Eclipse and Burst Pattern. This research enhances the security and resilience of blockchain consensus protocols by offering a scalable, energy-efficient solution for real-time attack detection in high-throughput, decentralized environments.

title: A Study on a Fault Diagnosis System for a Diesel Engine Fuel Injection System
authors: Lingdan Shi, Feng Shen, Wei Liu, Hui Ruan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper primarily introduces the application of artificial neural network technology in the fault diagnosis of diesel engine fuel systems. For the first time, neural networks are combined with virtual instrument technology and successfully applied to the fault diagnosis of diesel engine fuel systems. The study attempts to compare the learning speed and diagnostic performance of Radial Basis Function (RBF) and Back Propagation (BP) neural networks. Research indicates that combining RBF neural networks with virtual instruments for diesel engine fault diagnosis yields favorable diagnostic results and holds promising prospects for practical engineering applications engineering applications.

title: PFHFF: Parameter-Friendly Hierarchical Feature Fusion Module for Enhanced Epicardium Segmentation in CT Images
authors: Ibuki Naka, Jain Rahul Kumar, Yin-hao Li, Xianhua Han, Atsuyuki Wada, Atsunori Kashiwagi, Yutaro Iwamoto, Kiyosumi Maeda, Yen-Wei Chen, Yuji Tezuka
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Epicardial adipose tissue (EAT) is strongly correlated with diabetes risk, but its diagnosis is easily affected by external factors such as diet. CT-based quantification of EAT requires accurate epicardium segmentation, which remains challenging due to low contrast and thin boundaries. Conventional CNN-based models like DenseUNet effectively capture local details but fail to model global context, leading to over-extraction and irregular contours. To address this, we propose a dual-encoder network combining MedViT and CNN, integrated through a novel Parameter-Friendly Hierarchical Feature Fusion (PFHFF) module. PFHFF aligns multi-scale features from both encoders and fuses them using element-wise multiplication, functioning as a lightweight gating mechanism to emphasize regions jointly activated by global and local features. This design enhances boundary delineation while maintaining computational efficiency. Experiments show that our method achieves smoother contours closer to the ground truth and improves F1 score by over 3.5% and IoU by 2.4% compared to conventional approaches.

title: FIL-Quant: An Efficient Federated Incremental Compression via Error-Regulated Structured Pruning for Consumer Electronics
authors: Weiyu Wang, Xiuheng Liao, Jinhua Chen, Tong Liu, Tao Yu, Shilong Zhang, Keping Yu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative model training without centralizing raw data. In consumer electronics, particularly smart home camera scenarios, the dynamic environment and long-term deployment require continuous learning of new categories. However, the weak uplink bandwidth typical of consumer home Wi-Fi networks and the low-frequency communication used by consumer camera devices for energy conservation necessitate Federated Incremental Learning (FIL). Traditional uniform compression strategies cause compression noise to accumulate across learning stages. This accumulation exacerbates catastrophic forgetting of old categories and destabilizes decision boundaries for new categories, making it difficult to maintain performance while reducing communication overhead. This paper proposes a federated incremental model compression method specifically designed for consumer electronics smart home scenarios. The method addresses three key constraints: staged incremental learning, coexistence of old and new categories, and the bandwidth limitations inherent to consumer devices. It performs structured compression while controlling how compression errors affect old category representations and new category decision boundaries, balancing stability and efficiency. Experimental results show the method compresses uploaded models to one-sixth of their original size without accuracy loss, achieving a 6× compression ratio. This significantly reduces transmission burden between consumer devices and the cloud, outperforming baseline compression schemes and meeting the practical requirements of consumer electronics applications.

title: Design and Analysis of a Linear Induction Motor Based Syringe Pump for High-Precision Biomedical Applications
authors: Raju T, Sharmili Das
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The precise and pulsation-free actuation of syringe pumps is a critical requirement in modern biomedical instrumentation. To meet this demand, this paper explores the use of a Linear Induction Motor (LIM) as a direct-drive solution, which avoids the mechanical backlash and jitter of traditional gear systems. We present the detailed design and analysis of a 2pole, single-sided LIM with a single-layer winding. A 3D model was constructed and simulated in ANSYS Maxwell to perform a transient analysis under three-phase voltage excitation. The study investigates critical performance metrics such as the magnitude and stability of the thrust force, induced secondary effects, and the overall magnetic field distribution. The finite element analysis demonstrates that the proposed LIM design meets the stringent requirements for smooth and precise motion, making it a viable and effective actuator for next-generation medical infusion systems.

title: Electrode-Level Association Analysis of EEG Connectivity in Children with ADHD Using Positive Pointwise Mutual Information
authors: Lakshmi Kalpana Roy K, Shreya Venkatesh, Malar B
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Computational Neuroscience is an interdisciplinary field leveraging mathematical and computational methodologies to develop theoretical models for the diagnosis of various brain disorders via neural connectivity analysis. Positive Point-wise Mutual Information (PPMI), a non-linear measure traditionally used in Natural Language Processing, is introduced in this study as an innovative analytical tool for assessing functional brain connectivity in electroencephalography (EEG) data. This work discusses the application of PPMI in neurological signal analysis to characterize altered connectivity patterns in children diagnosed with Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) compared with age-matched healthy controls. By quantifying the probability of strong associations between EEG electrode pairs, PPMI provides a robust framework for identifying connectivity alterations that conventional linear measures may overlook. The study employs multiple visualization methods-including line graphs to represent connectivity probabilities, bar graphs illustrating pairwise association strengths, network graphs depicting global organization, and topoplot-based spatial projections-to reveal region-specific connectivity deviations. Results demonstrate significant reorganization of functional networks in the ADHD group. Several electrodes, particularly Fp2, C3, C4, P3, and Fz, consistently exhibit elevated probabilities of forming strong associations, indicating localized hyperconnectivity. In contrast, evaluation of specific electrode pairings such as Fp1-Pz, F3-F7, and Fp2-F4 uncovers a complex interplay of both heightened and reduced connectivity, reflecting heterogeneous network disruptions. Overall, the findings highlight PPMI as a promising biomarker for capturing subtle yet meaningful connectivity variations in ADHD. Its capacity to elucidate non-linear functional interactions positions it as a valuable tool for advancing computational neurodiagnostics and improving understanding of ADHD-related neural dysregulation. c

title: A Computer Vision Based Approach for Real Time Classification and Placement
authors: Vishwa Priya V, Ladson Samuel Newton, Hariharan S, Kavishvar S, Niranjan R
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: This paper explains a ball sorting system that uses computer vision and a robot arm. A YOLOv8 model is trained to detect red, green, and blue balls. The model is tested with images and a live camera feed to make sure it works in real time. A conveyor belt moves the balls to a fixed pickup point. A camera above the conveyor captures the image of the ball. The YOLOv8 model identifies the color of the ball. The system then sends commands to a robot arm in RoboDK. The robot picks the ball from the pickup point and places it into the basket that matches its color. The system is made using Python, YOLOv8, OpenCV, and the RoboDK API. The results show that the robot can detect and sort balls correctly.

title: Research on Wind Load Monitoring Technology for Transmission Lines Based on Distributed Optical Fiber Sensing
authors: Kunpeng Ji, Chang Liu, Jingshan Han, Peng Li
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper addresses the need for wind load monitoring on high-voltage transmission lines by conducting research on wind-induced vibration monitoring utilizing Distributed Acoustic Sensing (DAS) technology. A sensing system was deployed on a 500 kV transmission line, successfully capturing the conductor vibration responses during the transit of Typhoon “Bebinca” (No. 13) and Typhoon “Pulasan” (No. 14). The system achieved real-time, distributed monitoring of the vibration status along the Optical Fiber Composite Overhead Ground Wire (OPGW), obtaining the spectral characteristics, energy distribution, and spatiotemporal evolution patterns of conductor vibrations during the typhoons. The study demonstrates that DAS technology can effectively monitor the dynamic response of transmission lines under wind loads, providing a novel technical approach for line condition assessment and wind disaster early warning.

title: Stretching Effect Estimation Model Based on Surface Electromyography
authors: Takuma Asano, Teppei Araki, Hiroshi Kawaguchi, Shintaro Izumi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Static stretching is a fundamental conditioning practice; however, objectively and quantitatively evaluating its physiological effects remains a significant challenge. Conventional surface electromyography analysis has predominantly relied on single-channel derived indices, such as Root Mean Square and Median Frequency. However, relying solely on single scalar metrics is insufficient to comprehensively capture local variations in muscle activity and alterations in complex neuromuscular control, suggesting that the establishment of more multifaceted evaluation methodologies is valuable. In this study, we propose a novel stretching evaluation method based on spatiotemporal pattern recognition using 16-channel surface electromyography and deep learning, specifically aimed at application in consumer electronic devices such as wearable devices for personal fitness management. Specifically, scalograms generated by Continuous Wavelet Transform and information regarding the sensor's spatial arrangement are input into a Convolutional Neural Network to construct a model that classifies the degree of stretching. Experimental results with five healthy adults demonstrated that the proposed model achieved high classification performance, with an F1-score of 88.2%. Furthermore, visualization analysis of the feature space using t-SNE confirmed that data from an intermediate stretching duration, which was excluded from training, was continuously mapped to the intermediate region between the two classes. This suggests that the proposed model learns not only discrete class classification but also the continuous transition dynamics—reflecting the depth of physiological change—of the muscle state associated with stretching intervention. This method holds potential for application as an objective biomarker for defining optimal stretching durations tailored to individual muscle characteristics for next-generation personal training assistants.

title: Effect of Patient Movements on Non-invasive Microwave Temperature Monitoring during Head and Neck Hyperthermia: Numerical Study
authors: Barbora Smahelova, Jakub Kollar, Jan Vrba
publish: 2026 International Conference on Medical Microwave Imaging Applications (ICMWIA)
abstract: Cancer treatment often requires multimodal approaches, where hyperthermia (HT) is an adjuvant therapy to radiotherapy and chemotherapy. Accurate temperature monitoring of HT is essential for both safety and efficacy. Differential microwave imaging (MWI) has been proposed as a non-invasive monitoring method, but it relies on the assumption of a static background between the reference and monitoring phases. In clinical practice, however, patient movement and device displacement are inevitable and may compromise reconstruction quality. In this study, numerical simulations were performed to evaluate the effect of patient and applicator movements on differential MWI during head and neck HT. Two scenarios were tested: positioning differences relative to the reference electric field model and movement occurring during the differential monitoring phase. The results showed that even minimal positioning differences substantially degraded reconstructions, with ultra-wideband (UWB) antenna rotation of only 5° reducing the Dice similarity coefficient (DSC) to 23% of the reference value. Movements of the HT applicator had a weaker effect, confirming that misalignments of the imaging antennas are more critical to MWI than applicator displacements. Most critically, any movement during the monitoring phase resulted in complete reconstruction failure (DSC = 0), eliminating the ability to detect the heated region. These findings highlight that precise patient positioning, robust immobilization, optimized antenna placement, and motion-robust system design are essential prerequisites for successful clinical translation of differential MWI in HT monitoring.

title: The Role of Small Language Models in Edge Computing and On-Device AI: A Review
authors: Jersho Shafin D J, Lankesh M, Allin Geo Vargheese
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Small Language Models (SLMs) have surfaced as effective and efficient substitutes for Large Language Models, particularly in low-power, memory-limited, and privacyconscious settings. This paper offers a thorough examination of the architectures, optimization strategies, and deployment methodologies that facilitate high-performance on-device and edge-based inference utilizing SLMs. Contemporary encoder- and decoder-based SLMs-including DistilBERT, TinyBERT, MobileBERT, MiniLM, Qwen2, and Llama-3 8B-are analyzed with an emphasis on their architectural design decisions, compression techniques, and computational trade-offs. Essential optimization approaches such as pruning, quantization, and knowledge distillation are investigated, along with deployment-specific improvements including operator fusion, hardware-aware tuning, and adaptive model scaling. Practical deployment workflows for edge devices like the Raspberry Pi 5 are also outlined to illustrate real-world applicability. Additionally, the paper underscores significant areas where SLMs are transforming edge intelligence, including on-device voice assistants, customer support systems, predictive text engines, industrial IoT analytics, and healthcare diagnostics. The findings indicate that SLMs provide low-latency, privacy-preserving, and energy-efficient AI functionalities, underscoring their importance in the forthcoming generation of intelligent edge systems.

title: Implementation of System Identification for an EV Powered with Renewable Sources
authors: Gondu Vykunta Rao, Sudha Radhika
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: In the current industrial era, electric vehicle sales and technologies have become a significant area of interest for the commoner and industrialists, which has created a new scope for novel ideas in every sector of EVs. This work proposes a new methodology to meet the needs of academics and industries. The proposed work aims for the modelling and system identification (S.I) of a renewable-powered hybrid vehicle using a transfer function approach rather than current optimisation techniques because of the computation time and complexity in implementation for central sub system of the vehicle, like motor and fuel cell (FC), which further integrated with other subsystems of the vehicle sources as battery and super capacitor. The paper introduces traditional methods of nonlinear system identification, then implementation tendency and prospects of S.I., and validation in MATLAB, along with real-time hardware.

title: Multi Modal AI Driven Closed-Loop Governance of Electricity Bill Settlement Risks
authors: Chao Zhang, Haibo Ren, Pupu Guo, Hongli Su, Ning Niu, Xiuyan Hao, Xiuliang Liu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: The deepening of commercial operation of the power system has put forward higher requirements for the accuracy, timeliness, and safety of electricity bill settlement, which is a key power engineering issue to ensure the economic benefits of power grid enterprises and the stable operation of the electricity market. The traditional manual review method is no longer able to cope with the constantly emerging risks and challenges. This article aims to address the key challenge of transitioning from “identification” to “closed-loop governance” of electricity bill settlement risks. Starting from the actual needs of power engineering, we propose an innovative governance framework that integrates multimodal artificial intelligence (AI) and process intelligent agents. This framework utilizes multimodal AI to identify anomalies from diverse data sources (text contracts, bills, electricity meter images), and employs software agents to automatically assign disposal tasks, track rectification processes, and verify risk mitigation, thus forming a complete governance loop. A practical case in a city in Zhejiang Province shows that this scheme can significantly improve the accuracy and efficiency of risk identification and disposal, achieve closed-loop management of risks throughout the entire process, and provide a practical and scalable intelligent solution for risk management in power engineering.

title: Daily Mutual Fund Return Prediction using Optimized Stacked LSTM
authors: Rishita Das, Tapas Si
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Mutual fund performance prediction remains challenging due to complex, non-linear market fluctuations that traditional machine learning regression models cannot effectively encompass. While tree-based models like Random Forest (RF) and XGBoost show promise in financial forecasting, they lack the capability to model temporal dependencies inherent in financial time series data. This study presents the first application of optimized Stacked Long Short-Term Memory (SLSTM) neural networks for predicting daily mutual fund returns in India. Using comprehensive data from SBI BlueChip Fund (January 2016 to December 2024, 3,289 observations), we implemented a 2-layer SLSTM architecture with optimized hyperparameters, validated through 10-fold cross-validation. Performance of the models is evaluated using six well-known metrics such as Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Mean Squared Error (MSE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), Coefficient of Determination or R-Squared (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), and Adjusted R-squared $\left( {R_{adj}^2} \right)$. Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (MCDA) using TOPSIS method has been conducted using these aforementioned metrics, resulting SLSTM as the best model and SLSTM achieved mean values of 0.6691 MAE, 2.7991 MAPE, 0.9857 RMSE, 0.9798 MSE, 0.0335 R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, and 0.0031 $R_{adj}^2$. This research constitutes the first systematic application of SLSTM for Indian mutual fund analysis, demonstrating that temporal modeling outperforms traditional methods during market volatility, providing crucial insights for fund managers and investors.

title: Temperature-Adaptive Wide Bandgap-Based Domestic Induction Heater for Cooking Applications
authors: Tumpa Das, Molay Roy
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The domestic induction heating (IH) offers a clean and safe cooking practice. Their ability to be integrated with both AC grids and DC nanogrids provides a solution for the decarbonization of cooking practices and a sustainable future. Silicon carbide (SiC)-based WBG devices demonstrate negligible reverse recovery, low on-resistance, reduced losses, and high thermal conductivity, making them suitable for high-voltage, compact circuits with limited cooling capacities. Their advancement contributes to the development of high-frequency power electronic converters (PEC). The miniaturization and highdensity integration of power converters reduces size, cost, and increases efficiency and reliability. In domestic IH, implementing temperature control is necessary to prevent overcooking or material damage during the cooking process. Therefore, this paper presents a digital temperature control method that employs a power control strategy for thermal management. The controller operates in two modes: full power and half power. The control logic is implemented using the TMS320F28379D MCU and experimentally validated by operating a SiC-based full-bridge series resonant inverter (FBSRI) at a frequency of 101 kHz, delivering an output power of 305 W. The inverter is designed to function with zero voltage switching (ZVS). A dry heat test demonstrates that while utilizing a maximum inverter power of 305 W, the surface temperature increases from 25° C to 150° C within 15 minutes. Subsequently, the controller effectively maintained the vessel's temperature at a constant level.

title: A Hierarchical Method for Maximum Hosting Capacity of Distributed Generation in Distribution Networks Considering Protection Constraints
authors: Yingjie Tan, Heng Zhang, Guomin Lou
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The large-scale integration of inverter-interfaced distributed generators (IIDGs) alters fault current characteristics in distribution networks, leading to variations in overcurrent protection ranges under fixed settings. To characterize the allowable IIDG penetration without modifying existing protection settings, this paper proposes a hierarchical hosting capacity assessment method constrained by protection range requirements. According to their impacts on protection currents, IIDGs are classified into boosting zones, sinking zones, and adjacent feeder zones, and IIDGs within each zone are aggregated into equivalent sources to reduce computational complexity. Based on fixed protection settings, an upper-level model derives the admissible IIDG current contribution boundary imposed by protection range constraints, while a lower-level model allocates the allowable capacity to individual interconnection points under secure network operation constraints. Simulation results on a representative feeder illustrate that, within the derived hosting capacity boundary, the original protection settings preserve the intended protection ranges and coordination, demonstrating the applicability of the proposed method.

title: Factors Influencing Empathy for Narratives in Virtual Reality
authors: Hua-Hsiang Chen, Kuan-Wei Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: This study investigates the underlying factors that trigger empathy in VR cinema, a domain where existing research has seldom classified such elements. Drawing on semi-structured interviews with twelve participants and grounded theory analysis, the study identifies 22 empathy-inducing factors. It finds that audience trust is essential for fostering immersion, necessitating high audiovisual fidelity to construct a convincing make-believe world. Effective interaction design should grant users agency and a sense of embodiment, enabling intuitive narrative engagement. However, redundant interactions can obscure the story's message, highlighting the importance of balancing narrative flow with interactivity. Visual composition and plot structure also synergistically enhance emotional resonance and empathetic response. The findings may offer practical guidance for creators seeking to design emotionally engaging VR content.

title: Application of Sentiment Analysis Model in ESG Public Opinion Monitoring Based on Deep Learning
authors: Shiyu He
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of traditional sentiment analysis methods in ESG public opinion monitoring field, such as insufficient efficiency, lack of semantic understanding depth and weak adaptability to technical terms and multidimensional contexts, this study proposes a hybrid deep learning model integrating BERT and Bi-LSTM. BERT pre-trained model is used to extract deep semantic representation of text, and Bi-LSTM network is combined to capture serialized long-term dependencies within text. A multi-layer network architecture including pre-trained semantic coding layer and context sequence modeling layer is constructed. Applying this model to process ESG-related public opinion texts effectively improves the analysis difficulties of complex semantics and multidimensional emotion interweaving of domain texts, significantly improves the accuracy of emotion classification, strengthens the early recognition ability of negative public opinions, and provides a more reliable intelligent analysis tool for enterprise risk management and investment decision-making.

title: Driver Behaviour Analysis for Lane Change Maneuvers via Explainable Artificial Intelligence
authors: Batuhan Günaydın, Hakan Yılmaz, Mert Eren, Meryem Kaya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: End-user acceptance plays a key role in autonomous driving feature development. To maintain objectivity, autonomous functions should be assessed with key performance indicators (KPI) derived from physical parameters before they advance to the deployment stage. This study investigates the factors that prompt drivers to perform lane change maneuver and translates those findings into human readable explanations. Real-world highway data with manual lane change maneuvers was used. After preprocessing the data, an algorithmic pipeline automatically detected lane change events in the collected trajectory data and computed a wide set of physics based KPIs describing safety, comfort, and perceived safety. Model transparency was provided through SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), ranking each KPI by its contribution to the lane change decision. The top-ranked SHAP features were then fed to a large language model (LLM) that generated concise natural-language rationales for every detected maneuver. By coupling accurate machine learning classification with explainable, driver-friendly feedback, the proposed framework aims to strengthen trust in autonomous lane change functionalities and promote their wider acceptance.

title: Active Gate Driver Based on Gate Voltage Sensing for SiC MOSFET Module
authors: Sanket Kumar, Samir Hazra
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Silicon Carbide (SiC) devices, due to fast switching and presence of parasitic inductance and device capacitance, often experience voltage and current overshoot during switching transients. These effects can significantly reduce the reliability of SiC MOSFET-based power converters because of the premature failure of power devices. This work investigates the switching behaviour of SiC MOSFETs to establish a relationship between gate driving voltage and overshoots in drain-source (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$v_{d s}$</tex>) voltage and drain current (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$i_{d}$</tex>). Based on these investigations, an Active Gate Driver (AGD) with adjustable gate drive voltage is proposed. The AGD involves a dynamic monitoring and feedback mechanism of gate voltage, which enables the active variation of gate voltage during switching events and suppresses overshoots in voltage and current. The proposed approach is experimentally validated.

title: Research on Dynamic Optimization of Automatic Cable Laying Path Based on Digital Twin and BIM
authors: Hui Liu, Xuehong Zhang, Xuezheng Chu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: With the rapid development of intelligent construction and smart cities, the demand for efficient, safe and intelligent cable laying in complex engineering projects is increasingly prominent. Cable laying, as a key link in engineering construction, has problems such as complex path planning, difficult real-time adjustment, and low construction efficiency in traditional methods. To solve these problems, this paper proposes a dynamic optimization method for automatic cable laying paths based on Digital Twin (DT) and Building Information Modeling (BIM). First, a digital twin model of the cable laying scene is constructed by fusing BIM multi-dimensional data and real-time perception data of the construction site. Then, an improved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm combined with dynamic obstacle avoidance is designed as the core path optimization algorithm, which can adapt to the dynamic changes of the construction environment. Finally, the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed method are verified through a real engineering case. The experimental results show that compared with the traditional path planning method, the proposed method can reduce the laying length by 8.3 % on average, shorten the construction period by 12.5 %, and improve the safety of the construction process. This research provides a new technical approach for the intelligent and automatic cable laying in complex engineering projects, and has important theoretical significance and practical application value.
