title: Fully Passive Integrated Optical NOT Gate Using a Phase-Apodized Spiral Bragg Grating
authors: Mauricio Tosi, Laureano A. Bulus Rossini, Jos´e Aza˜na, Pablo A. Costanzo-Caso
publish: 2026 Argentine Conference on Electronics (CAE)
abstract: We report the first demonstration of a fully passive on-chip optical NOT gate based on a phase-only waveguide Bragg grating (WBG) implemented on a silicon-on-insulator (SOI) platform. The device consists of a 9-mm-long phase-apodized spiral WBG occupying only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.25 ~\text{mm}^{2}$</tex>, designed to provide a narrowband <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pi$</tex>-phase shift suitable for high-speed multilevel data inversion. The grating response is synthesized using an inverse layer peeling algorithm and realized through precise modulation of the corrugation spacing. Experimental characterization confirms excellent agreement between the measured and target spectral responses, with a phase peak bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 9 \text{GHz}$</tex>. Using this filter, we demonstrate passive inversion of 50-Gbps PAM-2 and 45-Gbaud PAM-3 data streams, representing the first integrated implementation of a WBG-based multilevel NOT gate. The device operates entirely passively, with energy consumption limited by insertion losses, and offers a scalable path toward compact, low-power, ultrafast optical logic circuits.

title: Adaptive SPMSM Stator Winding Connection for Single Phase Integrated Onboard EV Chargers
authors: Deepak Sai Duvvapu, Barry Venugopal Reddy
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The prevailing demand for extended driving range and reduced charging time in electric vehicles can be addressed by expanding battery capacity and deploying high-power charging infrastructure. However, relying solely on off-board high-power charging stations may not always be feasible; hence, it becomes essential to enhance the power density of onboard chargers by utilizing existing electric drive components, such as motor windings, as an inductive element in the front-end converter during charging mode when vehicle is parked. Furthermore, the non-concurrent nature of propulsion and charging in an electric vehicle enables the repurposing of the traction inverter and motor drive to implement integrated onboard charging (IOBC) functionality. This approach facilitates a reduction in the size, weight, and cost of the onboard charger by leveraging shared components, including motor windings, inverter stages, and associated hardware elements. To eliminate unwanted pulsating torque during charging, the stator winding current must be aligned exclusively along the rotor's d-axis, as no alternative method can fully suppress torque generation under these conditions. This paper addresses the issue of pulsating torque in an efficient manner by introducing a phase mapping technique that connects the grid supply to the motor windings in synchrony with the rotor position in surface mount permanent magnet synchronous motor (SPMSM). The proposed steady-state operation and control strategies are validated through detailed simulations in MATLAB/Simulink, and the results demonstrate the practical viability and efficiency of the IOBC design for electric vehicle applications.

title: Research on Multi-stage Integrated Text Mining Method Based on IMTP Framework
authors: Duo Zhao, Fangying Ma, Mingxia Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the rapid growth of the scale of multi-text data, how to achieve deep semantic understanding and interpretable prediction while maintaining efficiency has become a research hotspot. This paper proposes a multistage integrated text mining framework, IMTP, which integrates deep sentiment analysis, TF-IDF keyword extraction, LDA topic modeling, K-means clustering and XGBoost regression prediction, to achieve an end-to-end processing flow from raw text to structured output. First, the BERT model is utilized to extract context-sensitive semantic features and complete sentiment classification; Subsequently, multi-level feature representations at the word level and the topic level were obtained by combining TF-IDF and LDA; Then, through K-means clustering, potential patterns are identified to generate clustering characteristics; Finally, an XGBoost regression model was adopted for precise prediction, and SHAP analysis was combined to enhance the interpretability of the model. The experimental results show that this framework achieves an accuracy rate of 97.2% in the emotion classification task, with an F1 value ranging from 82.1% to 93.5%; in the prediction task, the RMSE has decreased to 0.3700, and the model has achieved stable convergence. Research has shown that multi-module collaboration can effectively enhance the performance of text mining, and it also possesses good scalability and reproducibility.

title: Analysis of the Influence of Urban Rail Transit Drainage Device on Stray Current and Geoelectric Field
authors: Jun Liu, Shanpeng Zhao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To investigate the influence of stray current drainage devices on stray current distribution and geoelectric field in urban rail transit, a three-dimensional (3D) simulation model of traction return system was established in this study, and verified using a resistance network model. The influence of drainage device on stray current, rail potential, soil potential gradient and surface potential was systematically analyzed. The results show that the connection of the drainage device changes the current reflux path, and the stray current distribution changes from parabolic to monotonically decreasing type, with its peak reaching four times that without drainage. At the same time, due to the shift in reference potential caused by the connection of the drainage device, the peak value of the rail potential also doubled. In addition, the peak value of surface potential gradient increased significantly from 13.94 mV/m to 105 mV/m, which significantly increased the corrosion risk of buried pipelines. As stray current leakage intensifies, the surface potential shifts negatively from about 2.35 mV to about 15 mV, further aggravating the DC bias of the transformers along the line. In summary, the operation of the drainage device will aggravate the leakage of stray current, raise the rail potential and increase the soil potential gradient. Hence, it is necessary to further optimize the drainage strategy.

title: LAWFORT AI: An NLP-Driven Cyber Law Assistant for Intelligent Legal Query Processing
authors: Kavi Amudan S, N. Palanivel, Sanjay. V, E. Pavithran
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Cyber laws are often complex and difficult to decipher by the general public without knowing the law. To counter this problem, LawFort AI is proposed as an intelligent cyber law assistant that leverages Natural Language Processing (NLP) and hybrid query processing techniques to improve access to legal materials. The model incorporates Fuzzy Wuzzybased fuzzy search, xAI API for semantic understanding, and Hugging Face Transformers for contextual analysis of user queries. It supports both text and voice search, allowing users to ask natural questions such as “What are the penalties for phishing?” and receive clear, law-informed responses. Designed as a lightweight and scalable web framework, LawFort AI enhances knowledge of cyber law and makes legal information available by providing accurate, real-time, and understandable explanations derived from the Indian IT Act. Experimental evaluation indicates that the system achieves high accuracy and responsiveness, making it suitable to be utilized in legal education, sensitisation programs, and public online platforms.

title: The Construction and Structure of English Teaching from the Perspective of Human-Machine Collaboration
authors: Xiaoyong Ma, Haiguang Fang, Xinmei Kong
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: The educational transformation driven by artificial intelligence (AI) development is fundamentally a structural change. English language instruction in compulsory education, characterized by standardized curricula and developmental continuity, inherently exhibits structural features. In practice, teachers and students collaboratively construct the actual teaching structure through English pedagogy. During this process, the structural organization of disciplinary knowledge, AI's knowledge representation framework, and the activity structure of human-AI collaboration in English teaching demonstrate intrinsic alignment. Consequently, in human-AI collaborative teaching, the technological development trajectory and application pathways reflect distinct regulatory mechanisms and resource allocation models, manifesting as four structural features of human-machine collaboration. Ultimately, this collaboration constructs and advances AI-integrated English teaching, achieving both individual pedagogical significance and broader societal educational value.

title: Cautosim-Modelica-Based Electric Vehicle Battery Cooling Modeling
authors: Endi Huangluo, Qiuye Yu, Shaoqing He
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: With the rapid development of the new energy vehicle (NEV) industry, power battery thermal safety has become a key factor influencing vehicle performance, reliability, and service life. Under high-rate charge–discharge and extreme environmental conditions, temperature non-uniformity and localized overheating significantly affect battery efficiency and durability, highlighting the need for accurate and computationally efficient thermal management models. To address this challenge, this study develops a one-dimensional electro–thermal coupled battery cooling model on the Cautosim platform using the Modelica language. The model integrates electrical, thermal, and fluid domains to simulate air-cooled battery packs under various operating conditions, and its accuracy is validated. Results show that the proposed model achieves deviations of less than 5% in key thermal and electrical parameters, accurately reproducing dynamic temperature responses and heat transfer characteristics. Further analysis reveals the effects of airflow rate on cooling performance and temperature uniformity, providing quantitative insights for optimizing air-cooling strategies. The developed Cautosim–Modelica-based model offers a reliable and efficient tool for battery thermal management design, control strategy development, and early-stage system optimization in new energy vehicles.

title: A Novel Power Flow Calculation Method Based on the Integration of Physical Mechanisms and Heterogeneous Graph Transformer
authors: Zongpeng Li, Mengtao Sun, Liangli Su, Jiuning Gao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: With the development of new-generation power systems, the operational states and power flows of power grids have become increasingly variable, imposing higher demands on power system analysis and operational control. This paper proposes a novel power flow calculation method that integrates a Heterogeneous Graph Transformer (HGT) network with physical mechanism constraints. The method innovatively employs an HGT model to construct a heterogeneous representation of grid topology, capturing complex correlations through meta-relationship modeling of node attributes and edge relations. Furthermore, it introduces power balance equations as regularization terms into the loss function, enhancing computational speed while ensuring the physical feasibility of solutions. Experiments on the IEEE 9 bus system demonstrate that the model achieves a relative mean error below 4.4%, reduces computation time by 91.28% compared to the conventional Newton-Raphson method, and enables adaptive calculations without requiring line parameters. This approach is particularly suitable for big-data scenarios involving high-penetration distributed generation.

title: WasteSense: An EfficientNetV2B2-Based Smart Waste Classification and Monitoring Framework Using IoT and Cloud Integration
authors: Vedula Visweswar Sai Vignesh, P Naga Venkata Krishnakanth, Sidhant Chaku, Jafar Ali Ibrahim S
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: WasteSense offers an applicable/public utility infrastructure for intelligent and low-cost waste identification through computational-efficient deep learning embodied in EfficientNetV2B2 along with IoT-based sensing and cloud analytics. The system is developed to operate under practical limitations, seamlessly from image-acquisition edge towards inference-level-of-fog and cloud-level analysis. Evaluations for eleven waste categories exceeded 96% accuracy and revealed the model's generalisability to various materials and lighting conditions. With lowcomplexity design and low-cost hardware, the proposed solution is ready for large-scale use in smart city waste management. The system can have manual uploads or IoT automation through edge devices equipped with ESP32-CAM. The prototype is able to get a 96% test accuracy thus, it is capable of being deployed in the real world. The overall system cost is very low, which makes it a feasible option for extensive use in smart cities.

title: A Review on Federated Learning with Dual Knowledge Transfer
authors: Hemapriya N, Gukan T K
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: With smartphones and edge devices being commonplace, expansive personal photo data has become incredibly common. As we embark on applications that utilize personal photo data to train effective classification models, including smart photo organization, the risks of privacy and the complexities of deploying centralized training weigh heavily on us. With the capabilities of Federated Learning (FL), users can collaboratively train models with other users without the sharing of raw personal data. However, FL is challenged by non-IID (non-independently and identically distributed) data, as dataset uniqueness stemmed from clients' unique photo collections is problematic because it yields non-IID client datasets that are often unbalanced too. FL methods, such as FedAvg, traditionally develop an adaptable global model while providing poorly personalized local models to users; newer approaches, like FedRep, improve personalization, but can instead worsen the performance of the global model. This paper presents an innovative dual knowledge transfer framework that mitigates the impact on i) local model personalization, and ii) global model generalization. The framework combines synthetic feature generation and bidirectional knowledge distillation to ensure users receive a personalized model while the users collaboratively improve the performance of the global model, all without compromising an individual's privacy.

title: SA-BirdClassifiernet: A Hybrid Attentiondriven Deep Learning Model for Real-Time Classification and Monitoring of Migratory Birds
authors: Shree Pranav S, Tiruvikraman V, Lokesh S
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Understanding bird migration patterns is essential for ecological research and conservation. This paper presents an AIdriven framework for automated bird migration monitoring, integrating deep learning models for accurate detection and classification. We employ YOLOv12 for real-time bird detection and propose a novel classification architecture-SA-BirdClassifierNet-which integrates transfer learning from EfficientNetV2-L into a ConvNeXt-based backbone enhanced with attention mechanisms. The model incorporates Selective Attention Network (SANet), Multi-scale Efficient Channel Attention (MECA), and Similarity Activation Map (SimAM) to refine feature representations and improve robustness against occlusions, motion blur, and low-resolution imagery. This hybrid architecture leverages pre-trained EfficientNet features to accelerate learning and improve fine-grained species recognition. Our system achieves a classification accuracy of 98.35\%, significantly outperforming traditional CNN baselines. This approach delivers a scalable and accurate solution for bird migration monitoring in complex, real-world environments.

title: Analysis of the Influence of Rotor Magnetic Bridge Parameters on the NVH Performance of 800V Hairpin Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors
authors: Xiaohong Pan, Kai Qian
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Based on the fundamental principles of cogging torque, this paper investigates the laws governing the influence of rotor magnetic bridge width on the electromagnetic and vibro-acoustic characteristics of hairpin permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs). The study focuses on analyzing the impact of variations in magnetic bridge width on cogging torque, torque ripple under specific operating conditions, and radiated sound power. Furthermore, in combination with stator modal characteristics, we investigate the impact of primary mode shapes on the motor's NVH performance. The research results indicate that as the magnetic bridge width increases from 1 mm to 4 mm, the cogging torque fluctuation decreases from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8.8 ~\mathrm{N} \cdot ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> to 5.2 N. m; however, under specific operating conditions, the torque ripple rises from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$26.3 ~\mathrm{N} \cdot ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30.6 ~\mathrm{N} \cdot ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>. Concurrently, under low-speed conditions, the radiated sound power decreases by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5 ~\text{dB}(~\mathrm{A})$</tex>; conversely, under high-speed conditions, the radiated sound power increases by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 ~\text{dB}(~\mathrm{A})$</tex>. Modal analysis results indicate that the first few orders of stator radial mode shapes have a significant influence on the motor's vibro-acoustic characteristics. The study demonstrates that reasonable adjustment of the magnetic bridge width can achieve an effective balance between cogging torque and NVH performance, offering a significant reference value for the low-noise design of hairpin PMSMs.

title: EnergizeAir: Intelligent O2 Infusion Mechanism for Superior Indoor Air Quality
authors: Sumiksha Shetty, Smitha A B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: The present paper introduces a new Oxygen Supply Device (OSD) that promises to transform the interior air quality management industry by providing constant monitoring and the selfgeneration of oxygen. The conventional static building air quality systems cannot peak the environmental changes which are dynamic, hence become inconsistent in indoor air conditions. The suggested gadget overcomes these shortcomings by allowing the use of CO2 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O 2$</tex> sensors, temperature sensors, and water level measurement solutions with an ESP32 microcontroller, and automatically optimizing the air quality. Production of oxygen is efficient through the use of a potassium hydroxide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{K O H}$</tex>) solution under electrolysis conditions allowing a constant (24/7) scalable oxygen source. Advanced algorithms interpret sensor information in real-time so that the creation of optimal conditions and minimisation of manual control creates a pathway using oxygen production and airflow dynamically. It has the ability to log the data in a system using the SD card to monitor the performance and supporting maintenance. It boasts main characteristics: energy efficiency, compact and modular that one can incorporate with existing HVAC systems, and strong fail-safe controls to eliminate the risk of oxygen oversaturation. Moreover, it has an interface that is easy to operate, and thus can be employed in both small houses to big healthcare institutions. The solution proposed in this project is sustainable and cost-effective, and therefore, fits into both environmental and health priorities of the world in relation to controlling the increasing challenges of indoor air pollution. Keywords Indoor Air Quality (IAQ), Oxygen Supply Device (OSD), Electrolysis, Electrochemical potassium hydroxide (KOH), ESP32 microcontroller, Real-time monitoring, CO2 and O 2 sensors, Automation, Energy-efficiency, HVAC integrations, Environmental sustainability, Data logging, air quality optimization, intelligent air management, safety mechanisms.

title: Neural Imitation of Human Perceptual Response to High-Resolution Audio
authors: Baicheng Huang, Xinyi Pan, Haiwei Chai, Dong Liu, Feng Zhu, Xiaoyong Pan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Computer audition systems based on neural processing provide deep perceptual understanding of digital audio signals but often rely on extensive manual annotation. In this paper, we propose an unsupervised framework to imitate human perceptual processing for high-resolution audio quality assessment. We employ a data format conversion method to quantize continuous audio waveforms into discrete token sequences. A Large Language Model is then utilized to model these sequences via next-token prediction, capturing complex temporal dependencies without labeled data. Higher prediction accuracy is observed on truly recorded high-resolution content, while artificially up-sampled content induces significant prediction drops. Experimental results demonstrate that this unsupervised approach effectively distinguishes between true and synthesized high-resolution audio. Manual verification result shows strong agreement with the results provided by the proposed neural model.

title: Performance Comparison of Interleaved Bidirectional Flyback and Buck-Boost Converters in High-Power Renewable Energy Systems
authors: R. Ninitha Ashwini, Ila Rai
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: This research paper presents a comprehensive simulation-based comparison of interleaved bidirectional flyback converters and interleaved Bidirectional buck-boost converters, focusing on their performance in high-power applications within renewable energy systems. The study aims to address the existing gap in the literature regarding a direct evaluation of these converter topologies under real operating conditions, particularly concerning efficiency, control complexity, component stresses, and suitability for various applications. Utilizing advanced modelling techniques, the research analyses key parameters such as voltage regulation, efficiency, bidirectional power flow, and state of charge management. Results indicate that the interleaved bidirectional flyback converters outperform the interleaved Bidirectional buck-boost converters, maintaining a steady output voltage of approximately 140 V with minimal ripple (2 %) and achieving efficiencies of 92-94%, compared to the Interleaved bidirectional Buck-Boost converter, 130 V output, and 88-90% efficiency. Additionally, the interleaved bidirectional flyback converters demonstrate superior transient response and smoother SOC variations, indicating better energy management and reduced stress on energy storage systems. These findings underscore the advantages of the flyback converter in applications requiring high efficiency and reliability, providing valuable insights for power electronics engineers and contributing to the advancement of renewable energy integration strategies. Overall, this study highlights the critical role of converter topology selection in optimizing the performance of modern electrical grids.

title: Storm Shield: A 3D Strategic Co-Op Game to Increase Awareness and Aid People in Preparation for Upcoming Typhoons
authors: John Albert Miguel Joson, Hannah Martha Tipo, Ezekiel Tiratira, Samuel Ghenvic Faith Tolentino, Abricam Tinga, Ronel Ramos
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: Typhoons remain among the most frequent and destructive natural disasters in the Philippines, underscoring the need for innovative preparedness strategies. This study presents Storm Shield: A 3D Strategic Co-op Game, a local multiplayer educational game that simulates the three phases of a typhoon–Before, During, and After–through mission-based levels emphasizing teamwork, planning, and decision-making. Players assume roles within a disaster response team and perform tasks such as preparing emergency kits, assisting civilians, and managing post-typhoon recovery. The game integrates action, strategy, and educational mechanics, supported by a Content Management System (CMS) website that reinforces learning. Effectiveness was evaluated through post-play surveys using Key Performance Indicators (KPI) covering functionality, entertainment, player experience, and educational value. Results show that Storm Shield engages players while improving knowledge of disaster preparedness and response. By merging learning with entertainment, the project demonstrates the potential of serious games to support disaster risk reduction and contributes to Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 11 by promoting resilient communities through interactive education.

title: Energy Management Strategy for Extended-Range Electric Vehicles with Miniaturized Range Extenders
authors: Jigao Niu, Jiangke Jiu, Kunming Jin
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper addresses the energy management strategy for extended-range electric vehicles (E-REVs) equipped with a miniaturized range extender. Four rule-based control strategies are developed using a sequential optimization approach. A full-vehicle simulation platform is built in Cruise/Simulink to evaluate the fuel economy performance of these strategies. The results indicate that the range extender's average efficiency and the energy losses during battery charge/discharge are the primary factors affecting fuel economy. Consequently, EVs with miniaturized range extenders are not well served by conventional engine start/stop or power-following control strategies. Instead, an improved optimal-curve control strategy achieves superior fuel economy and overall performance.

title: Video Data-Driven Approaches for Monitoring Hazard Sources in Emergency Response Scenarios at Coal and Renewable Energy Facilities
authors: Xiangdong Zhang, Songyu Zou
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The rapid expansion of coal and renewable energy enterprises has introduced complex, heterogeneous visual environments-ranging from low-light coal mines to high-glare photovoltaic stations-which pose significant challenges for safety monitoring. Conventional surveillance strategies often fail to meet intrinsic safety requirements due to poor environmental adaptability and the “long-tail” scarcity of accident samples. To address these limitations, this study proposes a Full-Transformer MultiModal Risk Perception Framework. Unlike loose assemblies of independent models, we construct a unified collaborative system that synergizes static detection, dynamic behavior recognition, and anomaly identification. For static perception, we integrate adaptive illumination calibration and an Asymptotic Feature Pyramid Network (AFPN) into RT-DETR to robustly handle occlusions and small targets specific to industrial scenes. For dynamic behavior, a Semantic-Aware Masked Spatiotemporal Modeling strategy is employed to enhance violation recognition efficiency. Furthermore, we design a Multi-Scale Local Manifold Estimation model based on FastFlow to detect unknown anomalies without over-generalization. A key conceptual innovation, the Bidirectional Cross-Attention Fusion (Bi-MCAF) module, semantically aligns these heterogeneous features to output interpretable risk levels and causality. Validated on our “Energy MultiScenario Safety Dataset (EMS-Dataset),” the method achieves an mAP@ 0.5 of 86.4% (static), Top-1 Accuracy of 93.1% (dynamic), and AUROC of 97.3% (anomaly). These results significantly surpass YOLOv5 and CNN-LSTM baselines, demonstrating the framework's potential for reliable hazard monitoring in complex energy facilities.

title: Optimization of LSTM-Based Icing Prediction Model for Transmission Lines
authors: Hongfei Wang, Zepeng Sun, Yuzhi Shi, Kunpeng Ji, Junhui Li, Bin Zhao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of transmission line icing prediction. Based on the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model, it investigates transmission line icing prediction methods and optimizes the prediction model. The results show that the model exhibits excellent performance in short-term predictions (2-hour and 4-hour), with the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) below 4% and the coefficient of determination (R2) exceeding 0.89, indicating a high degree of fit between the predicted and actual values. To address the issue of gradient dissipation in long-cycle predictions, the study finds that using a dataset with a 1-hour time interval can balance prediction accuracy and model generalization. This approach maintains controlled Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) while significantly improving the R2 metric, effectively solving the training failure problem caused by excessively large time steps. This achievement provides a high-precision and robust technical approach for transmission line icing prediction.

title: Context-Aware Transformer for Supervised Anomaly Detection in Smart Building Energy Consumption
authors: Murad Almadani, Shadi Atalla, Yassine Himeur, Hamzah Alkhazaleh, Wathiq Mansoor, Abbes Amira
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Anomaly detection in building energy management is critical for ensuring operational efficiency, maintaining occupant comfort, and supporting decarbonization goals. Conventional rule-based and unsupervised outlier methods often achieve high precision but suffer from low recall, missing many impactful anomalies. In this work, we propose a Dual-Head Context-Gated Transformer (DH–CGT) that jointly models temporal dependencies in energy meter and weather time series while conditioning predictions on building-specific context such as site and use type. The architecture integrates a context-gating mechanism that adaptively emphasizes relevant features and employs an auxiliary regression head to stabilize training under severe class imbalance. We evaluate our model on a large-scale multi-building dataset aligned with the LEAD-1.0 benchmark. Results demonstrate strong and consistent performance, with a mean ROC–AUC of 0.958 ± 0.003 and AUPRC of 0.802 ± 0.011, substantially outperforming popular unsupervised baselines where F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> typically remains near 0.40. Decision curve analysis further confirms the practical utility of DH– CGT, showing positive net benefit across relevant threshold ranges. These findings establish context-gated transformers as a robust and deployable solution for smart digital twin platforms, enabling proactive and scalable anomaly detection in building operations.

title: Post Image Processing for Improving Robustness of CNN-Based Traffic Light Detection on Unmanned Mobile Vehicles
authors: Takumi Obayashi, Tatsunosuke Shiota, Yukito Taguchi, Ryuki Tsuruda, Ryosuke Oshima, Shota Koga, Nozomi Tsutsui, Sakuya Yamaguchi, Tan Jin Hung, Hiromu Matsuzaki, Masahiro Kiyota, Ryuto Matsunaga, Nagisa Yoshikawa, Kento Nakai, Masahiro Nishimura, Taito Manabe, Yuichiro Shibata
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This paper discusses post-processing methods to enhance the robustness of traffic signal detection tasks for SoC FPGA-based autonomous mobile vehicles. Although a machine learning object detection approach, such as the use of YOLO, has been proven to be effective, it often faces challenges with false detections in locations without traffic lights and color misclassification, both of which are caused by changes in the operating environment. To solve this problem, we implemented a traffic light and color recognition algorithm in software as a post-processing step, which uses luminance and hue information to complement the YOLO detection. This enables robust recognition that is less affected by lighting conditions or the background, effectively preventing misdetections.

title: Cognitive Load Assessment Model Based on Deep Learning Network
authors: Jiayang Fang, Jiaxu Zhang, Songhao Shi, Jiahui Duo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: As the complexity of human-computer interaction systems continues to increase, the assessment of operator mental load has become an important area of research to ensure safety and efficiency<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[1]</sup>. Based on deep learning network technology, this study analyzes the effects of task complexity and personality characteristics on mental load and work performance. The experiment simulates a multi-task situation through the MATB-2 platform, collects electroencephalogram signals (EEG) from subjects in low, medium and high complexity tasks, and constructs a comprehensive assessment model of mental workload. The results show that task complexity and personality characteristics significantly affect the level of mental workload, and the classification accuracy of the convolutional neural network model reaches 88.785%. This study provides technical support for real-time brain load monitoring in complex working environments and provides a theoretical basis for optimizing the design of human-machine systems. At the same time, the role and significance of the comprehensive assessment model of mental load in multi-use scenarios are discussed.

title: Optimization of Hybrid Energy Storage Capacity Configuration for Mitigating Wind Power Fluctuations
authors: Yiwen Shi, Zijun Sun, Jiayi Chai
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: This study proposes a novel capacity optimization framework for hybrid energy storage systems (HESS) to mitigate the detrimental power fluctuations associated with integrating large-scale wind farms into the grid. The core innovation lies in synergistically combining Empirical Mode Decomposition (EMD) with the complementary strengths of lithium-ion batteries and supercapacitors. The EMD algorithm adaptively decomposes the volatile wind power signal into distinct high-frequency and low-frequency components, providing a precise foundation for targeted mitigation. Strategically, the high energy density of lithium batteries is leveraged to manage the long-term, low-frequency power imbalances, while the rapid response and high power density of supercapacitors are dedicated to absorbing the short-term, high-frequency fluctuations. To determine the optimal configuration, a comprehensive capacity optimization model is formulated. It simultaneously minimizes three critical objective functions: the total investment cost for the hybrid storage system, its projected operation and maintenance expenditures, and the financial penalties incurred from exceeding permissible grid connection fluctuation limits. This economic model is rigorously constrained by key operational realities, including the charge-discharge power limits and safe state-of-charge windows for each storage device, ensuring the solution is technically viable. Validation through a detailed case study on a major wind farm demonstrates the framework’s superior performance. Simulation results confirm that the proposed hybrid strategy achieves a significantly greater reduction in grid connection power variability compared to systems utilizing only a single type of storage. By delivering a more stable and predictable power output, this approach effectively enhances the grid’s ability to accommodate higher levels of renewable penetration, thereby contributing to overall system stability. The research offers a scalable and systematic pathway for facilitating the secure integration of variable renewable energy sources.

title: Memory-Optimized AES-128 on GPUs: A Cross-Platform Benchmark of ECB and CTR Modes
authors: Mohammed Rauf Ali Khan, Hamza Ashfaq, Ayaz H. Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The need for efficient data encryption has grown with the increasing adoption of GPU-based acceleration in modern computing. This paper presents an implementation and comparative evaluation of Advanced Encryption Standard (AES-128) encryption across two major block cipher modes, Electronic Codebook (ECB) and Counter (CTR). Three GPU memory configurations were investigated: Key - T-tables in Global (KT-G), Shared (KT-S), and Constant-Shared (KT-CS). The experiments were conducted on two different architectures, the high-performance NVIDIA RTX A4500 and the embedded Jetson Nano Orin, to assess scalability across computing tiers. The study explores performance variations under multiple input sizes and thread-block configurations, highlighting the tradeoffs between latency and memory access efficiency. Results show that KT-CS consistently achieves the best performance on both platforms, with speedups exceeding 650x on the RTX A4500 and up to 60x on the Jetson Nano Orin compared to CPU execution. The findings emphasize the influence of GPU memory placement and architectural tuning on AES encryption performance, providing valuable guidance for secure and efficient cryptographic implementation in both high-end and embedded GPU environments.

title: ALERT: A Closed-Loop SOC Agent for Web and API Security with CVE-Based Intelligence and Adaptive Anomaly Detection
authors: Thirawat Sooksomstarn, Qian Wu, Atsushi Kanai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Web and Application Programming Interface (API) services are central to modern digital infrastructure but remain vulnerable to injection attacks, authentication bypasses, and zero-day exploits. Traditional Security Operations Center (SOC) tools rely on static, signature-based pipelines that generate high false positives, struggle with concept drift, and provide limited decision support. This paper presents ALERT (Agentic Learning for Event-driven Response and Threat Detection), a lightweight, GPU-free (CPU-only), closed-loop SOC agent with a human-in-the-loop (HITL) that integrates Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) severity prediction, log anomaly detection, and Language Model (LLM)-assisted semantics into a cost-sensitive decision framework. ALERT ingests multimodal inputs, including vulnerability feeds, runtime logs, and analyst annotations, and dynamically adjusts responses (ALLOW, MONITOR, or BLOCK) through online policy adaptation. Evaluation on a synthetic dataset of 10,000 Apache-style logs achieved a precision of 0.991, a recall of 0.983, and an F1-score of 0.987, detecting 100 percent of critical threats while keeping false positives below 0.5 percent. Designed for scalability and transparency, ALERT’s modular architecture generalizes to Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) and Internet of Things (IoT) telemetry, supporting practical, adaptive, and explainable SOC automation.

title: Research on Optimal Operation of Microgrid Based on Digital Twin and Intelligent Decision-making
authors: Jiajun Feng, Wenjun Zhu, Jiahai Zhang, Qiang Zhou, Dongjun Han
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Aiming at the core problems of micro-grid with high proportion of new energy access, such as the separation of sensing data, the lag of decision response and the lack of equipment coordination, this paper proposes an optimal operation framework integrating digital twin and intelligent decision-making. By constructing a three-dimensional architecture of physical layer, digital layer and decision layer, the holographic mapping of equipment with centimeter-level accuracy and millisecond-level dynamic regulation are realized. A long-term prediction model of photovoltaic power based on deep learning is innovatively developed, which maintains a prediction error of only 7.8 % for 384 hours under complex rainy weather conditions. An intelligent optimization algorithm with carbon cost constraints is designed, which significantly improves the solution efficiency and reduces the average daily operating cost by 18.2 %. Combined with electrothermal energy collaborative utilization technology and multi-dimensional fault preview mechanism, the reliability and energy efficiency level of the system are comprehensively strengthened. The empirical research shows that this method can effectively improve the new energy consumption rate to 97.1 %, and the annual carbon emission reduction is 12.7 tons, which provides a technical paradigm with engineering landing value for the construction of new power system.

title: Research on Image Recognition Based on Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Chunxi Guan, Xing Yu, Lijuan Wan, Xiangyu Song, Zhiming Lei, Liusheng Lai, Junfeng Xuan, Xianguo Wang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Traditional methods recognize images by extracting their features. However, extracting image features poses certain difficulties, especially for high-dimensional images and those with complex backgrounds. To address this issue,a recognition method based on Convolutional Neural Networks(CNN) is proposed. Under the improved LeNet5 framework, sample images are directly used as input parameters, and the model is trained through a Convolutional Neural Networks to obtain output results, thus eliminating the need to extract complex features. Experimental verification shows that the above method can accurately recognize images. The method proposed in this paper does not require the extraction of complex features, making it highly valuable in image processing.

title: Value Iteration-Based Optimal Tracking Control for Robot Visual Servoing Under Time-Varying Disturbances
authors: Yidi Liu, Xiaolin Ren, Haobing Cui
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper proposes an optimal tracking control scheme based on value iteration for robotic visual servoing under time-varying disturbances. The control input and the time-varying disturbance is used as two opposing players in the zero-sum game framework. A value iteration algorithm is used to online solve a zero-sum game for the robust optimal policy, while a neural network is employed as a function approximator to adaptively fit the value function and optimal control law, thereby achieving optimal tracking control. The asymptotic stability of the closed-loop manipulator system is then rigorously ensured based on Lyapunov stability theory. Finally, comprehensive numerical simulations confirm the algorithm's practical effectiveness and robustness.

title: Research on Performance Evaluation Algorithms of Machine Learning Models Based on Big Data in Remote Intelligent Terminals
authors: Zhang Guangdi, Yang Yi, Han Yiliang, Li Xue, Zhou Jingyun, Wang Zuocheng, Wang Xiaoying
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the deep integration of big data and Internet of Things (iot) technologies, the deployment of machine learning models on remote intelligent terminals has become an important trend. However, the resource-constrained characteristics of terminal devices and the concept drift problem existing in data streams pose severe challenges to the continuous performance guarantee of the model. For this issue, this paper conducts an in-depth study on the performance evaluation algorithm of machine learning models based on big data in remote intelligent terminals and proposes an elastic evaluation algorithm named online sequence ensemble and drift detection. The core innovation of this algorithm lies in the deep integration of online ensemble learning and a lightweight concept drift detection mechanism, constructing a dynamic evaluation system with autonomous adaptability. This system maintains a heterogeneous model integration with variable weights and dynamically adjusts the internal structure of the integration based on real-time performance feedback and statistical detection signals. Specifically, the algorithm designs a multi-level update strategy, fine-tuning the weights during the stable performance period, redistributing the weights when performance fluctuations are detected, and initiating the model's elimination and regeneration mechanism when severe concept drift is identified.

title: Multimodal Data-Driven Machine Learning for High-End Equipment Remaining-Useful-Life Prediction: An Industrial Engineering Practice
authors: Baicun Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Predictive-maintenance of high-end equipment demands accurate Remaining-Useful-Life (RUL) estimation under stringent industrial constraints. Single-modality approaches ignore complementary information embedded in physical signals, process parameters and maintenance logs, yielding poor generalisation. We propose a multimodal data-driven framework that fuses tri-modal industrial data (vibration, temperature, current; speed, load; textual maintenance records) through a cross-modal attention CNN-LSTM architecture. A preprocessing pipeline combining LSTM-based imputation (92 % accuracy), Isolation-Forest anomaly removal (3.5 % outliers) and timestamp alignment guarantees data fidelity. The network is pruned to 0.9 MB and deployed on an NVIDIA Jetson Xavier NX edge gateway. In a 14-month CNC spindle case-study (N = 12 machines, 2.1 billion samples), the proposed model achieves RMSE = 28.4 h (28.3 % lower than single-modal LSTM, 15.6 % lower than feature-concatenation baseline). Early RUL (> 500 h) MAPE is 8.2 %; inference latency 0.12 s (< 0.5 s requirement); RMSE variation under 10 % noise injection is 5.8 %. Field deployment reduced spindle downtime from 48 h to 12 h, saving ≈ 1.2 M CNY yr-1, cutting maintenance cost by 18 % and extending spindle life by 15 %. The work offers a deployable, cost-effective paradigm for predictive-maintenance in precision manufacturing.

title: A High Gain Metasurface-Based Filtering Antenna for Next-Generation Wireless Systems
authors: Kushagra Pratap, Shiven Shivsen Kumar, Dilip Kumar Choudhary
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: A compact, highly directional and selective filtering antenna employing a metasurface for performance enhancement is proposed in this paper. This module is intended for operation in the wideband. The two-dimensional metasurface possesses a 4×4 uniform array of copper rectangular patch cells. A copper cylindrical column through the substrate is introduced for shorting in the separation between the etched structure and DGS slots. This is helpful in bestowing robust filtering capability. This paired with the metasurface endows the structure with a superior roll-off rate at the fringes of the stopband. The metasurface offers improvements in radiation efficiency, which in turn results in enhancement of bandwidth and gain parameters. The substrate has dimensions of 50×50×0.813 mm cubic units. The structure resonates at frequencies of 5.9 GHz and 6.7 GHz, has a fractional bandwidth of 23.4%, covering 5.45 to 6.9 GHz, and offering a peak gain of 7.5 dB in the working band.

title: Variable-Rate DDM Compression Using Modulated Autoencoder with Window-Based Attention
authors: Chun-Han Chen, Pei-Jun Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This paper addresses the bandwidth and storage constraints in GNSS-Reflectometry missions by proposing a modulated autoencoder with window-based attention for Delay-Doppler Map (DDM) compression. The model enables variable-rate compression through a modulation network with single weights and enhances critical reflection regions (glistening and annulus zones) via localized attention. Experimental results show that it achieves superior rate–distortion performance compared with JPEG2000 and WebP. Moreover, wind speed retrieval using reconstructed DDMs incurs negligible accuracy loss, confirming the scientific reliability of the compressed data.

title: Caption Intelligence: Unified Vision-Language Models for Automated Description
authors: J. Mullai, Periya guru G, Vijayakumar B, Vinothkumar ER, Venkatesh M, Samuvel Godson
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Image captioning remains a central challenge in unified vision-language intelligence, requiring models to convert complex visual content into coherent natural-language descriptions. With the rise of large-scale multimodal transformers, the field has advanced beyond traditional CNNRNN pipelines toward architectures capable of stronger semantic grounding and contextual reasoning. This paper presents Caption Intelligence, a unified vision-language framework designed for automated image description through end-to-end multimodal representation learning. The model integrates visual-textual self-attention, region-aware cross-modal fusion, and contrastive pretraining to improve referential precision and linguistic fluency. Simulation experiments conducted on MS-COCO, Flickr30k, and a synthetic dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5, 0 0 0}$</tex> images demonstrate the model's effectiveness. Caption Intelligence achieves CIDEr 133.7, BLEU-4 41.9, METEOR 29.4, and SPICE 23.8, exceeding baseline CNN-LSTM systems by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{2 7 \%}$</tex>. Ablation studies show that cross-modal attention boosts referential accuracy by 14.2 %, while enhanced visual grounding reduces hallucinations by 21.5 %. Zero-shot evaluations across medical, indoor, and aerial imagery reveal only a 6.8 % drop in caption quality, confirming strong generalization. Latency tests indicate real-time capability at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 3 ~ m s}$</tex> per image on an NVIDIA A100 GPU. These results highlight the model's potential for accessibility, autonomous perception, and large-scale content summarization. Remaining challenges include knowledge grounding, bias mitigation, and hallucination control, motivating future research in ethical and interpretable multimodal captioning.

title: Design of an Intelligent Aquaculture System Based on STM32
authors: Yongsheng Xu, Guozhong Qin, Wei Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Constructing an innovative intelligent aquaculture system to address the technological upgrade needs in the aquaculture sector holds crucial practical value for promoting the industry's intelligent transformation and sustainable development. This research designs an intelligent aquaculture system centered around an STM32 processor. The system adopts a solar power supply scheme and utilizes various sensors, including those for temperature, turbidity, total dissolved solids (TDS), water level, light intensity, and video capture, to achieve real-time and precise collection of water body data. It employs modules such as relays and servo motors to design drive circuits for implementing functions like automatic feeding, water temperature regulation, water quality adjustment, and automatic oxygen supply. Furthermore, the system utilizes a cloud platform to enable remote monitoring and control functions via a mobile APP. Testing demonstrates that the system efficiently collects environmental data from the aquaculture pond and realizes automatic control. Users can operate the system flexibly either on-site or remotely via the mobile APP, meeting personalized aquaculture requirements. This provides robust support and assurance for the intelligent transformation of the aquaculture industry.

title: Research on Reinforcement Learning Containerization Method Based on RISC-V Architecture
authors: Andi Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence, reinforcement learning (RL) has shown great potential in intelligent decision-making, autonomous control, and adaptive systems. However, most existing RL frameworks rely on closed architectures such as x86 or ARM, which limit flexibility, scalability, and hardware customization. As an open-source instruction set architecture (ISA), RISC-V offers modularity, extensibility, and low power consumption, providing an ideal foundation for next-generation intelligent computing. This paper proposes a containerized RL framework based on RISC-V, aiming to build an efficient and portable environment for training and inference. The framework introduces a layered container architecture with instruction-level optimization, lightweight runtime, and fine-grained resource isolation. Experimental results on a RISC-V simulation platform demonstrate that the proposed method reduces startup latency and resource contention while improving computational efficiency and energy utilization, establishing a solid foundation for scalable AI deployment and RISC-V–based heterogeneous computing.

title: Impact of Network Loading and Disturbance Types on the Performance of ROCOF-Based Islanding Detection
authors: Qusay Salem, Rafat Aljarrah, Mazaher Karimi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Distributed generation (DG) integration into the distribution network increased rapidly in the last decades. However, the quality and supply continuity have to be guaranteed as the penetration rate of DGs is growing more. One essential obstacle that resist this integration is the unintentional islanding problem and its consequences. That's why different and wide anti-islanding methods and standards are adopted for DG integration. Rate of change of frequency (ROCOF) islanding detection is one of the passive methods which has slightest non-detection zone in comparison to other passive methods. Hence, this paper aims to investigate the characteristics of ROCOF islanding detection, its performance and detection speed in detecting unintentional islanding events. To achieve this target, different network loading states and various disturbance types have been proposed to clearly investigate the performance of ROCOF islanding detection under such variable conditions. The simulation case scenarios have been carried out in Matlab/Simulink to obtain the needed measurements that assess the variability of ROCOF detection times.

title: Trajectory Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles Using LSTM-VAE: A Variational Autoencoder Approach
authors: Prachi Kumari, Shardul Bharadwaj, Rashmi Panda
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Trajectory prediction is a significant function in many areas, such as autonomous driving, robotic navigation, surveillance, and human-robot interaction. The precise prediction of the future position of an entity is crucial for the avoidance of collisions, motion planning, and strategic decision-making. This paper proposes a Variational Autoencoder-based LSTM (LSTM-VAE) for trajectory prediction in autonomous vehicles using multiple features. This work also attempts to compare existing algorithms to predict the behavior of a vehicle to enable safer and more efficient travel. Different deep learning techniques used for trajectory prediction are explored, specifically LSTM, VAE, GAN, and LSTM-VAE. The standard metrics such as RMSE, ADE, and FDE are used for evaluating the models using the NGSIM dataset. The proposed method, LSTM-VAE, achieves average ADE, FDE and RMSE of 0.3965, 0.4064, and 0.3767 respectively on the NGSIM dataset. The study demonstrates the robustness of LSTM-VAE as it achieves improved prediction accuracy.

title: HybridMalConv: An Adversarially Robust Malware Detection System Using Integrated Features
authors: Yuto Kobayashi, Qian Wu, Atsushi Kanai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Machine learning-based malware detection systems have become increasingly prevalent, typically utilizing features derived from either static or dynamic analysis. While existing research has primarily focused on individual analysis methods, we propose HybridMalConv, an end-to-end malware detection model that effectively integrates both static and dynamic features without requiring manual feature engineering. We constructed a dataset containing 7,601 samples (3,056 malware and 4,545 cleanware) with both static binary data and dynamic analysis reports. Our experimental results demonstrate that HybridMalConv achieves superior detection performance compared to models using only static or dynamic features, with an accuracy of 97.66 percent and AUC of 0.9973. Furthermore, we show that HybridMalConv exhibits significantly improved robustness against FGSM adversarial attacks, maintaining 91 percent accuracy against adversarial samples compared to 71 percent for static-only models. This work highlights the effectiveness of feature integration in building both accurate and adversarially robust malware detection systems.

title: Distributed Relay Selection For Cooperative Relaying in Double Selective Fading Channels
authors: Satoshi Denno, Hiroki Nishimura, Yafei Hou
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This paper proposes a relay selection technique named as "distributed selection" for cooperative relaying. The proposed relay selection allows relays to determine by itself whether to forward signals, which makes it possible to implement the cooperative relaying system without additional frequency resource. The performance degradation due to the Doppler shift in a packet is taken into account in the proposed selection to keep high selection performance even in double selective fading channels. Computer simulation reveals that the proposed distributed selection achieves superior transmission performance in high mobility environment.

title: Research on Cross-Domain Power Data Fusion and In-Depth Analysis Based on Multimodal Model
authors: Zhanjie Liu, Xiaonan Gao, Jiaqi Yuan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: A fault classification method based on CNN-LSTM-Attention hybrid neural network is proposed in this paper to meet the challenge of multi-modal data fusion and parsing of power grid equipment. By integrating multi-source data such as time series, image and text, the model realizes the collaborative diagnosis of four kinds of faults, namely, sudden change of electrical quantity, deterioration of insulation medium, abnormal mechanical and thermodynamic, and coupling of information physics. The experimental results show that the overall accuracy of the fusion framework is 0.942, and the macro average <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> score is 0.93, which is significantly better than single modality and the early fusion baseline. In particular, the F1 score of 0.91 is achieved in the cyber-physical coupled fault diagnosis, which verifies the effectiveness of cross-modal dynamic feature alignment and collaborative learning. This study provides a reliable technical path for intelligent diagnosis of complex power grid faults, and further promotes the precision and automation of operation and maintenance decision-making.

title: Real-World Energy Optimization in Edge Computing Infrastructure: A Comparative Evaluation of DRL Algorithms
authors: Korukonda L K M Prem Chand, Chalapala Sowmya, Anju S Pillai
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Edge computing provides two essential advantages that optimize network performance through reduced delays and increased bandwidth usage by placing computing resources at data creation points. The study examines two deep reinforcement learning systems, which include Deep Q-Network (DQN) and Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), for sleep state management in edge devices. The work draws its data from an industrial environment which contains performance indicators that stem from operating edge computing cluster systems. We have a multiobjective reward function that is designed to minimize energy consumption and reduce latency at the same time it conserves the costs of state transitions between the four power states: Active, Idle, Sleep, and Deep Sleep. The experimental results demonstrate that PPO achieves better performance with 58.5% sleep state utilization and 36.5 % active state usage while ensuring system responsiveness. The analysis detected a direct correlation between CPU resource consumption and system power usage, which validates the operational efficiency of the proposed method. PPO provides better learning efficiency, converging within 200,000 timesteps, and provides the highest energy savings of 17.56 %, outperforming DQN, which converges more slowly (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx$</tex> 30 episodes) and achieves 16.47 % power reduction. These results justify the effectiveness of reinforcement learning in optimizing sleep-state management in edge computing environments.

title: MultiFake: A Multimodal Deep Learning Approach for Fake News Detection
authors: Hemkiran S, Kavyasree S, Harshavarthinie RJ, Devadharshini S
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The exponential rise of online content has made social media a primary source of news consumption, yet it has also accelerated the spread of misinformation and manipulated media. Detecting such fake news requires analyzing both textual and visual cues, as deceptive content often combines misleading text with fabricated or unrelated images. This work proposes MultiFake, a multimodal deep learning framework designed to identify fake news by integrating linguistic and visual information. The framework employs DistilBERT, a lightweight transformer-based language model, to extract high-level semantic representations from textual data, and ResNet-18, a convolutional neural network, to capture discriminative visual features from accompanying images. The proposed system is trained on a balanced dataset of multimodal news samples, ensuring diverse representation of real and fake content. The study also incorporates visualization modules, including confusion matrices and ROC curves, for interpretability and validation of the framework's behavior. This multimodal approach aims to bridge the gap between textual and visual semantics, offering a scalable and adaptable solution for misinformation detection across digital platforms.

title: Comparison of Vector Control Schemes Of Synchronous Reluctance Motor with Single Current Sensor
authors: Mansi Katoch, Himanshu Misra
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Synchronous Reluctance Motor (SynRM) operates on the principle of magnetic reluctance. SynRM is gaining popularity due to various reasons in the industry such as lack of magnets and windings in the rotor which makes it simpler as well as cost effective, high efficiency (IE4-IE5) class, noiseless, less torque ripples and smooth operation. Control of machine with the help of a single current sensor provides a faulttolerant operation. In this paper, the field-oriented control and direct torque control methods for SynRM are implemented with single current sensor. Further the proposed method of current estimation is simulated in MATLAB/Simulink to justify the operation and control technique.

title: Research on the Design of Human-AI Collaboration Classroom Teaching Framework Supported by Artificial Intelligence Educational Robots and its Collaborative Teaching Strategies
authors: Xin Hong, Haiguang Fang, Yang Deng, Hui Gu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: Human-AI collaboration teaching supported by educational robots refers to a process in which teachers maximize instructional effectiveness by orchestrating classroom elements and dynamically integrating robots with diverse pedagogical components to enable intelligent classroom practices. Drawing on Ihde's theory of human–technology relations, this study conceptualizes human-AI collaboration as a multi-layered interaction mediated by classroom elements, involving teachers, instructional resources, robots, and classroom environments. Building on this perspective, we develop a framework for human-AI collaborative teaching across three dimensions—data-driven processes, scenario-based applications, and technological support—and propose four collaboration strategies. Demonstrated through implementations in mathematics, English, and history, this study offers theoretical insights to guide the design and practice of human-AI collaborative classrooms while enhancing teachers' pedagogical competencies.

title: HT-DRIVE: Heterogeneous-Temporal GNN-Aided Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Resource Allocation in Platoon-Based V2X Networks
authors: Yanming Huang, Fengxia Han, Shengjie Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: Platoon-based Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) is a promising communication paradigm for intelligent vehicular networks. In such networks, each platoon leader (PL) engages in a high-throughput vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I) link to deliver entertainment data, while maintaining low-latency and high-reliability vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) links with multiple platoon members (PMs) to convey safety-critical information, thus posing conflicting performance requirements. Besides, the temporal variations and co-channel interferences further complicate the problem. To address these issues, we propose a heterogeneity-aware and dynamics-adaptive resource allocation framework, termed HT-DRIVE, which integrates an advanced heterogeneous temporal graph neural network (HTGNN) with a tailored multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) mechanism. Specifically, we design an HTGNN module to capture heterogeneous and temporal patterns within the platoon-based V2X network, generating structured embeddings that serve as state inputs to a modified multi-agent twin delayed deep deterministic policy gradient (MATD3) algorithm for downstream decision-making. To facilitate end-to-end optimization, several enhancements are also incorporated, including a hybrid action design to jointly handle discrete subchannel selection and continuous power control, a dual-critics architecture with reduced computational complexity, and customized loss functions for collaborative training. Extensive simulations demonstrate that HT-DRIVE significantly outperforms MATD3, multi-agent deep deterministic policy gradient (MADDPG) and heuristic methods under various settings, validating its effectiveness and robustness.

title: Tensor-Based Joint Channel and Symbol Estimation with Subspace-Based Parameter Extraction for Multi-RIS Uplink MIMO Systems
authors: Xi Han, Peiyang Li, Jiaxi Ying, Feifei Gao, Chau Yuen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: This paper investigates joint channel and symbol estimation in a multiple user uplink multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) system assisted by several reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs). With Khatri–Rao space–time (KRST) coding at the users, A PARATUCK2-based tensor framework is developed. To tackle the challenges of multi-RIS deployment, an adaptive accelerated fitting for PARATUCK2-based decomposition (AAF-PDE) algorithm is proposed. The algorithm employs an accelerated adaptive fitting (AAF) mechanism with dynamic step-size optimization, jointly estimating the RIS-base station (BS) channels, the composite user-RIS channels and the user symbols. Based on the accurately estimated channel matrices, geometric parameters can be further extracted via a two-dimensional ESPRIT procedure without dedicated radar signal. Simulation results verify that AAF-PDE outperforms benchmark algorithms in terms of channel estimation, symbol detection and convergence efficiency. Moreover, the root mean square error (RMSE) performance demonstrates that the geometric parameters estimation is enabled with high accuracy. Theoretical analysis on identifiability, complexity, and the Cramér–Rao bound (CRB) demonstrates the soundness of the proposed algorithm.

title: Precision Characterization of Graphite-based RTK Silicone Compounds for Repeatable Microwave Imaging Phantoms Manufacturing
authors: Nadim Conti, Pablo Giaccaglia, Valentina Lidoni, Alessia Rosa, Luca Maria Sconfienza
publish: 2026 International Conference on Medical Microwave Imaging Applications (ICMWIA)
abstract: The development of emerging medical imaging technologies, such as microwave imaging, requires precise phantoms and models to replicate realistic human conditions. These human phantoms require stable materials with dielectric properties close to those of biological tissue. However, there is heterogeneity in phantom recipes, materials, and fabrication methods. The aim of this study is to define a low-cost and easily reproducible formulation for human phantoms based on silicone and graphite for microwave imaging. An open-ended coaxial probe was used to measure the dielectric properties of each material sample as a function of internal composition, and an empirical formula was derived to ensure reproducibility.

title: BreastXAI Ultrasound Tumor Segmentation and Classification
authors: Swetha Priya V R, Rekha R
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Breast cancer remains one of the most common causes of death for women all over the world, and early detection drastically increases the likelihood of survival. Medical ultrasound has become one of the preferred methods for breast cancer screening due to its safety, non-invasive nature, and relatively low cost. Ultrasound images remain difficult even for experienced radiologists to interpret or classify due to differing image quality and fine differences between benign and malignant lesions. This work presents and propose a deep learning-protected framework for detection and classification of breast tumors from ultrasound images. In our framework, we propose a U-Net to segment Tumors accurately and followed by using lightweight but very effective classification models (EfficientNet, MobileNet, and ShuffleNet) to classify breast tumors into normal, benign, and malignant. We also build on the use of Grad-CAM to provide explainable results and better support clinical trust in the framework and the discriminate image regions that contribute to the model decision. The proposed framework is evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall and F1 score to establish robustness. The results demonstrate the promise of the framework as an effective and supportive tool for breast cancer diagnosis.

title: FONTA: Fog Node as Trust Authority for Trust Management in Edge Devices
authors: Shahid Mahmood, Moneeb Gohar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The rapid growth of edge devices has increased the cyber-attack surface, especially in resource-constrained environments. While digital certificates are widely used for authentication and data integrity, their processing demands often exceed the capabilities of edge devices. This paper proposes FONTA (Fog Node as Trust Authority), a fog-based trust management framework that offloads certificate validation and revocation tasks to resource-rich fog nodes. FONTA enables secure, real-time trust evaluation by deriving trust values from device behavior, minimizing the computational burden on edge nodes. Designed for constrained devices under IEEE 802.15.4 (e.g., WBANs and PANs), the framework ensures scalable and secure communication using transport layer security mechanisms.

title: A Low-Voltage Distribution Network Planning Method for Adapting Flexible Plug-and-Play Resources
authors: Wei Xu, Chuanguang Fan, Xinyuan Yang, Li Fan, Jie Ming
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: A multi-agent deep reinforcement learning framework is developed to address the challenge of low-voltage distribution network planning under high penetration of flexible plug-and-play distributed energy resources (DERs). The proposed method formulates the planning problem as a Markov Decision Process, leveraging a comprehensive state representation that encodes electrical, topological, and resource-related features. A multi-objective reward function aims to simultaneously regulate voltage, reduce network losses, and maximize distributed energy resource integration. Additionally, it imposes stringent penalties for constraint violations and suboptimal topologies. Transfer learning techniques based on topology-invariant features significantly enhance policy generalization capabilities in diverse and dynamically changing network configurations. Simulation studies on typical distribution feeders demonstrate that the Multi-Agent Approximate Policy Optimization (MAPPO) method achieves faster policy convergence and exhibits superior voltage control performance compared to traditional rule-based and single-agent approaches. Even under frequent topology reconfiguration and resource insertion/removal, the agent ensemble can still enhance resource utilization, reduce losses, and maintain operational feasibility. This demonstrates that advanced planning frameworks based on deep reinforcement learning are both effective and scalable for adaptive, datadriven management in modern distribution networks.

title: Potato Leaf Disease Classification Using Cnn and Web-Based Deployment for Early Detection
authors: Dhanashri Sonawane, K Amuthabala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Potato (Solanum tuberosum) is one of the most staple foods consumed all over the world. It holds the fourth position in the food chain. However, potato crops are easily affected by several leaf diseases, considering Early Blight (Alternaria solani) and Late Blight (Phytophthora infestans), which affect crop yield, quality, and formal income. Also, with the sudden changes in the weather conditions, the health of the potato leaves is getting affected badly, which decreases production of the crop. These leaves are affected by bacteria and fungi, which are required to be detected as early as possible in order to reduce the losses in crop yield. Manual inspection by experts, the traditional approach to disease identification, is not only timeconsuming and laborintensive but also prone to inaccuracies. In recent years, deep learning models have brought tremendous change and improvement in recognizing the accurate results of image classification and object detection systems. The proposed solution presents an Enhanced CNN, achieving an accuracy of 99.30 % for 3 classes. The model is trained using the PlantVillage dataset sourced from Kaggle, which includes annotated images of healthy and diseased leaves. Data augmentation and image preprocessing are used to enhance model generalization and improve accuracy across various lighting conditions. The model architecture incorporates global average pooling in place of the flatten layer to improve generalization. Beyond model development, the proposed model emphasizes real-world applicability by deploying the trained model through a webbased interface. The application is built using FastAPI for backend integration and a React-based frontend, making the solution highly accessible to farmers, agricultural officers, and researchers, even in remote areas with limited access to diagnostic facilities.

title: Distributionally Robust Low-Carbon Economic Dispatch for Virtual Power Plants Considering Wind and Solar Uncertainty
authors: Yangshu Luo, Ziwen Zhang, Lei Zhao, Xingwei Cao, Binghong Wang, Jinzhu Xia, Zhao Luo
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper presents a distributionally robust optimization (DRO) model for low-carbon economic dispatch in virtual power plants (VPPs), addressing the uncertainties of wind and solar power generation. The proposed two-stage day-ahead scheduling framework integrates carbon trading and green certificate mechanisms to enhance economic efficiency and promote renewable energy consumption. Through case studies, the model demonstrates a balance between conservatism and costeffectiveness, outperforming traditional stochastic and robust optimization methods by reducing operational costs while ensuring system reliability.

title: Assessment of Collective Fish Behavior Using Imaging Sonar Under Low-Visibility Conditions
authors: Tetsuo Imai, Ryo Harada, Kazuki Fukae, Daiki Togawa, Kenichi Arai, Hideyuki Takahashi, Hirofumi Miyajima, Mitsuru Hattori, Toru Kobayashi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: We evaluate imaging sonar as a practical alternative to underwater optical cameras for measuring collective fish behavior under low-visibility conditions such as nighttime and high turbidity.
