title: Dual Conformal Feed Dielectric Resonator Antenna With Triangular Metallic Upper Patch For 5G FR1 n79 Frequency Band
authors: Turio Sinha, Vibha Rani Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: In this manuscript a compact dual feed dielectric resonator antenna (DRA) integrated with a metallic patch is presented for n79 band of 5G FR1, aiming to enhance bandwidth and reduced size. The integration of a triangular metallic plate on top of square shaped DRA serves as a critical element in achieving these objectives. The configuration analyzed using CST Microwave Studio demonstrates an impedance bandwidth spanning from 4.18 to 5.66 GHz, effectively covering the desired n79 band and exhibiting a peak gain of 6.8 dBi, thereby establishing its suitability for 5G communication systems.

title: A Deep Learning Framework for Depression Detection from Speech Patterns
authors: Jayashree M Kudari, Ganesh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Depression Detection is essential for effective mental health involvement. This study provides a deep learning framework which integrates audio/speech representation learning through the HuBERT model for the better depression detection. This model is tested on the dataset DAIC-WOZ, which consisting of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5, 4 5 4}$</tex> pre-processed speech samples. For the data enhancement feature engineering played significant role in performing the segmentation, merging, and tokenization. In this study the HuBERT model for binary detection which is received with the classification of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 7. 7 1 \%}$</tex> accuracy and for multi-class scenarios achieved with the accuracy of 96.33 %. In addition to this, the RoBERTa-Large model was adjusted through a learning speed of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \mathrm{e}-5$</tex> and batch size of 16, by achieving a validation accuracy of 89.59 % and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{F 1}$</tex>-score of 92.43 %. By freezing the HuBERT's feature encoder which enhances not only classification performance but also reduces computational cost. Taking the mean through pooled examples (Mean pooling out) performed max pooling, which improved the results by 1.12 % (binary) and 5.94 % (multi-class). This model even proved the robust discriminative capability with an AUC of 0.98 on the ROC curve. The offered method focuses on integrating self-supervised learning for AIdriven mental health screening. Further studies aim to integrate Large Language Models (LLMs) to improve appropriate understanding and support real-world clinical implementation.

title: Enhancing Early Stroke Detection Using Machine Learning Models:A Comparative Study with XGBoost
authors: R. Dinesh Kumar, N. Krishnamoorthy
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: A stroke is a neurological disorder that occurs when the flow of blood to a specific part of the brain is disrupted either due to a blocked blood vessel, leading to an ischemic stroke, or because of a ruptured vessel, resulting in a haemorrhagic stroke. This interruption leads to the damages of the brain cells and, further on, develops into different neurologic problems. Although the possibility of stroke is directly related to the age factor, the risk increases with every passing year more than 60 % of the patients develop it before they turn 70 years, and almost 16 % before they turn 50 years. Among common symptoms are difficulties with speaking, sudden weakness in limbs, problems with balance, difficulties with vision on one side, and memory impairment. Risk factors include smoking, sedentary lifestyle, inadequate nutrition, high consumption of alcohol, increased cholesterol levels, obesity, hereditary susceptibility to stress, and mental disorders, such as depression. A stroke may cause longlasting effects like partial paralysis, impairment of intellectual functions, speech disorders, and loss of memory. Doctors typically diagnose a stroke with neurological examination and imaging studies - CT scan, MRI, Doppler ultrasound, cerebral angiography. Imaging techniques are very important for confirming stroke and its type. In this work, the XGBoost algorithm is implemented for stroke prediction using Kaggle data and gave an accuracy of 97 % on the test set.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Intelligent Misinterpretation Detection Algorithm for Cross-Cultural Communication
authors: Shaomin Ye, Liping Ge
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper proposes an intelligent misinterpretation detection algorithm for cross-cultural communication that integrates cultural feature embeddings. The algorithm constructs a three-layer architecture: data preprocessing - cultural feature fusion - misinterpretation detection. The data preprocessing layer generates structured word segmentation sequences; the cultural feature fusion layer combines Hofstede’s cultural dimensions to generate culturally enhanced word embeddings through weighted fusion, and designs a dynamic cultural attention mechanism to enhance the capture of culturally sensitive information; the misinterpretation detection layer implements end-to-end detection based on an improved Transformer and MLP classifier. Experiments using 89,000 cross-cultural corpora compare the proposed algorithm to four baseline models: SVM, Bi-LSTM, original Transformer, and BERT. The results show that the proposed algorithm achieves an accuracy of 92.3% and an F1 score of 91.1%, respectively, which are 3.1 and 3 percentage points higher than BERT. The algorithm also demonstrates a significant advantage in detecting misinterpretations associated with non-verbal behavior, achieving an accuracy of 88.7%, a 9.4 percentage point increase over BERT. Ablation experiments show that removing the two modules of the cultural feature fusion layer reduces the accuracy to 85.9%, a decrease of 6.4 percentage points. In real-time testing, the algorithm took 6.5 seconds to process 10,000 texts, meeting real-time detection requirements and validating the algorithm’s dual advantages in performance and efficiency.

title: Photometric Stereo and Deep Learning-Based Method for Surface Defect Detection of Automotive Sheet Metal Panels
authors: Xudong Ran, Pan Zhang, Longbing Zhao, Zhongwei Li, Kai Zhong
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Driven by the demands of lightweight design and intelligent manufacturing in the automotive industry, aluminum alloy body panels are increasingly used but are prone to cracks and missing punched holes during stamping. Their highly reflective surfaces make reliable automated inspection difficult. This paper proposes an online defect detection method that integrates photometric stereo with deep learning. A multi-sensor photometric stereo imaging system is deployed at the end of the stamping line. By illuminating the parts from multiple controlled directions, the system reconstructs surface normals and height information, which are then combined with two-dimensional grayscale images to form a multi-modal defect representation. On top of YOLOv8, a CGC-YOLO network is designed with GSSPPF, CAF, and CSPAN modules to enhance multi-scale feature extraction and channel-aware fusion. A real production dataset of cracks and missing holes is collected and augmented for training. Experimental results show that, at a conveyor speed of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5-1 \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>, the proposed method achieves high precision and recall for both crack and hole defects while satisfying real-time online inspection requirements.

title: Ground Point Extraction from LiDAR Point Clouds in Diverse Terrain Environments and Corresponding DEM Construction
authors: Zhen Li, Ruicong Zhai, Hanfei Zhang, Jinhua He
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To improve the accuracy of digital elevation model (DEM) generation under complex terrain and heterogeneous surface conditions, and to address the challenges posed by the uneven density of airborne LiDAR point clouds, this study proposes an integrated method combining cloth simulation filtering (CSF) and inverse distance weighting (IDW) interpolation. The proposed approach was evaluated across three distinct terrain types: flat bare ground, flat areas with high vegetation, and complex terrain regions. The DEM generation process consists of three main steps: first, voxel-based equidistant sampling is applied to remove outliers and reduce data volume; next, ground and non-ground points are separated using the cloth simulation filtering algorithm; finally, the ground points are gridded, and a DEM is generated using inverse distance weighting interpolation. Experimental results demonstrate that the combined CSF and IDW approach achieves high overall accuracy and performs reliably across varying terrain and surface cover conditions. This study provides a valuable reference for DEM construction in diverse and topographically complex environments.

title: Research on Model Optimization Using Deep Learning in Intelligent Employment Guidance Systems
authors: Wei Zhou, Yalan Cheng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper proposes a dynamic attention fusion employment guidance model (DAF-EGM). This model optimizes matching performance through the collaboration of multiple modules: a weighted fusion CNN with multiple convolutional kernels extracts implicit skills and collaboration requirements from job descriptions. An LSTM with a temporal decay mechanism captures dynamic trends in job seekers' career histories. A dynamic attention module is constructed by combining a time decay factor (to adapt to changes in job search demand) with a job competitiveness coefficient (to quantify market dynamics such as the number of applicants and supply-demand ratio) to achieve adaptive adjustment of feature weights. Experiments are conducted on 72,863 valid samples preprocessed from three types of real-world data: 50,000 recruitment platform data, 10,000 graduate tracking data, and supply-demand data from 12 industries. The model is compared with traditional collaborative filtering (CF), static attention deep learning (SA-DL), and CNN-RNN fusion (CR-Fusion) models. Results show that in the Top-10 recommendation scenario, DAF-EGM achieves 89.2% precision, 86.7% recall, 87.8% F1-score, and 0.083 MAE. These improvements are 26.9, 28.2, and 27.6 percentage points, respectively, compared to traditional CF, with a MAE reduction of 0.132. Compared to SA-DL, DAF-EGM achieves a 13.6 percentage point improvement in precision and a MAE reduction of 0.075. Simulation plots demonstrate that DAF-EGM's precision decreases by only 3.2% with increasing Top-K (5-25) (compared to 7.4% for traditional CF), and MAE drops below 0.1 after 15 training epochs (compared to 25 for SA-DL). Ablation experiments confirm that the time decay factor and the job competition coefficient synergistically improve F1-score by over 14 percentage points, providing technical support for the intelligent upgrade of employment guidance systems.

title: Deep Learning-based Air Pollutant Particle Prediction Model
authors: Shaohu Han, Mianyu Jiang, Hongkai Wang, Zhanfeng Chi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper proposes a deep learning-based air quality prediction model aimed at accurately forecasting key air quality indicators, PM2.5 and PM10. The study innovatively replaces the multi-head attention mechanism in the encoder of the Transformer model with a Patch-wise Attention mechanism, while also incorporating the SCI-Block module and the FeatureMixingMLP module to construct an efficient and precise prediction model. The encoder extracts time-series features through multiple layers of Patch-wise Attention, and the decoder utilizes the Transformer architecture to make accurate predictions of future air quality. Additionally, the SCI-Block feature mixing module is introduced to effectively merge feature information at different levels, enhancing the model's ability to capture complex air quality fluctuation patterns. Experimental results show that the model performs excellently in predicting PM2.5 and PM10, achieving R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values of 0.977 and 0.951, respectively, demonstrating outstanding predictive performance and generalization ability. This research provides a new approach for air quality prediction, and the proposed model is expected to offer strong technical support for environmental monitoring and public health decision-making, aiding in more precise air quality monitoring and early warning.

title: Comparative Analysis of Inner Slot Loading in Square Patch Reflectarray Elements
authors: P.Jothilakshmi, Chris Calvin P, Afra Parveen Jameel
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This work examines the influence of a concentric inner slot introduced into a conventional square microstrip patch element for high frequency reflectarray applications. Two element configurations are evaluated on a Rogers RT Duroid 5880 substrate, with patches of identical outer size that include a centered square slot in small and large apertures. Full wave simulations in CST Microwave Studio are performed from 10.7 to 11.7 GHz under an infinite periodic boundary model. The slot increases the usable reflection phase range from 310° to 342° while preserving the monotonic dispersion required for single layer phase compensation. The enhancement arises from reduced capacitive coupling between the slotted region and the surrounding conductor, which also improves return loss. The proposed slot loaded element provides a broader dynamic phase range and better bandwidth without added fabrication complexity, making it a practical alternative to plain square patches for compact Ku band reflectarrays.

title: Research on Operational State Prediction and Optimization Methods for New Energy Equipment Based on Digital Twins
authors: Yutong Wu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: To address the complex operational characteristics, fluctuating efficiency, and insufficient predictive capability of new energy equipment, this study proposes a digital twin-based operational state prediction and optimization framework. The approach establishes a high-fidelity virtual-physical mapping system integrating real-time sensor data, process models, and historical operational records. A hybrid prediction model combining Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN) and gated recurrent units (GRU) is constructed to capture nonlinear temporal dependencies and improve fault trend prediction accuracy. The optimization layer introduces a multi-objective strategy based on Pareto dominance, balancing energy consumption, system stability, and prediction precision. The system architecture encompasses data acquisition, model training, and optimization control, achieving closed-loop synchronization between the physical and virtual environments. Experimental validation on a wind–solar hybrid energy platform demonstrates that the proposed model improves prediction accuracy by $9.4 \%$, enhances energy optimization efficiency by $12.7 \%$, and reduces latency by $31 \%$ compared with conventional methods. The results confirm that digital twin-driven state prediction provides effective support for intelligent operation and maintenance, enabling adaptive scheduling and real-time fault prevention in new energy systems. This research offers practical guidance for constructing intelligent, efficient, and sustainable energy management frameworks in future industrial application.

title: Simulation-Guided Real-Time Traffic Congestion Detection Using YOLOv8n
authors: Avanthika Shydh, Vibusha Magesh, Anju S Pillai
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Rapid urbanization has significantly increased vehicle density, leading to frequent congestion in metropolitan road networks. This not only impacts travel efficiency but also contributes to environmental and economic challenges. In order to guarantee smooth movement of transport and improve the management of roads, effective real-time traffic monitoring is essential. This paper describes a real-time system for localizing and categorizing traffic congestions with the help of CARLA, an autonomous driving simulator, coupled with a pre-trained YOLOv8n object detection model. The suggested arrangement uses GPU-accelerated inference via ONNX Runtime to perform the task rather than the need for an additional model training or physical sensors. Various traffic densities, weather conditions, and lighting scenarios in the camera feeds have been simulated to vehicle counts and congestion levels dynamically. Multithreading usage has allowed throughput to be increased and the average data processing rate to be kept above 30 frames per second even when traffic is heavy. The proposed system achieved 0.934 mAP @ 50, 165 FPS, and a congestionclassification F1-score of 97.4 % validating the stability of the proposed system and demonstrating its suitability for real-time intelligent transportation applications.

title: A Novel Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Intelligent Fire Scene Reconstruction Based on Multi-Source Data
authors: Jinfeng Zhang, Zhengyi Feng, Kaiming Zhu, Wenjing Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address issues in fire scene reconstruction— including multi-source data fusion difficulties, poor deep learning model adaptability, and insufficient reconstruction accuracy—a novel hybrid deep learning framework based on multi-source data is proposed. The framework performs hierarchical preprocessing and feature extraction on fire scene multi-source data (e.g., surveillance videos, sensor data, personnel trajectories), then enables collaborative data analysis via a hybrid architecture integrating CNN (image feature processing) and RNN (time-series modeling). It also adopts an evaluation-guided resource allocation scheme to eliminate processing bottlenecks and a step-by-step packing strategy to balance CPU utilization and GPU memory overhead. Experiments show: 82% GPU utilization in throughput-priority mode (for large-scale data), 0.73s average latency in latencypriority mode (for real-time rescue), and 68.8% average CPU utilization in balance mode. Compared with traditional methods (e.g., MediaPipe), it achieves 1077% higher detection frame rate and over 35% improved accuracy for key scene info (e.g., fire spread path).The framework effectively solves multi-source data collaborative analysis in fire scenes, providing a reliable solution for intelligent reconstruction on CPU-GPU heterogeneous servers, and can be applied to fire investigation, safety assessment, and emergency planning.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Using Hybrid Action Strategy for Service Restoration in Distribution Network
authors: Rony Seto Wibowo, Ontoseno Penangsang, Dimas Fajar Uman Putra, Omar Fabian, Faisal Taufiq Rochman, Muhammad Naufal Nail Annafiu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Reliable distribution operation is increasingly stressed by frequent disturbances and variability from distributed generation (DG). We propose a two-stage FLISR framework that decouples fast rule-based isolation from learning-based service restoration. Stage-1 uses a graph-only path-screening routine to identify the minimal set of sectionalizing switches for isolation without running power flow. Stage-2 trains a topology-aware Deep Q-Network (DQN) to restore service with a hybrid action design that mixes small micro-composite packets and single-switch moves, plus an explicit STOP action. Feasibility is enforced at decision time via execution-time masking that rejects actions causing non-radial topology, served-load degradation, or voltage-screen violations avoiding pre-mask bias while keeping exploration intact. The framework is implemented on a modified IEEE 33-bus system with embedded photovoltaics (PV) and evaluate it by load served, active-power losses, voltage profile, and training time. Experiments show the hybrid policy achieves fast, high-quality restoration under topological and voltage constraints, offering a favorable balance between the stability of decomposed actions and the rapid gains of composite packets. The results indicate a practical path toward self-healing distribution operation under DG penetration.

title: Optimization of Breakdown Performance of Nano-MgO/XLPE Composite Insulation Dielectrics Based on Orthogonal Experiment
authors: Jiawei Chen, Yue Shao, Manshi Qiu, Yunjie Zhou, Yashuai Liang, Yunlong Sun
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: In this paper, the orthogonal experiment is employed to investigate the effects of filler particle size, doping concentration, coupling agent type, and atmospheric pressure dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) treatment duration on the DC breakdown performance of MgO/XLPE composite insulating dielectrics during the nano-doping process. The results demonstrate that the DC breakdown strength of the modified MgO/XLPE composites is significantly enhanced. Meanwhile, the uniformity of the breakdown performance of the modified composites is also improved. Within the range of factor levels studied in this paper, with the increase of MgO particle size, the DC breakdown field strength of MgO/XLPE composites increases first and then decreases, indicating the existence of an optimal doping particle size. The increase in doping concentration can improve the DC breakdown performance of the composites. The types of coupling agents exert no significant difference in promoting the DC breakdown performance of the composites, whereas the composites doped with DBD-treated MgO exhibit superior DC breakdown performance compared to those directly doped with MgO. The influence degree of the four factors on the breakdown performance of the composites from high to low is as follows: doping concentration, particle size, DBD treatment duration, and coupling agent type.

title: Research on Chinese Named Entity Recognition Based on ResNet Algorithm
authors: Xihui Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper proposes a convolutional neural network optimization strategy for detecting the dispersion state of crushed stones in synchronous crushed stone seals. The study constructed a dataset of gravel scattering states and analyzed the high linear correlation between image channels using the Pearson correlation coefficient. Subsequently, deep convolution was adopted instead of standard convolution to optimize the ResNet series models. The experimental results show that the computational complexity of the optimized ResNet50, ResNet101 and ResNet152 models is significantly reduced, and the average cross-union ratio is improved, verifying the effectiveness of the optimization strategy. Meanwhile, the research also attempted to combine the self-attention mechanism with IDCNN and apply it to the recognition of named entities in judicial documents, achieving good recognition results. This paper not only provides an efficient method for detecting the dispersion state of crushed stones, but also offers new ideas for the field of named entity recognition.

title: Performance Comparison of PMSM Operated Using Solar PV and Battery Energy Storage
authors: Naman Verma, Prateek Singh Kushwaha, Abhinay Pratap Singh, Rajesh Gupta
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: This paper compares the performance of a 2 kW six-phase Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) powered independently from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$72 / 48 \mathrm{V}$</tex> battery, and a 1.6 kW solar photovoltaic (SPV) array. The main objective of this paper is to evaluate the performance and behavior under varied power supply conditions. Both no-load and on-load conditions are used to test the motor operation. Two scenarios are considered for on-load conditions: first one is a constant mechanical load and second one with a linear increase in load. The PMSM was selected for this study because of its excellent torque-to-weight ratio, higher efficiency, smaller size, and ability to precisely control the speed. These qualities make it perfect for solar PV/battery driven systems such as water pumping. Furthermore, PMSMs work very well even at varying speeds and require little maintenance, which makes them more appropriate for applications with variable power input source like solar PV. The study mainly uses hardware experiments conducted in the laboratory. This study offers guidance on how to choose suitable energy sources for PMSM applications that runs on solar power/battery energy storage.

title: A Research on Medical Big Data Security Technology Based on Fog Computing
authors: Baoling Qin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the rapid development of intelligent medical digitization and people's aspirations for a healthy life, the issue of medical data security has become increasingly prominent. The sensitivity and value of medical data demand that we take effective measures to safeguard data security. This not only pertains to the risks of data leakage and personal information abuse but also to issues of social stability. To address the problem of medical data security, the advantages and characteristics of the distributed architecture of fog computing are primarily utilized, and RSA algorithm technology is integrated. This enables the effective processing and storage of the large volume of heterogeneous data generated by medical service systems in the context of the Internet of Things at fog nodes, achieving low-latency processing and efficient storage of medical data and ensuring the data security of data sources. Simultaneously, technical means such as identity authentication and access control, fine-grained permission management, and data encryption in intelligent medical service systems are employed to enhance data security protection. Research has demonstrated that the integration of fog computing and RSA algorithm technology can effectively reduce computational costs, ensure the security of medical data flow, protect patient privacy, and prevent improper information access and abuse. The intelligent medical data security technology based on fog computing is of great significance for protecting patient privacy and data security. Meanwhile, it plays an important role in safeguarding medical data, improving service quality, and promoting industry development, and it represents an important research direction in the future intelligent medical field.

title: Enhancing IoT Security with Lightweight XGBoost Based Intrusion Detection System
authors: Shymala Gowri S, Mukilan A, Pari M
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The recent rise in both the frequency and sophistication of cyberattacks in particular, those directed towards distributed and resource constrained, cyber physical environments justifies the need for timely, effective, and adaptive intrusion detection in IoT networks, particularly for lightweight, efficient IoT edge implementations. The work presented in this paper addresses the design of a lightweight, feature efficient intrusion detection system using the XGBoost machine learning algorithm. A classification model was trained on both signal and statistical based subsets of features extracted from a dataset, where the model learned to classify the network traffic data as either benign or malicious. A key aspect of the comparison was to separate the performance of the XGBoost model from the performance of ConvNeXT, which seemed to use an image-based approach to represent the network traffic. Results show that the XGBoost model was both accurate and precise, with higher recall and classification accuracy with less misclassification and ConvNeXT showed poor performance for both classes with class imbalance and a higher false negative rate than XGBoost. Results of this study demonstrate that tabular learning methods such as XGBoost can be effective solutions for both static and realtime intrusion detection for IoT environments in an efficient, applicable manner, with the capacity to be feasibly and scalable as an edge deployment without an overly complex processes to go through to transformation a data model.

title: Probabilistic Forecasting of Photovoltaic Power with Missing Data Using CEEMDAN-STGNN and Quantile-KDE Modeling
authors: Zhenchen Zhou, Haohong Zhang, Bohao Zhang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: As the penetration of photovoltaic power generation in power systems continues to increase, its high-frequency volatility and uncertainty place higher demands on grid dispatch and energy management. To address the common data missingness issues and difficulty constructing prediction intervals in photovoltaic power series, this paper proposes an ultra-shortterm photovoltaic power forecasting method that integrates modal decomposition, dynamic graph neural networks, and probabilistic modeling. This method first uses Complete Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition (CEEMDAN) to perform intrinsic mode decomposition on the original output series to extract more stable multi-scale feature signals. Subsequently, a dynamic spatial adjacency graph is constructed based on each mode and fed into a spatiotemporal graph neural network (ST-GNN) model to achieve deep fusion of spatiotemporal features and fill missing values. Finally, through a joint modeling of quantile regression and kernel density estimation, probabilistic interval predictions at multiple confidence levels are achieved. Experiments on the realworld Desert Knowledge Solar Centre (DKASC) dataset demonstrate that this method outperforms several mainstream deep learning methods in both point prediction accuracy (e.g., MAE, RMSE) and interval prediction performance (e.g., PICP, Pinball Loss), demonstrating enhanced robustness and generalization. This research provides an effective new approach for building highly reliable and interpretable photovoltaic power forecasting systems and holds great promise for engineering applications.

title: Scanpath Prediction Based on Areas of Interest
authors: Towa Ohno, Junya Hara, Yuichi Tanaka, Hiroshi Higashi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a region-based scanpath prediction method that models gaze transitions among visually salient regions. Existing coordinate-based approaches aim to predict a 2-D coordinate of the next fixation in a scanpath. However, they do not fully align with human visual processing, where attention inherently shifts between discrete attentional units rather than specific spatial points. Our method first identifies areas of interest (AOIs) from a saliency map and assigns each fixation to its corresponding AOI. It then integrates the visual features of each AOI with the temporal gaze history to predict a probability distribution over the candidate AOIs for the next fixation. Experimental results on standard eye tracking benchmarks demonstrate that our approach achieves superior accuracy in the next fixation region prediction to existing coordinate-level methods.

title: Design and Implementation of the 1.5 GHz Frequency Synthesizer for the Superconducting Harmonic Cavity System of HALF
authors: Fengxia Xu, Yelong Wei, Kai Zhang, Guangyao Feng, Shancai Zhang, Fangfang Wu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The superconducting third harmonic cavity system of the Hefei Advanced Light Facility (HALF) operates in a passive mode, with the operating frequency 1.5 GHz that is three times the frequency of the main cavity (499.8 MHz). To implement tuning control for the harmonic cavity, the Low-Level Radio Frequency (LLRF) system requires a 1.5 GHz reference signal that is coherent with the main 499.8 MHz RF signal. To meet this requirement, this paper presents a detailed engineering design of a 1.5 GHz frequency synthesizer intended for the HALF superconducting harmonic cavity system. The design covers the overall module architecture, key technical specifications, component selection, circuit design, and layout. Performance tests were conducted on the developed synthesizer. Experimental results demonstrate that all parameters achieve the design specifications, satisfying the operational requirements of the LLRF controller for the HALF superconducting third harmonic cavity system.

title: A Quantum Frequency-Domain Multi-Qubit Encoding Framework for Robust Video Transmission
authors: Udara Jayasinghe, Thisarani Atulugama, Anil Fernando
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Video transmission over noisy channels faces significant challenges due to temporal correlations and high data rates. Time-domain quantum communication partially addresses these issues but remains susceptible to noise. This paper presents a frequency-domain quantum encoding framework that overcomes the limitations of existing systems by compressing video streams using Versatile Video Coding (VVC), then channel encoding, followed by multi-qubit quantum encoding, and finally transforming the data into the frequency domain using the Quantum Fourier Transform (QFT). This approach distributes video information across frequency components, enhancing resilience against channel noise and distortion. Video sequences with Group of Pictures (GOP) sizes of 8, 16, and 32 are examined to assess the effects of temporal compression on transmission performance. At the receiver, the Inverse QFT (IQFT) reconstructs the time-domain signal, followed by multi-qubit, channel and source decoding to recover high-quality video frames. Experimental results, evaluated using Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) and Video Multi-Method Assessment Fusion (VMAF), reveal that increasing qubit allocation significantly enhances visual quality and noise robustness, achieving up to a 4 dB channel SNR gain over time-domain methods, thereby offering a scalable, efficient, and noise-tolerant solution for quantum video communication.

title: Dynamic Fault ARC Feature Extraction and Recognition Technology Scheme in Multi-source Load Scenario
authors: Chong Wang, Fei Yan, Zhenxiang Li, Hui Zhang, Ziyi Liu, Xiaoyun Liao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: In modern power systems, with the widespread access of distributed power sources and frequent switching of flexible loads, the complexity and dynamics of power systems have increased significantly. Traditional arc fault detection methods are difficult to meet the accurate and stable identification requirements. In this paper, a dynamic fault arc feature extraction and recognition method based on multisource load scenarios is proposed. Through recursive twodimensional principal component analysis (R2DPCA), multivariable abnormal behavior adaptive detection and temporal logic clustering, the accuracy and stability of arc signal detection are improved. It can cope with the complex environment of high noise and load fluctuation, and realize the accurate identification of fault arc. This method significantly reduces the rate of false detection and missed detection, and provides efficient and reliable fault diagnosis support for power systems.

title: A Novel Quantum-Classical Approach to Digital Differentiation with Kalman Filtering and Quantum Dots
authors: Deepak Vats, Vishal Srivastav, Vasudev Grover
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: The fusion of quantum technology with classical digital signal processing opens up promising avenues for high precision computational system. In this work we propose a novel approach for the design of a quantum enhanced digital differentiator by leveraging the unique quantum properties of quantum dots-based spin qubits in conjunction with Kalman filtering. Quantum dots offer discrete energy levels, high sensitivity and extended coherence time which are utilized to improve the resolution and stability of the differentiation process. To address inherent quantum noise and uncertainties in the signal, we integrate a Kalman filter that adaptively estimates and refines the differentiated signal in the real time. This hybrid quantum classical framework is theoretically developed and validate through comprehensive numerical simulations. The results demonstrate enhanced accuracy, noise resilience and computational efficiency, making the proposed method suitable for advanced application in control system, precision measurement, and quantum-assisted processing.

title: Multi-Frame Optimized Virtual Reference Frame for Low-Delay Predictive Video Compression
authors: Nimesh Pollwaththage, Yasith Ganearachchi, Prabhath Samarathunga, Joseph El Gemayel, Anil Fernando
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Conventional hybrid video codecs in low-delay configurations employ the first frame of a Group of Pictures (GOP) as the reference anchor in their prediction hierarchy, resulting in deterministic but non-adaptive reference structures. Although multiple reference frames are supported, the selection process remains purely temporal and lacks content-driven optimization. To address this limitation, we propose a Virtual Reference Frame (VRF) designed to act as an intelligent and statistically optimized substitute for conventional reference frames. The proposed VRF is synthesized through a constrained multi-frame optimization process that minimizes the cumulative mean squared error between the VRF and all other frames within a GOP. As a result, the VRF becomes a single representative frame that captures the dominant spatio-temporal characteristics of the GOP, reducing global residual energy and consequently the entropy of the transform coefficients to be encoded. Experimental evaluations conducted on grayscale video sequences using a low-delay encoder configuration demonstrate consistent bitrate savings and quality improvements compared to the baseline, confirming the efficiency, compatibility, and codec-agnostic applicability of the proposed approach within existing hybrid video coding frameworks.

title: Toe Grip Strength Training Application for Preventing Falls Among the Elderly
authors: Ayumi Miyazawa, Daichi Fukui, Kei Watanabe, Namiko Koreishi, Mutsuhiro Nakashige
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Falls among the elderly are largely influenced by declining physical function. Strengthening toe grip strength is considered effective. However, conventional towel gather exercises are boring and difficult to maintain. In this study, using the toe grip strength measurement device HAJICHECK, we developed three types of training apps incorporating gamification. Each app targets specific aspects: maximum toe grip strength, left-right balance, and force application timing. Interview evaluations at an exhibition received positive feedback from rehabilitation facility staff and others. Furthermore, an evaluation experiment confirmed the toe grip strength enhancement effects of the training apps. In the future, we plan to conduct long-term efficacy studies involving elderly subjects to verify its effectiveness.

title: Research on Helicopter Flight Safety Detection Algorithm Based on Weighted Calculation
authors: Hong Wang, Fushuang Liu, Yijiang Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: In recent years, helicopter flight control technology has developed rapidly, and research on helicopter flight safety has become a hot topic in flight data research and analysis both domestically and internationally. This paper addresses the problem of helicopter flight safety monitoring. Using trajectory data and attitude data, which reflect real-time position in helicopter flight training data, as the main data source, a weighted calculation-based instantaneous anomaly detection algorithm is proposed. This algorithm integrates five unsupervised anomaly detection methods: Local Outlier Factor (LOF), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Isolation Forest (Iforest), Principal Component Analysis (PCA), and Lightweight Online Anomaly Detector (LODA). It uses a feedforward neural network to perform weighted integration of the outputs of various algorithms, with AUC as the evaluation metric, to optimize the comprehensive performance of anomaly discrimination. The algorithm is validated on multiple public datasets and real helicopter flight data. The results show that the weighted fusion method is superior to a single algorithm in terms of average AUC value, and can effectively identify abnormal behaviors in flight states, with strong robustness and practicality. The study also extracts altitude, pitch angle, roll angle, and airspeed as key features from actual flight data, and analyzes anomaly patterns under multi-platform and single-platform scenarios, providing data-driven diagnostic methods for helicopter flight safety.

title: Hybrid XGBoost and ANN Model Based Ship Speed Prediction in Real Weather Conditions
authors: Brij Kishor Tiwari, T. Bala Krishna, Jawad Ahmad Dar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: An efficient and cost-effective ocean transportation is still a significant challenging area of research, specifically when ships encounter the severe weather situation during their voyages. The overall resistance acting over ship traveling at its designated speed in real weather critical conditions needs higher power consumption to maintain the constant speed. Therefore, this leads to increased fuel consumption in the critical weather situation, contributing to increase of Fuel Oil Consumption (FOC) and, subsequently, heightened carbon emissions, which is a key factor in global warming. In this paper we present an innovative approach for optimizing autonomous ship navigation by integrating conventional routing methods and with a hybrid model of machine learning combining Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) and Artificial Neural Networks (ANN). Investigated the comparative analysis for multiple navigational routes and demonstrated the optimized model that will lead to more accurate prediction of the optimal fuel consumption ship route.

title: Edge AI-Enabled Real-Time Gesture Recognition for UAV Control
authors: Weihang You, Junchen Wu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Gesture-based UAV control provides an intuitive alternative to conventional remote controllers, yet existing approaches often fail to satisfy real-time, robustness, and edge deployment constraints. This paper presents a lightweight edge AI framework for real-time UAV gesture control using a MobileNetV3-based convolutional neural network optimized for embedded platforms. The system supports ten control gestures, achieves sub- 20 ms end-to-end latency, and integrates directly with MAVLink-based flight controllers. Experimental results on multiple edge devices demonstrate favorable accuracy-efficiency trade-offs compared with state-of-the-art baselines. In addition, the proposed framework maintains stable performance across varying lighting conditions and backgrounds, highlighting its robustness for outdoor and indoor applications. A comprehensive ablation study further analyzes the impact of model depth, input resolution, and quantization on both accuracy and latency. The framework is evaluated in closed-loop flight tests, confirming that the recognized gestures translate into safe, smooth, and responsive UAV maneuvers.

title: Research on Stock Market Sentiment Analysis Methods and Models Based on Image Processing Algorithms in Financial Markets
authors: Xuan He
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Aiming at the limitations of traditional technical analysis methods in stock market sentiment analysis that rely on subjective judgment and manual feature design, this study proposes an end-to-end sentiment classification method based on deep convolutional neural network. The method converts stock OHLCV trading data into standardized K-line images, integrates three levels of visual information: shallow geometric details, mid-level morphological patterns and deep semantic abstraction through a multi-scale feature fusion mechanism, and uses a weighted cross-entropy loss function to deal with the class imbalance problem to realize the automatic recognition of bullish, bearish and neutral emotions. Experimental results verify the significant performance advantages of the proposed method over traditional machine learning and the existing deep learning baseline methods. Confusion matrix analysis and feature space visualization further confirm the good discriminative ability of the feature representation learned by the model. The research shows that the fusion of image processing algorithms and deep learning technology provides an effective solution for financial time series sentiment analysis, and the method has practical application value in the development of investment decision support systems and quantitative trading strategies.

title: Design of a Model for Building Excellent Management Capabilities for Multi-Agent Ecological Collaboration in Power Grids
authors: Zhiguang Hu, Hao Zuo, Jingjing Yang, Weide Gu, Zhaoyang Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Addressing the issues of low collaborative efficiency, data privacy leaks, and weak dynamic adaptability caused by the surge in multiple stakeholders in power grids under the "dual carbon" objective, this paper constructs a three-layer multi-agent ecological collaborative management capability model consisting of "core control - collaborative decision-making - execution feedback," and designs a Federated Attention Deep Reinforcement Learning (FA-DRL) algorithm. This algorithm integrates the gradient encryption transmission of federated learning, the high-value stakeholder focus of the attention mechanism, and the dynamic decision-making capability of reinforcement learning. It achieves a balance between privacy protection and collaborative optimization through a "local training - encrypted aggregation - weight optimization" process. Experiments are conducted based on actual data (1.2TB, 5-minute granularity) from a provincial power grid in 2023, comparing the results with traditional DRL and FL+DRL algorithms. The results show that the collaborative decision-making time of FA-DRL is 1.23 seconds, which is 65.4% lower than that of traditional DRL; the comprehensive energy consumption of the power grid is 4.82×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> kWh/day, which is 8.7% lower than that of traditional DRL; the renewable energy absorption rate reaches 92.3%, which is 4.1% higher than that of FL+DRL; the privacy risk value is 0.03, and the decision-making accuracy is still 92.7% under ±25% output fluctuation. This study demonstrates that the model and algorithm can effectively improve the collaborative management capability of the power grid and provide technical support for the operation of new power systems.

title: Line-of-Sight Probability in Macrocells: Framework, Statistical Models, and Parametrization From Massive Real-World Datasets in the USA
authors: Bassel Abou Ali Modad, Xin Yu, Yao-Yi Chiang, Andreas F. Molisch
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Accurate modeling of line-of-sight (LOS) probability is crucial for wireless channel description and coverage planning. The presence of a LOS impacts other channel characteristics such as pathloss, fading depth, delay- and angular spread, etc. Existing models, although useful, are based on very limited datasets. In this paper, we establish a framework to produce high accuracy LOS models from geospatial data in different environments, and apply it to create a LOS model for macrocells, using datasets of the United States (US) on a national scale, using more than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$13,000$ </tex-math></inline-formula> locations of real-world macrocells. Based on this we create a new, fully parameterized model that better describes macrocell deployments in the US than the 3GPP model. We furthermore demonstrate that for improved accuracy the LOS probability should be modeled on a per cell basis, and the model parameters treated as random variables; we provide a full description and parameterization of this novel approach and by simulations show that it better predicts the inter-cell interference at the cell-edge than an average-based model.

title: Affordable Low-Wind Rooftop Wind Turbine Energy System Design for Rural Electrification
authors: Jawaharlal Bhukya, Somasri Yaswanth Reddy Alavala, Vivek Chandra Kodamasimham, Yaswanth Koyaneni
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The electricity demand is still rising due to India's fast economic development and urbanization, while rural households in more than 10,000 villages continue to get inconsistent or non-existent supplies. Despite its extensive use, solar energy remains constrained by the availability of land, seasonal fluctuations, and high capital requirements. However, conventional wind turbines are not suitable for rural areas with weak grids, as they require high cut-in speeds and substantial structural support. Decentralized, small-scale, affordable, and compact renewable solutions are therefore desperately needed. The proposed design is a rooftop wind energy system that is flexible and built to function well in low-wind circumstances. The system utilizes a permanent magnet generator for efficient power conversion, a planetary gearbox for torque multiplication, and three micro-turbines equipped with 3D-printed, aerodynamically designed blades that enhance the tip-speed ratio. Tests of the prototype demonstrate dependable power acquisition at speeds ranging from 3 to 5 m/s and startup at speeds of 2.5 to 3 m/s. In addition to scaling output and distributing loads for safe rooftop use, the two-turbine configuration allows for smooth rooftop solar hybridization, ensuring ongoing access to renewable energy.

title: Sustainable Delta-Attention Based Transformer for Efficient Phishing URL Detection
authors: Liang Zhou, Akshat Gaurav, Varsha Arya, Meena Malik, Ching-Hsien Hsu, Kowk Tai Chui, Brij B. Gupta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: In recent years, phishing detection has become an essential aspect of cybersecurity, necessitating models that are accurate, sustainable, and efficient for real-time decision-making. Conventional deep learning models such as Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), Gated recurrent unit (GRU), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Transformers are known for their high computational costs and a vast number of trainable parameters. This characteristic renders them impractical for deployment in lightweight or edge computing environments where resource constraints are a significant concern. To address these challenges, our paper proposed a delta-attention-based Transformer model. This model leverages a top-k sparse attention mechanism, optimized by periodic dense sampling, effectively approximating full attention without the heavy computational burden. The proposed attention mechanism streamlines the complexity typically associated with quadratic attention models while retaining the capacity to capture vital semantic information. As a result, our model not only maintains competitive performance but does so with a considerably reduced number of trainable parameters when compared to prevalent standard models. Evaluations conducted using the UCI Phishing Dataset highlight the model’s efficacy, recording a precision of 94% and an AUC of 0.9873. The specialized design of our model supports sustainable deployment, offering an optimal blend of model complexity, accuracy, and interpretability in the realm of phishing URL detection.

title: Correlating Teacher Facilitation Strategies with Student Engagement in AI Chatbot-Supported Asynchronous Learning Environments
authors: Ronel Francisco Ramos, Angelo Condol Arguson, Ace Carpio Lagman, Roland A. Calderon
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: This paper investigates the effects of facilitation approaches of teachers on the engagement of the students in the asynchronous learning environment mediated by the AI chatbots. Though the chatbots provide the benefits of immediate feedback, personality-based feedback, and constant interaction, the outcome of the educational technology is largely dependent on the facilitation approach of the teachers. With the mixed-methods correlational approach, the study collected the data related to the usage logs of the chatbots, sentiment, scores of the quiz, and facilitation inputs with 100 undergraduate IT majors and 20 teachers. The results show that the engagement of the chatbot (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r =.74, \beta=.45)$</tex>, emotional sentiment <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{r}=.66, \beta=.33)$</tex>, and facilitation inputs of the teachers (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=.61, \beta=.29$</tex>) are all reliable predictors of academic performance, together explaining the collective variance of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 5 \%}$</tex> for the scores of the quiz. The results of the study provide evidence on the complementary effects of human facilitation to optimize the effects of AI-facilitated learning. Furthermore, this study also promotes Sustainable Development Goal 4 (SDG4) to provide inclusive, effective, and quality learning for the users through the collaboration of human and AI resources.

title: Research on AI-Based Optimization Design of Energy Heat Transfer Systems
authors: Yiwei Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Using artificial intelligence-based analytical methods, this study conducted structured modeling and optimization research on operational data from energy heat transfer systems. By collecting 4,956 multi-state operational samples, classification recognition and energy efficiency regression models were constructed, enabling intelligent identification of system states and precise prediction of the coefficient of performance (COP). A multi-objective evolutionary algorithm was employed for comprehensive optimization of heat exchange power, pressure differential, and energy efficiency, yielding a Pareto front solution set. Results indicate a 96.2% accuracy rate for normal state identification. For COP prediction, the LSTM model achieved RMSE values of 0.091 and 0.138 under normal and low-efficiency states respectively, with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> reaching up to 0.948. A three-objective optimization model combined with the NSGA-II algorithm generated a Pareto optimal solution set satisfying operational constraints. Results demonstrate the system’s strong performance in both accuracy and adaptability.

title: Design of Micro-wind Power Ecological Lighting Device Based on Blade Element-Momentum Theory
authors: Jiaxin Su, Qian Wang, Biao Wang, Huaying Yang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: With the increasingly severe global energy crisis and environmental pollution, the development and utilization of clean and renewable energy technologies have become a research focus. In this context, this paper presents the design of an ecological lighting landscape device based on micro-wind power generation. By changing the chord length distribution and twist angle of the blades, the energy capture efficiency in low wind speed environments has been improved. The experimental results show that the improved micro-wind power generation system can provide a more stable power supply for LED landscape lights while maintaining harmony with the urban environment. The device aims to harness wind energy in low-speed wind environments, converting it into electrical energy that is stored and used for LED lighting. This solution not only provides a green and sustainable energy approach but also adds distinctive aesthetic value to urban landscapes. The study holds practical significance for promoting the adoption of green energy and advancing the development of low-carbon cities.

title: A Computational Framework for Long-Tailed Traffic Sign Detection: YOLOv8n Enhanced by Color-Constrained Data Augmentation
authors: Jiayi Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper proposes a color-constrained data augmentation strategy for long-tail detection and its application on the YOLOv8n model. It focuses on exploring the mechanism and experimental results of this strategy in addressing the scarcity of long-tail class samples and the vulnerability of their features. First, a closed-loop process is established: "long-tail class identification and filtering → color prior modeling → constrained sample generation." Samples are transformed from RGB to HSV space to separate core features from environmental noise. A CLASS_COLOR_MAP is constructed to define feature constraint standards, enabling targeted processing of target classes. Second, three ablation experiments (baseline, generic augmentation, color-constrained augmentation) were designed for YOLOv8n using specific datasets. Performance was evaluated using core metrics including Precision, Recall, mAP50, and mAP50-95, with consistent experimental parameters to ensure fairness. Finally, experiments demonstrate that the color-constrained group achieves optimal metrics with significantly improved detection performance for long-tail classes. Visualization results validate its effectiveness in eliminating interference and enhancing detection confidence. This strategy optimizes YOLOv8n's performance in long-tail detection scenarios, offering advantages such as preserving feature consistency, allocating computational resources precisely, and exhibiting strong domain adaptability. It avoids feature degradation and overfitting risks associated with generic enhancement.

title: An Anonymous and Secure IoT Session Sharing for Industrial Automation Using Ring Signature Scheme
authors: Firoz Khan, Amala Jayanthi Maria Soosai, Lakshmana Kumar Ramasamy, Emad Abd Al Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) systems play a pivotal role in enabling unified shared communication and automation among interconnected devices in the era of Industry 4.0. However, ensuring trust and secure and anonymous session sharing within these networks remains a critical challenge due to the sensitive nature of industrial data and the dynamic characteristics of IoT environments. This paper proposes an innovative framework for Anonymous and Secure IoT Session Sharing in industrial automation systems by using the secure ring signature scheme. The proposed technique ensures information confidentiality, authenticity, and user anonymity while enabling secure collaboration between IoT devices involved in industrial automation. The ring signature-based approach allows any member within a defined group to sign a message on behalf of the group, ensuring anonymity for the signer. This capability is particularly suited for session sharing in IIoT scenarios, where user and device identities must remain concealed to protect privacy and prevent targeted attacks. The framework is evaluated for its computational efficiency, scalability, and resistance to security threats such as eavesdropping, impersonation, and session hijacking. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves robust security with minimal overhead, making it suitable for real-time industrial applications. This work contributes a secure, efficient, and anonymous solution to enable trust and collaboration in IIoT-based industrial automation systems.

title: Reinforcement Learning in Personalized Vocabulary Training for Esl Students
authors: Barnokhon Shamsiyeva, Muslima Temirova, Qobiljon Nasritdinov, Maftuna Ne'matova, Dilafruz Saidvalieva, Alisher Ravshanov
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Individual vocabulary training is very important as it helps improve English as a Second Language (ESL) by modifying the acquisition of words individually to suit the needs of the learner. Reinforcement Learning (RL) is an efficient model to maximize this personalization through the continuous adaptation of learning probes according to the performance of the learner. Nevertheless, the current methods of vocabulary training typically use frozen lists and either repetitive drilling methods, which do not consider the diversity of learners, their engagement and long term memory. In order to address the limitations, the developed approach uses an advanced RL algorithm, Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to dynamically balance exploration and exploitation when choosing vocabulary tasks. In this framework, it is possible to present the words in an adaptive way, practice by spacing, and practice based on the context, which guarantees the effective and learner-specific acquisition of vocabulary. The given strategy has the benefit of enhancing retention as well as encouraging motivation and using contextual words. The results of the experiment show that mastering vocabulary is better, the level of conscious participation of learners is better, and the results of the training process are better than when using traditional training methods.

title: Generative AI and the Evolving Multimedia Professional: Insights from Graduating Students on Identity, Value, and Adaptation
authors: Liam Theresa A. Angala, Edward Jay M. Quinto
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: The study examines how graduating multimedia students view their evolving professional identities in response to the emergence of generative AI. Through thematic analysis of in-depth interviews, key themes emerged: AI as a creative aid rather than replacement, fears of job displacement, the value of human emotion and authenticity, ethical concerns, and calls for educational reform. Findings reveal that students are actively redefining their roles, emphasizing adaptability, ethics, and uniquely human creativity. These insights contribute to understanding how emerging professionals navigate and negotiate their value within an AI-integrated creative landscape.

title: Design of a Fire Warning Device for New Energy Vehicles
authors: Yuning Han, Xingna Zhao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The rapid global adoption of new energy vehicles has heightened safety concerns, particularly regarding fire risks. Traditional fire alarm systems often exhibit limitations in response speed, detection accuracy, and intelligent capabilities, making them inadequate for real-time monitoring in the complex, enclosed, and high-energy-density environments of new energy vehicles. To address these challenges, this paper proposes the design of an intelligent fire warning device capable of monitors in-vehicle environmental parameters such as temperature and smoke concentration in real time. The system also features remote communication via a WiFi module and a door sensor for intrusion detection, enabling data transmission to mobile devices for remote user monitoring. Experimental results demonstrate that the device enables stable and reliable data upload with low false alarm rates, exhibiting excellent real-time performance, reliability, and practicality. This design offers an efficient and intelligent active safety solution for new energy vehicles and provides a technical reference for fire warning systems in other enclosed spaces.

title: Improved Lyapunov Stability Criteria of Delay Systems and Application to Uncertain Complex Networks: New Uncertainty Estimation Method
authors: Fanchao Kong, Yongduan Song, Chaoxu Mu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: This paper addresses theoretical limitations in Lyapunov-Krasovskii methods for time-varying delay systems through three principal contributions. First, a novel delay-dependent inequality for the derivatives of the Lyapunov function is proposed, which fundamentally distinguishes our approach from classical Halanay-type inequalities through explicit time-varying parameterization. An enhanced prescribed-time stability theorem for Filippov systems with time-varying delays and uncertainties is established by employing variable substitution and constant variation techniques. This effectively avoids the misuse of the comparison principle. Second, two innovative event-triggered intermittent control (ET-IC) strategies are introduced. These strategies incorporate hybrid intermittent logic to ensure that triggers occur exclusively during control intervals while maintaining system stability during rest intervals. Third, considering the non-eliminability of uncertainties in time-varying delay systems, adaptive mechanism is adopted based on a novel uncertainty estimation method. This method eliminates singularity issues when the estimation errors of uncertainties approach zero. These contributions lead to significant theoretical advances, including the rigorous exclusion of Zeno behavior through guarantees of minimum inter-event times and the prescribed-time stabilization of uncertain complex networks with time-varying delays within a unified framework that addresses both uncertainties and switching topologies. Finally, numerical simulations of a network consisting of two Lorenz systems are given to illustrate the correctness of the main results.

title: Beyond the Voice: Inertial Sensing of Mouth Motion for High-Security Speech Verification
authors: Ynes Ineza, Muhammad A. Ullah, Abdul Serwadda, Aurore Munyaneza
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biometrics, Behavior, and Identity Science
abstract: Voice interfaces are increasingly used in high-stakes domains such as mobile banking, smart-home security, and hands-free healthcare. Meanwhile, modern generative models have made high-quality voice forgeries inexpensive and easy to create, eroding confidence in voice authentication alone. To strengthen protection against such attacks, we present a second authentication factor that combines acoustic evidence with the unique motion patterns of a speaker’s lower face. By placing lightweight inertial sensors around the mouth to capture mouth opening and evolving lower-facial geometry, our system records a distinct motion signature with strong discriminative power across individuals. We built a prototype and recruited 43 participants to evaluate the system under four conditions: seated, walking on level ground, walking on stairs, and speaking with different language backgrounds (native vs. non-native English). Across all scenarios, our approach consistently achieved a median equal-error rate (EER) of 0.01 or lower, indicating that mouth-movement data remain robust under variations in gait, posture, and spoken language. We discuss specific use cases where this second line of defense could provide tangible security benefits to voice authentication systems.

title: Invited: Navigating the Frontier of Optimality and Complexity: Advanced Design Automation for Wavelength-Routed ONoCs
authors: Zhidan Zheng, Mengchu Li, Tsun-Ming Tseng, Ulf Schlichtmann

title: DualBAN: Unifying Intra- and Inter-molecular Features for Compound-Protein Interaction Prediction
authors: Shida He, Zhongyu He, Yuting Zhang, Weidong Ye
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Accurate identification of compound-protein interactions (CPIs) is critical for drug discovery. In recent years, neural network-based CPI prediction methods have demonstrated remarkable performance. However, most existing approaches primarily focus on interaction patterns derived from known data, without fully leveraging both intra- and inter-molecular interaction information within compound-protein pairs. This limitation constrains the representation learning capability of models for compounds and proteins, hindering further improvements in predictive accuracy. In this paper, we propose DualBAN, a novel CPI prediction model that integrates intra- and inter-molecular interaction information from both compounds and proteins. Specifically, DualBAN employs pretrained biological large language models to obtain sequence features and extracts atomic features of compounds and residue representations of proteins. To comprehensively capture intra- and inter-molecular interactions, DualBAN fuses atomic and residue representations using a bilinear attention network and combines sequence representations through cross-attention, jointly utilizing both components for CPI prediction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed DualBAN significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods on CPI prediction tasks and maintains robust performance under cross-domain and cold-start settings.

title: Low-Complexity Algebraic Soft Decoding of Hermitian Codes with Re-Encoding Transform
authors: Jiwei Liang, Li Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: Algebraic codes are important in communication and storage systems where soft information is limited and decoding efficiency is critical. Among them, algebraic-geometric (AG) codes are promising candidates to replace the widely used Reed-Solomon (RS) codes. However, their more sophisticated algebraic structure results in high decoding complexity, hindering their practical application. This paper proposes a novel interpolation-based algebraic soft decoding (ASD) for one of the most important AG codes - Hermitian codes. Given a designed maximum decoding output list size (OLS), the interpolation module is constructed, and the decoding is formulated as finding a desired Gröbner basis of this module that contains the interpolation polynomial. The re-encoding transform (ReT) specifically designed for ASD is proposed, enabling the modified basis reduction (BR) interpolation to achieve lower complexity. The ReT is formulated by defining Lagrange interpolation polynomials over the Hermitian function fields and appropriately choosing the re-encoding points. By exploiting the linear code property, the ReT transforms a subset of interpolation points to have zero z-coordinates, which allows a common factor to be extracted from the module basis polynomials, thereby simplifying the interpolation. This paper further proposes an improved ReT employing more re-encoding points. It enables the common factor to have a greater degree, yielding a more significant complexity reduction. This also leads to an early termination for the decoding. Theoretical analysis and numerical results demonstrate that the two proposed ReT schemes efficiently facilitate the ASD for Hermitian codes.

title: From Specialist to Large Models: A Paradigm Evolution Towards Semantic- Aware MIMO
authors: Keke Ying, Zhen Gao, Tingting Yang, Jianhua Zhang, Xiang Cheng, Tony Q.S. Quek, H. Vincent Poor
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: The sixth generation (6G) network is expected to deploy larger multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) arrays to support massive connectivity, which will increase overhead and latency at the physical layer. Meanwhile, emerging 6G demands such as immersive communications and environmental sensing pose challenges to traditional signal processing. To address these issues, we propose the "semantic-aware MIMO" paradigm, which leverages specialist models and large models to perceive, utilize, and fuse the inherent semantics of channels and sources for improved performance. Moreover, for representative MIMO physical-layer tasks, e.g., random access activity detection, channel feedback, and precoding, we design specialist models that exploit channel and source semantics for better performance. Additionally, in view of the more diversified functions of 6G MIMO, we further explore large models as a scalable solution for multi-task semantic-aware MIMO and review recent advances along with their advantages and limitations. Finally, we discuss the challenges, insights, and prospects of the evolution of specialist models and large models empowered semantic-aware MIMO paradigms.

title: Power Budgeting of Homogeneous 3-D Processors in Dark Silicon
authors: Hai Wang, Yanhong Luo, Jincheng Guo, Yu Chen, Jiming Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: The performance of 3-D processors is constrained by the power budget, defined as the maximum power consumption to guarantee the system’s thermal safety. In this work, we present a dynamic power budgeting method for homogeneous 3-D processors in dark silicon. The new method provides a power budget that leads to higher system potential performance over the existing methods with two new techniques. First, a sub-optimal active core distribution in 3-D space is computed on the fly which increases the total power budget. Second, we introduce an intelligent power assignment strategy across die layers with optimal power ratios computed offline, which further increases the potential system performance. Experiments on two homogeneous 3-D processors with different architectures show that the new method leads to a higher system performance, compared with the existing techniques.

title: SeeGait: Synergistic Co-evolving Representations for Multimodal Gait Recognition via Hierarchical Multi-Stage Fusion
authors: Hanyue Du, Xianye Ben, Xiankai Lu, Zunxiao Xu, Qingshuo Gao, Qiang Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Gait recognition offers non-contact, long-distance identification but struggles with robustness against covariates like clothing variations, carrying conditions, and viewpoint changes. Existing methods predominantly rely on single modalities (e.g., silhouettes or skeletons) or employ shallow multimodal fusion, such as simple concatenation, which treats modalities as independent and static, failing to exploit their complementary strengths, shape cues from silhouettes and structural kinematics from skele-tons. To address these limitations, we introduce the Synergistic co-evolving representations (See) principle, enabling modalities to iteratively interact, guide, and refine each other across semantic hierarchies, fostering a unified, robust identity representation resilient to complex environments. This is realized through SeeGait, a novel multimodal framework featuring hierarchical multi-stage fusion. At its core, the Bidirectional Hierarchical Cross-Attention Synergy Module (BiHCASM) employs adaptive cross-modal attention to dynamically align and reweight features bidirectionally, allowing structural insights to enhance appearance focus and vice versa. Complementing this, the Hierarchical Spatiotemporal Transformer Encoder (HSTE) captures long-range skeleton dynamics, overcoming GCN limitations, while the Hierarchical Convolutional Silhouette Encoder (HCSE) extracts multi-scale silhouette pyramids for rich shape priors. Finally, a Holistic Feature Aggregation (HFA) strategy consolidates features from all stages for deep supervision, ensuring comprehensive optimization. By promoting mutual refinement, SeeGait mitigates covariate disruptions through enhanced complementarity, yielding superior discriminability. Extensive experiments show state-of-the-art performance, with 97.1% average Rank-1 accuracy on CASIA-B, and top results on CCPG and SUSTech1K.

title: Pancreatic Tumor Detection in CT Images Using a Hybrid Deep Learning Framework
authors: J. Bennilo Fernandes, T. Pradeep Kumar, J Esther Jerlin, C. Ganesh, N. Sree Neha, S Sharmishta Jansi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Pancreatic cancer remains a major health threat due to its aggressive nature and because early detection methods are not yet practical. The contribution here provides a novel framework by integrating deep learning and image processing techniques for the appropriate detection of pancreatic tumors. The proposed framework includes CLAHE enhancement of CT images to allow for better contrast and details within the subtle regions of the tumor. Subsequently, tumor segmentation with a U-Net architecture allows for very accurate delineation of malignant tissue. A CNN classification network is then used, with a Stochastic Gradient Descent with Momentum optimizer, to further optimize learning and model convergence. An end-to-end implementation was done in MATLAB using the above steps and was tested on a dataset containing 1,000 pancreatic CT scans. The integration of CLAHE-based enhancement, U-Net segmentation, and SGDM-optimized CNN classification presents a fast, automatic, and robust technique that has the potential to prove a useful CAD tool for early detection of pancreatic tumors, and hence for improving the clinical outcome.
