title: Adaptive Prediction-Based State Estimation in Edge-Enabled Active Distribution Networks
authors: Muneeb Mushtaq Sheikh, Himani Mattoo, Sreenath J G, Praveen Tripathy
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: State estimation (SE) in active distribution networks (ADNs) faces significant challenges due to limited measurement availability, continuously changing system states, and high communication overhead. To address these issues, this paper proposes an edge computing-enabled multi-area forecasting aided state estimation (MAFASE) framework that decentralizes computation and improves real-time SE performance. The network is partitioned into clusters, each managed by an edge estimation point (EEP) that performs local SE and exchanges limited information with neighboring clusters. To ensure adequate observability with a minimal number of measurement devices, micro-phasor measurement units (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex> PMUs) are strategically placed using the depth-of-one unobservability criterion. Each cluster in MAFASE uses a novel regularized prediction model with adaptive measurement-driven weighting. This design prevents overfitting and prioritizes validated data for robust SE. The proposed approach is validated through simulations on the IEEE 123-bus system using real load and solar generation profiles, demonstrating superior performance in tracking states under varying load and distributed generation conditions.

title: Design of a Multi-Core Hybrid CMOS-Memristor Chip for Analog Programming
authors: Luis Camuñas-Mesa, Roberto Méndez-Romero, Teresa Serrano-Gotarredona, Bernabé Linares-Barranco
publish: 2026 Argentine Conference on Electronics (CAE)
abstract: The emergence of memristive devices and their integration with CMOS technology promises a revolutionary leap in ultra-efficient in-memory analog computing, particularly for neuromorphic and AI applications. However, this potential is currently limited by the challenge of precisely programming intermediate, analog resistive values. Conventional iterative Program-and-Verify algorithms are time-consuming and accelerate device degradation due to numerous write/erase cycles. To overcome this, we propose a novel analog programming circuit that uses a ramp voltage generator and a current comparator to set the desired resistive value in a single iteration, minimizing programming time and device degradation. In this work, we have implemented a single-iteration programming circuit within a multi-core hybrid CMOS-memristor architecture with 9k memristors on a chip fabricated using GlobalFoundries 22 nm FDSOI technology, which will be used to characterize the proposed approach, demonstrating a viable path toward high-precision and robust large-scale analog computing systems.

title: Enhancing Role-Playing Language Models via Similar-Scene Generation
authors: Hong Yan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of large language models, role-playing language agents (RPLAs) have attracted increasing attention for their ability to simulate diverse personas and enable personalized human-AI interaction. However, existing approaches to building RPLAs often require substantial human effort for manual data construction or annotation, and are constrained by the limited scale of available character-specific dialogue data, which hinders their scalability and practical deployment. To address these challenges, we propose a novel framework for constructing customized role-playing language agents based on LLMs. Our approach integrates two key components: (1) diverse instruction generation, achieved through an iterative self-expansion process combining seed QA examples with similarity-based filtering to ensure both reliability and diversity; (2) similar-scene-enhanced response generation, which retrieves semantically related QA samples as contextual guidance to produce consistent and persona-aligned responses. Comprehensive experiments on representative characters from the RoleBench dataset demonstrate that our method significantly improves performance across multiple evaluation dimensions, including personality consistency, background integration, language style, and overall response quality. Further ablation studies confirm that both the similar-scene enhancement and dual filtering mechanisms are crucial for achieving robust and natural role-playing behavior. Our work provides a practical and extensible paradigm for developing high-fidelity, multi-persona conversational agents, advancing the frontier of personalized dialogue systems and human-like language generation.

title: TinyGate: A Confidence-Aware Lightweight Framework for Real-Time EEG Dementia Screening
authors: Hyeonseo Jo, Injae Lee, Jin-Hwi Park, Young Chul Youn, Joonki Paik
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Efficient and reliable EEG analysis is essential for real-time and home-based dementia screening. However, existing deep learning models remain too computationally heavy for low-power consumer devices and often perform redundant inference on easily classifiable signals. To address these limitations, we propose TinyGate, a lightweight and confidence-aware EEG framework designed for adaptive abnormality screening using only a few EEG channels. TinyGate dynamically determines whether an EEG segment can be confidently classified as normal or abnormal and provides direct feedback on whether further medical consultation is needed. For ambiguous cases, the system seamlessly transfers the data to a more advanced diagnostic backend for refined analysis. This selective inference approach significantly reduces redundant computation while maintaining diagnostic reliability. In general, TinyGate demonstrates a promising step toward real-time, scalable, and energy-efficient EEG screening for next-generation consumer healthcare devices.

title: Design and Energy Management of Solar PV-BESS Integrated Off-Grid Electric Vehicle Charging Station
authors: Mahad Mohamud Awad, Ankur Bhattacharjee, Kazi N. Hasan
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Rapid adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) in transportation and energy systems requires an innovative and sustainable charging infrastructure. Off-grid electric vehicle charging stations (EVCS) with integrated solar photovoltaic (PV) systems and battery energy storage (BESS) Mitigates the grid burden, decreases operational expenses and minimizes carbon emissions. This paper presents the design and simulation of an off-grid EVCS configured as a standalone DC microgrid. The system integrates a 16.78 kW PV array, a 50 kWh lithium-ion BESS, and EV chargers interconnected through a 600 V DC bus. Maximum power extraction is achieved using a Perturb-and-Observe (P&O) maximum power point tracking algorithm with a boost converter, while a bidirectional buck—boost converter regulates BESS charging and discharging within a 25-95% state-of-charge (SoC) window. A buck converter ensures constant-current/constantvoltage (CC/CV) charging of EVs. A coordinated control strategy combining proportional—integral (PI) regulation and rule-based energy management maintains DC bus stability, optimizes PV utilization, and safeguards BESS health under fluctuating solar irradiance and dynamic EV load demand. Simulations are carried out in MATLAB/Simulink under representative sunny, cloudy, and nighttime conditions. Results show stable DC bus operation within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 5 \%$</tex>, uninterrupted EV charging and efficient power flow between PV and BESS, demonstrating the feasibility of the proposed off-grid PV-BESS charging system as a reliable alternative to diesel-based solutions for remote areas.

title: Photovoltaic Power Prediction Using XGBoost with Feature Selection and Sparrow Search Algorithm Optimization
authors: Jiaxin Xiao, Jing Hu, Xin Gu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: With the rapid development of renewable energy, photovoltaic power generation, as an important green energy source, has become increasingly important for grid scheduling and energy management. This work presents a photovoltaic power forecasting paradigm driven by XGBoost model, feature selection, and the Sparrow Search Algorithm (SSA). During data preprocessing, feature selection is used to remove redundant features, improving model training efficiency and prediction accuracy. The Sparrow Search Algorithm optimizes the optimization of XGBoost’s hyperparameters to boost model fitting. Results from experimental studies show that the developed model performs markedly better than baseline methods such as linear regression, support vector machines (SVM), and long short-term memory networks (LSTM), demonstrating significantly higher prediction accuracy. The proposed approach substantially enhances the accuracy of photovoltaic power forecasting and provides strong support for the optimal scheduling of smart grids and renewable energy.

title: PACU: Real-Time SELinux Policy Audit and Control Utility for Embedded Android Systems
authors: Gabriel M. Villacrez, Cristiano C. Goes, David Alan De O. Ferreira, Janislley O. De Sousa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: SEAndroid (Security-Enhanced Android) extends Android’s security architecture by enforcing Mandatory Access Control (MAC) through SELinux policies. SELinux restricts access to system resources and data for processes and applications, thereby mitigating potential vulnerabilities and minimizing the impact of malicious software. While effective, current policy refinement workflows remain largely manual, time-consuming, and costly within the Android development process. In this work, we present PACU, a lightweight, real-time, and native auditing tool for analyzing SELinux policies directly on physical Android hardware devices. PACU supports customizable Access Violation Control (AVC) denial log analysis, integrates both kernel and platform log sources, and is compatible with Android Open Source Project (AOSP)-based builds. Experimental results show that PACU achieves low resource usage and fast processing times, making it ideal for debugging and policy enforcement in resource-constrained environments. This approach enhances policy visibility, simplifies rule tuning, and strengthens SEAn-droid deployment in embedded Android systems for consumer electronic devices.

title: A Novel Energy Storage Sources Capacity Planning Method under Extreme Weather Events with Constrained Renewable Energy Sources Outputs
authors: Jin Yu, Guokang Yu, Chaoshan Xin, Honghao Guan, Peng Yao, Qi Zhou
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: To balance power supply and demand under extreme weather events with constrained renewable energy sources (RESs) outputs, this paper proposes a novel energy storage sources (ESSs) capacity planning method based on the timescale of typical day timescale and extreme weather events. The extreme weather event is defined according to duration of RES low output. Based on the number and duration of extreme weather events for a typical year, a novel model of ESSs capacity planning is established to minimize costs with integrated load demand. The particle swarm optimization (PSO) is adopted to find a global solution for ESSs capacity planning. Numerical simulations are conducted on an actual 34-bus power system to verify the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: Enhancing EV Load Forecasting in Data-Scarce Environments Through Cluster-Informed Transfer Learning
authors: Srinand S, Rahul Satheesh, Mohan Lal Kolhe
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Electric Vehicles (EVs) play a very important role in the modern-day electrical ecosystem. Throughout the world, there is a surge in the number of EVs in the market owing to an increase in public demand and as a general policy by governments and manufacturers in order to foster sustainable, clean energy sources for transportation, which has minimal impact on the environment. While this takes place, on the other hand increase in demand of EVs over time has resulted in considerable stress in existing power systems and grids, which need to be analysed. This paper aims to perform electrical vehicle load forecasting using data from multiple charging stations which are split into a data-rich source domain and a data-scarce target domain. Transformer-based models are trained on the entire source domain and its respective clusters, obtained through Kmeans clustering based on usage patterns. A base model (BM1) is first trained on the entire source domain and another fine tuned model (FTM) is trained on clustered data. These models are then applied to the data-scarce target domain to analyse the impact of transfer learning in such scenarios. Based on the result obtained, the transfer learning model which was fine tuned using cluster based FTM in the source domain, i.e Transfer learning model 2 (TLM2) was able to outperform models trained from scratch on the target domain,i.e Base model 2 (BM2) and model trained on the entire source domain Base model (BM1),i.e Transfer learning model 1 (TLM1) by almost 3 percent in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> metrics and the results show an improvement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 0 1}$</tex> RMSE compared to 3.4 using traditional Bi-LSTM based models. This shows how the integration of attention-based architectures with cluster-based knowledge provides an effective mechanism for load forecasting.

title: Off-line LLM-based decision tool to help advise Farmers in sub-Saharan Africa for Enhanced Decision Making in Goat Care
authors: Jocelin Calas, Gerald Barry, Abraham G. Campbell
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The objective of this work is to assist goat farmers based in Sub-Saharan Africa in determining whether veterinary consultation is necessary when their animals are unwell, thereby helping to improve the farmers ability to care for their animals and also avoid unnecessary costs. The study presents a prototype offline mobile application that guides users through a process using a decision tree and user-provided responses. The work has investigated the potential of large language models (LLMs) for generating decision trees and diagnostic tools in data-scarce domains such as veterinary medicine. Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) was employed to enhance result quality and accuracy. Decision trees were generated with the Gemma 32B-parameter model; due to complexity and size limitations, large trees were constructed incrementally with carefully designed prompts. Diagnostic generation used the DeepSeek 14B-parameter model, which consistently produced the most precise and reliable outputs. Key limitations included hardware performance—directly affecting computation time as a function of model size and machine specifications—and the models’ tendency toward overly cautious answers. To partially mitigate these constraints, techniques such as trigger words, verification prompts, and scoring methods were applied under specific conditions. Nevertheless, expert validation remains necessary, as these verification methods cannot be considered 100% reliable.

title: Research on Optimization and Control Mechanism of Intelligent Microgrid for Vehicle-Grid Interaction
authors: Jiajun Feng, Yuanzhi Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of new energy vehicles (NEVs) and the increasing penetration of distributed renewable energy, the integrated "source–grid–load–storage" smart microgrid system has emerged as a critical architecture for future power systems. This paper focuses on the bi-directional energy interaction and multi-level responsiveness of NEVs within microgrids, proposing a comprehensive system structure model that incorporates photovoltaic generation, flexible grid interaction, dynamic load control, and coordinated energy storage mechanisms. A multi-timescale coordinated scheduling strategy is developed to facilitate effective renewable energy consumption and accurate load-side responses. Additionally, a power-prediction-based algorithmic control platform is designed, integrating key functional modules such as load regulation verification, intelligent charging response, and IoT- based system integration. From a digitalization perspective, a digital twin–driven framework combining information sensing and intelligent decision-making is introduced to support energy management and strategy optimization under dynamic operational conditions. Results demonstrate that NEVs, acting as both controllable loads and mobile energy storage units, significantly enhance the regulatory capability, operational resilience, and scenario adaptability of microgrid systems, providing a solid technological foundation for promoting green and low-carbon transformation.

title: Improved Random Forest and Multi-source Feature Fusion Method for Partial Discharge Warning of Sensors in Distribution Network
authors: Chuang Sun, Xu Wang, Zijun Tian, Liquan Fu, Junhong Lin
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: The false positive rate and false negative rate of partial discharge warning of distribution network sensors are high, and the detection delay is too long. An improved random forest and multi-source feature fusion method is proposed. Firstly, a multi-source feature pool containing time domain, frequency domain, and environmental features is constructed to screen high-correlation features; secondly, a dynamic weight allocation mechanism is designed to optimize the combination of subjective weights of analytic hierarchy process and objective weights of random forest feature importance based on game theory to minimize the sum of comprehensive weight deviations; finally, the random forest algorithm is improved, and adaptive tree depth adjustment and weighted voting mechanism are introduced. Experiments show that the average false positive rate of this method in the measured data is 9.43%; the false negative rate is reduced to $12.0 \%-18.0 \%$; the average response delay is 64.2 ms, which provides reliable technical support for active defense of distribution network.

title: Ultra-Short-Term Wind Power Forecasting via a Dish-CONET-TimesNet Hybrid Model
authors: Haowen Zheng
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Accurate ultra-short-term wind power forecasting plays a critical role in maintaining the secure and economic operation of modern power systems. However, wind power time series usually exhibit strong non-stationarity, distribution shift, and multi-periodic characteristics, which significantly degrade forecasting accuracy. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a hybrid ultra-short-term wind power forecasting model based on Dish-CONET and TimesNet. First, Dish-CONET is employed to decompose wind power sequences and normalize distribution-shifted components. Then, the processed sequences are fed into TimesNet to capture multi-periodic temporal dependencies through two-dimensional variation modeling. Finally, the forecasting results are reconstructed via inverse normalization. Case studies based on real wind farm data demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms several benchmark methods in terms of prediction accuracy and robustness.

title: Research on Short-Circuit Current Limiting Measures Considering Changing the Local Structure of Large-Scale Power Grids
authors: Xin Wang, Gengsheng Nie, Chun Zheng, Mingming Bo, Xiaojun Tang, Qidi Huo
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This study tackles the critical issue of excessive short-circuit current in large-scale power grids and carries out in-depth exploration of current-limiting strategies that leverage power grid structural adjustments. First, it explores the calculation mechanism of short-circuit current, highlighting that the total short-circuit current is formed by the combination of periodic and aperiodic components. In engineering practice, priority should be given to calculating the subtransient current corresponding to four typical short-circuit fault types. Based on the positive-sequence equivalent circuit of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$500-220 \text{kV}$</tex> substations, this research identifies that enhancing key parameters-serves as the core logic for short-circuit current control. On this foundation, two specific measures-busbar section operation and electromagnetic loop network splitting-are thoroughly examined. The former increases system impedance by disconnecting the bus tie circuit breaker; in a real-world application example, it reduces the short-circuit current of the target substation by approximately 1.8 kA and exerts a mild current-limiting impact on adjacent substations. For the latter, an innovative hierarchical and zonal precise loop-splitting technique is proposed: the power grid is divided into a 500 kV backbone layer and a 220 kV distribution layer based on voltage levels, and three types of regions (including core load areas) are categorized according to load attributes to develop differentiated strategies. Research results demonstrate that busbar section operation is easy to implement and effective, while hierarchical and zonal precise loop-splitting can achieve large-scale current limitation while ensuring power grid stability and new energy absorption.

title: Research on Distributed PV Disturbance Monitoring Technology Based on Dual-Peak Spectral Line Interpolation Algorithm
authors: Wang Yang, Jing Ping, Zhao Lei, Wang Yuejiao, Yang Song
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the rapid growth of distributed photovoltaic generation, its multi-point access, dynamic fluctuations, and complex disturbance characteristics have raised higher requirements for the safety of grid operation and power quality. As an important means of disturbance monitoring, spectrum analysis is limited by finite frequency resolution and spectral leakage effects, leading to insufficient accuracy in disturbance frequency estimation. To address this issue, this paper proposes a high-precision frequency identification method for distributed photovoltaic disturbances based on a bimodal spectral line interpolation algorithm, achieving rapid and accurate localization and quantitative analysis of transient disturbance events. Through simulations and empirical data verification, this method maintains high frequency estimation accuracy even under low signal-to-noise ratio and short data window conditions, exhibiting better real-time performance and robustness compared to traditional methods. This study provides effective technical support for online power quality assessment and dynamic adaptive control of distributed photovoltaics.

title: Enhanced Fault Tolerance and Self-Healing DC-DC Converter for Uninterrupted Conversion in PV System
authors: M. Mary Linda, Deepak Joshi S, Gunarajan N, Arjun S
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: A DC-DC converter plays a prominent role in solar energy systems by enabling efficient DC voltage regulation at a relatively low cost. Despite their effectiveness, conventional converters often don't have sufficient fault tolerance, which may necessitate manual switching or intervention during faults. Enhancing the converter's ability to automatically detect and respond to faults remains a key area for improvement in order to increase system reliability and reduce reliance on human actions. Therefore, this paper proposed a design that incorporates a PWM modulation approach combined with a self-healing, isolation strategy, and reduces output ripple voltage. This results in a converter to improve System performance stability and reduce maintenance needs. The proposed topology and strategy are verified and checked using MATLAB/Simulink.

title: A Hybrid Model for Short-Term Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Based on CNN-BiLSTM and Scenario-Adaptive LSTM
authors: Junjie Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper proposes a prediction model based on time series features, focusing on multi-model architecture design and feature fusion methods. First, a theoretical model is constructed to analyze short- and long-term periodic features and to clarify the deviation mechanism between theoretical and actual values. Next, a CNN-BiLSTM model based on sliding windows and time information encoding is designed. A 1D convolutional layer is used to extract local features, and a bidirectional long short-term memory network captures the temporal dependencies. The subsequent period predictions are achieved through global average pooling and a fully connected layer. The training process is optimized using a mean squared error loss function and cosine annealing strategy. Finally, real-time feature data is incorporated, and scenarios are classified using principal component analysis for dimensionality reduction and clustering algorithms. An improved long short-term memory network model is built, dynamically adjusting feature weights to enhance prediction accuracy under complex conditions. The model effectively learns temporal patterns without relying on external data, and after feature fusion, further reduces prediction errors. It accurately captures both periodicity and volatility, demonstrating strong generalization ability and prediction stability across multiple scenarios.

title: Intelligent Sensing and Data Processing Model in a Slurry Solidification Mechanical Filter Press System
authors: Wei Xiang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the low filtration efficiency and delayed fault warning issues of traditional slurry solidification mechanical filter press systems, this paper designs an intelligent sensing architecture and proposes an adaptive weighted kernel principal component analysis (AW-KPCA) algorithm to construct a system condition monitoring and fault diagnosis model. First, a sensor network covering parameters such as concentration, pressure, and humidity is established, and data is transmitted and preprocessed via Industrial Ethernet. Second, an adaptive weighting mechanism is introduced based on KPCA, quantifying feature importance based on information gain to address the insufficient key feature extraction accuracy of traditional algorithms. Finally, experimental verification is conducted, and 1200 data sets (800 normal and 400 faulty) are collected for simulation under operating conditions of 15%, 20%, and 25% slurry concentrations and 0.8 MPa, 1.0 MPa, and 1.2 MPa filter press pressures. The results show that when retaining the top three principal components, the AW-KPCA achieves a variance contribution rate of 92.3%, exceeding both PCA (78.5%) and KPCA (85.1%). The fault identification accuracy is 96.5%, with a false alarm rate of 2.1% and a false negative rate of 1.8%, significantly outperforming the comparison algorithms. The optimized control model based on this algorithm reduces filter cake moisture by 3.2%, shortens filtration time by 12.5%, and reduces energy consumption by 8.7%, validating the effectiveness and practicality of the research proposal.

title: Supporting Capacity Evaluation of Pumped Storage Unit Based on Evolutionary Combination Weighting-Cloud Model
authors: Ziwei Zhong, Lingkai Zhu, Yuheng Zhang, Wei Zheng, Panfeng Shang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Pumped storage units play a key supporting role in the safe and stable operation of new power systems due to their flexible operational characteristics. Based on the massive operational data of pumped storage units, an operational characteristic characterization model covering peak regulation, frequency regulation, phase regulation, emergency reserve and black start capability is established. An evaluation index system for pumped storage units is constructed, including 4 secondary indicators and 14 tertiary indicators, which covers power and electricity regulation capacity, multi-condition conversion capacity, wide-load operation capacity and long-term phase regulation response capacity. An evaluation method based on evolutionary combination weighting-cloud model is proposed: subjective and objective weights are obtained through analytic hierarchy process and entropy weight method, combined with game theory for combination optimization, and an evolutionary response function is introduced to realize dynamic weight correction so as to adapt to the long-term development and evolution needs of power systems. A pumped storage unit in East China Power Grid is taken as a case for simulation verification. The results show that this method can scientifically quantify the unit's supporting capacity, identify key optimization indicators, and provide a theoretical basis for the optimal selection and operational improvement of pumped storage units.

title: Real –Time RNN-Based Energy Management for HEV using Battery Capacitor Hybrid with Cloud Feedback
authors: Rijo V Thomas, Prathibha S Babu, Ilango Karuppasamy
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Hybrid Electric Vehicles (HEVs) involving batteries and supercapacitors are an energy-efficient alternative to conventional vehicles with internal combustion engines. However, considering the variation in the power demands, optimally allocating power between the battery and the supercapacitor in HEVs is challenging. This paper proposes an AI-based Energy Management System (EMS) based on Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) which can predict the required power and optimally allocate the energy required between battery and supercapacitor. Further, this method is implemented on a dual-system; a MATLAB/Simulink model and a Python-based UI with a cloud-based communication interface. The model is hence validated with real-time monitoring thereby improving the effectiveness of the RNN-based EMS over the existing conventional methods. Proposed RNN model is compared with CNN and LSTM models using Mean Square Error (MAE) as performance matrix. Proposed model works superior to CNN and LSTM with an MAE of 30.59. The framework is also equipped to send the optimally allocated power values back to the Simulink model to facilitate a feedback mechanism in the real-time monitoring. The experiment helps in preventing battery degradation and optimised energy distribution in HEVs.

title: A High Performance Intelligent Spectrum Sensing under Low SNR in Cognitive Radio Networks using Hybrid Framework
authors: Gouti Yogesh Kumar, Himansu Shekhar Pradhan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This work demonstrates high-performance intelligent spectrum sensing framework for cognitive radio networks operating under severely low Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) conditions, specifically at -15 dB. A hybrid deep learning approach is adopted by integrating Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) based feature extraction with a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) classifier to enhance detection reliability. The proposed model is benchmarked against conventional classical methods and optimization-based technique. Simulation results demonstrate that the hybrid DWT-CNN framework achieves a Probability of Detection (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</inf>) of 0.703 and a Probability of False Alarm (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">fa</inf>) of 0.138 at -15 dB SNR, outperforming all other exiting techniques in the literature. These findings highlight the potential of deep learning integrated with signal processing for robust and adaptive spectrum sensing in the interference-prone and dynamically changing wireless environments.

title: Research on Battery Powered Hybrid Propulsion Scheme Design and Performance Optimization for Small Aircrafts’ Mission Requirements
authors: Shengze Bao, Yu Zhang Avic, Le Kang, Junkui Mao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: The importance of small aircrafts in aviation industry is steadily increasing, and hybrid propulsion systems have become a key solution for meeting their energy conservation and emission reduction requirements. Aiming to enhance the flight duration of small aircrafts, this study approaches the problem from hybrid propulsion system architecture design. Four different hybrid propulsion schemes are constructed. On this basis, a performance simulation model of the hybrid propulsion system is developed. For each of the four hybrid schemes, architecture optimization is conducted under a unified flight–propulsion coupling framework to explore their respective performance advantages and application potential in extending aircrafts’ flight duration. The results show that, among the schemes, the distributed electric propulsion architecture demonstrates superior performance after flight path optimization, achieving a flight duration of 48.10 h, a 33.87% improvement over the baseline. In addition, a comparative study on performance optimization was conducted for the power system equipped with fuel cells, which demonstrates significant potential for application in small aircraft. Overall, the findings of this study provide reliable and instructive guidance for selecting propulsion system schemes for current small aircrafts.

title: Fingerprint Biometric Recognition Using Dabc and Tanh-Based Fuzzy Activated Neural Network
authors: Parvathy J, Poorima G Patil
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Fingerprint recognition systems rely on extracting and classifying distinctive ridge and minutiae patterns to identify individuals. However, high-dimensional feature vectors and noisy sensor inputs can degrade accuracy and increase computational complexity. This research proposes a fingerprint biometric recognition system that employs Deep Artificial Bee Colony (DABC) in the feature extraction and optimization unit and a tanh-based fuzzy activated neural network (tFNN) in the classification unit. The DABC algorithm adaptively selects the most discriminative features from raw fingerprint descriptors such as minutiae features, global ridge features, and texture-based coefficients, reducing redundancy and noise to optimize recognition performance. The tFNN classifier then leverages fuzzy membership functions combined with tanh activation to accurately distinguish between genuine and imposter fingerprint patterns based on the optimized feature subset. The proposed model is evaluated on a comprehensive fingerprint dataset comprising impressions from FVC2000, FVC2002, UPEK, MINEX validation, Neurotechnology UareU, and Neurotechnology CrossMatch, containing an overall 2196 samples. Experimental results demonstrate that our DABC-tFNN approach achieves high recognition accuracy with low false match and false non-match rates, outperforming baseline fingerprint-only systems. Specifically, the model's accuracy, precision, and recall exceed those of comparable methods by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8-15 {\%}$</tex>. These outcomes highlight the effectiveness of DABC-driven feature optimization and fuzzy neural classification in fingerprint recognition. The study concludes that integrating DABC and tFNN provides a robust framework for secure, high-performance fingerprint identification, with potential applications in access control, surveillance, and forensic investigations.

title: A New Approach for Object Detection Using YOLO v11 and Trajectory Estimation Using an IMM Filter
authors: Mena Abdel-Maguid, Aldo Morales
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This paper presents a trajectory estimation framework for tracking a maneuvering ego vehicle using the Interacting Multiple Model (IMM) filter. Ground-truth data, including position, velocity, and acceleration, is obtained from the KITTI dataset’s onboard sensor measurements. To simulate realistic observations, Gaussian noise with a standard deviation of 5.0 meters is added to the ground-truth trajectory, and these noisy measurements are used as inputs to the IMM filter. The IMM framework combines Constant Velocity (CV) and Constant Acceleration (CA) motion models to adaptively estimate the ego vehicle’s state over time. Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) is used to evaluate tracking accuracy, and 50 Monte Carlo simulations are conducted to assess robustness under varying noise conditions. The initial design assumes both motion models are equally likely. For the baseline configuration, the process noise standard deviations are set to σ_CV = 0.1 and σ_CA = 0.1. In addition, in this work we implement early sensor fusion by projecting LiDAR point clouds onto camera images and performing object detection using YOLOv11. Although these detections are not yet used within the IMM framework, they establish a foundation for future work involving integrated tracking and fusion across modalities.

title: An Agentic AI Framework for Strengthening Preventive Healthcare
authors: Arul G, Balasastha E, Nithya Jayakumar, Ramya Jayakumar
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: This paper introduces an Agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) framework designed to enhance preventive healthcare through intelligent automation and data-driven decision-making. Traditional healthcare systems often face inefficiencies such as manual scheduling, fragmented records, and inadequate follow-up, leading to reduced patient compliance. The proposed framework employs multiple autonomous agents—namely, Smart Scheduling Agents, Predictive Agents, and Conversational Agents—to optimize appointment coordination, predict missed visits, and deliver personalized reminders. The system is implemented using Android (Kotlin with Jetpack Compose) for the client interface and Spring Boot for backend services, integrated with Gemini Agentic AI for intelligent coordination. By streamlining workflows, enhancing patient engagement, and providing real-time analytics, the framework enables proactive, personalized preventive healthcare while maintaining data privacy and ethical AI governance.

title: Theoretical Estimation of Positive-Sequence Reactive Current Limits During Asymmetrical Grid Faults for Cascaded Delta-Connected STATCOM
authors: Aarthi A, Saranya S R, Debdeep Samajdar, Subhrasankha Ghosh, Shashidhar Mathapati
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: With increased penetration of renewable resources into the grid and stricter grid codes, Static Synchronous Compensators (STATCOMs) are crucial for reactive power compensation and voltage regulation. Among multilevel cascaded topologies, Single Delta-connected Bridge Cells (SDBC)-based STATCOM is widely used in medium voltage grid applications to deliver or absorb reactive power under balanced and unbalanced conditions. During unbalanced operating conditions, maintaining per-phase energy balance of SDBC STATCOMs becomes challenging. Fundamental frequency zero-sequence current injection is commonly used to equalize phase energies. However, zero-sequence current injection can significantly increase the rms current amplitude of the STATCOM, particularly under asymmetric fault conditions, unless the reactive current reference is reduced according to the severity of grid asymmetry. In existing literature, zero sequence current has been derived in terms of complex relationships between positive and negative sequence components of voltages and currents. No existing literature furnishes closed-form expressions for the reactive current maximum limit for an SDBC STATCOM under asymmetrical fault conditions in terms of voltage dip magnitude. This work furnishes quantitative analytical expression of the maximum amount of positive-sequence reactive current that can be injected during asymmetrical grid faults. The theoretical findings are validated using MATLAB/Simulink simulations.

title: Research on Fault Scenario Modeling and Planning Technology for Distribution Networks Based on Monte Carlo and Clustering Methods
authors: Pengpeng Ren, Mao Miao, Qingyu Zhao, Hong Zhang, Chen Chen
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To support resilience planning for distribution networks with high proportions of renewable energy integration, this study addresses the problem that existing fault scenario modeling methods fail to accurately capture the spatial-temporal correlation of faults and generate insufficiently representative fault scenarios. A hybrid modeling method that integrates meteorological and equipment status data is proposed. This method constructs a spatiotemporal fault rate model based on a non-homogeneous Poisson process, combining basic fault rates, meteorological sensitivity increments, and equipment aging increments, and generates massive random fault sequences through Monte Carlo simulations. Furthermore, an adaptive density peak clustering (CFSFDP) algorithm based on K-nearest neighbors is introduced to automatically extract typical fault patterns and build a probabilistic fault scenario library. Simulations based on the improved IEEE 33-node system show that the generated fault scenario library exhibits good validity and robustness. Stress testing of planning schemes based on this scenario library indicates that compared to the traditional “<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}-1$</tex>” scheme, the enhanced scheme incorporating distributed photovoltaic and energy storage significantly improves system resilience. In meteorological-dominated and extreme clustering fault scenarios, the system's average outage duration index (SAIDI) decreases by 42.7 % and 58.3 %, respectively, while the expected energy not supplied (EENS) decreases by 52.1 % in agingdominated scenarios. The study demonstrates that this method can generate fault scenarios that closely resemble real-world conditions with clear physical meaning, providing a quantitative analysis tool for detailed distribution network resilience planning.

title: Design and Implementation of a Game Data Management System Based on SpringBoot and Vue
authors: Bohao Xiang, Nan Xie, Lewen Chen, Zhi Yang, Weimin Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the rapid development of the current gaming industry, the management and analysis of gaming data have become increasingly important. Game operators must efficiently manage gaming data to optimize the gaming experience, enhance user satisfaction, and formulate effective market strategies accordingly. At the same time, players are also highly eager to access abundant gaming information, participate in interactive gaming communities, and provide objective evaluations of games. However, traditional gaming data management methods often suffer from issues such as fragmented data, low management efficiency, and poor user experience, making it difficult to meet the demands of the current gaming industry. This paper designs a game data management system based on Spring Boot, offering rich and essential functionalities along with excellent scalability. The frontend utilizes Vue.js and Element components, while the backend employs the SpringBoot framework and the JWT authentication package to ensure user security and privacy protection. The database is implemented using MySQL. Additionally, an AI customer service powered by the Deep Seek large model is integrated, enabling the rapid construction of stable and efficient backend services. This provides a solid technical foundation for the development of the game data management system. The development of the system significantly enhances user experience, fosters interaction between players and operators, and most importantly, prioritizes user-centric design. It focuses on enabling users to perform comprehensive operations within a single platform, providing robust support for the sustained and healthy development of the gaming industry while achieving efficient management of gaming data.

title: Electrical characterization of screen-printed electrodes for toxic heavy metal detection
authors: Olanrewaju Bola Wojula
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Heavy metals are among the most toxic of all water contaminants and can have various harmful consequences for humans. Conventional toxic heavy metal (THM) detection equipments are laboratory-based, bulky and expensive. In addition, using the equipments require specialized expertise, and also involves extensive processes, thereby defeating the crucial need for outdoor application. This raises the need for the development of easy-to-use, portable alternatives. This study presents results of initial investigations carried out on the possibility of using screen-printed electrodes (SPEs) as sensing elements for a portable, user-friendly, affordable equipment for on-the-spot detection of toxic heavy metals. Initial results show that when the same SPE is used repeatedly, there is a gradual change in measured values. In addition, there is noticeable variation in the SPE’s electrical property. For the batch of SPE considered, the resistance of the working electrode spread from 252 Ω and 472 Ω, equivalent to a range of 220 Ω, with a mean and the standard deviation of 318.67 Ω and 79.49 respectively. This is equivalent to a spread of about of about ±25% from the mean. A spread of about 55% was also observed for measured peak current for cadmium. This significant variation indicates the possibility of manufacturing defects, and also points to an origin of uncorrelated results that are sometimes obtained in electrochemical measurements. The results underscore the need for better quality control in the manufacturing process used for SPEs. The investigation also show that although SPEs are potential candidates for the development of portable, handheld instruments for on-site measurements of THM, they may not be suitable for real-time, continuous monitoring and remote sensing applications.

title: DSP-Based HIL Emulator for Power Converters
authors: Kavin Mathalai, Linda Mary Zacharia, Nabivullah Syed, Farhan Ahmad Khan, Harish Sudhakaran Nair
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) testing bridges the gap between simulations and physical testing by enabling real-time interaction between control algorithms and emulated systems. This paper presents a cost-effective HIL platform implemented on a TMS320F28379D Digital Signal Processor (DSP) for real-time emulation of buck and boost converters. State-space equations were solved using Euler's method to compute voltage and current waveforms, which were visualized on a Digital Storage Oscilloscope (DSO). The system includes non-idealities such as switch-on-resistance, capacitor equivalent series resistance (ESR), and inductor winding resistance. Experimental validation shows steady-state errors under 7 % and transient errors below 15 %, showing reliable performance under varied conditions. The proposed system enables safe and affordable converter testing, suitable for research, teaching, and controller development.

title: Intelligent Agricultural Product Quality Monitoring System Leveraging Sensor Network Data
authors: Yu Wang, Pingjing Jiang, Lidi Yuan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the significant differences in monitoring requirements across agricultural production scenarios, such as greenhouses, fields, and fruit trees, and the difficulty of traditional time series algorithms in effectively capturing spatiotemporal characteristics, this paper designs a multi-scenario adaptive intelligent agricultural product monitoring system and proposes a spatiotemporal attention fusion LSTM (STA-LSTM) algorithm. The system comprises three core modules: the sensor network layer is deployed according to specific scenarios (50 m2/node in greenhouses, 20 m2/node in fields, and a combination of "trunk and ground" deployment for fruit trees). It utilizes devices such as the SHT30 temperature and humidity sensor (accuracy: ±0.5°C) and the JWSK-600 soil sensor (accuracy: ±0.1 pH). The data transmission layer adapts ZigBee (for greenhouses), LoRa (for fields), and 4G (for long-range networks), employing AES-128 encryption and CRC-16 checksum to ensure data security. The intelligent analysis layer, based on the STA-LSTM algorithm, integrates six core monitoring data types (temperature, humidity, soil parameters, etc.) using a time period adjustment factor (α = 0.2) and a spatial weighting model to improve prediction accuracy. The experiment used 12,600 samples (7,200 for tomatoes and 5,400 for cucumbers) collected over 60 days at a planting base in Jiangsu Province, comparing them with traditional LSTM, GRU, and CNN-LSTM algorithms. Results show that STA-LSTM achieved an RMSE of 0.032 (a 28.9% reduction compared to traditional LSTM) and an Acc of 96.8% (an 8.2% improvement) in tomato monitoring, and an RMSE of 0.035 (a 26.3% reduction) and an Acc of 95.6% (a 7.5% improvement) in cucumber monitoring. The system also achieved an abnormal response time of 0.8s, with accuracy dropping only 2.1% when resistant to 10% noise. Regarding system performance, the system achieved a response time of 5.01s under a high load of 10,000 data items, a node optimization lifespan of 98-111 days (energy consumption reduced by 44%-45%), and an average daily failure rate of ≤0.3 in a 30-day stability test, meeting the real-time and reliability requirements of precision agricultural monitoring.

title: Research on Modeling and Prediction Algorithm for Multivariate Time Series Data Based on Fusion of Spatial Statistics and Machine Learning
authors: Xinwu Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: The problems of insufficient spatial association mining of multivariate time series data, low prediction accuracy of a single model and weak method capability are solved. An integrated algorithm framework combining spatial statistics and machine learning is proposed. Firstly, random forest weight analysis is used to screen key influencing variables and eliminate redundant features, and then Kriging interpolation model is used to construct the spatial association model of key variables and auxiliary variables to quantify the spatial scope and intensity of variables, and finally LSTM prediction model is established by combining data refinement. Based on the time series data set of test sites, four main influencing components were screened out, and the key variables were verified to have significant spatial positive correlation and the correlation range was quantified. After optimization, the RMSE of LSTM model was reduced, the R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> was increased, and the generalization ability was enhanced. The fusion algorithm can improve the modeling accuracy and prediction reliability of multivariate time series data, and provide an efficient solution for their spatial joint analysis.

title: Efficient Control System Design of Isolated Bidirectional DC/DC Series Resonant Converter in Traction Application
authors: Sandeep Kumar Chowdhury, Peeyoosh Gupta
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Isolated bidirectional DC/DC Series Resonant Converters (SRC) are desired for their high power density and efficiency. This paper discusses the control system design of an efficient isolated DC/DC SRC that can be deployed in a traction application deploying state plane trajectory concept. A constant frequency modulation strategy ensuring soft switching over a wide load range is envisaged. Based on the same, the design parameters of the converter are calculated for maximum efficiency. The controller parameters are tuned in MATLAB Simulink for optimum performance. The response of the converter over a wide load range and dynamic change in load is simulated in PSIM. The simulated waveforms of controlled variables viz. output voltage and load current agree with the results envisaged.

title: When Bigger Isn’t Stronger: Reassessing Security in Hardware Obfuscation
authors: Sonam Sharma, Dipanjan Roy, Digambar Pawar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Existing obfuscation techniques at the High Level Synthesis (HLS) stage largely focus on maximizing the Key Search Space (KSS) for higher security, assuming that a larger number of keys guarantees stronger protection. However, bigger isn’t necessarily stronger, since many keys can be functionally redundant, and yield the same behavior. Such redundancy inflates the perceived security without actually strengthening the design. Moreover, maximizing KSS typically requires inserting more OUs, which increases power, area, and delay overhead without proportionally improving security. To address these limitations, this work introduces the EQUILIBRIUM framework, which evaluates obfuscation strength in terms of functional diversity, the number of unique functionalities generated by an obfuscated design. EQUILIBRIUM identifies redundant keys, and explores resource configurations that achieve a perfect balance between higher security, and lower design overhead. Experimental results indicate that the proposed framework can be integrated with any existing HLS-based obfuscation methodology to effectively enhance their obfuscation strength, as evaluated using SCORE.

title: A New Fault Isolation Method Based on HBSM-MMC + DC Circuit Breaker
authors: Liqun Shang, Jingqi Wang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Currently, there are two key issues that need to be considered and addressed in the research on fault isolation and protection technology for DC distribution networks. One is to ensure the selectivity of fault removal, and the other is to guarantee the economic efficiency of fault isolation technology. This paper takes the radial DC distribution network with Modular multi-level converter (MMC) as the main power supply as the object and proposes a new type of “Half Bridge Sub-Module”. The fault isolation method of “HBSM-MMC interlock + DC circuit breaker”. Firstly, propose a solation Method of HBSM-MMC+DC Circuit Breaker; Then,based on the fault isolation method given in this paper, the fault characteristics of the MMC converter before and after locking during inter-pole short circuits were analyzed; Finally, through simulation, the validity, reliability, selectivity and sensitivity of the criterion under different transition resistances at different fault points were verified. The simulation results show that the fault isolation method presented in this paper not only ensures the selectivity of DC inter-pole short circuits, but also reduces the high-speed dynamic requirements for DC inter-pole short circuit protection, and has good economy.

title: Explainable AI for Smart Power Converters: Interpretability and Trust in ML-Based Control Systems
authors: Vijayanand Selvaraj
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) into smart power converters has significantly enhanced control precision, fault detection, and efficiency optimization. However, the opaque nature of most ML models raises critical concerns over transparency, trust, and regulatory compliance. This study explores the application of Explainable AI (XAI) techniques, specifically SHAP and LIME, to a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) controlled DC boost converter. The proposed XAI framework offers both global and local interpretability, enabling operators to visualize feature influences, detect anomalies, and audit control decisions in real time. Experimental results under varying load and input voltage conditions demonstrate that XAI integration improves operator trust, supports fault diagnostics, and facilitates compliance without compromising system performance. A modular XAI enhanced control architecture is proposed for scalable deployment in mission critical applications such as electric vehicles, renewable energy inverters, and smart microgrids. The findings underscore the potential of XAI as a cornerstone for developing transparent, reliable, and trustworthy intelligent power electronics.

title: Multi-Area Dynamic Optimal Power Flow Considering Carbon Cap-and-Tax in High Renewable Energy Penetration System
authors: Ni Ketut Aryani, Imam Robandi, Ontoseno Penangsang, Fariz Rifqi Naufal Arrafi, Daffa Irdina, Muhamad Ais Faza Rohmat
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To support of growing demand for electrical energy, power plants must be able to supply the required electricity. The high value of greenhouse gas emissions from fossil fuel power plants has become a global concern, thus providing a separate impetus for the transition to cleaner energy sources. This problem is further complicated by environmental regulations such as Carbon Cap-and-Tax policies, which pose additional challenges due to the potential high tax burden due to carbon emissions produced by thermal power plants. This research aims to improve the operational efficiency of electric power systems from the perspective of operational costs through Multi-Objective Dynamic Optimal Power Flow using the Mixed-Integer Nonlinear Programming (MINLP) method, and evaluate the optimization of operational costs, power losses, and emissions in modern Multi-Area electric power systems with a high portion of renewable energy integration and energy storage due to consider Carbon Cap-and-Taxes value.

title: Assessment of Skin Contact for Ultra-Wideband Microwave Breast Scanner
authors: Alexandra Prokhorova, Marc-Patrick Heppner, Ingrid Hilger, Marko Helbig
publish: 2026 International Conference on Medical Microwave Imaging Applications (ICMWIA)
abstract: A breast fit assessment procedure for microwave breast imaging is presented in this paper. Since poor or uneven contact between patient’s skin and measurement mold can significantly decrease the quality of the resulting images, methods for detection and localization of the air gaps are needed. Three algorithms, which primarily analyze the crosstalk component of the ultra-wideband signals, are proposed, implemented and validated based on a comprehensive phantom study. Initial results show that each of the methods can successfully detect different types of air gaps and that the choice of a specific algorithm mainly depends on the clinical scenario.

title: Computational Efficiency Analysis of the Water Cycle Algorithm in Path Loss Modeling for Agricultural Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Vijay Rayar, Prabhakar Manage, Vinayak Dalavi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are essential for modern agricultural applications, enabling real-time monitoring and data collection across vast fields. Accurate path loss modeling is critical for the competent design and operation of these networks. This study evaluates the computational efficiency of the Water Cycle Algorithm (WCA) in modeling path loss for WSNs in an outdoor agricultural scenario with sugar cane crops. We implemented three different path loss models—polynomial, Gaussian, and exponential—using the WCA. Our analysis focuses solely on the computational aspects, specifically the computation time and the number of iterations required for convergence. The results demonstrate that the choice of path loss model significantly impacts the algorithm's performance, with the polynomial model being the most computationally intensive and the exponential model achieving the fastest convergence. This study explores the balance between accuracy and computational efficiency in path loss modeling for agricultural wireless sensor networks (WSNs), emphasizing the potential of the WCA to enhance network performance.

title: Transformer-Aware Graph Convolutional Network for Anomalous Bitcoin Transaction Detection
authors: Junlin Fan, Yapeng Mao, Shili Zhang, Ye Tan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the rapid development of cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin, their anonymity and immutability have made them a vehicle for some illegal underground transactions, posing a serious threat to the security and stability of the financial system. To purify the financial market and combat illegal transactions, it is imperative to effectively identify potential malicious transaction addresses in blockchain networks. Building on relevant research, this paper proposes a blockchain node classification model that integrates a graph attention mechanism with a graph convolutional network. This model aims to simultaneously capture global structural dependencies and local neighborhood features in the transaction graph. Specifically, the model first introduces a multi-head TransformerConv layer as a global feature extraction module, combined with edge embedding based on node degree sums to enhance the perception of graph topology. Subsequently, a linear projection layer and an ELU activation function are used to compress the high-dimensional output and introduce nonlinear representation. Furthermore, two layers of GCN graph convolutional layers are stacked to refine local structural features and output classification results. Finally, a sigmoid activation function is used to convert the output into probabilities for binary classification. Experimental results on the Elliptic dataset demonstrate that the proposed model achieves significantly better detection performance than traditional GCN and GAT. The baseline model demonstrates its robustness and effectiveness in financial fraud detection and graph data analysis.

title: Design and Development of Gyrotropic Material to Effectively Shield High Frequency Signals
authors: Rayadurgam Pavan Sankarsh, Sunitha K
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The work reported in this paper is in support of applications of YIG (Yttrium Iron Garnet) in patch antenna operating at frequencies ranging from 1 MHz to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \text{THz}(1 \text{MHz}$</tex>,, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \text{GHz}, 50 \text{GHz}, 500 \text{GHz}$</tex> and 1 THz). Computation of field, surface charge (i.e., induced surface charge), surface current and polarization are done to prove the gyrotropic characteristics of selected configuration design. Both <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex> are tensors which are gyrotropic.ANSYS HFSS software simulation tool is used for this work.

title: Performance Analysis of Parallelized GPS Navigation System Using OpenMP and CUDA
authors: A Sivaranjani, P Priya Ponnusamy, C P Shabariram, S Bhuvana
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Parallel computing using CUDA is used in various scientific applications like image processing, Gaming, Graphics and computer vision. GPUs are used in CUDA to run the applications and achieves parallel execution. Our proposed algorithm shows the performance improvement of the GPS navigation application by implementing using CUDA (Compute Unified Device Architecture). This GPS navigation application uses breadth first technique to navigate the locations and directions of 15 cities. The cities are considered as vertices in breadth first algorithm and is implemented using CUDA parallel processing architecture. GPS navigation application is implemented and testing in both CUDA and OpenMP (Open Multiprocessor) environments. Performance measure of GPS navigation for different cities within a particular distance is taken into consideration. The algorithm's runtime is analyzed for the different hardware configurations of CPU and the GPU systems. By varying the number of threads per each block in CUDA, time taken for each city is measured. By varying the number of threads and cities, the result is compared for both the algorithms. The result shows CUDA performs 70 % better in processing the cities than openMP. is varied and the time taken for each set of vertices in the graph are noted. The performance of execution time is measured and significantly reduced in CUDA since it is processed with high performance parallel computing technology and the same is compared with openMP which is traditional methods uses CPU.

title: HypeMed: Enhancing Medication Recommendations with Hypergraph-Based Patient Relationships
authors: Xiangxu Zhang, Xiao Zhou, Hongteng Xu, Jianxun Lian
publish: ACM Transactions on Information Systems
abstract: None

title: Construction and Application of University Agent Space Based on Ecosystem Theory
authors: Mingtao Wang, Haiguang Fang, Wen Fang, Zheng Zeng
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence technology, the application of intelligent agents based on large language models in the field of education is increasingly receiving attention. In order to overcome the concerns of university users about data privacy risks, insufficient personalized services, and technological dependence of public agent platforms, it is necessary to propose the construction of education specific agent spaces. The concept framework of university agent space is proposed based on the theory of integrated ecosystem, and a university agent space that supports multi-agent interaction is designed and implemented. Verify the operational logic, application efficiency, and optimization path of localizing the deployment of intelligent agent space through the BISTU-BookBot library agent case. The agent space in universities is expected to play a role in intelligent teaching assistance, academic research collaboration, and intelligent campus management, providing strong support for the digital transformation of universities.

title: Self-Contained Edge AI for Offline Lecture Summarization: A Raspberry Pi-Based System with Integrated Secure FTP
authors: Omsubhra Singha, Wanbanker Khongbuh, Preetisudha Meher, Priyam Gupta, Roshan Sahani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Traditional methods of taking notes during lectures, presentations, or meetings are often slow, cumbersome, and prone to omissions, leading to missed or inaccurately recorded information, thereby hindering comprehension and review. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a practical and efficient solution: a self-contained Edge AI system designed using Raspberry Pi to overcome the limitations of conventional note-taking and enhance lecture comprehension in educational and professional settings. The system is configured using a custom-trained Whisper model for accurate speech-to-text conversion, followed by a summarization pipeline that generates concise and coherent summaries of spoken content. Developed using Python, the system is engineered to be low-cost, portable, and easily deployable, making it accessible for widespread use in classrooms and institutions, particularly for offline lecture summarization. Summarization performance is evaluated using standard metrics such as ROUGE to ensure quality and effectiveness. The primary goal of this project is to enrich the learning experience for students and professionals, supporting in delivering spoken content more effectively, and provide institutions with tools for monitoring and tracking progress, all while ensuring integrated secure FTP for data management.

title: Credit Card Fraud Detection System Using Machine Learning And Deep Learning Algorithms
authors: Karthik Mungara, Kuchipudi Santhosh Kumar, C.N.S Vinoth Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Credit card fraud has emerged as a significant issue within the digital payment framework due to an increase in online payments. This paper provides a fraud detection mechanism using ML and DL methods focusing on a hybrid model based on CNN and ANN. For the purpose of the research project, this study utilized the European credit card transaction dataset. They also utilized SMOTE for resolving any data imbalance which aided in efficient model fit, reliability and sensitive fraud detection. The proposed CNN-ANN model achieved an accuracy of 95.8%, which significantly improved on the baseline models of logistic regression and decision trees. The results show the hybrid deep learning method can reduce false negatives and ultimately improve the identification of fraudulent transactions in real time.

title: RABEM: Risk-Adaptive Bayesian Ensemble Model for Credit Card Fraud Detection
authors: K. Dhinakaran, Yeturu Guru Dinesh Reddy, Sagabala Suryaprakash Reddy, Parumalla Hari Krishna Chowdary, Mukesh Balaji M P
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Credit card fraud has emerged as one of the most pressing challenges in the financial sector due to its high economic impact and sophisticated evolving tactics. Traditional machine learning methods often struggle to adapt to the dynamic nature of fraudulent behavior, especially under highly imbalanced datasets where genuine transactions vastly outnumber fraudulent ones. This research proposes RABEM (Risk-Adaptive Bayesian Ensemble Model), a novel framework that integrates Bayesian inference with ensemble learning techniques to enhance fraud detection accuracy. The model leverages a risk-adaptive strategy, dynamically weighting individual classifiers based on transaction risk profiles and historical behavioral patterns. By combining multiple base learners—such as Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Neural Networks—within a Bayesian ensemble, RABEM effectively balances sensitivity to rare fraud cases while minimizing false positives. Experimental evaluation on benchmark credit card datasets demonstrates that RABEM achieves superior performance in terms of precision, recall, F1-score, and Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC) compared to conventional single-model and standard ensemble approaches. The results highlight the model's robustness, scalability, and potential for real-time deployment in banking systems.

title: High-Quality K-12 Educational Datasets for Generative AI: Definitions, Key Technologies, and Pathway Mechanisms
authors: Hui Gu, Haiguang Fang, Xin Hong, Yiming FU, Ke Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: With the accelerated adoption of generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) in K-12 education, the structural quality, semantic expressiveness, and task adaptability of educational data have become increasingly critical for enhancing model performance and advancing system intelligence. Adopting a dual perspective that integrates K-12 instructional scenarios with generative task requirements, this paper systematically defines the connotations and components of high-quality educational datasets within this context. A six-stage construction process is proposed, emphasizing key enabling technologies including metadata construction, data annotation, and multimodal fusion. Drawing upon the heterogeneity of instructional contexts and the diversity of task requirements in K-12 education, this paper further identifies three representative dataset construction pathways: standard-oriented, taskintegrated, and scenario-embedded. The findings aim to provide both theoretical foundations and practical guidance for the efficient generation, scenario adaptation, and intelligent application of high-quality educational data resources, thereby fostering the deep integration of GenAI and basic education.

title: GVICA: A Multi-Channel EEG Hierarchical Noise Reduction Framework Based on GWO Dynamically Optimized VMD-ICA Fusion_supp1-3677897.docx
authors: Yongcheng Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Objective: Electroencephalogram (EEG) signal acquisition is prone to artifact interference, which affects the interpretation of neural activity. This paper aims to develop a new method that can automatically and effectively remove ocular and electromyographic artifacts from multi-channel EEG, while maximally preserving neurophysiological information. Methods: We propose a multi-level denoising method called GVICA. This method is implemented by integrating optimized Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) and Independent Component Analysis (ICA). The signals were first grouped by noise level. The Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) was then employed to determine the optimal VMD parameters adaptively. Anomaly detection and correction were performed on the obtained intrinsic mode functions (IMFs). Finally, an ICA-based entropy denoising module was combined to objectively select sub-signals in the frequency domain for accurate artifact removal. Results: Evaluations on both simulated and real EEG data show that GVICA can effectively remove artifacts, achieving lower root mean square error (RMSE) and higher Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) under different signal-to-noise ratios (SNR). Compared with existing methods, GVICA can simultaneously and automatically remove EMG and EOG artifacts and more accurately restore brain responses. Conclusion: The GVICA method significantly improves the quality of multi-channel EEG signals through its group-based multi-level and adaptive denoising framework, achieving artifact removal with minimal loss of neurophysiological information. Significance: This study provides a reliable tool for cleaning electroencephalogram (EEG) data in clinical settings and demonstrates significant potential for application in the analysis of motor execution EEG during stroke rehabilitation.
