title: Cross-Dialectal Transfer Learning for Offensive Language Detection in Arabic
authors: Ebtihal Enfes, Nooredeen Awwad, Kolawole J. Adebayo
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Automated offensive language detection (OLD) is essential for online safety, yet remains particularly challenging for under-resourced languages and their dialectal variations. This paper addresses these challenges by evaluating OLD across three low-resource Arabic dialects: Egyptian, Libyan, and Levantine, using newly collected dialect-specific datasets and a novel parallel corpus for controlled cross-dialect analysis.We benchmark a wide range of models, including encoder-only, encoder–decoder, and decoder-only Large Language Models (LLMs), alongside traditional machine learning baselines (XGBoost, LightGBM), under zero-shot, few-shot, and fine-tuned settings. Our experiments reveal that task-specific fine-tuning consistently delivers the highest performance, especially for models pre-trained on Arabic corpora such as AraBERT, while few-shot learning yields modest and sometimes unstable gains. Surprisingly, classical models leveraging AraBERT embeddings remain competitive in zero-shot settings. Cross-dialect evaluations further highlight substantial performance variability driven by dialectal distance, lexical ambiguity, and contextual offensiveness. These findings offer a comprehensive benchmark for OLD in dialectal Arabic and provide practical guidance for developing robust moderation systems in linguistically diverse, low-resource environments.

title: Fuzzy Clustering Means Integrated Multi-Level Learning for Contrastive Multi-View Clustering
authors: Meng Li, Jiawei Cao, Qiumei Sun
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Multi-view clustering (MVC) effectively mines complementary information from diverse modalities, yielding more accurate and robust results than single-view methods. Recent approaches address key challenges in traditional MVC, target conflict and private information interference, through multi-level feature decoupling and fusion-free mechanisms. However, during the transition between contrastive learning and fine-tuning stages, existing methods often rely on rigid pseudo-label generation techniques such as hard labels. This rigidity overlooks the inherent fuzziness of category boundaries in real-world data, leading to issues including view discrepancy, which causes ineffective cross-view semantic alignment, and manifold blindness, which denotes failure to capture complex data structures. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes FCM-MFL-MVC, a novel multi-view feature learning framework that integrates Fuzzy C-Means (FCM) clustering within a contrastiveMVCparadigm for pseudo-label guided MVC. Our key innovation lies in generating probabilistic pseudo-labels via FCM, naturally modeling cluster assignment uncertainty and mitigating erroneous label propagation. This core idea is supported by a dynamic learning rate scheduler to stabilize optimization across stages and a balanced dual-objective loss to enhance clustering quality while suppressing noise-induced overfitting. Extensive experiments on high-dimensional datasets including CCV, Caltech, ACM, MNIST-test and EYB-fc demonstrate that FCM-MFL-MVC significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving marked improvements in both clustering performance measured by ACC and NMI and in model robustness.

title: Graph-Based Representation Learning with Beta Uncertainty for Enhanced Multimodal Emotion Recognition
authors: Axel Gedeon Mengara Mengara, Sathishkumar Moorthy, Sathiyamoorthi Arthanari, Hyo-won Kim, Yeon-Kug Moon
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: The performance of multimodal emotion recognition (MER) systems critically depends on their ability to effectively integrate information from heterogeneous sources. However, existing fusion methods including attention-based mechanisms, auxiliary regression, and sampling-based approaches often lack efficient strategies for managing uncertainty in the presence of noisy or conflicting inputs across modalities. To address this limitation, we propose a novel framework grounded in an Uncertainty-Aware Fusion Mechanism. Our system employs a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture with specialized Transformer encoders to learn modality-specific representations. Within the fusion module, each modality expert predicts the parameters of a Beta distribution, yielding a bounded reliability score within the [0, 1] interval. In contrast to computationally intensive sampling techniques or generic Gaussian-based models, our method enables efficient, single-pass confidence estimation. These Beta-derived reliability scores dynamically modulate the contribution of each modality, attenuating the influence of less reliable sources. The resulting uncertainty-weighted representation is subsequently refined by a Dynamic Graph Convolutional Neural Network (DGCNN), which captures high-order, sample-specific relationships among modalities. We evaluate our approach on three diverse benchmarks: the DEAP dataset for physiological, the MELD and IEMOCAP datasets for conversational emotion recognition. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements over state-of-the-art methods, highlighting the effectiveness of the Beta-distribution-based fusion strategy for robust emotion recognition.

title: Path Planning for UAV Entry and Exit in the Electric Field of UHV Transmission Lines
authors: Shanhe Zhang, Ziling You, Wei Zhao, Longjiang Hu, Gang Luo, Changhui Li
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To address the multi-objective and multi-constraint challenges in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) path planning within the electromagnetic environment of ultra-high-voltage (UHV) transmission lines, an Enhanced Plant Rhizome Growth-based Optimization (E-PRGO) algorithm is proposed. Based on the traditional PRGO algorithm, electric field gradient constraints and dynamic stochastic perturbations are introduced, and the random differential term is replaced with fixed parameters, resulting in a multi-objective optimization model that jointly considers both path length and electric-field intensity. The EPRGO algorithm is compared with the traditional PRGO algorithm in a three-dimensional electric field of a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 800 \text{kV}$</tex> UHVDC transmission line. The results demonstrate that E-PRGO exhibits superior optimization performance, achieving an 83.3 % improvement in iteration speed compared to PRGO. The generated optimal path not only satisfies the electric field safety constraints but also significantly reduces disturbances in highgradient regions, with a path length of 350.32 m, representing an 11.7 % reduction relative to PRGO. The above findings provide a theoretical basis for the safe entry and exit of UAV in UHV transmission lines and for their path planning.

title: Research on Real-time Verification Method for Low Frequency and Low Voltage Load Shedding Control Scheme
authors: Zhihang Lin, Defa Cao, Xiaozheng Gao, Ruiming Zeng, Junbao Zhang, Chengwei Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: The integration of renewable energy sources, particularly photovoltaic (PV) solar power, has brought significant benefits to power generation but also posed challenges in maintaining grid stability due to their intermittent nature. Low-Frequency and Low-Voltage Load Shedding (LF-LV-LS) control strategies are employed to stabilize the grid by disconnecting non-essential loads during disturbances. However, traditional offline verification methods fail to capture real-time grid dynamics, especially with fluctuating renewable generation. This paper proposes a novel real-time verification method for LF-LV-LS control strategies, consisting of load importance analysis and grid topology consistency verification. The method evaluates the impact of load shedding on grid stability through real-time power flow analysis and ensures alignment with the current grid topology. By combining these steps, the proposed method enhances the effectiveness and adaptability of LF-LV-LS strategies, ensuring grid stability and better integration of renewable energy sources.

title: On the de Bruijn Sequences Representation of User Clickstream Data
authors: Tse-Yu Lin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The user behavior clickstream data reflect the dynamics of user actions and sequential interactions between consecutive actions that the usual vector or matrix embeddings often fail to capture. In this paper, we consider user behavior action sequence clickstream data as a directed path in the de Bruijn graph, allowing for the comparison of different users’ behaviors in terms of the distance between paths in the same directed graph. This provides a framework for studying the cost of achieving expected behavioral change in graph models.

title: Combination of Traditional Teaching and AI-Guided Self-Directed Learning on Theoretical Learning and Academic Performance in High-Workload Immunobiology Courses
authors: Maricruz Sepulveda-Villegas, Miriam Irene Jiménez-Pérez, Angélica Lizeth Sánchez López, Karla Nohemi Rubio-Martin del Campo, Gildardo Sanchez-Ante, Diego Eloy Navarro-Lopez
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: This study examined the impact of integrating an AI-guided self-directed learning tool (MagicSchool) into the theoretical module of a high-workload immunobiology course. Academic performance in 2025 was compared with that in 2023 and 2024, and student motivation was assessed using the MUSIC Model of Academic Motivation Inventory. The AI-assisted group achieved higher theoretical module and exam scores than the previous scholar periods (p < 0.0001) and higher final course grades by 2024 (p = 0.0238). The motivation scores were high for usefulness, success, caring, and empowerment, although interest was low, suggesting the need for various learning strategies. These findings indicate that AI can complement traditional teaching to improve performance and support motivation; however, sustaining curiosity requires diverse and interactive methods.

title: Comparative Analysis of Gradient Boosting and Transformer Based Models for Multiclass Haemophilia Classification Using Genetic and Clinical Data
authors: Anitha Kumari K, Premanth P
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Haemophilia is an inherited bleeding disorder due to mutations in the F8 gene, which leads to reduced or abnormal Factor VIII (FVIII) activity. Essential for diagnosis and personalized treatment is severity classification. This study compares the efficacy of the Gradient Boosting models LightGBM, CatBoost, and AdaBoost and the Transformer-based TabTransformer for multiclass haemophilia diagnosis. We utilise two datasets, namely F8 Numb, which contains numerical clinical and genetic features and F8 Cato, which contains categorical representations for Transformer learning. Normal vs Haemophilia (binary) and Severity 1-3 (multiclass) classifications were done. The binary classification of this problem achieved perfect scores because the class was clear, whereas the multiclass severity prediction did exhibit some differentiation in performance. LightGBM reached 97.6% accuracy in clinical data and 74.9% accuracy in genetic data, which was better than CatBoost, AdaBoost and TabTransformer. The result of study shows that the gradient boosting algorithm is efficient on structured biomedical data. Besides this, using clinical and genetic data predicts haemophilia severity reliably.

title: Stability Analysis of Vehicle-Grid System Considering Improved Phase-Locked Loop
authors: Zixiang Zhang, Ying Wang, Luping Kang, Junjie Gu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: In high-speed railway vehicle-grid coupling systems, low-frequency oscillation (LFO) phenomena frequently occur, posing a threat to stable operation. This paper proposes a stability optimization strategy based on an Improved Second-Order Generalized Integrator Phase-Locked Loop (SOGI-PLL). By introducing a low-pass filter (LPF) with damping characteristics into the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\beta}$</tex>-axis channel of the SOGI, the disturbance rejection capability of the synchronous reference frame transformation is effectively enhanced. A detailed dq-frame impedance model of the improved SOGI is derived, and the influence of PLL parameters on the input impedance characteristics of the rectifier is analyzed. Simulation results demonstrate that the improved PLL significantly increases system low-frequency damping, effectively suppresses oscillations in grid voltage and current, and enhances the operational reliability of the traction power supply system.

title: MedGuardChain: Blockchain-Based Intelligent Cyber Defense Framework for Secure Electronic Health Records
authors: G. Shanmugapriya, A.S. Shanthi, Abilasha N, Dharanisankaran T S, Karthi Saravanan T
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Electronic Health Records (EHRs) are widely adopted digital repositories that allow healthcare institutions to securely manage, store, and exchange patient health information. Although EHRs have transformed clinical workflows, they also expose sensitive medical data to cyberattacks and unauthorized manipulation. To overcome these challenges, this paper proposes MedGuardChain, a blockchain-integrated intelligent cyber defense system that leverages machine learning, Zero- Trust architecture, and smart contracts for secure data handling. For anomaly detection in access patterns, MedGuardChain adopts SVM and LSTM models, implements continuous authentication through Zero-Trust automation, and records events immutably using the Hyperledger Fabric blockchain. Experiments demonstrate improved detection accuracy of 96 percent, reduced false positive rate of 8 percent, and faster response latency of 0.8 seconds. The framework ensures data integrity, privacy, and decentralized collaboration among hospitals, setting a new benchmark for secure EHR management.

title: Research on Plug-and-Play Communication Mechanism for Distributed Energy Storage Systems
authors: Shengqing Hu, Xiaoqiang Peng, Yanni Kou
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper addresses the issues of “large data volume, diverse device types, and slow device access” in distributed battery energy storage system terminal equipment by proposing a registration/identification mechanism based on the Universal Plug and Play (UPnP) protocol. This mechanism enables rapid identification and connection between distributed battery energy storage system terminals and the cloud master station. Built on the foundation of system terminal self-describing configurations, the mechanism achieves identity authentication by obtaining certificates or credentials from the cloud master station, ensuring data security. The cloud master station validates the effectiveness of the model and the legitimacy of certificates by receiving message packets sent by the terminals, thereby realizing rapid identification and connection. Finally, the introduction of an information interaction mechanism based on the Message Queuing Telemetry Transport (MQTT) protocol provides the energy storage system with a more unified and standardized information interaction mechanism.

title: Evaluating Generalization and Robustness of U-Net Based Image Steganography
authors: Dhanush Adithiya, Bhuvana Jayabalan, Beaulah Soundarabai P, Peter Augustin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: This paper investigates the effectiveness and generalization ability of U-Net based image steganography models across multiple datasets, with comparisons to the classical Least Significant Bit (LSB) substitution method. Models were trained on STL-10, CIFAR-10, and Stanford Cars datasets and evaluated both in-distribution and on out-of-distribution internet images. Results show that the STL-10 model consistently achieved the best trade-off between imperceptibility and recovery quality, while the CIFAR-10 model failed to generalize due to its low resolution and limited diversity. Baseline experiments confirmed that LSB achieves extremely high PSNR and SSIM at low payloads, but suffers sharp increases in Bit Error Rate (BER) under higher payloads or even mild distortions such as JPEG compression and Gaussian noise. By contrast, the U-Net model provided more stable recovery and greater robustness, highlighting the advantages of learned feature embeddings over handcrafted substitution. These findings underscore the importance of dataset diversity and robustness testing in developing practical steganographic systems for real-world deployment.

title: Research and Design of Precision Feeding Systems for Smart Pig Farms
authors: Ran Jiang, Tinghang Guo, Yue Sun, Yu Han, Guangda Lu, Zhuanping Qin, Runze Li
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Traditional large-scale pig farming often employs group feeding methods, making it difficult to account for individual differences. This approach can lead to reduced feed utilization efficiency and hinder the timely detection of changes in individual animal conditions. To address these issues, this paper designs and analyzes an individual pig feeding management system by integrating multiple sensor data acquisition methods with software control techniques. The system utilizes multi-source sensor data to collect pig body temperature, weight, and feedingrelated metrics. It adjusts feeding parameters under predefined constraints, enabling automated management of individual feeding processes. This system supports precision feeding applications while ensuring normal growth requirements are met, providing a viable technical solution for pig farming management.

title: Online DC Bias Compensation in Wind Turbine Transformers Using Converter-Embedded Intelligence for Vibration Mitigation
authors: Jianhua Huang, Bin Tang, Xinming Yan, Youfu Qin, Yifei Liu, Ting Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper addresses the operational challenge of DC bias in transformers connected to wind power converters, a condition that precipitates increased vibration, acoustic noise, and thermal stress, thereby compromising transformer reliability. Direct sensing of DC flux is impractical for in-service equipment. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes a novel, noninvasive method for online detection and active compensation of DC bias, leveraging the intrinsic sensing and control capabilities of the grid-side converter. The core of the proposed method is a highfidelity algorithm that extracts the second-order harmonic component from the converter-side AC current, which is analytically shown to be a robust indicator of the DC bias magnitude and polarity. Based on the detected harmonic signature, a closed-loop compensation controller is designed and seamlessly integrated into the converter's standard control framework. This controller injects a precise, counteracting DC current component, effectively nullifying the DC bias in the transformer without requiring any additional hardware. The efficacy and dynamic performance of the proposed “converter-embedded intelligence” are rigorously validated through both detailed simulations and experiments on a kW -scale hardware prototype. Experimental results demonstrate that the strategy reduces the second-harmonic current by over 90 % and correspondingly suppresses the 100 Hz core vibration component by 18.4 dB, confirming the viability of this cost-effective solution for enhancing the operational integrity of wind energy systems.

title: Deep Learning-Based Accent Analysis for Pronunciation Improvement in Language Classrooms
authors: Mahliyo Mamatkulova, Nargizaxon Qodirova, Bahrom Xo'janiyozov, Shavkat Khaydarov, Gulnoza Ochilova, Botir Raxmatullayev, Maftuna Tojiahmedova
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Pronunciation plays a vital role in effective language learning, and accent variations often hinder learners from achieving native-like fluency. Deep learning provides promising avenues to analyze and improve pronunciation in classroom environments automatically. Existing pronunciation training methods rely heavily on manual feedback or traditional speech recognition systems, which often lack precision, scalability, and adaptability across diverse accents. To address these limitations, the proposed framework employs a Residual Neural Network (ResNet) for accent analysis, leveraging its ability to capture fine-grained acoustic features and deep contextual representations. The system provides learners with real-time, phoneme-level feedback, highlighting pronunciation deviations and suggesting corrections specific to their accent patterns. The experimental findings demonstrate significant improvement in learners' pronunciation accuracy and accent reduction compared to conventional methods. This framework enhances personalized learning, supports multilingual classrooms, and empowers teachers with AIdriven assistance.

title: Research on Quantifying Model Uncertainty in Complex System Simulation Tests
authors: Kai Wang, Jianfeng Li, Bing Han, Tongtong Shi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: In order to address the uncertainty issues of virtual simulation models for test cases in complex airborne electronic and electrical systems/equipment simulation testing application scenarios for aviation equipment, the formation mechanism and basic components of model uncertainty are analyzed. According to the construction and operating principles of simulation test case models, and based on the signal generation and operating mechanism of a simulation testing system, a structured method for model uncertainty is proposed. Specific simulation test case models are analyzed and studied from two aspects, i.e. quantification of epistemic uncertainty and quantification of aleatoric uncertainty in complex systems. Two calculation methods are provided for uncertainty quantification. Finally, a comprehensive quantification method for overall uncertainty of the simulation test case model is provided.

title: Teaching the Machine, and Trusting the Machine: Unpacking the Pitfalls of LLMs in Education
authors: Ioannis Patias
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: The rapid integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into educational contexts has sparked both enthusiasm and concern. While these systems offer unprecedented opportunities for personalized learning, scalable tutoring, and content generation, they also introduce significant risks—including factual inaccuracies, embedded biases, pedagogical misalignment, and threats to privacy and professional roles. This paper critically examines the pitfalls of LLM-based educational tools and proposes a structured response through a tripartite framework—input, model, and output. By categorizing technical and design interventions according to the stage at which they operate, this framework provides a clear and actionable roadmap for mitigating harm and enhancing educational value. Input-level strategies focus on ethical data sourcing, inclusive prompt design, and curriculum alignment. Model-level solutions address architectural transparency, bias correction, and adaptive learning pathways. Output-level approaches emphasize human oversight, critical thinking scaffolds, and culturally sensitive content delivery. This layered analysis not only clarifies the interdependencies between technical and pedagogical challenges but also underscores the need for holistic governance. The proposed framework offers educators, developers, and policymakers a practical lens for evaluating and improving the deployment of LLMs in learning environments—ensuring that these tools support, rather than compromise, the integrity of education.

title: SCaLT-Based Deep Learning Framework for Signal Classification Using MobileNetV2
authors: Ashwath P, Karthika Renuka D
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Accurate detection of cardiac diseases in a timely manner is very important for preventing any severe complications in the human body. Electrocardiogram (ECG) signals have proven to be a key tool that cab be used for diagnosis in this aspect. ECG analysis that is done manually can be very time-consuming and it also requires a high level expertise in this domain, which highlights the need for solutions that are automated and requires no manual intervention. This paper presents SCaLT (Scalable and Portable Classification), a deep learning framework that employs MobileNetV2 model, data augmentation technique, and transfer learning for efficient ECG signal classification. The created model has been evaluated on the NHFB 2023 dataset. The approach effectively categorizes the ECG signals into Normal, Arrhythmia, and Myocardial classes, and it demonstrates both scalability and high accuracy in the analysis of clinical ECG.

title: Research on the Implementation of Chinese Painting Artistic Concepts in Computer-Assisted Film and Television Animation Creation
authors: Hua Zhu, Chi Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the difficulty of existing style transfer algorithms in accurately transferring the artistic conception of Chinese ink paintings, a multi-feature fusion-based Chinese ink painting artistic conception transfer fusion algorithm is proposed. This algorithm constructs a three-step framework: feature layer extraction - dynamic weight fusion - physical simulation enhancement. It improves the VGG19 network to extract brush and ink features, combines it with a spatial attention model to capture composition and white space features, extracts animation dynamic features using optical flow and contour constraints, dynamically assigns feature weights based on information evaluation, and introduces a physical model of ink diffusion to optimize the generation effect. The experiments are conducted using CycleGAN and StyleGAN2 as benchmarks, and are validated on a dataset consisting of 400 Chinese paintings for training and 2800 animation frames. Results show that the proposed algorithm achieved an average subjective evaluation score of 8.52, representing improvements of 40.8% and 28.1% over CycleGAN (6.05) and StyleGAN2 (6.65), respectively. Objectively, the algorithm achieved average PSNR of 32.5dB, SSIM of 0.89, and FSIM of 0.92, respectively, representing improvements of 9.4%-17.9% over the baseline algorithms. The algorithm converged after 12,000 iterations, 25% faster than CycleGAN, effectively achieving the precise coupling of the artistic conception of Chinese ink painting with film and television animation.

title:
MA
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    -VD: Cross-Modal Vulnerability Detection Across Source and Binary Code via Modal Adapters and Memory Alignment
authors: Qinghao Li, Jizhong Li, Chunling Liu, Tieming Liu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Vulnerability detection across source and binary code is an important yet challenging task in software security research. Existing deep learning methods are mostly limited to a single modality and fail to effectively leverage complementary feature information between source and binary codes, thereby limiting detection capabilities and application scenarios. In this paper, we propose a cross-modal vulnerability detection framework based on modal adapters and memory alignment, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MA}^{2}$</tex>-VD. Specifically, we first address heterogeneity differences across modalities using modality-specific adapters. Then, we construct a cross-modal feature memory bank based on FAISS, which implicitly aligns features by dynamically retrieving matching cross-modal features during inference to achieve memory alignment. We evaluated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MA}^{2}$</tex>-VD on the dual-modal NVD dataset that contains both source and binary code samples. The results show that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MA}^{2}$</tex>-VD outperforms the existing baseline methods by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.21 \%, 1.23 \%, 9.86 \%$</tex>, and 5.71 % in terms of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score, precision, recall, and accuracy, respectively. Ablation studies and incremental experiments further demonstrate the effectiveness of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MA}^{2}$</tex>-VD in cross-modal vulnerability detection.

title: Water Surface Garbage Detection and Autonomous Navigation Cleaning Technology Based on an Enhanced YOLO Visual Algorithm
authors: Yuechen Tian, Yifan Zhou, Lingling Tan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This study addresses the engineering challenges associated with the removal of small floating debris from water surfaces, with a core focus on algorithmic customization for specific scenarios, deployment on low-computational-power hardware, and system-level integration and optimization. To address the challenges posed by high surface reflectivity and the prevalence of small targets in aquatic environments, the spatial-channel attention mechanism is specifically adapted and optimized for this application context. The enhanced model is integrated within the YOLO framework and successfully deployed on the STM32H743 embedded platform. Furthermore, the system incorporates multi-sensor fusion for navigation and an expandable mechanical structure, enabling a fully integrated closed-loop workflow encompassing detection, navigation, and debris collection. To enhance the detection performance for small-sized objects and garbage in complex environmental conditions, the study implements targeted optimizations to the object detection algorithm by integrating an attention mechanism and training on a custom-built dataset that closely reflects real-world scenarios. The final algorithm maintains a lightweight architecture and high-speed inference capability while ensuring a high average recognition accuracy.

title: Teaching in the Age of AI: Exploring the Role of Microsoft Copilot Vision in Redefining Teacher-Student Interaction and Promoting Educational Equity
authors: Roman M. De Angel, Ronel F. Ramos, Alfredo L. Calimbo, Juan Paulo H. Magcuyao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: In the evolving landscape of education, artificial intelligence (AI) is reshaping traditional pedagogical models and redefining the roles of teachers and students. This paper explores the transformative potential of Microsoft Copilot Vision, a multimodal AI tool, in enhancing teacher-student interaction and promoting educational equity. By integrating visual AI capabilities into classroom practices, educators can create more dynamic and adaptive learning environments that can personalize instruction, provide real-time feedback, and support diverse learning needs. The study examines how Copilot Vision facilitates inclusive learning environments, particularly for students with disabilities, language barriers, or limited access to resources. It also investigates the shifting role of teachers-from content deliverers to facilitators of AI-enhanced learning-and the ethical considerations surrounding human-AI collaboration in education. Anchored in the framework of Sustainable Development Goal 4 (SDG4), which advocates for inclusive and equitable quality education, this research highlights the opportunities and challenges of deploying AI vision tools to bridge learning gaps and foster meaningful engagement in the digital classroom setup.

title: Research on Adaptive Defense Mechanisms for Cybersecurity in IPv6-Based Smart Substation Networks
authors: Jun Chen, Mengxue Zhou, Rui Gong, Haibin Zhao, Shuqi Wang, Zhuang Han, Huichao Liang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: To establish a highly reliable communication security framework for ipv6-enabled smart substations, this study investigates a collaborative control environment integrating station control, bay, and process domains. It analyzes how ipv6 neighbor discovery, extended header chain, active probing, and multicast mechanisms expand attack surfaces in real-time mission scenarios, proposing an adaptive defense architecture driven by security control planes. the research focuses on three core technologies: 1) constructing an ipv6 traffic characterization system aligned with power industry operational patterns through semantic unification of multi-dimensional elements across packet layer, session layer, and device layer; 2) describing intra-station device interactions via dynamic heterogeneous graph structures composed of node types, edge relationships, and time slices, implementing structured detection of RA flood, ND spoofing, and deep header extension attacks through relational grouping convolution and temporal encoding mechanisms; 3) mapping detection outputs to policy space and introducing a deep reinforcement learning-based adaptive policy update mechanism to generate executable defense actions while maintaining real-time communication performance constraints for goose and sv protocols. Results demonstrate that the adaptive defense framework significantly improves detection accuracy and policy response efficiency in typical attack scenarios, achieving closed-loop regulation of policy optimization processes and validating the method’s effective adaptation to ipv6 smart substation network security requirements.

title: Cognitive Perturbation Search: A Simple and Adaptive Algorithm for Continuous Optimization and Neural Network Training
authors: Mohd Herwan Sulaiman, Zuriani Mustaffa, Amir Izzani Mohamed, Saifudin Razali
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Autonomous Systems (ICoIAS)
abstract: This paper presents Cognitive Perturbation Search (CPS), a simple yet adaptive single solution optimizer designed for continuous optimization problems and neural network training. Unlike traditional metaheuristic algorithms that rely on population-based strategies or metaphor driven operators, CPS adopts a memory-based search mechanism without requiring gradient information. Each parameter dimension is equipped with a lightweight cognitive memory that reinforces successful perturbation directions and suppresses unproductive ones. This adaptive strategy allows CPS to strike a practical balance between exploration and exploitation. The performance of CPS is first evaluated using three classical benchmark functions, namely Sphere, Rosenbrock, and Rastrigin, which represent unimodal, narrow valley, and multimodal landscapes. CPS is then applied to neural network training tasks, including the XOR classification problem and a real-world chiller energy prediction case study. Results demonstrate that CPS offers competitive accuracy, stability, and convergence behavior compared to standard methods such as Adam, making it a promising alternative for optimization tasks involving complex, noisy, or non-differentiable objective functions.

title: A Real-Time Power Quality Evaluation Method Based on the Dual-Track Integration of Dynamic Empowerment and Data Grading
authors: Heng Zhang, Bei Chu, Shiming Lin, Kefeng Wang, Tiantian Liu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper proposes a real-time power quality evaluation method based on the dual-track integration of dynamic empowerment and data grading. To address the power quality challenges brought by the large-scale integration of power electronic devices such as distributed photovoltaic systems and electric vehicle charging facilities in the new-type power system, a comprehensive evaluation system covering multiple indicators including voltage deviation, frequency deviation, and harmonic content is constructed. The state entropy weight method is adopted to realize dynamic weight adjustment, which not only maintains the advantage of objectivity but also enhances time-domain resolution. A dual-track TOPSIS evaluation method with separated data and grading is used to avoid the impact of mixed data grades on evaluation results. Through theoretical analysis and experimental verification, the method can respond quickly under both single-index and multi-index deterioration scenarios, accurately reflect the power quality status, and provide effective technical support for real-time monitoring and management of power quality in distribution networks.

title: Hybrid Clustering Algorithm for Language Proficiency Profiling in Online Tutoring Systems
authors: Dilafruz Abduxamidova, Nasiba Sabirova, Rano Aliyeva, Javlon Sharofov, Maqsuda Azizova, Ogabek Sultanov, Navruzbek Soybnazarov
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The online tutoring systems are vital in improving language learning through the delivery of the personal content and dynamic feedback. First, the language proficiency of learners needs to be profiled accurately to be able to adjust the instructional approach and enhance the learning process. The available methodologies would tend to use standardized tests or simplistic clustering and these cannot demonstrate the multidimensionality of learner behaviour and performance thus resulting in inaccurate or rough profiling. This paper attempts to overcome these challenges by suggesting a Spectral-Spatial Clustering (SSC) framework that combines spectral analysis to obtain similarity patterns globally with spatial clustering to obtain the local structure. The hybrid nature of this approach allows the system to divide the learners into a more accurate category, according to performance indicators, interaction patterns and learning progress. The suggested approach enables online tutoring systems to create dynamic and granular proficiency representations and enables the use of this information to deliver content and provide adaptive learning solutions. Experimental testing proves that SSC is accurate and brings about significant learner splitting in comparison to conventional technologies.

title: Multimodal Pipe Defect Diagnosis with Transformer-Based Cross-Modal Learning
authors: Ping Zhong, Gang Li, Weijiao Kong, Yue Yu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: The integrity of underground pipelines is critical for urban infrastructure and environmental safety. Traditional inspection methods, such as Magnetic Flux Leakage (MFL) and optical imaging, often suffer from inherent limitations, failing to provide a comprehensive diagnosis. MFL excels at detecting volumetric defects like corrosion and pitting but is insensitive to non-metallic or planar defects such as cracks and deformations. Conversely, optical imaging identifies surface cracks and structural anomalies but is highly susceptible to turbid water and limited lighting conditions. This paper proposes a novel approach for multimodal defect diagnosis by synergistically fusing MFL and optical imaging data using a Transformer-based cross-modal learning framework. Our method leverages the self-attention mechanism of the Transformer to effectively model the intricate dependencies within each modality and, more importantly, the cross-attention mechanism to learn a shared, robust feature representation that captures complementary information from both data streams.

title: Real-Time Early Warning Model of Psychological Abnormality of Plateau Emergency Personnel Based on Multimodal Physiological Signals
authors: Naijun Cheng, Yang Zhang, Yang Ci, Dingxing Xie, Bin Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper addresses the need for real-time monitoring and early warning of the psychological state of emergency personnel in the unique environment of the plateau. To address the challenges faced by existing methods in plateau hypobaric, hypoxic, and high-stress tasks, such as strong signal interference, large individual differences, and insufficient real-time performance, a real-time early warning model for psychological abnormalities based on multimodal physiological signals is proposed. First, a physiological signal database for plateau conditions is constructed, then, a spatiotemporal convolutional network incorporating an attention mechanism is designed, an adaptive threshold learning mechanism is introduced, finally, a lightweight edge computing deployment solution is constructed to achieve low-latency, real-time early warning. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves an F1 score of 0.891 in the psychological abnormality recognition task and an average F1 score of 0.852 in the cross-individual generalization test. The edge inference latency fully meets the application requirements. Overall, the proposed model strikes a good balance between performance, real-time performance, and resource consumption, making it feasible for practical deployment.

title: Design and Implementation of a 2 kbit EEPROM Circuit
authors: Hongni Zhu, Yonghe Chen, Jianghui Zhai
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Based on the 180nm 2P4M EEPROM process, this paper proposes a 2 kbit electrically erasable programmable readonly memory (EEPROM) circuit. The circuit adopts a dual-supply voltage scheme of 1.8V and 3.3V, where the charge pump module is independently powered by 3.3V to improve charge-discharge speed and thereby shorten the boost time. Meanwhile, an RC oscillator is adopted in the clock generation circuit, with a targeted replacement of the resistor branch by a proportional current mirror to suppress intrinsic power consumption and effectively reduce the layout area. Subsequently, through the optimization of the topological structure of the front-end module circuit and the adoption of a compact layout in layout design, the final layout area achieved is 0.17mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, which significantly enhances the chip's area efficiency. Finally, the EEPROM is integrated into an RFID tag chip and fabricated using the aforementioned process. Posttapeout verification confirms that its core functions, including read, erase, and program operations, all comply with the design specifications.

title: Compact High-Frequency Antenna Design for 25.72 GHz Short-Range Radar Systems
authors: P.Jothilakshmi, Chris Calvin P, Afra Parveen Jameel
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This study introduces a small, microstrip patch antenna suitable for radar systems operating at 25.72 GHz. The designed antenna displays excellent impedance matching properties, with an return loss (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>) value of -27.32 dB and a 1.33 GHz impedance bandwidth. It generates a focused far-field radiation pattern, attaining a peak gain of 5.64 dBi and positioning its primary lobe at 8.0<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</sup>, thereby providing illumination primarily in the forward direction. The antenna features a 3 dB angular width of 45.9<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</sup>. During simulation, it achieved a total efficiency of 79.17% on a Rogers 5880 substrate with dielectric constant 2.2. Being compact makes this antenna ideal for deployment in space constrained systems. Its excellent performance in the millimeter wave band makes it favorable for high resolution short range radar which involves industrial sensing applications for accurate level probing and displacement detection of human presence, and for vehicular applications such as ADAS radar where high performance, compactness and good reliability is important.

title: Bayesian Network Algorithm-Based Safety Prevention Risk Assessment Method for Nuclear Power Plant Construction EPC Mode
authors: Zhendong Li, Yachao Zhong, Zhongyi Shen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: In the context of nuclear power plant construction under the Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) model, ensuring safety throughout the project lifecycle is of paramount importance. This paper presents a novel safety prevention risk assessment method based on the Bayesian Network (BN) algorithm, which is designed to effectively evaluate and mitigate potential risks in the construction phase. The proposed method leverages the probabilistic graphical model of Bayesian Networks to analyze complex dependencies between various risk factors, providing a comprehensive risk assessment framework that can identify both direct and indirect safety hazards. By incorporating expert knowledge and historical data, the BN model offers a dynamic and adaptive approach to safety management, enabling proactive risk mitigation strategies. The performance of the method is evaluated through a case study of a nuclear power plant construction project, demonstrating its practical applicability and effectiveness in improving safety outcomes. The results show that the Bayesian Network-based risk assessment significantly enhances the decision-making process, supporting the EPC mode in achieving higher safety standards and reducing construction-related incidents. This approach is expected to contribute to the broader adoption of advanced risk management techniques in large-scale industrial projects, particularly in high-risk sectors such as nuclear energy.

title: Transfer Learning for Predictive Maintenance in E-Mobility Using IoT and Digital Twins
authors: Harikrishnan Miraj Chathankandath, Adith P, Pranakanth C, Nandan Rajesh, VenkateshKumar M
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: As electric vehicles (EVs) are becoming more popular, intelligent, data-driven maintenance is essential. Smart maintenance is the only way to guarantee a longer lifespan, reliability, and safety. A concept for e-mobility system maintenance prediction is the first thing that comes to mind in this paper. The focus is primarily on the three subsystems: heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC), thermal management, and lithium-ion battery condition. The design involves digital twin technologies and transfer learning. These techniques, which are able to achieve accurate remaining useful life (RUL) estimation and early fault detection even in the case of limited labeled data, are combined. Real-time sensor data is used together with pretrained models to allow learning to be carried out across different EV configurations. As a result, the system becomes more generalizable and flexible. The Internet of Things (IoT)-based setup is responsible for the continuous monitoring of the plant. Simultaneously, the digital twins are leveraged to track and predict battery, thermal, and HVAC system conditions. The foremost methods are deep learning, model predictive control (MPC), and adaptive thermal diagnostics. Moreover, the system is designed for the cloud and can be scaled up or down. Transfer learning is used to lessen the differences between the various domains. System intelligence is also improved with digital twins-based cyber-physical synchronization. All these enhance the reliability and efficiency of future EV maintenance systems.

title: The research on the application of computer vision based on depth camera in robotic arm control
authors: Shaojun Xiong, Xiangtao Qi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the rapid development of of artificial intelligence and automation, computer vision (CV) and deep learning (DL) have become integral technologies in robotic arm control. Traditional robotic systems rely on pre-programmed trajectories and sensor feedback, which limits their adaptability and efficiency in dynamic environments. In contrast, the fusion of vision-based perception and deep neural networks has enabled robotic manipulators to achieve human-like visual cognition and autonomous decision-making. This paper studies the basic principles and applications of deep learning algorithm and robotic arm system, and proposes a set of feasible algorithmic solutions for the visual perception and target recognition problems of the robotic arm camera. First, the camera is calibrated to ensure the imaging quality and accuracy of the camera-captured photos. The YOLOv8 target detection algorithm is used to accurately identify the target object, and the robotic arm PID control algorithm is used to achieve precise motion control of the robotic arm to grasp the target object. Experimental results show that the algorithm can achieve fast and accurate recognition of the target object, and the robotic arm can accurately control the motion according to the target position.

title: Predictive Maintenance Model and System Integration for Warehouse Equipment Based on Digital Twins and Deep Learning
authors: Ruizhong Kong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper addresses the current challenges of warehouse equipment operation, such as the difficulty in timely fault detection and the inefficiency and high cost of traditional scheduled maintenance. This paper proposes a predictive maintenance model and system integration approach based on digital twins (DT) and deep learning (DL). First, a digital twin of the warehouse equipment is constructed, mapping key operational data such as temperature, vibration, and current collected by sensors into a virtual model in real time, enabling dynamic interaction between the physical entity and the virtual space. Second, a deep learning architecture combining convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and long short-term memory (LSTM) is used to extract features and model health status from multi-source time series data. Transfer learning is then used to optimize the modeling process to address the sample shortage issue across different equipment. Finally, anomaly detection and remaining life prediction modules are integrated to achieve a closed-loop monitoring, analysis, and decision-making process at the system level. Experimental results, based on operational data from 1,000 units, show that failure predictions consistently approach or reach 72 hours in advance, with a prediction accuracy of 97%. The average maintenance cost is approximately 12.3k¥. This model not only improves the operational reliability and safety of warehouse equipment but also enables the implementation of predictive maintenance.

title: Road Safety for Everyone: An AI-enabled Pothole Detection System
authors: Adebayo Bamitale, Pardeep Kumar, Carsten Maple, Abdullah Khalid, Martin Duffy, Andrew Lambert
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Camera systems have evolved from passive imaging tools into active sensing platforms capable of supporting automated roadway condition assessment. This paper presents an AI-enabled, camera-centric framework for pothole detection that leverages consumer mobile devices to capture geo-contextualised image data using EXIF metadata. Our method transforms smartphones into distributed infrastructure-monitoring nodes by combining real-time pothole detection with geospatial analytics and automated reporting. The proposed system follows a five-stage computational pipeline: (1) image capture using mobile cameras with GPS-tagged EXIF metadata; (2) object validation via a YOLO-based deep learning classifier; (3) metadata extraction to pair detections with geolocation and timestamp; (4) spatial analysis using Haversine-based distance calculations and clustering to identify localised road degradation; and (5) reporting with maintenance summaries generated from cluster statistics using a large language model (LLM). Preliminary results from a prototype implementation indicate that consumer-grade devices, when combined with embedded metadata and real-time computer vision, can reduce the cost and latency of municipal road monitoring while enabling scalable, crowdsourced data acquisition. The integration of machine-learning-based detection, geospatial clustering, and LLM-driven interpretation highlights the potential for emerging consumer technologies to support data-driven roadway maintenance and planning.

title: Distributed Photovoltaic Electricity Generation Prediction Considering Spatiotemporal Complementarity Characteristics
authors: Nan Teng, Wei Li, Hongbing Wu, Zhe Cao, Ying Su, Honggang Qu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: With the rapid development of distributed photovoltaic, its consumption problem has become increasingly prominent. Starting from the prediction of distributed photovoltaic electricity generation, the influencing factors related to electricity generation are explored. And based on deep learning networks, the prediction of time series electricity generation is carried out to screen out prediction methods with high accuracy; Considering the spatial complementary characteristics of different distributed photovoltaic power generation systems, the improvement level of prediction accuracy by different prediction strategies is quantified, which can provide important support for accurate prediction and control of regional distributed photovoltaic power generation systems.

title: Hyperspherical Representation Learning of Axial Data via Axial VAEs with Watson Distribution
authors: Zhiwen Luo, Wentao Fan, Manar Amayri, Nizar Bouguila
publish: ACM Transactions on Knowledge Discovery from Data
abstract: None

authors: Ana Cavalcanti, Robert M. Hierons

abstract: None

title: Research on Software Reliability Prediction Methods for Intelligent Measurement Equipment
authors: Bensong Zhang, Jinyang Du, Xin Rong, Leping Zhang, Hanfang Ye
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Aiming at the reliability prediction challenges of intelligent measurement equipment software in dynamic update environments, this study proposes a prediction method integrating multi-model entropy weight optimization and improved particle filtering. By constructing an evaluation framework encompassing five categories (12 models including NHPP and Markov classes), the entropy weight method screens the top-five model combination, reducing prediction errors by 36.0% (RMSE) and 47.5% (MAPE) under dynamic conditions compared to single models. A local Parzen window state space decomposition and adaptive resampling strategy are designed, reducing RMSE to 9.3 hours and MAPE to 6.2%, with a single prediction time of 28ms. A full-life-cycle multi-metric monitoring framework is proposed, improving detection sensitivity by 7.69 percentage points and reducing false alarm rates by 15.53 percentage points in dynamic adaptability scenarios with a 200% fault rate surge. Validated on 220 fault data from smart meter software frequent updates, the method achieves 92.4% prediction accuracy. The achievements have been applied to the management platform of S Electric Power Company, supporting the establishment of a unified testing standard system.

title: Path Planning for Substation Cable Laying Based on A-Star Algorithm
authors: Xiaofan Song, Chen Cheng, Jinfeng Zhang, Kai Li, Wenjie Xue, Shuangqing Bai, Qi Li
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To address the challenges of low efficiency, poor utilization rate, and significant errors in traditional two-dimensional design for transformer substation cable layout, this paper proposes a three-dimensional digital design method for cable laying based on the A-star algorithm, addressing issues such as low efficiency in traditional two-dimensional design, low practical utilization during construction, and significant errors in the design of substation cable laying. First, a mathematical model of the cable laying space is constructed using a three-dimensional grid method. Next, following the actual construction process specifications, the paper designs cable laying rules while ensuring safety, and takes into account actual economic viability and construction efficiency to establish a layout constraint mathematical model. Then, by combining practical cable laying conditions, a heuristic function is designed, and the A -star algorithm is applied to search for the optimal cable laying path. Finally, through simulation experiments, it is verified that the proposed method can achieve collision-free and non-crossing path planning in complex cable laying environments, meeting cable laying specification requirements, improving the efficiency of cable laying design.

title: Performance Benchmarking of DC-DC Converter Controllers for Hydrogen Electrolyzer Load
authors: Tejaswi M. Timsani, Animesh Kumar Sahoo, Pratyasa Bhui
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Hydrogen electrolyzers (HEs) are considered as flexible loads in the modern power systems that can provide several grid ancillary services. They require multiple stages of power conversion for their grid integration to operate as a low voltage, high current load. This requires the right choice of power electronic converters and digital controllers. Dual active bridge (DAB) dc-dc converter has been proven as promising solution to be used for grid integration of HE load due to inherent galvanic isolation and high voltage ratio conversions. The digital controller for the DAB converter can be linear or non-linear. This paper provides a comparative study among three different output voltage controllers for the DAB converters when used for the HE load. They are: a) proportional plus integral (PI) control, b) fast integral terminal sliding mode control (FITSMC), and c) backstepping control (BSC). All these controllers along with a 500 kW DAB converter are implemented using MATLAB/Simulink. HE load is modelled as its dynamic electrical equivalent circuit. Test scenarios like input voltage disturbances and output voltage tracking are considered. During these events the overshoots and settling times are compared for all the three types of controllers considered. It is observed that FITSMC and BSC offer similar responses while the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P I}$</tex> controller is affected the most during input voltage disturbances.

title: An Intelligent RL-Enable Energy Aware Routing for Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Sunandita Debnath, Kumar Vishant, Shashank Shekhar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Wireless sensor networks face significant challenges due to limited battery power, which restricts network lifetime. Traditional protocols such as LEACH and PEGASIS extend lifetime to some extent but lack adaptability under dynamic network conditions. Heuristic methods such as EER-RL offer improvements, yet they still strive to capture optimal long-term strategies. This research work proposes an EER-DQN, an energy-efficient routing protocol that formulates cluster head selection as a deep reinforcement learning problem to maximize network lifetime in energy-constrained network environments. The DQN model uses real-time network states (energy, distance, hop count) and employs experience replay with a fixed target network to ensure stable learning. Simulation results show that EER-DQN significantly outperforms existing protocols, extending the time to first node failure by approximately 275% compared to LEACH, 25% compared to PEGASIS, and 15% compared to EER-RL. Notably, by round 1000, while LEACH has consumed more than 75% of total network energy and PEGASIS around 25%, EER-DQN maintains a controlled consumption rate comparable to EER-RL, demonstrating that the DQN learns more effective load-balancing and node-preservation strategies to maximize overall network lifetime.

title: A Wavelet Transform Approach for Short-Circuit Fault Diagnosis in Smart Transformer-Based Meshed Hybrid Microgrids
authors: Lakshmi Kant Rao, Sahil Gaurav, Chandan Kumar
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a fault detection and classification strategy based on the wavelet transform (WT), utilizing current signals in smart transformer (ST)-based hybrid microgrids. The Haar wavelet is chosen as the mother wavelet function to extract features from current signals due to its effectiveness in capturing critical fault characteristics. Fault detection is performed by identifying the maximum values of the detail coefficients of the AC currents, while fault classification is achieved with the help of the maximum detail coefficients of the three-phase AC currents along with the ground current. The proposed method demonstrates reliable performance under various fault scenarios, including different fault types, fault location, fault resistances, and ground resistances. This approach is effective in detecting both symmetrical and unsymmetrical faults, ensuring accurate classification across diverse fault scenarios. The proposed method is validated on an ST-based LV meshed microgrid model developed in the MATLAB/Simulink environment.

title: RFID–Edge–Federated Learning Decision Support for Retired Power Equipment: Recovery, Dismantling, and Recycling in the Circular Economy
authors: Weijian Yang, Dongyu Zeng, Yufeng Yang, Quanyi Li, Zechun Su
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the continuous expansion and upgrading of global power infrastructure, a large number of power equipment units are entering the retirement phase. Traditional scrap disposal models face challenges such as information opacity, low efficiency, and insufficient resource recovery rates. This study proposes an intelligent decision support system based on Radio Frequency Identification-Edge Computing-Federated Learning fusion architecture to address circular economy decision-making for retired power equipment. The system constructs a three-layer architecture: the RFID perception layer achieves equipment lifecycle information collection through passive tags, the edge computing layer deployed at recycling sites completes localized data processing and preliminary decision-making, and the federated learning decision layer coordinates multi-site collaborative training of global models. The system innovatively integrates differential privacy protection mechanisms into the Federated Averaging (FedAvg) algorithm, transmitting only model parameters rather than raw data, effectively addressing data silos and privacy leakage issues faced by traditional methods. Experimental validation is conducted based on a real dataset of 5000 units covering transformers, circuit breakers, and switchgear. Results demonstrate that the system achieves a decision accuracy of 94.3%, improving upon traditional centralized methods by 4.6 percentage points and single edge node methods by 9.1 percentage points. System processing latency is reduced to 45 milliseconds and response time shortened to 0.8 seconds, representing a 75% reduction compared to traditional methods. Regarding communication overhead, cumulative communication volume for 100 training rounds is only 230MB, representing an 85.4% reduction compared to centralized methods. Economic benefit evaluation shows that recycling sites using this system achieve an average revenue increase of 18.5% and resource recovery rate improvement of 12.3%, with decision scheme execution success rate reaching 91.2%. This study provides a feasible technical pathway for retired power equipment circular economy management, holding significant importance for promoting intelligent transformation and sustainable development in the power industry.

title: Comparative Evaluation of DNNs and Template Matching for GUI Validation on Android Devices
authors: Abda Myrria, Alice Castro, Caique Muniz, Heryck Barbosa, André Silva, Luiz Cordovil-Jr, Roger Porty, Thiago Souto
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into software engineering has revolutionized the testing and validation processes for Graphical User Interfaces (GUIs), particularly in enhancing productivity, maintainability, scalability, security, and quality assurance (QA). This paper investigates the application of Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) to automate GUI testing within Android devices using computer vision algorithms. Specifically, we focus on automating the Home Host Test Case validation process from the Google Mobile Services Test Suite, a critical component in certifying Android devices for running proprietary Google applications. Our study evaluates the effectiveness of two DNN models — Faster R-CNN and SSD — in detecting objects within screenshots captured during testing, comparing their performance against the traditional Template Matching method. The comparative analysis highlights the strengths and limitations of each approach, considering the standardized nature of objects in these GUI tests. Through experimental evaluations, this research demonstrates how AI-driven automation can significantly enhance the efficiency and reliability of software QA processes for Android devices by reducing manual intervention and accelerating development cycles. Our findings underscore the transformative potential of DNNs in automating GUI validation tasks, contributing to a more agile and high-quality software development environment.

title: Invited: Addressing Electromigration Challenges in 3D Integrated Circuit (3DIC) Wafer-On-Wafer Technology: EM analysis in 3DIC – status and challenges
authors: Ingo Kühn, Ivan Valentinov Petkov
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Symposium on Physical Design
abstract: None

title: SD-Net: A Synergistic Dual-Branch Network for Fine-Grained Building Extraction
authors: Weidong Tao, Yanting Li, Zunfang Liu, Yidan Zhao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Building footprint extraction from high-resolution remote sensing images (RSIs) is essential for applications such as urban planning, disaster assessment, and infrastructure management. Existing methods often struggle to maintain semantic consistency while precisely delineating boundaries due to complex backgrounds and variations in building scale and morphology, leading to fractured contours, blurred edges, or mis-segmentation. To address these challenges, a Synergistic Dual-Branch Network (SD-Net) is proposed, which jointly models semantic and boundary information via a shared encoder with separate semantic and boundary decoders constrained by a joint loss, with gradient detachment to stabilize multi-task optimization.A multi-scale interaction module, the Interactive Dual-LKA Block (ID-LKA), enhances context modeling for buildings of varying scales. Furthermore, a lightweight Attention Refinement Cascade Module (ARCM) refines high-level features by adaptively selecting discriminative channel and spatial information. Experiments on multiple public datasets demonstrate that SD-Net outperforms mainstream segmentation models in mean Intersection over Union (mIoU) and F1-score (F1) and achieves more stable boundary integrity. Ablation studies validate the contribution of each module, confirming the method's effectiveness and efficiency for resource-constrained automatic building extraction tasks.

title: Deep Learning for Channel Estimation in RIS-NOMA-assisted THz Communications over Generalized Fading Channels
authors: Tooba Khan, Adnan A. Cheema, Gökhan Seçinti, Berk Canberk, Trung Q. Duong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: This paper investigates the channel estimation problem in a multiple-input single-output (MISO) reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA)-assisted terahertz (THz) communication system where users experience mobility and varying small-scale fading characterized by the generalized α − μ distribution. Reliable channel estimation becomes challenging when passive RIS elements and superimposed NOMA signals operate under high attenuation of THz band. We propose a novel deep learning framework, THz RIS-NOMA Channel Estimation (TRiNCE), for cascaded channel estimation. TRiNCE is designed as a GRU-based conditional Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network with Gradient Penalty (cWGAN-GP). TRiNCE performance is evaluated under various α − μ fading conditions, the number of RIS elements, NOMA power allocation factors, and the number of BS antennas. The model achieves an R2 score of 0.85 under Rayleigh fading and up to 0.95 under milder α–μ conditions. It further demonstrates strong robustness, maintaining high estimation accuracy with less than 2% performance variation across different RIS sizes and NOMA power allocation factors, and only ∼ 7% degradation when the number of BS antennas increases from 1 to 5. Results show that TRiNCE not only provides accurate and reliable channel estimation across all tested network configurations but also significantly outperforms convolutional neural network (CNN), long short-term memory (LSTM), and gated recurrent unit (GRU) baseline models while requiring substantially fewer trainable parameters. This establishes TRiNCE as a computationally efficient and effective solution for channel estimation in RIS-NOMA-assisted THz communication systems.

title: The Meta Distribution of the SINR in Joint Communication and Sensing Networks
authors: Jiaqi Zhu, Chenyuan Feng, Giovanni Geraci, Chenxi Liu, Meng Zhang, Howard H. Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Joint Communication and Sensing (JCAS) has emerged as a key enabler for sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks by providing a unified platform for communication and sensing functionalities. In this paper, we present a novel mathematical framework for evaluating the performance of JCAS wireless networks, utilizing stochastic geometry as an analytical tool. Specifically, we derive closed-form expressions for the JCAS signal-to-noise-plus-interference ratio (SINR) meta distribution, which enables a fine-grained quantification of individual user or radar performance beyond conventional spatially averaged SINR coverage metrics. The analytical results, validated through simulations, demonstrate how the JCAS SINR meta distribution is affected by antenna patterns, blockages, and network deployment density. The results reveal important design insights: network densification significantly enhances sensing performance, whereas communication performance does not necessarily improve monotonically with base station density, and large antenna arrays are beneficial for joint communication and sensing. These findings provide fundamental guidelines for the design and deployment of future JCAS-enabled wireless networks.

title: GridFusionX: Network-Aware Probabilistic Forecasting for Multi-Regional Power Systems
authors: Quoc Bao Phan, Abdulrahman Takiddin, Gelli Ravikumar, Olugbenga Moses Anubi, Tuy Tan Nguyen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Smart grid networks exhibit complex spatial–temporal dependencies where regional nodes are interconnected through electrical transmission, economic coupling, and shared meteorological patterns. Traditional forecasting methods model regions independently, neglecting network topology and spatial correlations that govern system-level behavior. Graph neural networks capture spatial structure but provide only deterministic forecasts, while probabilistic transformers quantify uncertainty yet ignore network topology, preventing risk-aware coordination across interconnected systems. This paper presents GridFusionX, a multimodal transformer model that resolves this tension through the oretically grounded uncertainty quantification in graph-structured systems. We introduce dual-head transformer encoders with asymptotic calibration guarantees, precision-weighted fusion that adapts to spatially varying data reliability, and tight bounds for uncertainty propagation across networked sequences. Unlike prior approaches that sacrifice either spatial awareness or probabilistic rigor, GridFusionX achieves both through convergence-rate guarantees for network-aware uncertainty estimation, optimal precision weighting under modality independence conditions, and calibrated confidence intervals for operational decision making. Experiments on ten interconnected European regions demon strate 4.8–55.9% accuracy improvements and 39.5–66.1% reductions in reserve sizing compared to deterministic graph and topology-agnostic probabilistic baselines, achieving 98.4±0.2% reliability with 90.0±1.6% prediction interval coverage. GridFusionX establishes a theoretically grounded framework for probabilistic forecasting in networked infrastructure.

title: Multi-Scale Convolutional Attention Model for GNSS Jamming Recognition
authors: Danyal Hussain Shah, Hailiang Xiong, Fen He, Wenli Li, Bo He, Gangqiang Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) functionality is highly vulnerable to jamming, which disrupts signals. Existing datasets and models for jamming classification are inefficient for low-power GNSS jamming. Additionally, current machine and deep learning models struggle to balance accuracy with efficiency. To address this, we develop the Multi-Featured GNSS Jamming Dataset (MFGJD), which includes 16 distinct jamming types along with a clean GNSS signal. Building on MFGJD, we propose the Multi-Scale Convolutional Attention Model (MSCAM), designed to operate in the precorrelation phase using spectrograms as input. MSCAM integrates channel, spatial, and temporal features, enabling accurate and robust jamming classification. Experimental results show that MSCAM outperforms current models, achieving 96.95% accuracy with 0.09 million parameters and 0.30 milliseconds inference time per sample, offering high efficiency and robustness.

title: Iterative Token Refinement for Remote Sensing Image Super-Resolution
authors: Songze Tang, Le Sun
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Recent advances in remote sensing image super-resolution (RSISR) have leveraged transformer architectures to achieve state-of-the-art results. Nevertheless, most existing transformer-based approaches treat the token sequence as a unified global context, which often fail to preserve critical local structures and cross-region semantics. To address these issues, we propose an iterative transformer framework that progressively refines visual tokens with contextual awareness. Specifically, we initially represent an input image as discrete tokens based on vector quantized (VQ)-GAN. Then, building upon this tokenized representation, we propose a predictor–evaluator mechanism that iteratively refines the high-resolution (HR) token sequence. In each iteration, the predictor generates all HR tokens and retains those with the highest confidence estimates, while masking the remainder for subsequent refinement. Concurrently, the evaluator, which is trained to assess the reliability of each token, guides the selection and regeneration of tokens to enable progressive reconstruction. To preserve structural consistency with the low-resolution (LR) input, we further introduce a Consistency-aware LR-conditioned Decoder module, which leverages the LR image to regulate the HR tokens. Experiments show that our method achieves competitive performance on multiple remote sensing benchmarks.

title: Inter-Agent Beliefs for Learning to Communicate in Large-Scale Multi-Robot Visual Object Search_supp2-3677944.mp4

title: Integrating Historical Rules and Curriculum-Enhanced Embeddings for Temporal Knowledge Graph Forecasting
authors: Weizhou Wang, Aichun Zhu, Tianyuan Hu, Shiping Wang, Yuanfei Dai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Temporal knowledge graph forecasting (TKGF) has become a crucial tool for predicting future facts based on temporal knowledge graphs. Traditional methods face inherent limitations: rule-based reasoning approaches are restricted to exploring events that are related to historical rules and fail to fully utilize global graph information. In contrast, although embedding-based methods have the ability to capture global graph information through vector representations, they struggle to effectively incorporate historical rules. To address these issues, this article proposes a novel framework that combines historical rule-based reasoning with embedding-based prediction enhanced by curriculum learning (CL) to predict unknown events. The framework consists of two complementary modes: the rule mode, which extracts temporal rules through time-filtered weighted sampling and applies them for prediction, and the embedding mode, which progressively represents entities, relations, and time as vectors from simple to complex through CL and generates predictions based on these embedding vectors. The framework effectively integrates rules with the entire graph information by combining the predictions from two models, resulting in a more comprehensive prediction.
