Extensive experiments on multiple public datasets demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches in terms of performance, validating the effectiveness of its hybrid approach, and highlighting the significant potential of CL in TKGF.

title: LMDA: Lightweight Multi-authority CP-ABE Scheme based on Dynamic Attributes for System Wide Information Management
authors: Lizhe Zhang, Yiao Ma, Zhijun Wu, Kenian Wang, Ruiqi Li
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The System Wide Information Management (SWIM) system serves as an advanced platform for managing and sharing diverse aviation data, such as aeronautical, flight, and meteorological information. With the exponential growth of aviation data, ensuring data security and privacy within SWIM has become increasingly important. Ciphertext-Policy Attribute-Based Encryption (CP-ABE) is widely regarded as an effective cryptographic technique for ensuring secure and private data access. However, traditional CP-ABE schemes rely on computationally expensive bilinear pairings, which are unsuitable for resource-constrained aviation Internet of Things application scenarios in SWIM, such as Aircraft Access to SWIM. To address this limitation, we propose a lightweight multi-authority CP-ABE scheme based on dynamic attributes. This scheme replaces bilinear pairing operations with efficient scalar multiplication on elliptic curve points and outsources most decryption operations to outsourcing decryption nodes, thereby significantly reducing computational overhead. In addition, this scheme introduces a dynamic attribute mechanism to support fine-grained access control, and outsources data verification and attribute revocation to the blockchain to ensure data integrity. Security analysis shows that our scheme achieves indistinguishability under adaptive chosen-ciphertext attacks and is resistant to collusion attacks. Experimental evaluations further demonstrate that our scheme significantly enhances computational and communication efficiency.

title: Marginal Effect-Driven Participant Selection With Local Differential Privacy for Mobile Crowdsensing_supp1-3676248.pdf
authors: Mengzhe Tian
publish: N/A
abstract: In prior research, spatial coverage has been the primary metric for assessing the quality of mobile network data, with various participant selection strategies developed for data brokers operating within budget constraints. This paper introduces a novel data participant service quality model that prioritizes participant privacy while ensuring creditworthiness and participation willingness. This paper proposes a selection strategy for mobile network data participants that operates under the principles of local differential privacy, designed to maximize the marginal gain under a cost budget constraint. Drawing upon the principle of marginal gains from economics, our strategy advocates for the selection of participants whose marginal gains exceed their associated costs, thereby optimizing the selection process based on relative marginal costs. This paper formalizes this approach as the marginal problem of data participant selection in mobile network data markets, constructing a model and providing theoretical analysis to establish its NP-hard. To address this problem, we develop the Random Response Adaptive Algorithm (RRAA), designed to select participant groups that maximize mobile network data gains. Extensive experiments on both real and simulated datasets demonstrate the algorithm's efficiency and effectiveness. Additionally, to safeguard participant privacy, we propose the Random Response Self-Adaptive Algorithm (RRSAA). Our experimental evaluation on real datasets not only validates the algorithm's privacy-preserving capabilities but also elucidates the impact of privacy preservation parameters on marginal gains.

title: Scalable and Efficient Reinforcement Learning for Virtual Machine Rescheduling in Cloud Data Centers_supp1-3674891.pdf
authors: Xianzhong Ding
publish: N/A
abstract: Managing a vast number of virtual machines (VMs) efficiently is a critical challenge in modern large-scale data centers. The continuous creation and termination of VMs lead to resource fragmentation across physical machines (PMs), necessitating periodic VM rescheduling to optimize resource utilization. Despite its significance, VM rescheduling has received limited attention in the literature. A key challenge is that, unlike conventional combinatorial optimization problems, the efficiency of rescheduling algorithms is heavily impacted by inference time, as VM states evolve dynamically during execution. This scalability bottleneck hampers existing methods. To address this, we propose VMR <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> L, a reinforcement learning framework tailored for VM rescheduling. VMR <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> L integrates a two-stage decision-making process to accommodate complex operational constraints, a feature extraction mechanism that captures critical relational information for rescheduling, and a risk-aware evaluation strategy that enables users to balance execution speed and rescheduling accuracy. Extensive experiments using real-world data from a production-scale data center demonstrate that VMR <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> L achieves near-optimal performance while reducing inference time to a matter of seconds. To facilitate reproducibility, we provide access to our implementation and datasets.

title: Using AI and Corpus-Based Tools for Teaching Terminological Collocation in the Field of Aerospace Engineering
authors: Andrey S. Korzin
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: This study explores an integrated methodology for teaching terminological collocations in English for Specific Purposes (ESP) in the domain of aerospace engineering. A 1-million-word Aerospace Corpus, compiled from the MDPI Aerospace journal, was examined using Sketch Engine to identify high-value adjective + orbit collocations (e.g., Low Earth orbit, circular orbit, prograde release orbit, off-nominal orbit). Collocational strength was quantified using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta P$</tex> to distinguish conceptually bound terms (e.g., parking orbit, halo orbit) from operationally contrastive descriptions (e.g., circular orbit, elliptical orbit). These data were then used to select and prioritise targets for instruction, supporting claims that terminological collocations are central to conceptual understanding in specialised discourse and should be taught as units rather than as isolated words. Instructional tasks were sequenced according to Bloom's revised taxonomy so that learners first identify and interpret collocations, then apply them in controlled production, and ultimately justify orbital design choices and produce mission-style discourse using these terms. AI (ChatGPT) was incorporated as a complementary tool to generate draft exercises (e.g., verbcollocation gap-fill items, definition matching tasks, mission briefing prompts) and B2-level explanations of technical orbit types, while instructors curated output for technical accuracy, register, and cognitive level. The result is a replicable workflow in which (i) the corpus determines what to teach, (ii) Bloom's taxonomy structures how to teach it, and (iii) AI expands how much material can be produced efficiently without replacing expert judgment.

title: Recent Advances in Battery Management System Topologies for Electric Vehicles
authors: Garima Singh, Deepak Chaturvedi, Deepak Verma, Prashant Singh Rathore
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The Battery Management System (BMS) serves as the core supervisory layer that safeguards the performance, safety, and longevity of electric vehicle (EV) battery packs. This paper presents an in-depth review of contemporary BMS architectures, tracing their progression from conventional centralized and modular designs to distributed, decentralized, and emerging wireless frameworks. Forty-one recent approaches reported between 2021 and 2025 are analyzed and fifteen compared in terms of scalability, reliability, communication latency, balancing efficiency, cost, and state-ofcharge (SoC) estimation accuracy. Quantitative data compiled from the reviewed literature indicate that recent systems achieve balancing efficiencies above 90 %, communication delays below 200 ms, and SoC estimation errors typically under 2 %. Beyond architectural comparisons, the paper explores the infusion of intelligence into BMS design through data-driven algorithms and cyber-physical integration. Key advancements include artificial intelligence and machine-learning techniques for SoC and state-of-health <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{SoH})$</tex> prediction, digital-twin platforms for predictive diagnostics, blockchain for secure data exchange, and hybrid wired-wireless configurations aimed at scalable deployment. The discussion also encompasses functional safety (ISO 26262), cybersecurity frameworks (ISO/SAE 21434 and UNECE WP. 29 R155), and industrial implementations such as Tesla's modular BMS and GM's Ultifi wireless platform. The synthesis of these developments highlights both the maturity and the remaining challenges of modern BMS research. Standardization of hybrid topologies, mitigation of wireless interference, integration of edge-AI-based control, and enhanced fault tolerance are identified as emerging priorities toward achieving reliable, intelligent, and failoperational energy management for the next generation of electric vehicles.

title: A Multi-Timescale Optimal Scheduling Strategy for Virtual Power Plants with Coordinated Diverse User-Side Resources
authors: Jun Zhan, Min Luo, Xiaojia Sun, Yang Li, Yuchen Lai, Ting Huang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: To address the challenges of stochastic sourceload variations and electricity market price volatility faced by Virtual Power Plants (VPPs) aggregating diverse user-side resources, as well as to minimize scheduling deviations and operational risks in multi-market participation, this paper proposes a multi-timescale optimal scheduling strategy leveraging synergistic coordination of user-side resources. Firstly, a two-stage day-ahead (DA) and real-time (RT) scheduling framework is established. In the DA stage, scenario analysis is employed to model uncertainties in wind/solar power output and electricity prices. A reward-penalty ladder-type carbon trading mechanism is introduced to economically maximize low-carbon benefits, forming a preliminary operating plan. In the RT stage, Information Gap Decision Theory (IGDT) is adopted to dynamically adjust the DA schedule under deep uncertainties arising from prediction errors. This model provides decision-makers with two operational strategies-Risk-Averse (RA) and Opportunity-Seeking (OS)-to respond to volatile market conditions. Case studies validate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy. Results demonstrate that the approach fully leverages the complementary advantages of multi-source user-side resources to mitigate uncertaintyinduced risks through coordinated multi-timescale optimization. It enhances VPP profitability in joint energy and ancillary service markets while ensuring low-carbon operation, offering operators flexible and economically viable decision support.

title: Research on the Balance of Public Interests and Optimization of Energy Efficiency Governance in AI Collaborative Control of New Energy Microgrids in the Greater Bay Area
authors: Zhipeng Zhuang, Fachen Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Under the accelerated digital transformation and improved energy governance in the Greater Bay Area (GBA), this study seeks to balance microgrid stability and operational efficiency, providing technical support for enhancing regional energy governance efficiency. It first analyzes the inertia and frequency support capabilities of grid-forming energy storage. Then, aligned with GBA’s needs for efficient energy dispatching and resource allocation, it clarifies microgrid optimization goals, builds a multi-dimensional constraint system (covering power balance, equipment operation, system inertia, and reserve capacity), and proposes a microgrid optimal dispatching strategy (considering grid-forming energy storage support) using the nonlinear solver in Optimization Toolbox. This strategy informs the "technical scheme formulation - efficiency evaluation - process optimization" link in GBA’s energy governance administrative processes. To test the strategy’s effectiveness, kernel density estimation and Copula function are used to construct wind-solar power joint probability distribution, and K-means clustering generates typical new energy scenarios. Results show the method fully leverages grid-forming energy storage, improves new energy consumption, provides key indicators for GBA energy governance efficiency evaluation, and supports optimizing related administrative processes (e.g., technical scheme review).

title: Early Detection of Plant Diseases Using IoT Sensors and Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Vishal Das, Jossy George, Pawan Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Agriculture is one of the most important and necessitates of the world. This paper is a study to detect plant diseases using IoT sensors and ML Algorithms for early detection of plant diseases using IoT sensors and machine learning algorithms. A primary dataset from Indian Agricultural Research Institute (ICAR) was used for the research. The dataset comprised the following features: temperature, humidity, soil moisture, leaf wetness, and dew point. Five different machine learning algorithms were explored for the implementation: Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, CatBoost, and LightGBM. Upon comparative analysis, it was found that the LightBGM model performed the best with an accuracy of 93.4 % using cross-validation, implying remarkable performance for real-time plant disease monitoring.

title: Efficacy of a Conventional Kalman Filter for Side-Channel-Noise (SNI) Mitigation in an SST-Based Power System
authors: Luiz Fernando M. Arruda, Debotrinya Sur, Uzair Asif, Harsha V. R. Modugu, Mohammad Shadmand, Sudip K. Mazumder
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Solid-state transformers (SSTs) enhance smart grid control and flexibility but are vulnerable to side-channel noise intrusion (SNI) attacks, which disrupts voltage regulation and stability. This paper outlines a conventional Kalman filterbased SNI mitigation, which operates in continuous or adaptive mode, with an intrusion detection system (IDS) detecting attacks and switching to the Kalman-filter-based estimation within 12 ms. Implementation requires only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 \mu \mathrm{s}$</tex> per control cycle, enabling real-time operation without compromising dynamic response. Experimental results on an SST and simulations on a 14-bus SST-based power system demonstrate effective mitigation of SNI, preserving waveform integrity and ensuring stable operation. Our case study shows that Kalman filtering yields a practical and computationally efficient solution for enhancing SST cyber-resilience in modern smart grids.

title: Leveraging Deep Convolutional and Transformer Networks for Robust Leukemia Classification in Blood Smear Images
authors: Nivedaa S U, Madhumathi Ramasamy, Asha Chandola-Saklani
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Precise and timely diagnosis of Leukemia, especially Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL), is vital for patient survival and the progression of hematological treatment. This work presents the study of Deep Learning (DL) architectures for automating the identification of Leukemia. The distinct approaches evaluated are EfficientNet-B5, a compoundscaled Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) optimized for efficiency, InceptionV3 coupled with Vision Transformer (ViT), by integrating extraction of features with global self-attention modules, and InceptionV3 with Swin Transformer, leveraging hierarchical shifted-window attention for multi-scale contextual modelling. The experimental analysis demonstrates a promising result with InceptionV3-Swin achieving 98.5% accuracy and InceptionV3-ViT reaching 98.16%, outperforming EfficientNetB5 with an accuracy of 97.4 %. These results show significant improvements over existing approach like the Swin Transformer (ST) with Momentum Contrast, which achieved an accuracy of 92.5 %. The comparative analysis suggests that by combining the best of deep convolutional neural networks and transformers can help to develop an end-to-end and scalable solution that automates Leukemia diagnosis.

title: Prediction and Optimization of Hospital Bed Allocation Using Disease Pattern
authors: Abhimanya S, Harini Nair S, R. Manimegalai, Noor Mahammad SK
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Optimal allocation of hospital beds is an indispensable aspect of healthcare resource management and directly affects both organizational efficacy and patient care outcomes. The endemic uncertainty of disease incidence, combined with seasonality in admissions, is most likely to cause a mismatch between the supply and demand of bed capacities. In this paper, an end-to-end machine learning-based methodology is proposed that integrates temporal feature engineering, predictive modeling, and disease trend analysis for hospital bed allocation to solve the above-mentioned problem. The data used includes patient admissions according to the type of disease and ward assigned, month, and day. Pre-processing at the stage of obtaining the fine-grained temporal dynamics involves assigning numerical values to temporal variables, encoding months to ordered categories, calculating season groupings, and finally computing the day-of-the-year index. The categorical features of the type of ward and type of disease are to be label-encoded in order to make them suitable for machine learning algorithms. Extensive explorative data analysis was performed using sophisticated visualizations for structural pattern findings on line plots of monthly disease progress, stacked bar charts forward distribution, area plots based on incidence, and heatmaps for disease-ward co-occurrence. All these different visualization techniques indicated that strong relations between the disease prevalence and seasonal effects existed, while an imbalance in patient distribution within distributions specific for each ward called for prediction allocation strategies. Both group and individual learning techniques were used for predictive modeling. Gradient boosting models, such as XGBoost, can learn nonlinear relationships and thus were employed here. For improving generalisability, a stacked ensemble of XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost using ElasticNet as the meta-learner was developed. This combined ensemble outperformed the baselines set by the respective models with its smaller MAE and RMSE and proved stable in making day-to-day admission predictions for a given ward. To provide actionable and understandable results to present to the managers of the hospital, model predictions were combined to give several months and days of bed need forecasts at the ward level visualized as allocation heatmaps. It will provide proactive health planning with an accurate forecast of inpatients and present predictive results in the form of decisionable bed assignment plans. This system is optimal for the distribution of medical resources and reduces risks of crowding by fully utilizing hospitals' operational readiness, fusing temporal feature engineering, ensemble learning, and visualization-driven analysis.

title: A Fault Diagnosis Method for Aircraft Engine Bearings Based on Variational Mode Decomposition and Fused Features
authors: Shuoyan Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: As the core rotating component of an aircraft engine, bearings play a crucial role in ensuring operational reliability. Bearing failures can easily lead to cascading malfunctions, posing a significant threat to flight safety. Therefore, achieving accurate fault diagnosis of bearings is of great engineering importance. To overcome the limitations of traditional diagnostic methods-such as vulnerability to noise under complex operating conditions, insufficient feature extraction, and low classification accuracy in imbalanced datasets-this study proposes a novel approach for aircraft engine bearing fault diagnosis. First, variational mode decomposition (VMD) is applied to adaptively decompose the raw vibration signals and suppress noise, effectively mitigating the issue of background interference that obscures fault-related information. Second, multi-domain feature extraction is performed on the reconstructed signals to comprehensively capture dynamic characteristics across multiple scales and fault types. Subsequently, t-distributed stochastic neighbor embedding (t-SNE) is used to fuse the high-dimensional feature vectors, removing redundant information while preserving key discriminative features to enhance inter-class separability. Finally, an equilibrium optimization-based support vector machine (EO-SVM) is developed as the fault classification model. The equilibrium optimization strategy improves SVM parameter tuning, thereby enhancing overall fault recognition performance. Experimental validation using real aircraft engine bearing fault datasets demonstrates that the proposed method effectively addresses challenges in noise suppression, feature representation, and classification optimization, providing reliable technical support for the health management of aviation equipment.

title: Design and Implementation of Single Carrier Frequency Domain Equalization Based PHY Layer
authors: Nitin Tandon
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Single Carrier Frequency Domain Equalization (SC-FDE) is an efficient alternative to OFDM systems for the devices having power constraints. This work discusses the design of QPSK based physical layer employing SC-FDE scheme and its efficient implementation using Xilinx System Generator Blocksets. Golay sequence based preamble has been utilized for packet detection, symbol timing estimation as well as channel estimation. Rationale for selection of various physical layer parameters have been discussed in this paper. Simulation results ascertains the applicability of SC-FDE for multipath channels.

title: A Deep Learning Based Approach for the Detection of Landslide in Image Data
authors: Anshul Sharma, Ankush Agarwal, Pooja Sharma, Bhanu Pratap Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Landslides are among the most natural hazards that bring about huge damages to property, loss of lives, and adverse impacts on the environment. Risk assessment and timely warning systems depend on landslide detection. This research presents a comprehensive approach for landslide detection based on geographic reasoning, the application of convolutional neural networks (CNNs), and the use of advanced remote sensing technology. Extensive trials on a variety of landslide photos have been used to test the model against conventional machine learning techniques. The results showed that using a CNN model improved landslide detection capabilities and contributed to more successful disaster management strategies by outperforming conventional machine learning techniques in both accuracy and robustness across various environmental conditions. The impact of data fusion and network architecture on landslide detection is yet mainly unexplored. To determine which CNN design would work best, we examined several CNN architectures with various layer combinations. CNN is used in the suggested methodology for feature extraction and classification, and it achieves accuracy in detecting landslide-prone locations from training to testing (94.6% to 90%).

title: Research on User Behavior Prediction Based on XGBoost Model Enhanced by Energy Valley Optimizer
authors: Yaqi Lin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Overfitting and data imbalance pose challenges to traditional machine learning models. In order to improve the prediction accuracy, this study proposes the EVO-XGBoost algorithm, which innovatively integrates the Energy Valley Optimizer (EVO) and the XGBoost model and is interpretable. Before running the algorithm, the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) is first used to address the class imbalance problem, and then the EVO is applied to optimize the hyperparameters of XGBoost to enhance the model’s ability to learn and recognize complex data. The experimental results show that the F1-Score of EVO-XGBoost in the test reaches 0.662, which is 0.014 higher than that of the unoptimized XGBoost model, and significantly improves the accuracy and reliability of the prediction. Finally, SHAP analysis identifies the key features influencing purchase decisions. The research findings provide an effective method for tourism e-commerce companies to accurately predict user behaviors and determine marketing strategies.

title: Evaluating Foundational Depth Models for Zero-shot 3D Box Lifting from 2D detections
authors: Asmaa Loulou, Eren Aydemir, Mustafa Unel
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Monocular 3D object detection offers a low-cost alternative to LiDAR-based perception, but remains fundamentally limited by scale ambiguity and the absence of geometric depth cues in single images. Although recent deep learning approaches have advanced performance, they typically rely on large-scale annotated datasets and complex, fully supervised pipelines. In this work, we introduce a benchmarking framework designed to systematically evaluate zero-shot 3D object lifting from 2D detections using pre-trained monocular depth estimation models. Rather than proposing a new detector, our tool provides a modular and training-free platform that combines off-the-shelf 2D detectors, camera calibration, and existing depth models to project detections into 3D space. Our method does not require labeled 2D or 3D data, enabling flexible deployment across datasets and domains. The modular design allows plug-and-play evaluation of both 2D detectors and monocular depth models, facilitating controlled benchmarking without retraining the entire model. We evaluate our framework on the KITTI benchmark and show that, despite not using any learning or fine-tuning, it achieves competitive 3D detection results, particularly when strong foundation models are used. Our approach not only provides a practical tool for rapid 3D perception in label-scarce scenarios, but also serves as a baseline to analyze the effectiveness of different depth models for object-level spatial reasoning.

title: Balancing Intelligence and Efficiency: Lightweight AI for Intrusion Detection in IIoT
authors: Ahmad Mostafavi, Hossein Fotouhi, Maryam Vahabi, Johan Akerberg
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The proliferation of resource-constrained Internet-of-Things (IoT) and Industrial IoT (IIoT) devices has intensified the need for local, intelligent cybersecurity solutions that do not rely on cloud connectivity. This paper presents a TinyML-based Intrusion Detection System (IDS) framework designed for embedded edge controllers. Two machine-learning IDS models — a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and a Random Forest (RF) — were developed and evaluated on the TON_IoT dataset to identify malicious network traffic. Host-side evaluation established baseline performance, while the float-precision RF model was implemented as firmware and validated using hardware-in-the-loop emulation in the Renode RISC-V environment. The resulting binary occupies only 1.6 MB of Flash memory, achieving above 99% detection accuracy with 448 µs average inference latency. These results demonstrate that fully embedded, real-time intrusion detection is feasible within TinyML memory budgets, supporting IEC-62443 compliant anomaly monitoring for industrial devices.

title: Development of a Human–Computer Interaction System and Machine Learning-based User Satisfaction Prediction
authors: Siyuan Zhang, Ying Tang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Human–Computer Interaction (HCI) has become an important research area in modern society. However, traditional interaction platforms often face problems such as limited adaptability, low interactivity, and lack of personalized responses, which reduce user engagement and learning efficiency. With the rapid development of artificial intelligence and large language models (LLMs), it is now possible to design more intelligent and responsive digital systems. This study presents the development of an intelligent HCI system that integrates LLM-based dialogue generation and digital human technologies for interactive learning. The system enables real-time conversation, scene-based teaching, and automatic evaluation to improve communication. To assess its effectiveness, a questionnaire survey was conducted in more than ten primary schools, and the collected data were analyzed using six machine learning models to predict user satisfaction. Among them, the Support Vector Machine (SVM) achieved the highest accuracy (0.8108) and F1-score (0.8111). The results indicate that combining intelligent dialogue systems with machine learning-based satisfaction prediction can effectively enhance user experience and optimize adaptive interactions.

title: Research on Transformer Interturn Short-Circuit Fault Identification Method Based on Multiphysics Simulation and Swin Transformer Algorithm
authors: Yuan Ma, Wei Zhang, Liangyuan Chen, Wei Huang, Shaoming Pan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Transformer failures account for up to 90% of significant accidents occurring within power systems, while traditional multi-feature diagnostic methods often fail to accurately detect slight inter-turn short-circuit (ITSC) faults due to weak and noise-sensitive signals. To address this issue, this study develops a comprehensive magnetic, circuit, fluid, and thermal Multiphysics coupling model that systematically reveals the quantitative evolution of winding losses under ITSC faults occurring at different locations and severities (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \%-5 {\%}$</tex> shortedturn ratios). The results confirm the effectiveness of winding loss as a highly sensitive fault feature. To overcome the accuracy limitations of conventional neural network algorithms, a Swin Transformer model with a window-based attention mechanism is proposed for fault identification. Results show that winding loss is extremely sensitive to ITSC faults, with its variation rate positively correlated with the shorted-turn ratio. Distinct loss peaks appear at different fault locations, further validating its diagnostic value. The proposed Swin Transformer achieves an average accuracy of 87.7% across six operating states, significantly outperforming classical lightweight convolutional neural networks. This research demonstrates the engineering potential of winding loss as a highly sensitive diagnostic indicator.

title: AI-JiNi: Engineering a Culturally Competent, Empathetic, and Ethical (CCEE) AI Nurse Instructor That e-Trains Student Nurses in Mixed-Reality
authors: Jayfus T. Doswell, Saboor Salaam, Marcus Messam, Brandon Brockington, Arthur Chinery, Cotie Moss, Joan Tilghman
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: The healthcare profession is experiencing a significant nurse shortage, worldwide. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), ~29 million nurses exist with a projected shortage of ~4.5 million nurses by year, 2030. The United States (U.S.) faces a need for 1.2 million new registered nurses (RN)s by 2030 to meet the rising healthcare demands, driven largely by an aging and retiring nursing workforce. Consequently, the U.S. nursing schools recently turned away ~65,766 qualified applications from baccalaureate and graduate nursing programs due to low number of faculty, clinical sites, training space, clinical preceptors, and budget constraints. Similarly, Japan experiences a “painful” nurse labor shortage to care for its aging population requiring ~2.2 million nurses in urban areas by 2025 according to the Japanese Health, Labor, and Welfare Ministry. To ameliorate the aforementioned problems, the artificial intelligent (AI) driven Juxtopia<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> Intelligent Nurse Instructor (JiNi) intervention is engineered with capabilities to e-instruct, eevaluate, and e-assist nurses learn both clinical and cognitive judgment skills. Potentially, JiNi will educate the human nurse learner from novice to expert level faster than human instructors and yield learning and workforce outcomes of higher nurse certification pass rates and job placement, respectively. To accomplish this objective, AI-JiNi is personified to autonomously e-train student nurses with personalized nurse 'andragogy' (i.e., art and science of adult instruction); measurably improving hands-on clinical skill proficiency in both traditional and nontraditional clinical skill labs. Specifically, JiNi delivers multimodal, culturally competent, ethical, and empathetic (CCEE) instruction to student nurses equipped with JiNi Wear. When practicing clinical skills on patient manikins, nurses wear augmented reality (AR) eyewear to hold conversations w/ JiNi and receive holographically projected visual cues during JiNi training. Student nurses also wear Armlets when practicing hands-on clinical skills while JiNi e-evaluates correctly performed skills.

title: Designing Open Online Digital Learning Resources for Science Student: A Multifacet Rasch Model Analysis
authors: Insih Wilujeng, Desy Purwasih, Heru Kuswanto, Sabar Nurohman, Rizki Arumning Tyas, Rizki Zakwandi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: This study aims to evaluate the development of a digital learning platform using the Focus Group Discussion (FGD) method involving five experts in digital learning, learning media, and educational technology. The evaluation employed 33 indicators across nine key dimensions: usability, navigation, graphic design, content, compatibility, loading speed, functionality, accessibility, and language. Each indicator was rated on a fourpoint Likert scale to obtain a comprehensive understanding of the platform's quality and practicality. The FGD data were analysed using the Many-Facet Rasch Model (MFRM) to assess inter-rater reliability, indicator validity, and the difficulty level of each facet. The analysis revealed that most indicators demonstrated strong correlations and fell within optimal thresholds, indicating that the evaluation instrument was both valid and reliable in measuring platform quality. Overall, the experts provided positive assessments of the platform's usability, content, and graphic design, while recommending improvements in loading speed and accessibility.

title: KrishiMitra: An Intelligent IoT–AI Platform for Sustainable Agriculture
authors: Samruddhi Bisen, Nandini Mandhane, Anushka Raut, Ashvini Katore, Apeksha Sakhare
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: This paper introduces KrishiMitra, a bilingual farm-to-consumer and smart-farming platform for Indian smallholders that integrates agronomic decision support with an integrated market to enhance productivity, input use efficiency, and income security. The system consumes soil and climate characteristics (soil type, N-P-K, pH, rainfall, temperature, humidity) from sensors or manual input and uses supervised machine-learning pipelines for ranking appropriate crops with predicted yield ranges, creating crop-specific NPK regimes with basal/top-dress schedules, predicting commodity prices for short and medium horizons, and forecasting per-hectare and total yields for planning of operations; a role-based application layer then implements these results through inventory listing, cart checkout (UPI/COD/pickup/card), and two-sided order tracking with status transitions. Experimental rollouts in representative situations yielded season-specific crop advice with suitably high suitability scores, consistent price paths, confidence-rated yield forecasts, and actionable fertilizer schedules, while functional testing validated smooth ordering and farmer-level controls, showing the feasibility of an end-to-end advisory-commerce process. Through decreased decision uncertainty, restricting nutrient overuse, and supply chain shortening through direct farmer-consumer interfaces, KrishiMitra promotes sustainable input usage, improves farm profitability, and enhances local food resilience through affordable, multilingual digital services.

title: Three-Dimensional Object Localization Method Based on Improved YOLOv8 and Monocular Vision
authors: Yufeng Xie, Zhuohan Liang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the rapid development of Artificial Intelligence and computer vision technologies, three-dimensional (3D) object detection and localization have been widely applied in logistics and other fields. However, existing algorithms struggle to balance accuracy, efficiency, and cost. A novel regular-shaped objects detection and localization method combining improved You Only Look Once version 8 (YOLOv8) with monocular vision is proposed. The method incorporates GhostNet series modules into the backbone network, adopts a Slim-Neck architecture based on Group Shuffle Convolution (GSConv) for feature fusion, and implements a monocular distance measurement technique founded on similar triangle principles. Experimental results demonstrate that the improved model increases detection accuracy from 89.70% to 91.01% while simultaneously reducing model parameters by 27.52% and storage space by 21.94%, exhibiting higher robustness in complex scenarios. The suggested monocular ranging approach exhibits measurement errors that do not surpass ±0.03 m across the evaluated 10 m range. The proposed solution balances accuracy and efficiency for object detection and localization in complex indoor and outdoor environments, reduces deployment costs, and enhances system practicality.

title: Emotional Intelligence in Language Education: A Multimodal Analysis Framework Based on CLIP and Dynamic Weighting
authors: Qiu Bingqian, Wen Lu, Zhang Yushuo, Wang Xuyang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Within the field of language education—particularly in second language learning—emotional understanding between students and teachers is widely recognised as essential. It is crucial for enabling effective interaction and is closely associated with improved teaching and learning efficiency. This study presents a multimodal emotion analysis method that utilises a dynamic weighting mechanism, combined with a CLIP pre-trained model, to enhance emotional understanding. Using the Adaptive Language-Guided Multimodal Transformer (ALMT), the approach reconstructs cross-modal feature representations to boost the semantic quality of unimodal data, facilitating the integration of visual and linguistic features. The Adaptive Hyper Modal Learning Layer (AHL) features a dynamic weighting generation module that evaluates modality confidence in real-time, addressing information imbalances and reducing noise interference. Meanwhile, residual connections ensure stable parameter updates. Experimental results show a 3.2% increase in F1 score for emotion classification tasks on the CMU-MOSEI dataset, demonstrating improved robustness against missing modalities and noise. This method not only enhances the accuracy of emotion analysis but also supports emotional interaction in language education, thereby enriching the overall learning experience.

title: Voltage Reduction and Energy-Saving Technology in Oilfield Power Distribution Networks Based on Bird Mating Optimizer
authors: Dayong Zhu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: To address the issues of low energy-saving efficiency and delayed response in traditional oilfield power distribution control strategies under varying load conditions, this study proposes an enhanced Bird Mating Optimizer (BMO) algorithm that integrates a position update mechanism and a multi-objective weighted scoring structure for voltage-reduction energy-saving optimization in oilfield distribution networks. Building upon the original BMO framework, the method introduces a sine-perturbation-driven individual position update strategy to enhance global search focus around optimal regions. Additionally, it incorporates a weighted fitness function balancing energy consumption, voltage stability, and switching cost, thereby improving the effectiveness and efficiency of multi-objective control. Comparative experiments conducted in typical oilfield power supply scenarios demonstrate that the proposed model achieves energy-saving rates of $15.1 \%$ under high-load and $11.6 \%$ under low-load conditions, with a maximum power loss fitting error of only 2.3 kW and minimum single-round computation time as low as 42 ms. The results verify that the structurally integrated BMO algorithm exhibits strong energy-saving potential and rapid response capabilities under multi-constraint control tasks, providing both theoretical support and algorithmic solutions for intelligent scheduling and energy management in oilfield distribution networks.

title: Constraint-Guided In-Context Learning for Domain-Specific Triplet Extraction
authors: Wei Zhao, Shuaishuai Li
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The automation of high-quality knowledge graph construction hinges on the precise extraction of relational triplets from unstructured text. While large language models (LLMs) offer potential solutions, current approaches remain limited in specialized domains due to their reliance on manual annotation and model fine-tuning. Crucially, the absence of explicit constraints on knowledge reasoning introduces significant uncertainty and errors in domain-specific triplet extraction. To address these gaps, this study proposes OntoP2KG, a novel framework integrating domain ontology constraints with structured prompt engineering. The method first identifies domain-subdomain attributes of input text, retrieves relevant concepts from ontologies and few-shot examples, and constructs a knowledge-enriched context. Subsequently, through structured prompts, the LLM is guided to perform accurate triplet extraction, followed by redundancy resolution based on graph embedding similarity and rule matching. Experimental results demonstrate that OntoP2KG significantly improves precision and recall in domain triplet extraction without requiring parameter fine-tuning, providing a robust path toward automated construction of domain-specific knowledge graphs.

title: Comprehensive Repository of MRI-derived Breast Models for Microwave Imaging Research
authors: Ana C. Pelicano, Maria C. T. Gonçalves, Tiago Castela, M. Lurdes Orvalho, Nuno A. M. Araújo, Emily Porter, Daniela M. Godinho, Raquel C. Conceição
publish: 2026 International Conference on Medical Microwave Imaging Applications (ICMWIA)
abstract: Breast cancer has the second highest incidence rate of all cancers worldwide. While the incidence rate has seen a slight increase over the years, the mortality rate has sharply decreased. This decline is primarily due to improvements in screening methods, earlier diagnoses, and advancements in management and treatment options. Therefore, the development of new technologies for cancer detection and diagnosis, as well as treatment, remains essential to further reducing mortality rates associated with this disease. In recent decades, microwaves have been explored for both the detection and treatment of cancer. Breast models are paramount for the design, test and validation of new imaging and treatment equipment. Additionally, realistic breast models are crucial for creating effective treatment planning tools. In this paper, we present an update to our open-access repository of MRI-derived and anatomically and dielectrically realistic breast models, which now includes data from 117 patients with single and multiple benign and malignant tumours. In addition to increasing the number of available models, we have incorporated clinically relevant information such as volume density, body mass index (BMI), age, diagnosis, biomarkers (when available), menopausal status, and family history.

title: Attention Scaling in BiLSTM-Transformer Networks for Efficient IoT Intrusion Detection
authors: Maira Khalid, Ahmed Raza Mohsin, Jehad Ali, Byeong-Hee Roh, Young-Bae Ko
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The increasing connectivity of Internet of Things (IoT) networks has amplified exposure to sophisticated cyber threats, emphasizing the need for adaptive and interpretable intrusion detection systems (IDS). This paper presents an adaptive deep learning framework that integrates Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks with a Transformer encoder enhanced by an Adaptive Attention Scaling (AAS) mechanism. The BiLSTM component captures short-term sequential dynamics in network traffic, while the Transformer refines these representations through global self-attention. Unlike static attention architectures, the proposed AAS dynamically adjusts the number of active attention heads according to real-time traffic entropy and packet rate, allowing the model to allocate computational resources proportional to traffic complexity. The framework operates purely on flow-level metadata, ensuring scalability and applicability in encrypted or heterogeneous IoT environments. Extensive experiments on the CIC-IDS2017 and NSL-KDD datasets demonstrate detection accuracies of 99.88% and 98.24%, respectively, with up to 35% reduction in inference time compared to static Transformer-based baselines. Further-more, the attention-based interpretability enhances transparency by revealing temporal and contextual dependencies that drive classification outcomes.

title: Effects of White Noise Stimulation via Bone Conduction on Hearing
authors: Kohei Kawamura, Shima Okada, Naruhiro Shiozawa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Previous studies have reported that auditory noise stimulation based on the stochastic resonance (SR) theory, which enhances the sensitivity of sensory systems, can improve auditory function. However, these studies used headphones for noise stimulation, and the convenience or practicality of the stimulation method has not been investigated. Therefore, this study aimed to investigate the effects of noise stimulation via bone conduction, which can be used without occluding the ear canal, on auditory function. Ten healthy adults participated in the study. Noise intensities were set at 40%, 60%, 80%, 100%, and 120% of each participant’s auditory threshold level. Auditory perception thresholds for weak sounds obtained from three-interval-three-alternative forced choice task were compared between the no noise (control) and the optimal noise stimulus intensity (OS) condition that maximizes the SR effect. The results showed that perception thresholds were significantly lower under the OS condition compared with the control condition. These findings suggest that noise stimulation via bone conduction, which offers high usability, can enhance auditory function.

title: A Comparative Study of WPA2 and WPA3: Security Enhancements, Vulnerabilities, and Authentication Protocols
authors: Santhi. V, Ramapriya. S, Sruthi. S, Moumitha. K, Gayathri. K. S, Theetchanaa. Ra
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Wireless networks are extensively used in personal, public, and enterprise settings but are still exposed to a variety of security threats. The WPA2 security protocol, while widely regarded as the standard for years, is now obsolete and vulnerable to offline dictionary attacks, deauthentication exploits, and key reinstallation attacks like KRACK. WPA3 was then introduced with a major security boost, such as Simultaneous Authentication of Equals (SAE), required Protected Management Frames (PMF), and more secure encryption suites to enhance user privacy and network stability. This paper provides a comparative study of WPA2 and WPA3 based on handshake format, encryption techniques, and susceptibility to real-world attacks. Packet-level traffic was inspected using Wireshark, and a dictionary attack using aircrack-ng was done to show the simplicity of breaking WPA2 passwords when weak credentials are utilized. The attack was not possible on WPA3 because of its offline guessing resistance. Even with its advancement, WPA3 is still limited, particularly in transitional mode deployments where WPA2 compatibility poses potential vulnerabilities. This research places high emphasis on the need to migrate to WPA3, in addition to ensuring proper configuration and policy compliance for total Wi-Fi security.

title: A Secure Face Recognition System Using the Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG) and the Support Vector Machines (SVM) with the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES)
authors: S.Sherine Paul Jiglu, P. Lalitha, J. Vikram
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: The present paper represents a secure face identification system, which implements the principles of Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG) as a feature extraction method and Support Vector Machines (SVM) as a feature classification method, with Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) as a means to keep those features safe during storage and transmission. The presented system was written in Python and guarantees recognition performance and secret of the biometric information. The system reached an accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score of 94.2, 93.1, 92.7 and 92.9 respectively, on a dataset of 120 individuals (1,200 faces). The cross-validation with five folds resulted in 93.8 percent mean accuracy with a standard deviation of 1.2. The AES addition offers an increased security over regular HOG+SVM system at the cost of 4.3 ms overhead latency (per <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$128 \times 128$</tex> image). Strong discriminative power is observed in the 0.95 ROC-AUC and balanced confusion matrices. Compared to the state-of-the-art deep architectures, the proposed system provides slightly lower accuracy than the deep CNNs [2], [4], [5] with a successful trade off of efficiency, privacy and security between embedded and privacy-aware deployments.

title: Vehicle Kilometer Reading and Service Interval Monitoring System
authors: Rajani Narayan, Chinmay B A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: The Vehicle Kilometer Reading and Service Interval Monitoring System - a system for the documentation of service intervals based on vehicle kilometers, developed by the Software. The most common problem it addresses is overdue or non-performed servicing because of lack of automatic control. The system saves the and-is-a counter value by means of digital sensors or the vehicle's onboard diagnostics (OBD) data. Once the service mileage (preset) is reached by the car, the car will prompt automatic reminders/ alerts to ensure that the servicing is done in tandem with oil changes, check up of the brakes and filter replacements. The system also aids in reducing the risk of premature failure, and costly service repairs attributed to lack of service. The system includes a microcontroller, odometer sensors and a simple interface to retain digital miles and service history. It is designed for private car owners as well as fleet managers. A fleet manager can monitor multiple vehicles on one dashboard, e.g., receive alerts via mobile apps, SMS or email. Tracking many units in real-time via preventive maintenance leads to savings in fuel and vehicle longevity. Cloud integration provides off-site access to data and safe storage, while satellite GPS service can provide route tracking and service planning optimization It is economic, efficient, and easy to fit and thus suitable to be utilized in company and personal fleets. Apart from keeping the system from time to time serviced, it is also eco friendly as the value of cars is will not become less when they are in their good condition. purely it makes car maintenance a smart, reliable, and effective move.

title: Artificial Intelligence Based Image Classification and Compression Technique
authors: Richa Verma, Vineet Kumar, Asmita, Ananya Sharma, Nitin Anand, Kunal Raj, Nitesh Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Everyone wants to save money, but saving time is more valuable. This was made feasible to a certain extent by the advancement of technological advances. People are now buying the majority of their necessities in a single store termed a mega market as a result of lifestyle changes. Only certain manufacturing items' specifics could be read by the present bar code billing systems. Most of the time, fruits and vegetables do not come with bar codes. Sometimes the seller must weigh the things in order to fix the standardized tag some time recently the charging prepare, or the biller must physically sort the thing title. The errand is multiplied as a result, which takes a part of time. For a fast charging method, the proposed manufactured intelligence-based picture classification framework employments a camera to identify the fruits and vegetables.

title: A Mechanical Property Prediction Model for Nanoindentation Data Based on Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Jingchun Wang, Cong Li, Junhao Fu, Shoubin Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper proposes an artificial neural networkbased method for analyzing nanoindentation data to predict the mechanical properties of metal matrix composites. A dataset of indentation curves for various materials is constructed through finite element simulation, from which features such as contact stiffness and elastic/plastic deformation quantities are extracted. The performance of Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), and Residual Network (ResNet) is compared, with the MLP ultimately selected and optimized. Through data preprocessing, model structure tuning, and a dualloss function mechanism, the prediction accuracy is significantly improved. Experimental results demonstrate that for predicting elastic modulus and yield strength, the model achieves mean relative errors of 8.10% and 8.11%, respectively, validating its effectiveness for the rapid assessment of mechanical properties in materials.

title: An implementation of a quantum algorithm on a Cyclone FPGA
authors: Haruto Kurokawa, Minoru Watanabe, Nobuya Watanabe
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: In some algorithms, quantum computers can achieve a high computational power over widely used classical computers. This paper presents a simple quantum algorithm implementation example on a Cyclone V field programmable gate array (FPGA). Experimental result shows the FPGA implementation is faster than that of software operation on a personal computer.

title: A Dynamic Temperature-Aware Metaheuristic Approach for Real-Time Path Optimization in Agricultural Cold Chain Networks
authors: Yuan Zhu, Xi Lin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper proposes a dynamic temperature-aware firefly algorithm (DTA-FA) and constructs a three-dimensional coupling model of "path-temperature-time" for the first time. The algorithm innovatively designs embedded temperature compensation coding (1% precision refrigeration power adjustment), embeds real-time control parameters of cold chain vehicles in chromosomes (such as 75% refrigeration power from 14:00 to 15:30), and breaks through the limitation of traditional path planning that only focuses on spatial coordinates. The dynamic fluorescence decay model is used to quantify the decrease in storage stability of agricultural products with the extension of transportation time. When the transportation exceeds 2 hours, the temperature sensitivity coefficient is automatically increased by 40%. The experiment is based on a 200-node cold chain network in Beijing, Tianjin, Hebei and Shandong. The results show that the average path length of DTA-FA is 387km (89km shorter than the improved ant colony), the number of temperature violations (>5°C) is 1.2 times/trip (52% less than GA), and the spoilage cost is reduced to ¥217/vehicle (industry average ¥583). In the pilot of 1,200 vehicles in Xinfadi, Hebei, the algorithm achieved a real-time adjustment delay of 2.1s (national standard ≤30s), reduced spoilage by an average of 10.5kg per vehicle, and reduced energy consumption of cold chain equipment by 14.3%. Especially in the sudden rainstorm scene (vehicle speed dropped by 40%), the spoilage increased by only 3% (the control algorithm increased by 18%) through the temperature memory library backtracking mechanism (trigger probability 20%). Studies have shown that DTA-FA improves the temperature control accuracy to ±0.8°C (industry ±2.1°C) through integrated operation and control coding and time-sensitive fluorescence mechanism, providing a new paradigm for solving the spoilage problem of the "last mile" of agricultural product logistics.

title: Diabetic Retinopathy Prediction Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: N. Ramkumar, I Preethiev Raj, S Sujai
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: This paper presents the development of an intelligent healthcare system leveraging deep learning and machine learning models for the early detection of diabetic retinopathy (DR) severity and the prediction of kidney disease risk. The system uses EfficientNet-B3 and other CNN architectures to classify retinal fundus images into DR severity levels, supported by Random Forest classifiers for kidney risk prediction based on clinical parameters. Advanced techniques such as data augmentation, focal loss, and transfer learning are employed to enhance model performance. The solution ensures robust and accurate diagnostics, accessible through a userfriendly Streamlit web application for real-time predictions. Experimental results demonstrate high accuracy and usability, offering valuable support for healthcare professionals in early intervention and personalized patient management.

title: Fruit quality detection method via improved YOLOv8
authors: Junhao Li, Linlin Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To tackle the issues of inadequate detection accuracy for small objects and limited feature extraction capabilities in natural scenarios, an object detection model built on an improved YOLOv8 is proposed in this study. Using fruit quality detection as an application scenario, a dataset containing multiple varieties and quality levels is constructed. The original YOLOv8 model is optimized in three aspects: the SPD-Conv module is developed for the purpose of substituting the conventional convolutional layer, intended to prevent the loss of fine-grained features during sampling; the PSA attention mechanism is embedded in the backbone network to achieve global feature modeling at a lower computational cost; and the utilisation of the WIoU loss function as opposed to the CIoU loss function is conducive to enhancing the accuracy of bounding box regression. The experimental results demonstrate that the enhanced model attains a precision of 93.6%, a recall of 85.9%, and a mean average precision (mAP50) of 92.5% in the fruit quality detection task. The following improvements are observed: 0.9%, 4.3%, and 3%, respectively, in comparison to the baseline model, significantly outperforming other mainstream object detection models. This paper provides an effective model optimization solution for small object detection in complex natural environments.

title: Task Scheduling in Fog-Cloud Environments Using an Adapted Lemurs Optimizer
authors: Ammar Kamal Abasi, Nedal Ababneh, Moayad Aloqaily, Mohsen Guizani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Task scheduling in Fog–Cloud computing is a complex multi-objective and NP-hard optimization problem that requires a careful balance among latency, energy consumption, and operational cost within highly heterogeneous and distributed infrastructures. This paper introduces an Adapted Lemurs Optimizer (ALO), which tailors the original Lemurs Optimizer to the Fog–Cloud scheduling domain through task–node-aware initialization and normalized multi-objective fitness evaluation. The proposed ALO efficiently explores the scheduling space while preserving computational simplicity. Extensive experiments conducted with workloads of 100, 200, and 300 tasks across 5- and 10-fog-node configurations demonstrate that ALO consistently outperforms Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Genetic Algorithm (GA). On average, ALO achieves 18.6% lower makespan, 14.2% lower energy consumption, and 11.8% lower processing cost. These results confirm ALO’s capability to deliver scalable, energy-efficient, and cost-aware scheduling solutions suitable for real-time Fog–Cloud environments.

title: Application Model Construction of Digital Twin Technology in Machine Vision
authors: Haodong Cheng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the challenges of insufficient accuracy, poor real-time performance, and weak robustness in traditional approaches to digital twin and machine vision collaboration, this paper proposes a "four-layer, two-link" closed-loop application architecture and the Dynamic Feature Adaptive Alignment (DFAAA) algorithm. This architecture encompasses the physical entity layer (multi-dimensional data acquisition and preprocessing), the digital twin modeling layer (Unity3D high-fidelity modeling and virtual-reality synchronization), the machine vision perception and matching layer (DFAAA algorithm and GPU parallel computing), and the decision-making and application layer (implementation of scenario-based business functions). It achieves a closed virtual-reality loop through data and control links. The DFAAA algorithm integrates channels—spatial dual attention, dynamic threshold adjustment, and regularized PCA dimensionality reduction—to balance accuracy, real-time performance, and robustness. Experiments were conducted on Intel Core i7-12700K and NVIDIA RTX 3090 hardware, using MVTech AD, KITTI, and a self-developed bearing defect dataset for validation. DFAAA achieved a baseline accuracy of 98.7% and a real-time performance of 35.1fps on the MVTech AD dataset, representing improvements of 5.2%/10.0% and 2.5 times/27.6% over SIFT and ORB, respectively. On the self-developed bearing dataset, the model achieved an average positioning error of 0.07mm and a detection time of 0.02s per image, respectively, 61.1% and 71.4% reductions compared to the traditional HOG+SVM solution. The missed detection rate and false detection rate were reduced to 1.2% and 0.9%, respectively, validating the effectiveness of the architecture and algorithm.

title: Fault Characteristic Analysis of Phase-Shifting Process in LCC Rectifier Station and Phase-Shifting Angle Selection Criterion
authors: Shengbo Zhang, Tao Liu, Zichun Zhang, Zihao Dai, Ye Li
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Hybrid HVDC transmission system has both economy and no commutation failure on the inverter side, which is the optimal choice for large-scale and cross-regional energy transmission. However, LCC rectifier stations still face the risk of commutation failure during the phase-shifting stage following DC line faults. Under certain operating conditions, fault current may exhibits decaying oscillation, hindering subsequent system recovery. To address this, this paper analyzes the mechanism of commutation failure and proposes a fault characteristic analysis method for the LCC rectifier during the phase-shifting period, fully considering the influence of the phase-shifting angle on the fault characteristics. Based on this, the initial phase-shifting angle selection criterion for the phase-shifting blocking strategy is proposed, which can effectively enhance clearing speed while avoiding phase-shift failure. Finally, based on the PSCAD/EMTDC simulation platform, a hybrid DC transmission system was established. Through simulation cases, the accuracy of the proposed fault characteristic analysis and the effectiveness of the selection criterion were verified.

title: Topic Cluster Identification Using Spectral Clustering in Academic Research Organization Platforms
authors: Kim Tatyana, Burxonova Gulmira, Masud Djabborov, Nigora Erkinova, Otabek Safarbaev, Zilolakhon Ruzmetova, Dildora Djumayeva
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: To plan a successful academic research, it is necessary to find relevant groups of topics, which will facilitate better information search and discovery of knowledge on research platforms. Conventional clustering methods tend to have difficulties with high-dimensional, sparse, and complex relations between research articles, which translates to poor grouping and low interpretability. Current approaches, including hierarchical or k-means clustering, can be unsuccessful in encompassing non-linear structures as well as global connectivity in academic data, and thus produce incorrect or discontinuous groups of topics. This research proposes a model to deal with these issues; the model involves the Spectral Clustering Algorithm (SCA), which uses the eigenvalues of similarity matrices to naturally group data. SCA plays a crucial role in extracting detailed insights into complex relations among articles since it can reduce high-dimensional research data to a lower-dimensional spectral space, achieving coherence and more sense-making topic clusters. The suggested approach is implemented on the organization platforms of academic research to automatically cluster articles, authors, and research topics. This improves navigation, recommendation, and discovery of relevant research materials for scholars and institutions. Through experimental analysis, it has been shown that SCA is better at clustering patterns compared to the traditional clustering protocols in cluster cohesion, separation, and overall accuracy. The results show the enhancement of topic recognition, which helps to organize academic knowledge better and allows making research management decisions based on the results.

title: A Generative-AI Approach to Validating Blank Selections for Phrase Fill-in-Blank Problems in Web-Client Programming Learning Assistant System
authors: Zhikang Li, Huiyu Qi, Nobuo Funabiki, Htoo Htoo Sandi Kyaw, Wen-Chung Kao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Dynamic web-client programming with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript has become dominant in web application system. To assist its study by novices, we have developed a Phrase Fill-in-Blank Problem (PFP) in the Web-client Programming Learning Assistant System (Web-PLAS). Any answer is marked through string matching with the correct one. However, the current blank phrase selection algorithm implemented for helping a teacher to make a new PFP instance may not select blanks having unique answers. In this paper, we investigate a generative-AI approach to validating the selected blank phrases by the algorithm. In this approach, we first change the name of any user-defined identifier in a code so that the AI cannot use a memorized code for the answer. Then, we check the validity of the selected blanks by asking the AI to find their correct answers. After that, we repeat selecting a new phrase for blank and validating it by asking the AI to find the correct answer, until no new phrase can be selected. In either case, if the AI cannot find the correct answer, the blank is discarded. For preliminary evaluations, we manually apply this approach to the 10 PFP instances for basic topics in our previous study, using MS Copilot. The results show that several blanks in existing instances are not correct and additional blanks can be selected. The implementation of a program to automate the approach and its comprehensive evaluations with various codes will be in our next study.

title: 3D Reconstruction with Multi-View Stereo Vision for Dental Imaging
authors: Vijay Chandhar M A, Sasan Haghani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Accurate and accessible 3D scanning of anatomical structures is critical for advancing healthcare technologies such as teledentistry, personalized prosthetic design, and at-home monitoring systems. Traditional dental imaging methods rely on either manual impressions or expensive intraoral scanners, which often require specialized training and are not easily deployable outside clinical settings. This paper presents a robot-guided Multi-View Stereo (MVS) vision reconstruction system using low-cost RGB-D sensors to capture and reconstruct detailed 3D models of dental structures in real time. Leveraging a 7-Degree of Freedom (DoF) Kinova Gen3 robotic arm and Intel RealSense depth cameras, we implement a pipline comprising the RGB-D odometry and volumetric Truncated Signed Distance Function (TSDF) fusion to generate dense colored point clouds of a jaw model. Through systematic trials varying the sensor choice and trajectory, we demonstrate that active and robot-controlled multi-view capture significantly improves geometric completeness and fidelity compared to the single-view setup. The results indicate that consumer-grade depth sensors, when integrated with precise robotic positioning, can provide a low-cost and high accuracy 3D scanning system for dental imaging.

title: Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration Framework for Real-Time Payment Gateways
authors: Vimal Teja Manne
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Real time payment gateways handle authorization and clearing traffic for card and account based ecosystems under strict latency and availability targets. Most deployed gateways still rely on RSA and elliptic curve cryptography, which become vulnerable once large scale quantum computers can run Shor's algorithm. Simply swapping these algorithms for NIST standard post quantum schemes is not enough, because gateways must still meet service level agreements, maintain Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) compliance, and interoperate with acquirers and issuers that migrate at different speeds. This paper presents a Post Quantum Cryptography Migration Framework that gives an end to end architectural view of gateway, acquirer, and issuer communication paths. The framework introduces a crypto abstraction layer with pluggable algorithm providers, supports hybrid classical plus post quantum modes during transition, and uses policy driven selection of key encapsulation and signature schemes such as Kyber and Dilithium. It also defines a phased migration process from asset discovery and quantum risk assessment through controlled hybrid rollout to post quantum only operation. To validate the approach, we implement a gateway testbed that replays a PaySim transaction workload and measure the impact of post quantum schemes on authorization latency and derived throughput under classical only, hybrid, and PQC only modes. The experiments show that, with careful configuration and connection management, post quantum protection can be integrated into real time payment processing with performance overhead kept within operational limits.

title: From Clean to Corroded: Prompt-Guided Diffusion Inpainting for License Plate Degradation
authors: Muhammad Ali Farooq, Francisco Afonso Raposo, Bilal Alam Khan, Gabriel Costache, Peter Corcoran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Traditional OCR and Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) systems typically perform well under controlled conditions, such as clean, well-lit, high-resolution environments. However, their reliability diminishes in real-world scenarios involving visual degradations like dust, occlusions, rust, glare, or faded characters. To address this, we present a novel imaging pipeline that synthetically renders number plates under challenging conditions using diffusion-based generative models. Our approach integrates edge-conditioned ControlNet with inpainting-driven diffusion to generate spatially precise and semantically realistic distortions. The process is guided by using tailored text prompts generated via a large language model, enabling contextual degradation synthesis. A hybrid architecture combines SDXL and a stable inpainting backbone to operate on full-frame vehicle images, capturing variability in viewpoint, distance, and background clutter. We validate the effectiveness of our synthetic dataset by evaluating the performance of a mature OCR engine (EasyOCR), showing that standard models struggle with complex distortions—highlighting the potential of our method for training and benchmarking robust OCR systems. The complete synthetic number plate degradation dataset and the fine-tuned model, is available at (https://github.com/MAli-Farooq/Prompt-Guided-Diffusion-Inpainting-for-License-Plate-Degradation/).

title: Development of an Automated Hand Acupoint Localization Model Based on YOLO11n-Pose
authors: Chun-Yi Yeh, Chang-Yin Lee, Kun-Chan Lan, Chen Chi-Wei, Chih-Yu Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This study presents an automatic hand acupoint localization model based on the YOLO11n-Pose architecture, designed to improve the objectivity and accuracy of acupuncture point positioning. A hand acupoint dataset containing multi-angle images with precise annotations was established, and a five-fold cross-validation training strategy was employed to improve model stability and generalization. Experimental results demonstrated that all folds exhibited stable convergence, with validation losses ranging from 0.017 to 0.023. The average localization error on the validation set was 0.99 mm, indicating high precision and consistency. Overall, the YOLO11n-Pose model effectively recognized hand acupoint locations, verifying the feasibility of applying deep learning–based pose estimation for acupuncture localization tasks.

title: Concatenated Image Technique Approach to Optimize AI Detection Inference Time and Resource Utilization
authors: Bayhaqi Mohd Jailani, Muhammad Farhan Mustafa, Amirullah Hamzah, Hasmarina Hasan, Zulaikha Kadim
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Autonomous Systems (ICoIAS)
abstract: The rapid development of deep learning has significantly improved object detection performance, particularly in agricultural automation and robotics. However, real-time inference across multiple camera inputs in autonomous systems remains challenging due to high computational demands and limited processing resources. This research presents a comparative analysis of three inference strategies, which were conventional single-image inference, batch-9 inference, and a novel Concatenated Image Technique (CIT) by using the YOLOv11 object detection model for Fresh Fruit Bunch (FFB) detection in oil palm plantations. Two YOLOv11 models were trained using different data configurations, one with single images and another with concatenated 3 times 3 image grids. The conventional model achieved an F1-score of 0.98, while the concatenated model achieved 0.95, with an 81% reduction in training time. Inference performance was evaluated on both GPU and CPU platforms using 900 test images. On GPU, the batch-9 method achieved the highest throughput (77.56 images/s) but required higher GPU utilization (23.5%) and VRAM usage (1385.21 MB). In contrast, the CIT achieved comparable throughput (69.63 images/s) with the lowest GPU utilization (5.6%) and memory usage (1121.31 MB). Under CPU-only inference, CIT demonstrated superior performance with a 3.3 times faster processing speed and reduced CPU utilization (28.6%) compared to other methods. The findings show that the CIT offers a balanced trade-off between accuracy, processing speed, and hardware efficiency, making it highly suitable for lowpower, multi-camera autonomous systems operating in agricultural environments.

title: Research on Inverse Kinematics Solution of UR5 Robotic Arm Based on Improved Genetic
authors: Yang Zhang, Ying Yu, Yanru He, Tingwen Chen
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Inverse kinematics solution of robotic arm is a core issue in robot motion planning. Essentially, it is a nonlinear optimization problem with multiple solutions and strong constraints.
