In this paper, for the UR5 six-degree-of-freedom robotic arm, a Hybrid Adaptive Genetic Algorithm (HAGA) is proposed for inverse kinematics solution. This method integrates multiple strategies such as heuristic population initialization, adaptive parameter adjustment, simulated annealing local search and L-BFGS-B fine optimization. The algorithm uses binary coding to represent the joint space and evaluates the quality of the solution by comprehensively considering position error, attitude error and the degree of violation of joint limit in the fitness function. The simulation results show that, compared with the traditional genetic algorithm, the algorithm proposed in this paper improves the position accuracy by 99.99 %, enhances the computational efficiency by 35.3 %, and can effectively handle joint Angle constraints. This research provides an effective solution for the real-time motion control of complex robotic arms.

title: A Hybrid Model Predictive Control for PMSM Drives with Common-Mode Voltage and Switching Loss Reduction
authors: Muktapuram Venkata Rohith Reddy, Anugrah Gupta, Ankit Dalal, Mosaddique Nawaz Hussain
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: This work presents a model predictive control (MPC) approach designed to tackle two major challenges in permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drives: common-mode voltage (CMV) and switching losses, where suppressing one typically exacerbates the other. The work presents a comparative analysis of four model predictive control (MPC) strategies: conventional MPC, a reduced-switching MPC, a reduced-CMV MPC, and a hybrid proposed method. All four control strategies successfully achieve accurate speed reference tracking and load torque delivery. However, they involve notable compromises among CMV, switching losses and stator current harmonic distortion (THD). Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method successfully bounds the CMV amplitude to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm V_{d c} / 6$</tex>, eliminates spikes, and maintains a moderate increase in switching instances.

title: Analysis of the Application of Tree-Structure-Based Matching Algorithm in Social Work Development Information
authors: Fan Liang, Wenjie Xie, Runda Su, Yuxuan Luo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To empower social work development with AI and solve information processing inefficiencies in social work, this study proposes an improved Trie tree (a core of tree-structure-based matching algorithms) — its nodes record string and word-formation position info, and child nodes use hash lookup. Based on this, the AI-enabled Chinese word segmentation forward maximum matching algorithm is optimized: an automaton mechanism judges the longest word, solving the traditional algorithm’s string adjustment issue by word length. Experiments show its time complexity is 1.33 with faster segmentation speed.In social work development information application, this AI-driven algorithm boosts Chinese text processing efficiency (e.g., analyzing service demands, sorting resources, extracting case key info) and fits real-time dictionary updates (e.g., adding social work terms). It provides stable AI support for social work’s intelligent transformation, promotes accurate info mining, and empowers social work’s high-quality development.

title: A Comparative Study of Isolation Forest and Autoencoder Models for Proactive Zero-Day Attack Detection
authors: Shambhavi Anand, Sundus Zehra
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Zero-day attacks pose a major risk and are an evolving threat to cybersecurity, as they exploit software vulnerabilities that evade traditional signature-based detection methods that often fail to recognize them. To address this challenge, machine learning based anomaly detection approaches have gained increased attention for their potential to identify unusual behaviors of emerging threats attack patterns proactively. Among these, two approaches Isolation Forest and Autoencoder models use unsupervised techniques for anomaly detection, to distinguish malicious activities. These are selected for their complementary strengths: Isolation Forest for efficient anomaly isolation in high-dimensional data, and Autoencoder for capturing complex nonlinear data patterns. This paper presents a comparative study of these two models to evaluate their ability to detect proactive zero-day attack detection, using a UGRansome dataset exclusively, as it specifically targets ransomware and zero-day attack behaviours in network traffic. The study aims to evaluate the performance, strengths and limitations of each model in detecting zero-day threats. Through systematic experimentation the research contributes to enhancing proactive security solutions.

title: A Robust Visual SLAM System for Complex Indoor Environments Based on Improved Line Feature Extraction and Adaptive Nonlinear Optimization
authors: Zikang Hou, Huijun Yang, Wei Hu, Chenwen Zhang, Yichu Yang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Visual Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) systems suffer seriously from low positioning accuracy and insufficient robustness in indoor environment with weak texture and dynamics. To overcome the disadvantages, we propose a SLAM algorithm (SLAM-LB) combined with the improved line feature extraction algorithm and adaptive nonlinear optimization algorithm based on the ORB-SLAM2 basic system. Main improvements are divided into two aspects. The first is an improved Line Segment Detector (LSD) algorithm for the extraction of line feature, with operating parameters tuned: the image scaling factor is adjusted to 0.6, and the pixel coefficient threshold is adjusted to 0.5. Another adaptive gradient threshold screen part is designed to filter out low quality line segments, increasing the detection rate and matching precision of line features from weak texture regions. The second is an improved Levenberg-Marquardt (LM) nonlinear optimization algorithm.we construct an adaptive modification technique of damping factor to dynamically adjust the scale of optimization steps according to the feature matching residual, and construct a simultaneous change method for intermediate variables to accelerate the robustness and convergence of back-end optimization. Experimental analysis results based on the TUM dataset shows that our proposed SLAM-LB system reduces the ATE on average by 43.7% and 44.8% compared with the original ORB-SLAM2 and PL-SLAM, respectively. The method effectively improves the positioning effect and map stability of the SLAM system to complex indoor scenes, giving reliable technical support to robot indoor navigation tasks.

title: Adaptive Switching Mode Interleaved Totem-Pole PFC Converter for EV Charger Applications
authors: Sushant Gupta, Vignesh Kumar Vethanayagam, Karthikeyan Anbalagan
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: An adaptive interleaved totem-pole (AITP) power factor correction (PFC) converter with adaptable switching modes is presented for electric vehicle (EV) charger applications. The converter uses a single-pole double-throw (SPDT) relay to switch between an interleaved mode and a single-leg mode. The interleaved designs provide advantages during full load operations. However, the operations for low load conditions cause the line current distortions and compromise the power factor and converter performance. Hence, the proposed converter switches from an interleaved mode to the single-leg mode with both inductors in series for a load less than 50% using a SPDT switch. Similarly, the proposed converter switches to the interleaved mode from the single-leg mode for load 50 % and above. The single-leg mode with both inductors in series increases the effective inductance of the converter and helps to reduce the line current distortions. Also, the single-leg mode improves efficiency under low load by reducing the switching losses and core loss of the inductor. The performance of the proposed AITP PFC converter is analyzed with the design of 1.5 kW using MATLAB Simulink for an AC input supply of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$230 ~\mathrm{V}, 50 ~\text{Hz}$</tex>.

title: Digital Twin-Based Anomaly Detection for VSC-HVDC Converter Stations Using Deep Learning and Data Fusion
authors: Qionglan Na, Xian Li, Xin Li, Peng Zang, Hongyan Jia, Na Xiao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel anomaly detection method for Voltage Source Converter-based High Voltage Direct Current (VSC-HVDC) converter stations by integrating digital twin technology with deep learning and multi-dimensional data fusion. The proposed framework establishes a high-fidelity digital twin model of the Modular Multilevel Converter (MMC) and employs a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-Long ShortTerm Memory (CNN-LSTM) architecture to extract spatiotemporal features from multi-source operational data. An impedance-based resonance detection method is also incorporated to identify potential instability issues. Experimental results based on the Zhongdu ±500kV converter station demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an accuracy of 97.82% and an F1-score of 96.28%, significantly outperforming conventional machine learning approaches. The method enables early detection of anomalies up to 90 hours before fault occurrence, providing valuable support for predictive maintenance and enhanced operational reliability of VSC-HVDC systems.

title: A Chinese/English mixed text proofreading algorithm for Central Asian universities based on transfer learning
authors: Xingwang Li, Fangfang Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: The mixed Chinese and English texts in universities in Central Asia have unique language phenomena such as grammar confusion and semantic ambiguity, resulting in a high false positive and missed negative rate of traditional proofreading methods. To address this issue, this paper proposes a Chinese-English mixed text proofreading algorithm for Central Asian universities based on transfer learning. The core innovation lies in the design of a hierarchical transfer learning framework: Firstly, on the source domain task, a deep semantic representation model based on Transformer is pre-trained using large-scale general Chinese-English parallel corpora; Then, for the target domain task, a dynamic gated domain adapter module is introduced. This module can adaptively adjust the parameters of the source domain model and focus on learning the unique language blending patterns in the context of colleges and universities. Finally, by fusing the bidirectional gated recurrent unit with the self-attention mechanism, the long-distance context dependencies of the mixed text are captured to accurately identify and correct problems such as semantic incoherence and collocation errors caused by language switching. In the experimental section, the algorithm proposed in this paper is compared with the baseline method. The results show that in terms of the three key indicators of precision, recall and F1 value, the algorithm proposed in this paper achieves 92.1%, 89.7% and 90.8% respectively, significantly outperforming the baseline model. Especially in correcting specific error types caused by language habit interference, the algorithm proposed in this paper, with its powerful domain transfer and context awareness capabilities, has improved the proofreading accuracy by approximately 15.7% compared to the optimal baseline model, effectively demonstrating its superiority in practical applications.

title: LinguaGraph: An NLP-Driven Computational Framework for Interpretable Linguistic Analysis of Learner Writing
authors: Tejas Patil, Barnali Chetia
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: Understanding how learners gradually acquire linguistic competence remains one of the enduring concerns of language education and applied linguistics. Although computational models can evaluate grammatical accuracy or predict proficiency scores, they often fail to capture the deeper structural growth that reflects genuine progress in language learning. This study introduces LinguaGraph, a graph theoretic framework that examines how syntactic and semantic relations evolve in learner writing. The research draws on a collection of one thousand and thirteen essays written by novice and intermediate learners of English who participated in an academic communication program in India. Each essay was transformed into a linguistic graph where nodes represent words and syntactic units while edges mark grammatical and semantic relations. By examining features such as density, connectivity, and clustering, LinguaGraph traces subtle shifts in structural complexity that align closely with human rated proficiency levels. The findings indicate that as learners advance, their linguistic graphs become more cohesive and integrated, suggesting greater fluency and syntactic control. The framework offers an interpretable and pedagogically meaningful way of visualizing language development and invites a closer dialogue between computational analysis and classroom based language research.

title: FCMD: Fine-Grained Text-Driven Cohesive Motion Generation With Diffusion Model_supp2-3677594.pdf
authors: wenfeng song
publish: N/A
abstract: Generating continuous and expressive human motion from textual descriptions is a critical challenge in applications such as gaming and filmmaking. Existing methods often struggle to maintain global coherence, realistic frame continuity, and smooth transitions. To address these limitations, we propose FCMD, a novel diffusion-based model for generating cohesive motion sequences from fine-grained textual descriptions. FCMD introduces three key innovations: (1) Fine-grained Text Fusion, which integrates detailed textual cues with transitional narratives to enhance semantic consistency; (2) History Motion Guidance, ensuring motion accuracy and consistency across consecutive frames; and (3) Smooth Stitching Sampling, which leverages preceding and current motion information to achieve seamless transitions. Additionally, FCMD employs a large language model (LLM) to refine motion datasets by extracting fine-grained textual descriptions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FCMD outperforms state-of-the-art methods in generating coherent, natural, and highly controllable motion sequences.

title: Anti-Saturation Speed Regulation Using Dual-Parameter Sliding Mode Observer for PMSM Servo Systems
authors: Zhengjie Wang, Shaowu Lu, Shiqi Zheng, Manli Zhang, Bao Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Accurate state estimation is essential for designing high-performance permanent magnet synchronous motor servo systems. However, the saturation of system input will severely undermine the consistency between control commands and actual inputs, leading to deviations between observer’s state prediction and actual system state, further deteriorating control performance. To address these issues, this article presents an anti-saturation speed regulation scheme integrating a dual-parameter sliding mode observer (DPSMO) and a variable-structure sliding mode controller (VSSMC). The DPSMO is used to synchronously identify system inertia and lumped disturbance under the saturation conditions, where three saturation update conditions are constructed to determine in real time whether the estimation of system inertia should be updated. Meanwhile, an auxiliary term of the VSSMC is designed to enforce system input constraints, thereby mitigating the impact of input saturation on system control performance. Moreover, a novel reaching law considering terminal attractor and hyperbolic tangent function is proposed to enhance convergence speed and reduce sliding mode chattering, without the need to determine the disturbance upper bound. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme.

title: Viterbi State Selection for Discrete Pinching Antenna Systems
authors: Victoria E. Galanopoulou, Thrassos K. Oikonomou, Odysseas G. Karagiannidis, Sotiris A. Tegos, Panagiotis D. Diamantoulakis
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Pinching antennas enable dynamic control of electromagnetic wave propagation through reconfigurable radiating structures, but selecting an optimal subset of antennas remains a combinatorial problem with exponential complexity. This letter considers antenna subset selection for a waveguide-fed pinching antenna array serving ground users under a time-division access scheme. The achievable rate depends on the coherent superposition of the effective complex channel gains and is therefore highly sensitive to the relative phase alignment of the activated antennas. To address the prohibitive complexity of exhaustive search, we propose a Viterbi state selection (VSS) algorithm that exploits the phase structure of the combined received signal. The trellis state is defined by a quantized representation of the phase of the accumulated complex gain, and a Viterbi-based survivor rule is used to prune dominated antenna subsets across stages. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves the same antenna selection and rate as exhaustive search, while reducing the computational complexity from exponential to polynomial in the number of available antennas.

title: Trickle Algorithm Implementation in Routing Protocol for LLNs: A Survey
authors: Khadak Singh Bhandari, Juphil Cho, Zubaer Ibna Mannan
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Low-power and Lossy Networks (LLNs) are essential components of the Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystem. These networks are distinct due to their unique features, such as traffic patterns and limited resources. To satisfy the routing conditions of LLNs, a new IPv6 routing protocol has been developed and standardized by the IETF, known as RPL. The Trickle algorithm, an integral part of RPL, is responsible for broadcasting control messages and ensuring fast and reliable resolution of inconsistencies in large-scale networks. In this adaptive method, the mechanism of message dissemination relies on a Trickle timer, in which different Trickle parameters and variables play a significant role. Numerous studies have highlighted that the Trickle algorithm, in its current form, presents problems that restrict the efficiency and applicability of RPL. Therefore, several research efforts have been aimed at enhancing the performance of RPL by optimizing the Trickle mechanism, parameter selection, and amendment. This survey primarily aims to approach suggested enhancements for enhancing the Trickle algorithm and analyzing the effectiveness of the Trickle mechanism. We provide a thorough review of these enhanced Trickle versions, including their parameters, variables, and rules, to assess whether such suggestions successfully address the limitation of RPL. Although some shortcomings have been effectively addressed, others persist in deficiency, particularly in the mobile environment. We present our observations on the adaptation of the Trickle algorithm, highlighting its desired features and the factors that affect its performance in RPL.

title: Impact of Insulation Layers on Feeding Current Distribution and Transport Losses in CORC-like Cables
authors: Hasan N.H. Al-Ssalih, Harold S. Ruiz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: The high current-carrying capacity of Conductor-on-Round-Core (CORC) cables made from second-generation high-temperature superconducting (2G-HTS) tapes makes them promising for high-field magnet and power-transmission applications. Using a full three-dimensional H-formulation model, we examine the impact of insulated versus non-insulated current-feeding schemes in a tri-layer CORC-like cable. The results show that interlayer insulation and the magneto-angular anisotropy <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$J_{c}(B,\theta)$</tex-math></inline-formula> strongly affect current sharing, waveform distortion, and AC transport losses. In non-insulated cables, current redistribution causes significant phase shifts and distortion beyond <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\bf {60\%}$</tex-math></inline-formula> of the rated capacity when <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$J_{c}(B,\theta)$</tex-math></inline-formula> is considered, while insulation ensures uniform current paths and lower hysteresis losses by the consumption or disappearance of magnetization currents that occurs as the transport current increases. Conversely, at lower transport currents, the occurrence of magnetization currents becomes the dominant mechanism rendering to higher AC losses. These findings provide design guidance and new insight into how current-feeding strategies influence the electromagnetic performance of multi-layer CORC-like cables.

title: Hybrid Action-Based Reinforcement Learning for Multiobjective Compatible Autonomous Driving
authors: Guizhe Jin, Zhuoren Li, Bo Leng, Wei Han, Lu Xiong, Chen Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has shown excellent performance in solving decision-making and control problems of autonomous driving (AD), which is increasingly applied in diverse driving scenarios. However, driving is a multiattribute problem, leading to challenges in achieving multiobjective compatibility for current RL methods, especially in both policy updating and policy execution. On the one hand, a single value evaluation network limits the policy updating in complex scenarios with coupled driving objectives. On the other hand, the common single-type action space structure limits driving flexibility or results in large behavior fluctuations during policy execution. To this end, we propose a multiobjective ensemble-critic (MoEC) RL method with a hybrid parametrized action for multiobjective compatible AD. Specifically, an advanced MORL architecture is constructed, in which the ensemble-critic focuses on different objectives through independent reward functions. The architecture integrates a hybrid parameterized action space structure, and the generated driving actions contain both abstract guidance that matches the hybrid road modality and concrete control commands. In addition, an uncertainty-based exploration mechanism that supports hybrid actions is developed to learn multiobjective compatible policies more quickly. The experimental results demonstrate that, in both simulator-based and HighD dataset-based multilane highway scenarios, our method efficiently learns multiobjective compatible AD with respect to efficiency, action consistency, and safety.

title: Generalizable Hierarchical Skill Learning via Object-Centric Representation_supp2-3678126.mp4
authors: Dian Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: We present Generalizable Hierarchical Skill Learning (GSL), a novel framework for hierarchical policy learning that significantly improves policy generalization and sample efficiency in robot manipulation. One core idea of GSL is to use object-centric skills as an interface that bridges the high-level vision-language model and the low-level visual-motor policy. Specifically, GSL decomposes demonstrations into transferable and object-canonicalized skill primitives using foundation models, ensuring efficient low-level skill learning in the object frame. At test time, the skill-object pairs predicted by the high-level agent are fed to the low-level module, where the inferred canonical actions are mapped back to the world frame for execution. This structured yet flexible design leads to substantial improvements in sample efficiency and generalization of our method across unseen spatial arrangements, object appearances, and task compositions. In simulation, GSL trained with only 3 demonstrations per task outperforms baselines trained with 30 times more data by 15.5% on unseen tasks. In real-world experiments, GSL also surpasses the baseline trained with 10 times more data.

title: Modeling and Optimization of High-Voltage Electromagnetic-Interference Filters for Electric Drive Systems
authors: Xinglin Liao, Jin Jia, Chao He, Heming Zhao, Yun Long, Hang Wu, Qiubo Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: The direct current–link filter of an electric drive system is essential for maintaining its conductive electromagnetic interference (EMI) within regulatory limits. Accurate modeling is essential for EMI-filter performance simulation and optimization. Employing an actual filter as the physical prototype, this study develops a comprehensive high-frequency equivalent-circuit filter model that integrates the intrinsic parasitic parameters of each component, inter-component coupling, and parasitic effects of indispensable connectors, such as busbars, printed circuit board (PCB) traces, and grounds. This approach enhances high-frequency accuracy and provides a more effective guidance for filter optimization. The methodology utilizes the finite element method to extract parasitic parameters for busbars, grounds, and PCB traces based on the actual filter geometry. Dominant coupling parameters are further characterized via the impedance- and S-parameter-based tests. The model is validated at the filter-component and electric-drive-system levels. To also address the noise spectrum of the electric drive system exceeding regulatory limits at certain frequencies, such as 1.8 and 30 MHz, the original filter is optimized. Two optimization approaches are evaluated through simulation using the proposed modeling method to guide new filter manufacturing. Additionally, experimental results confirm that the optimized filter meets performance requirements, effectively eliminating the over-limit frequencies (1.8 and 30 MHz).

title: Where-to-Learn: Analytical Policy Gradient Directed Exploration for On-Policy Robotic Reinforcement Learning_supp1-3678143.mp4
authors: Hua Chen
publish: N/A
abstract: On-policy reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms have demonstrated great potential in robotic control, where effective exploration is crucial for efficient and high-quality policy learning. However, how to encourage the agent to explore the better trajectories efficiently remains a challenge. Most existing methods incentivize exploration by maximizing the policy entropy or encouraging novel state visiting regardless of the potential state value. We propose a new form of directed exploration that uses analytical policy gradients from a differentiable dynamics model to inject task-aware, physics-guided guidance, thereby steering the agent towards high-reward regions for accelerated and more effective policy learning. We integrate our exploration approach into a widely used on-policy RL algorithm, Proximal Policy Optimization, to test and demonstrate its effectiveness. We conduct extensive benchmark experiments and demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed exploration augmentation method. We further test our approach on a 6-DOF point-foot robot for velocity tracking locomotion, and conduct the simulation test and implement a successful sim-to-real deployment as the ultimate validation.

title: LLM-based text-to-JQL Generation: an automated test using execution and structural indicators
authors: Rayfran Rocha Lima, Irineu Evangelista Cruz de Brito, Jardel da Cunha Nascimento, Luiz Cordovil-Jr
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Evaluating natural language to query language (NL-to-QL) systems cannot rely on string matching alone, especially when queries involve dynamic data or complex query languages. We present APOLLO, an automated test framework that executes generated queries by Large Language Models (LLMs) against a live Jira instance, enabling execution-aware evaluation and detailed error diagnosis across syntax, unsupported features, and value inaccuracies. APOLLO introduces new metrics—field accuracy, value accuracy, and inference accuracy—that capture correctness beyond similarity. Using APOLLO, we evaluated two LLM-based conversion architectures: a retrieval-augmented generator and a specialized API-supported generation model that demonstrated both limitations and alternatives to address the drawbacks of generating dynamic non-pre-trained data. Future work will extend the framework to additional query languages, explore automated remediation strategies, and investigate richer semantic metrics for deeper diagnostic insight.

title: Dynamic Learning Style Classification (DLSC) Model in Adaptive E-Learning Platform
authors: P. Kiruthika, V. Vasanthi, V. Sulochana
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: In this research, we offer an effective and dynamic learner style classification model,” Dynamic Learning Style Classification (DLSC)” which uses hybrid machine learning and deep learning algorithms to describe learners' learning style in an e-learning environment. Rather than depending on traditional learning style models, we provide the “Cumulative Learning Style Model (CLSM)” which is based on implicit and explicit feedback, access records, and content exchanges inside the e-learning platform. The main concept of this methodology is to collect both implicit and explicit data from dynamic e-learning platform and apply it to the DLSC to successfully determine learning styles. The suggested model includes 17 learning types derived from an examination of exiting learning style ideas. To categorize learners' learning styles, a variety of implicit and explicit category attributes are collected, together with AIdriven behavioral tracking features. To improve the precision of learning style identification, explicit variables like learning objectives and preferred content formats are gathered in addition to implicit attributes like navigation patterns, time spent on material, and user interface interactions.

title: Research on Intelligent Obstacle-Avoidance Path Planning for Transport Robots Based on Improved Ant Colony Algorithm
authors: Wu Yang, Jianmin Chen, Weijun Su, Yuqi Chen
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: In the intelligent transformation of manufacturing and logistics, transport robots are key to automated logistics. Traditional methods struggle with sudden obstacles in complex dynamic settings. This paper proposes an IACO-based path planning method, integrating dynamic pheromone updates and multi-objective optimization for real-time obstacle avoidance.

title: CGA-LD: Feedback-Driven Cyclic Generative Augmentation with Layout Diffusion for Active Data Generation in Object Detection
authors: Bogyeong Kim, Hyeongseok Oh, Joonki Paik
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Object detection models often exhibit degraded performance when encountering rare or challenging scenarios, despite relying on large-scale datasets. To address this issue, we propose a novel Feedback-Driven Cyclic Generative Aug-mentation (CGA) framework that dynamically enhances detector robustness through self-regulated data generation. Our approach first measures the bounding box regression loss of each object in a detector, such as Faster R-CNN, to identify the Top-K hardest images and the Top-N most difficult objects. The layout information of these challenging objects is then provided to a LayoutDiffusion model, which generates new images simulating the difficult scenarios for the detector. The generated samples are immediately injected into the subsequent training batches, forming a feedback-driven cyclic learning loop. Experimental results demonstrate that this adaptive augmentation significantly improves mean Average Precision (mAP) and enhances robustness against difficult visual conditions compared to conventional data augmentation methods.

title: Batch Provisioning for Lightpath Establishment in Elastic Optical Networks
authors: Nitesh Kumar, Nikhil Shedshale, Jaisingh Thangaraj
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Existing lightpath provisioning in Elastic Optical Networks (EONs) serves the connections according to the order in which connection requests arrive. Sometimes supplying the light channel for a request produces an ideal result at first, but succeeding traffic makes it suboptimal; this is known as spectrum fragmentation, which may result in considerable blocking or resource waste in following operations. To address these issues, this study offers a batch processing technique for connection request setups in EONs. Our proposed method collects a large number of lightpath requests and executes them in batches by classifying connection requests according to the count of hops, necessary frequency slots, and arrival chronology. In this work, we provide a batch processing approach to evaluate its effectiveness in relation to the blockage likelihood. The simulation results show that the batch processing approach significantly improves resource allocation efficiency and reduces spectrum fragmentation.

title: Real-Time Multi-Defect Detection for Railway Tracks Based on Maintenance-Procedure Knowledge Graph Embedding and Lightweight MobileNetV3
authors: Xiangji Liu, Hao Tang, Liaoyu Chen
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Railway track defects are a key factor affecting train operation safety, and there is an urgent need for intelligent detection systems with high precision and real-time performance. This paper proposes a lightweight MobileNetV3-based real-time multi-defect detection model for tracks, integrating maintenance procedure knowledge graph embeddings. While maintaining the model's lightweight and high-speed inference capabilities, it effectively improves the recognition accuracy and interpretability for complex and rare defects. The model first extracts multi-scale spatial features through a visual perception backbone, then constructs a semantic-guided branch based on a graph attention network (GAT), encoding domain knowledge such as maintenance procedures into a structured graph, and fuses features through a cross-modal attention module, guiding the model to focus on small structural areas with professional semantic relevance. In addition, the model designs a context recalibration module to dynamically adjust feature representations according to actual inspection scenarios, enhancing the model's robustness and adaptability when deployed on edge devices. Experimental results show that the model outperforms existing lightweight detection models in recognition accuracy and recall rate for rare defects.

title: Soft-error measurement for a radiation-hardened RISC-V processor
authors: Masato Isobe, Minoru Watanabe, Nobuya Watanabe
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Under a strong radiation environment such as a melted nuclear power plant, processors must have high radiation tolerance. However, currently available processors are too weak for radiation and easily broken by radiation for a short period. Therefore, we have been developing a triple- modular-redundant RISC-V processor to increase the radiation tolerance. This paper presents the design of a triple modular redundant (TMR) RISC-V processor which was implemented onto a Cyclone V FPGA. In addition, we conducted a real radiation experiment using an 8 kBq americium alpha radiation source.

title: QoS-Aware Multichannel Resource Allocation in LoRaWAN Networks
authors: Rana Bajafar, Louai Al-Awami
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The ALOHA uplink and best-effort service model as implemented by LoRaWAN is inherently ill-suited to realizing differentiated QoS as device populations and traffic heterogeneity increase. This paper proposes a QoS-aware resource-allocation framework based on multichannel parallelism in LoRaWAN networks. The key idea is to exploit the spreading factors’ quasiorthogonality and the independence of frequency channels, which allow viewing the network as a pool of parallel transmission resources, and to dynamically distribute that pool across traffic classes. A proportional, priority-weighted policy allocates resources to classes based on their instantaneous weighted demand, and an efficiency-aware metric within each class biases nodes toward lower-airtime (faster) resources to improve utilization. The resulting design is still fully distributed, low-complexity, yet it consistently reduces latency and increases throughput for high-priority and high-reliability traffic without starving best-effort flows.

title: Predicting Mechanically Induced Thermal Runaway Severity in Multi-Chemistry Lithium-Ion Cells Using LightGBM for Battery Safety Applications
authors: Yash Nileshkumar Desai, Samsaptak Ghosh, Pallavi Bharadwaj
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries are central to electric vehicles and stationary storage, yet their safety under mechanical abuse remains a critical concern. Impacts such as nail penetration or crush can trigger thermal runaway (TR), with outcomes ranging from venting to explosion. Conventional schemes (e.g., EUCAR hazard levels) describe post-event phenomena and lack the predictive resolution needed for early BMS intervention. This work proposes a physically grounded severity framework for mechanically induced TR that defines five levels (SL-1 to SL-5), capturing precursor states through catastrophic failure. The framework is instantiated with a Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) trained on a publicly available Mendeley dataset spanning multiple chemistries; features include voltage, surface temperature, compressive load, and displacement, with systematic preprocessing and validation. On a held-out test set, LightGBM attains 98 % accuracy (macro-F1 0.96), with most errors confined to adjacent classes. To contextualize performance, a tuned <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) baseline using identical features and splits achieves 91 % accuracy (macro-F1 0.83), confirming the advantage of gradient-boosted trees for nonlinear, multi-sensor severity mapping. The resulting predictor provides granular, real-time severity estimation suitable for BMS integration, supporting predictive safety assessment in EV crash scenarios and advancing compliance with battery safety standards.

title: Design and Implementation of the Detection and Summation Module for BPM Signals of the Accelerator RF System
authors: Xiao Qu, Fangfang Wu, Kai Zhang, Guangyao Feng, Fengxia Xu, Yelong Wei, Xiaofang Hu, Yungai Tang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To achieve real-time monitoring of the beam intensity amplitude values in the RF cavities of the storage ring of a synchrotron radiation accelerator, this thesis provides a detailed engineering design of the BPM (Beam Position Monitor) detector summing module to be used in the superconducting main RF system of the Hefei Advanced Light Facility (HALF). The design covers several aspects, including the overall module design, major technical parameter specifications, component selection, circuit design, and process layout. The performance of the developed module was tested, and the results showed that the dynamic range and linearity meet the design specifications, enabling effective detection and summing of four beam intensity signals.

title: Predicting Air Quality Index Using Machine Learning: A case study of Delhi, India
authors: Pratham Chanchlani, Rushirajsinh Solanki, Pawan Bhansari, J. Angel Arul Jothi, Elakkiya Rajasekar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This work aims to develop a predictive model that accurately forecasts Air Quality Index (AQI) levels in Delhi, one of the world's most polluted cities. For this, we explore the application of Machine Learning (ML) models including Linear Regression, Lasso Regression, Ridge Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, AdaBoost, CatBoost, LightGBM and XGBoost. The study uses a comprehensive dataset of daily air quality measurement which contains various pollutants. All models are evaluated using 5-fold cross-validation and a hold-out method. Experimental results reveal that different models excel under the two validation techniques. In the 5-fold cross-validation experiments, the CatBoost model outperforms the other models, achieving an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.9153, MSE of 822.80, MAE of 20.29, RMSE of 28.62, and MAPE of 11.44. For the hold-out validation, the AdaBoost model with parameters optimized through a Randomized Search CV delivers the best performance, yielding a superior R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.9208, MAE of 19.00, and RMSE of 27.13. These results collectively favour the use of advanced ML models to accurately analyze and predict AQI.

title: Novel SPR Biosensor for Hela and Jurkat Cancer Cell Detection
authors: Rahul Rahul, Jaisingh Thangaraj
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This article examines a biosensor using surface plasmon resonance (SPR) for early detection of cancer cells. The proposed biosensor consists of gold (Au) and molybdenum disulfide (MoS2) as a sensing layer to enhance the sensitivity. The Finite Element Method (FEM) is employed in numerical analysis. The wavelength-interrogation approach detects alterations in the refractive index (RI) of human cells. By adjusting the characteristics of fiber and plasmonic materials, we established that this biosensor demonstrated a maximum wavelength sensitivity of 17857.14 nm/RIU and 17083.33 for Jurkat and HeLa cells respectively.

title: AI-Assisted Teaching Practices in Indonesia: Reflective Insights from Teacher Training on Prompting and Assessment Literacy
authors: Marisca Revani Putri
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: This paper presents a reflective-descriptive qualitative analysis of an AI-assisted teacher-training initiative conducted in three distinct regions of Indonesia in 2025 as part of the Microsoft elevAIte 2025 program supporting national digital transformation in education. The training involved approximately 300 teachers from diverse educational levels and sought to integrate generative-AI tools into instructional design while addressing challenges related to prompting, assessment, and critical thinking. Using a process-based categorization and thematic patterning approach, data from workshop activities, participant reflections, and facilitator observations were analyzed to capture teachers' evolving engagement with AI. Findings indicate that while regional differences in digital readiness were modest, teachers' critical engagement during iterative prompting played a central role in effective AI use. Participants who refined prompts through repeated reflection demonstrated increasing metacognitive awareness and recognized AI as a cognitive partner in pedagogy. The study also identifies persistent dilemmas in assessing AI-assisted student work, underscoring the need for process-oriented and transparent evaluation frameworks. The findings highlight how reflection and collaboration can transform training programs into innovative models of AI-assisted teacher learning. This study contributes to global discussions on AIintegrated pedagogy, offering grounded evidence from developing contexts and reaffirming that meaningful AI use begins with critical reflection and ethical awareness.

title: Socially Immersive Virtual Spaces and Student Well-Being: Insights into Mental Health, Belonging, and Social Connectedness in a Metaverse
authors: Manuel B. Garcia, Michael Agyemang Adarkwah, Ahmed Tlili, Don Eliseo Lucero-Prisno, Mohamed Mustaf Ahmed, Precious S. Garcia
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: As higher education increasingly adopts hybrid learning models, understanding the role of digital environments in supporting student well-being has become essential. While prior studies have examined the clinical and instructional applications of the metaverse, little attention has been given to its informal, socially immersive uses. This study explores how voluntary participation in a beach-themed, off-campus metaverse environment relates to students' perceived stress, mental health, social connectedness, and academic belonging. Using a quantitative observational design, data were collected from validated psychological scales and behavioral engagement metrics. Correlational analyses revealed that greater time spent in the metaverse was significantly associated with lower perceived stress and higher social connectedness. Multiple regression indicated that recurring peer interactions and event attendance were significant predictors of academic belonging, while time spent alone was not. ANOVA results showed that students with higher levels of metaverse engagement reported significantly greater perceived social support, with a trend toward lower psychological distress. These findings highlight the psychosocial value of informal metaverse spaces. When designed to support peer interaction and voluntary participation, such environments can serve as digital third places that promote emotional resilience, connection, and belonging in hybrid academic settings. Overall, this study extends the current literature by foregrounding the affective and social affordances of metaverse environments beyond structured therapeutic or instructional contexts.

title: Kaamready:A Job Application Platform for the Uneducated and Unemployed
authors: Manoj C
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: KaamReady emerges as a groundbreaking mobile application aimed at tackling the prevalent issue of unemployment among job seekers in India, particularly focusing on individuals between the ages of 20 and 50. The app caters to a diverse range of educational backgrounds, extending its reach even to those who have not completed their 10th standard education. Its main objective is to provide an easily accessible and inclusive platform for individuals to discover suitable job opportunities.To enhance user experience, KaamReady offers various features such as support for regional languages, voice-activated search functionality, and a straightforward profile creation process. These features are specifically designed to assist users with limited digital skills in navigating the application effortlessly. The core function of the platform lies in connecting job seekers with local employers through skill-based matching, displaying nearby job listings, and sending real-time alerts for new job openings. Employers, on the other hand, benefit from tools that facilitate candidate shortlisting, bulk hiring capabilities, and direct communication channels like chat and video interviews. Inclusivity and transparency are further promoted through candidate ratings and verified profiles, fostering a sense of trust within the community.KaamReady's overarching goal is to bridge the gap between unemployed individuals and potential job opportunities, ultimately empowering dividuals, reducing unemployment rates, and driving economic progress across both urban and rural areas of India. As a harbinger of social change, the app seeks to instill hope and deliver practical solutions to the millions of individuals in search of meaningful employment.

title: On-Device-First Hybrid LLM Inference on AI-PCs: Closing the Enterprise GenAI Divide
authors: Sultana Begum, Kris Fleming, Todd Lewellen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: AI-PCs and modern PCs equipped with capable CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs now make on-device inference for small language models (SLMs) practical across many enterprise workloads. This enables assistants that are low-latency, privacy-preserving, and resilient to network conditions, while frontier cloud models still offer higher accuracy, richer tool use, and stronger multimodality than is practical to run on every endpoint. This paper surveys and organizes the design space for an on-device-first hybrid architecture in which an SLM on the AI-PC handles most requests by default, and escalation to cloud models is reserved for difficult, tool-heavy, or long-context tasks. We synthesize advances in compact local on-device optimized model design including architectures that mix self-attention with state-space models (SSMs), parameter-efficient adaptation, multimodal and structure-aware retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and long-horizon memory, and connect these to hardware and serving optimizations such as quantization, mixed precision, and KV-cache management. Building on this, we propose routing and reporting patterns that make latency, energy, cost, and privacy explicit design parameters, and outline a total-cost-of-ownership view that clarifies when AI-PC–centric deployments outperform all-cloud strategies. We argue that such edge-first hybrid designs offer a practical path to narrowing the "GenAI Divide," in which impressive pilots fail to translate into durable, production-grade business impact.

title: Intelligent Diagnosis Method for Partial Discharge of Substation Equipment Based on Multi-Source Sensor Data Fusion
authors: Yongde Pan, Tuo Li, Hao Guo
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Partial discharge (PD) is one of the key indicators reflecting the insulation degradation of substation equipment, and its accurate diagnosis is crucial to ensure the safe and stable operation of the power grid. Aiming at the problems of single sensor data limitation, low diagnosis accuracy and poor antiinterference ability in traditional PD diagnosis methods, an intelligent diagnosis method for PD of substation equipment based on multi-source sensor data fusion is proposed. First, multi-source sensors (ultrasonic, ultra-high frequency, transient earth voltage, etc.) are used to collect PD characteristic data, and a series of preprocessing operations such as denoising, normalization and feature extraction are performed on the original data to construct a multi-dimensional feature set. Then, a multi-level data fusion framework is designed, which integrates data-level fusion based on improved wavelet transform, feature-level fusion based on attention mechanism and decision-level fusion based on D-S evidence theory. Finally, a hybrid intelligent diagnosis model combining deep residual network (ResNet) and support vector machine (SVM) is established to realize the accurate identification of PD types. The experimental results show that compared with the traditional single-source data diagnosis method and single intelligent algorithm, the proposed method has higher diagnosis accuracy, stronger anti-interference ability and better generalization performance. It can effectively identify different types of PD defects of substation equipment, providing a reliable technical support for the condition-based maintenance of power systems.

title: Energy-Efficient AI Video Analytics with MPEG-5 LCEVC: GPU-Optimised Results
authors: Guendalina Cobianchi, Manzoor Razaak, Ben Cope, Palanivel Guruvareddiar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The scaling of visual Artificial Intelligence (AI) for applications like smart cities and retail analytics is increasingly constrained by data pipeline inefficiencies rather than model computation. Conventional video codecs necessitate full-resolution decoding and extensive data movement, resulting in unnecessary power expenditure and GPU under-utilization. This paper evaluates MPEG-5 Low Complexity Enhancement Video Coding (LCEVC) [1] as a hierarchical solution that enables "base-first" operation, allowing AI inference to process only the low-resolution base stream. Using Intel’s Deep Learning Streamer framework on an integrated GPU architecture (Intel Core Ultra 7 258V), we quantify LCEVC’s impact in a high-density, eight Ultra High Definition (UHD) parallel AI inference stream environment compared to native AVC. Results confirm LCEVC-enhanced AVC achieves up to 3.4x pipeline speed-up, 71% lower average power consumption per channel, and a crucial 83% reduction in memory bandwidth utilization. These findings position LCEVC as a practical enabler for energy-efficient, high-density edge AI systems.

title: Improving Multi-Class OCT Classification under Class Imbalance via Class-Weight Balancing
authors: Chih-Ling Tseng, Pin-Han Wen, Cheng-Kai Lu, Jia-Kang Wang, Tzu-Lun Huang, Cheng-Hung Dixson Lin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Class imbalance is a critical issue in multi-class classification, particularly in medical image analysis, where rare diseases are insufficient. In this study, an improved class weight balancing (CWB) formulation, modified from the baseline method in [1], was proposed and applied to an eight-class imbalanced classification task for Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) images, to solve the imbalance data issue and enhance the performance. The modified CWB formula, derived from conventional weighting strategies, is applied to various convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures. In addition, basic data augmentation (DA) techniques are employed to address the few-shot problem in rare disease classes. The results show that AOCT-NET combined with the modified CWB and basic DA achieves 78.35% of accuracy and 0.7221 of F1-score, outperforming other lightweight CNN models, and demonstrating improvements over the original CWB method. Furthermore, the CWB method effectively addresses data imbalance, particularly in lightweight CNN models. Future work will focus on further enhancing the recognition of rare diseases and mitigating dataset limitations.

title: Low-Latency Architecture for Open-Circuit Fault Diagnosis in Electric Drive Inverter Power Switches
authors: Francisco Togni, Guillermo R. Bossio, Luis E. Venghi, Facundo Aguilera
publish: 2026 Argentine Conference on Electronics (CAE)
abstract: This work presents an FPGA-based strategy for detecting and isolating open-circuit faults in the power switches of electric-drive inverters. The method exploits the characteristic behavior of phase currents within the zero-crossing region, combining lightweight filtering, hysteresis-based detection, a zerocrossing time estimator, and derivative-sign analysis. Faults are identified by comparing the estimated and measured dwell times in the hysteresis band, enabling rapid detection and accurate switch-level isolation. Implemented on an Artix-7 FPGA with fixed-point arithmetic, the system achieves low computational cost and short diagnostic latency. Experimental results confirm robust performance under multiple fault scenarios, supporting integration into fault-tolerant drive systems.

title: An Ultrawideband Meta-Absorber for the UAV Based Mining Applications in Q, E and W Bands
authors: Aks Raj, Ashutosh Kumar, Ravi Kumar Gangwar, P. M. Sarun
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This paper presents an ultrawideband meta-absorber engineered for efficient suppression of electromagnetic energy across the 50–110 GHz millimeter-wave spectrum, with specific emphasis on UAV based mining applications. The absorber mitigates multipath reflections, scattering, and signal fading that degrade wireless links in mining tunnels and confined industrial environments thereby improving communication stability between UAVs and ground transmitters. Broadband absorption is achieved through a hybrid mechanism combining resistive loading and a four-subunit super-cell architecture. Chip resistors and air spacers have been used to improve the absorption bandwidth. The meta-absorber is realized using patterned copper on a PTFE substrate backed by a copper ground plane, ensuring zero transmission and robust resonance excitation. The compact cell dimensions of 0.41 λ × 0.41 λ × 0.0837 λ at 50 GHz enables lightweight deployment. With its broad absorption bandwidth, polarization stability, and mechanically simple structure, the proposed absorber is well suited for UAV-based mining operations and mmWave communication stability.

title: Research on the Application of Reinforcement Learning in Intelligent English Writing Correction
authors: Hongmiao Yuan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the shortcomings of traditional intelligent English writing correction systems, which rely on rule matching or supervised learning and struggle with complex grammatical error handling, semantic coherence assessment, and personalized feedback generation, this study proposes a dynamic reward-based hierarchical reinforcement learning algorithm (DR-HRL) and applies it to intelligent English writing correction. This algorithm employs a hierarchical architecture consisting of top-level decision-making, mid-level task execution, and bottom-level action selection to achieve fine-grained correction decisions. It also constructs a dynamic reward mechanism that integrates grammatical error features, semantic coherence, and learner-specific information to improve correction accuracy and targeting. Experiments were conducted on an Intel Xeon Gold 6248 server using a dataset of 50,000 English texts spanning different learning levels and writing types, comparing DR-HRL with algorithms such as BERT-ErrorCorrection and DQN-Writing. Results show that DR-HRL achieved grammatical error detection accuracy, recall, and F1 score of 88.6%, 85.4%, and 87.0%, respectively, representing improvements of 6.3, 6.9, and 6.6 percentage points over BERT-ErrorCorrection. Its semantic coherence assessment accuracy reached 82.8%, a 9.3 percentage point improvement over DQN-Writing. Its feedback generation relevance score reached 8.9, surpassing all compared algorithms. Ablation experiments showed that removing dynamic rewards or the hierarchical architecture reduced the algorithm's F1 score by over 6.8 percentage points, validating the innovative and effective design of the algorithm. The intelligent grading system based on DR-HRL can generate accurate and personalized feedback in real time, providing efficient technical support for English writing instruction.

title: E-Cart Automation for Expiring Products: A Sustainable Approach to Food-Waste Reduction
authors: A. Soundaryan, A. Sathya, K. Poongodi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The challenges associated with food waste in today's retail environment include: (1) lack of inventory visibility; (2) use of fixed pricing models; and (3) a lack of consumer awareness of products nearing expiration dates [3] [9]. Therefore, the objective of this paper is to propose an automated cart decision-making system (E-Cart Automation for Expiring Products), which utilizes artificial intelligence (AI) to automate the identification, prediction, pricing and recommendation of products nearing their expiration date. The AI-based automation system includes three main components: (i) a Long-Short Term Memory (LSTM)-based freshness prediction model, utilizing historical product data to predict a product's remaining shelf-life with greater accuracy than current methods [12]; (ii) a Deep Q-Learning (DQL) based dynamic pricing model that adjusts prices of near-expiring products on a real-time basis [18]; and (iii) a personalized recommendation component that takes into account each customer's past purchasing behavior [21]. In addition, a real-time alert/reporting module has been designed to provide both store management and customers with information regarding inventory movement and potential price discounts as they become available [27]. Results from testing demonstrate that utilization of the proposed model resulted in a 35 % decrease in food waste while increasing sales of near-expiration products by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 2 \%}$</tex> compared to existing retail practices [30]. Overall, the research presented here provides a viable and sustainable AI-based platform for reducing food waste through both environmental and economic means in the retail industry [33].

title: Study of Peak Hopping Algorithm for Maximum Power Point Tracking Under Partial Shading Conditions for a Photovoltaic Array
authors: Shashwat Dhurandhar, S. Kayalvizhi
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The paper investigates the prowess of the Peak Hopping Maximum Power Point Tracking (PH-MPPT) method for solar photovoltaic (PV) arrays operating in partially shaded conditions (PSC). A <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9 \times 9$</tex> series-connected PV array is modeled in MATLAB/Simulink, and the PH-MPPT method is applied to test its capacity to reliably locate the global maximum power point (GMPP). Unlike conventional MPPT algorithms, which are commonly stuck at local maxima, PH-MPPT uses a deterministic two-stage strategy. Simulation findings for several complicated shading conditions show that PH-MPPT delivers fast convergence, proper GMPP detection, and lower steady-state oscillations. The PH-MPPT has a tracking accuracy of 93.0835 % and a tracking time of 0.108 seconds. The findings show that PH-MPPT is an innovative approach for increasing the energy production of PV systems subjected to PSC.

title: Data-Driven Construction of Static Voltage Security Region in Distribution Networks
authors: Jingxuan Xu, Wenxuan Zhao, Chunming Liu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The penetration rate of distributed generation, represented by photovoltaic power generation, is steadily increasing in distribution networks. As distribution networks expand in scale, the safety and stability constraints they must account for grow increasingly complex. Distribution network power flow calculations face challenges such as heightened nonlinearity and substantial computational burdens. This paper proposes a data-driven method for constructing static voltage security region in distribution networks. This approach enables security region construction without relying on precise network parameters, simplifies operational constraints, and significantly accelerates the solution of operational optimization problems. Finally, using the IEEE33 node system as an example, the effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through simulation analysis and comparative studies.

title: Integration of Optical Sensing and Deep Learning for Reliable Defect Detection in Smart Packaging Systems
authors: Ya-Chen Liu, Amare Mulatie Dehnaw, Cheng-Kai Yao, Peng-Chun Peng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This study presents an integrated approach that combines optical sensing and deep learning for reliable defect detection in eco-friendly molded pulp packaging. The You Only Look Once (YOLO) version 8 deep learning model was applied to automate post-production inspection and improve the consistency of manual checks. The process involved image acquisition, annotation, dataset partitioning, model training, and simulated edge deployment. The proposed model achieved a validation accuracy of 98.75%, demonstrating a strong ability to identify surface defects. This result confirms the effectiveness of optical sensing–based deep learning for real-time and precise inspection in sustainable packaging manufacturing.

title: Construction of a New Generation of Primary School Artificial Intelligence Curriculum System Based on PEST Model: Innovation Path and Practice Exploration
authors: Jiayu Li, Haiguang Fang, Zhihong Zheng, Yang Deng
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: The rapid advancement of new artificial intelligence technologies plays a crucial role in driving the digital transformation of the education sector. It is imperative to introduce AI courses in primary schools. Implementing AI courses in primary schools is essential for enhancing the AI literacy of teachers and students and nurturing outstanding innovative talents. Currently, there are no national standards specifically addressing AI courses to guide the planning and implementation of AI teaching content at various educational levels. Instead, local curricula often draw on information technology courses for reference. As a novel learning content, AI-related teaching theories, content, and methods require further research. Therefore, it is necessary to develop and implement a new generation of AI course systems for primary and secondary schools in the intelligent era to meet the educational needs of this age group.

title: Time-Varying Causal Graph Learning for Distribution Network Scheduling
authors: Xiang Li, Meng Ye, Yipeng Xue, Zeming Chen, Zhenhuang Chen, Qianyu Zhao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The increasing integration of distributed energy resources (DERs) in active distribution networks necessitates efficient multi-timescale scheduling strategies to balance economic operation and system stability. However, existing deep reinforcement learning (DRL) approaches often lack interpretability and struggle to capture dynamic physical dependencies among DERs. This paper proposes a novel Causal Graph-Guided Dual-Timescale DDPG framework for explainable and adaptive scheduling. An online learning algorithm is developed to construct time-varying causal graphs that capture the evolving control-response sensitivities (i.e., physical causality) among DERs. Building upon this structure, a causal graph-guided attention mechanism enables selective feature aggregation, while an adaptive dual-timescale critic network dynamically balances short-term safety constraints and long-term economic objectives. Simulation results on the IEEE 33-bus system demonstrate that the proposed method significantly reduces the voltage violation rate to 35.4 %, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines including Soft Actor-Critic (SAC). Crucially, a physical feasibility analysis reveals that 88.2 % of the residual violations are physically unavoidable due to system capacity limits, indicating that the agent achieves near-optimal control with a gap of only 4.2 percentage points to the theoretical physical limit. Furthermore, the learned causal graphs align with the grid topology, providing intuitive interpretability for practical deployment.

title: Vision-Based Automatic Irrigation System for Greenhouse Tomato Using Leaf Keypoints
authors: Riku Ohnuma, Makoto Koike, Hiroshi Mineno
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Approximately 70% of the world’s freshwater is used for agriculture, making efficient water management in agriculture a critical challenge for achieving sustainability. In tomato cultivation, sensor-based automatic irrigation methods are widely adopted, however they require costly sensors and specialized knowledge for implementation and maintenance. Therefore, this study proposes a vision-based automatic irrigation system that determines irrigation timing based on leaf wilting quantified using keypoint detection technology (YOLO-Pose). The system consists of an HDR camera, environmental sensors (temperature, humidity, and illuminance), and a Raspberry Pi 4B, enabling low-cost implementation without specialized knowledge. In a tomato cultivation field trial, the proposed system achieved a 1.53 Brix increase in sugar content and a 12.6% reduction in water usage, confirming its effectiveness.

title: K-Means Clustering and CLIP-Based Semantic Embeddings for Scalable Video Content Filtering
authors: R Senthil Prabha, Sanjay A C, Sai Krishnan J, Raghav Somasundharam P L, Gokul K, Sanjay S
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The “K-Means Clustering and CLIP-Based Semantic Embeddings for Scalable Video Content Filtering” system is a machine learning based solution to the growing demand for automated video moderation. The vast increase in digital content in recent years has necessitated filtering out the explicit, violent, and inappropriate content in order to maintain safe online platforms. With this approach, meaningful semantic features are extracted from video frames using CLIP embeddings and these are then clustered using Kmeans clustering into appropriate categories. New videos arriving have their frames analyzed, assigned to existing clusters, or dynamically clustered into a new category. This adaptive classification requires little manual intervention and thus ensures scalable and automated moderation to increase accuracy of content filtering. Unsupervised learning for realtime content-based classification would thus, improve efficiency and reliability of the proposed video moderation framework.

title: Enhancing ARM-based Compute Core Performance using Task Priority and Proximity-Induced Thermal Thresholds in Heterogeneous Core Architecture
authors: Suranjan Chakraborty, Chintakunta Ratna Kumari, Nitesh Jain, Vishnu Vardhan Kasiliya Sudarsan, Mallikarjunan M
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Modern microprocessors featuring multi-core, multi-cluster CPU architectures incorporate multiple digital thermal sensors in heterogeneous cores, positioned within micrometer-scale proximity. Under high computational load, localized temperature elevation in an active core induces significant thermal coupling effects in adjacent cores, necessitating proactive thermal management. Experimental evaluation reveals that ARM-architecture based processors demonstrate up to a 200% temperature rise in neighboring cores that remain idle, underscoring the critical impact of spatial thermal interactions.The proposed solution establishes a "Proximity-Induced" thermal threshold for each compute core, dynamically governed by task priority. Prior to a core attaining its thermal limit, adjacent cores executing lower-priority tasks are proactively transitioned to reduced power states. This preemptive strategy mitigates premature thermal escalation in cores executing high-priority task, resulting in an observed increase of more than 30% in additional thermal headroom.

title: Intelligent Coffee Farming: Implementation of Agro-Artificial Intelligence-of-Things in the Soil Nutrient Management System
authors: Jasper M. Garcia, Jonardo R. Asor
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Autonomous Systems (ICoIAS)
abstract: The growing demand for sustainable agriculture underscores the need for intelligent systems that enhance input efficiency and productivity. This study presents the development and evaluation of an Agro-Artificial Intelligence of Things (Agro-AIoT) system designed to improve soil nutrient management in smallholder coffee farms in Brgy. Mabato, Calamba City, Laguna. Addressing challenges such as declining soil fertility, inefficient fertilization, and limited access to actionable data, the proposed system integrates real-time sensing, forecasting, and AI-powered recommendations to support precision agriculture. The SoilSense system consists of a solar-powered soil monitoring device, a fertigation module, a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) forecasting model, and an AI-based recommendation engine. A web application serves as the user interface for accessing sensor data and AI-generated nutrient prescriptions. The device maintained an uptime of 91.29% during a 78-day field deployment. Sensor accuracy was confirmed through paired samples t-tests, while the LSTM model demonstrated strong predictive performance with an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.9840 and a MAPE of 7.54%. AIgenerated recommendations were 93.33% aligned with expert assessments. The system demonstrates strong potential for enabling data-driven soil nutrient management in coffee farming.

title: GA-tuned Reinforcement Learning Nonlinear Model Predictive Control for Conical Tank Systems
authors: Kiruba R, Boobalan J, V. Radhika
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Conical tank designs' intrinsic nonlinear dynamics make controlling liquid levels a significant difficulty in industrial processes. For such systems, conventional linear control procedures frequently prove inadequate, highlighting the necessity for sophisticated control methodologies. This paper presents a new control system that combines Nonlinear Model Predictive Control (NMPC) for optimal trajectory tracking and constraint management, Reinforcement Learning (RL) for adaptive policy development, and Genetic Algorithms (GA) for parameter optimization. The proposed GA-tuned RL NMPC strategy is to maximize each component's strengths: RL provides adaptive guidance to the NMPC, allowing for dynamic adjustments to control objectives; NMPC guarantees optimal control actions while adhering to system restrictions; and GA fine-tunes the RL agent's parameters, allowing it to create robust control policies. Conical tank system conceptual simulations are shown to show how well the suggested approach works to achieve improved control performance. This includes enhanced setpoint tracking, reduced oscillations, and improved disturbance rejection, when compared to a baseline NMPC. The improvements in key performance measures are highlighted by tabular numerical data. With possible uses ranging from liquid level control to a wider spectrum of industrial systems, this research advances the creation of intelligent and adaptive control solutions for intricate nonlinear processes.

title: Utilizing Chain of Thought to Generate Code from Challenging Programming Requirements
authors: EmanAlofi, RehabIbrahim, AmnehAlAbdi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: With the rise in using Large Language Models (LLMs), research proved the feasibility of using LLMs in various software engineering tasks including code generation. However, it remains a major challenge to ensure the correctness and the efficiency of the LLM-generated code. This paper applies the "Chain-of-thought" prompting approach to improve GPT performance in code generation and its ability to convert coding requirements from HackerRank and LeetCode online coding platforms into correct Python, C++, and JavaScript code. We evaluated the performance of GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 across 558 programming challenges on HackerRank and LeetCode platforms. Our evaluation framework employed a comprehensive test case validation system, where each generated solution was tested against predefined input-output pairs, with success determined by exact matches between expected and actual outputs. The evaluation revealed significant variations in success rates across platforms (HackerRank: 46.83% vs. LeetCode: 7.35%) and programming languages (Python leading with 28.6% overall success rate). These results demonstrate that while the chain of thought approach enhances code generation accuracy, particularly for structured programming tasks, significant challenges remain in handling complex algorithmic problems.

title: Test Bench of Energy Storage System in UAVs to Maximize the Efficiency
authors: Tellapati Anuradha Devi, G Jagadish, Jatoth Rahul, K V Govardhan Rao
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: A proof-of-concept test bench has been developed to evaluate the electrical drive systems of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), with a focus on optimizing energy storage for maximum efficiency. UAVs, widely used in civil and military sectors, demand reliable and efficient power systems. This study analyzes battery-powered electric propulsion, incorporating regenerative braking to enhance energy recovery. The test bench assesses performance across various UAV types, including fixed-wing and rotary drones, under different load conditions. Results highlight the effectiveness of multi-motor drones, which dominate applications such as aerial photography, fault detection in transmission lines, and precision agriculture.

title: Integrating AI Development Tools in the Classroom: A Multi-Tool Case Study on Learning Outcomes and Code Quality
authors: Antonio Carlos Bento, Marcos Ribeiro Pereira-Barretto, José Reinaldo Silva, Sérgio Camacho-León, Elsa Yolanda Torres-Torres
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: This study investigates the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI)-assisted development tools—GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, Cursor, Gemini, and Replit—within an innovative project-based software engineering course. Through a 10-week case study with 25 undergraduate students developing a CRM system, we employed a mixed-methods approach to evaluate both quantitative efficiency gains and qualitative learning outcomes. The results demonstrate that AI tools can accelerate development, yielding 30-40% time savings in prototyping and debugging tasks. However, the study crucially reveals that these efficiency benefits are contingent on structured human oversight; without it, code quality and architectural integrity can be compromised. Pedagogically, the tools served as effective catalysts for skill development but also highlighted risks of over-reliance. This research contributes a practical framework for the balanced integration of AI in engineering education, positioning these tools as powerful assistants rather than replacements.
