The approach supports Sustainable Development Goal 4 (Quality Education), a claim bolstered by a 96% student satisfaction rate, indicating that structured AI adoption can enhance both educational equity and engagement while preparing students for a transformed technological landscape.

title: Multi-Object Tracking with Nonlinear Motion Modeling and Discriminative Appearance Features
authors: Xi Xu, HongFei Wang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Multi-object tracking (MOT) is a fundamental computer vision task that establishes target associations across consecutive video frames, playing a critical role in applications like autonomous driving and artificial intelligence. However, MOT remains highly challenging, especially when targets are small, move rapidly, or appear in crowded environments. A representative MOT algorithm, DeepSORT, faces limitations in both accuracy and efficiency due to its reliance on outdated detection and data association modules. This study proposes an enhanced DeepSORT version by improving its two core components: object detection and data association. Specifically, we replace the original Faster R-CNN detector with YOLOE, which achieves significantly higher detection accuracy and faster inference speed. For data association, we introduce a lowconfidence association module and an improved Kalman filter to further boost tracking performance. Furthermore, we incorporate a Fisher Linear Discriminant (FLD) strategy to enhance the discriminability of features for visually similar targets. This strategy effectively increases the accuracy of appearance matching and reduces frequent identity switches among pedestrians. Overall, our improvements substantially enhance the accuracy and robustness of DeepSORT in complex tracking scenarios.

title: Analysis and Design of AC Electronic Load for Testing of Power Electronic Converters
authors: Karthikeyan P, Kuncham Sateesh Kumar, Kumaresan Natarajan, Panchalai V. N.
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Electrical Systems play a major role in everyday life and hence it is very important to test their performance and reliability before deployment or large scale production. But testing of electrical equipment in real time environment is not practical. It is expensive to create a safe and real time lab setup. Thus load banks are employed to simulate real time operating conditions and analyze system behavior under controlled environments. This paper deals with the design of AC load banks for a given set of system requirements. Mathematical computations were done using Python and the developed system was verified by simulating it in MATLAB - Simulink.

title: GAN FET Based Dual Active Bridge Converter for Supercapacitor Integration in Electric Vehicle
authors: Sudip Sarkar, Samir Hazra
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: In electric vehicles (EVs), a hybrid energy storage system (HESS) can take advantage of batteries for long-term energy supply and supercapacitors for quick power needs, such as acceleration or braking. This paper presents the design and development of a GaN FET-based dual active bridge (DAB) converter for the integration of the supercapacitor into HESS for EV applications. A semi-active topology with capacitor control is implemented, supported by a control strategy enabling efficient bidirectional power transfer for charging and discharging the supercapacitor. The use of GaN FETs improves switching performance, reduces losses, and improves the overall efficiency of the converter. Simulation and experimental validation confirm the fast dynamic response of the converter and its suitability for high-performance automotive applications. Furthermore, the developed architecture lays a scalable foundation for the transition towards other advanced topologies for hybrid energy storage systems.

title: Research on Estimation Method of Occlusion Target Range Parameters for Low Signal-to-Noise Ratio GM-APD LiDAR
authors: Rong Li, Chunyang Wang, Da Xie, Xuyang Wei
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Geiger-Mode Avalanche Photo Diode (GM-APD) LiDAR, with its single-photon sensitivity, significantly enhances the three-dimensional imaging and reconnaissance performance of targets in occlusion environments. However, in complex occlusion scenarios, laser signals are susceptible to multiple scattering, resulting in the superposition of various echoes within a single pixel field of view, which makes target range information difficult to extract. Especially under low signal-to-noise Ratio (SNR) conditions, useful signals are severely submerged by noise, further exacerbating inaccuracies in range estimation. To address these issues, this paper proposes an occluded-target range parameter estimation method based on the Neyman-Pearson criterion. The method formulates multi-peak echo detection as a binary hypothesis testing problem and constructs a likelihood ratio detection model based on the statistical characteristics of GM-APD, enabling reliable decision-making for target signal intervals. Further combining the K-means clustering and differential analysis methods, the distance values of multi-peak echoes are estimated pixel-by-pixel, thereby accurately extracting the range information of occluded targets. The simulation results show that when the signal-to-noise ratio is as low as −12 dB, the algorithm proposed in this paper improves the restoration degree by at least 12.72 % and the variance by at least one order of magnitude compared with other comparison algorithms, which can effectively improve the accuracy of the target range information under occlusion conditions.

title: Ultra-Short-Term Wind Power Forecasting Based on Adaptive Time-Frequency Decoupling and Dual-Channel Fusion
authors: Feiyang Ni, Tao Wang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To address the strong non-stationarity inherent in wind power generation sequences and the limitations of conventional deep learning models in capturing global linear evolution trends, this paper proposes an ultra-short-term wind power forecasting method based on Optimized Variational Mode Decomposition (OVMD) and a deep linear fusion network, termed WTG-FusionNet. OVMD is employed as the core time-frequency analysis technique to adaptively decompose the original non-stationary wind power series into multiple narrowband intrinsic mode functions (IMFs) under a variational constraint framework, enabling effective separation of long-term evolution trends and short-term fluctuations in the time-frequency domain. Based on the decomposed components, a master-slave parallel prediction architecture is constructed. The nonlinear branch integrates a multi-scale temporal convolutional network (MS-TCN), a bidirectional gated recurrent unit (BiGRU), and an improved self-attention mechanism (ISA) to comprehensively extract multi-scale temporal features. In parallel, the linear branch explicitly models the global linear trend through an autoregressive (AR) pathway, thereby compensating for the fitting bias of deep nonlinear models. The final prediction is obtained via a residual fusion mechanism that integrates the outputs of both branches. Experimental results on a wind power dataset from the State Grid Corporation of China demonstrate that the proposed model achieves a coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) of 0.94 on the test set, with the root mean square error (RMSE) and other evaluation metrics outperforming those of benchmark models. These results validate the effectiveness of the proposed fusion architecture in handling highly non-stationary wind power time series. This study provides both theoretical significance and practical value for improving the reliability and economic efficiency of renewable energy grid integration and dispatching.

title: Design and Simulation of a Resonant Inductive Wireless Charging System for Electric Vehicles
authors: Kalicherla Reddeppa, Hema Rani P
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Wireless power transfer systems (WPTSs) with inductive coupling are advantageously used to charge the battery pack of electric vehicles. These systems consist of coil coupling, power supply circuitry on the transmitting side, and power conditioning circuitry on the receiving side. In inductive coupling, resonant Wireless power transfer (RWPT) systems play a crucial role in achieving efficient power transfer from source to load by maintaining resonance and utilizing advanced power electronics to control the charging process. Wireless charging for electric vehicles is promising because of its convenience and elimination of charging cables. However, existing WPT systems face challenges such as high resonant peaks, reduced efficiency, and limited builtin safety features. In the event of a short circuit on the load side, a high current can flow through the primary side, risking damage to the inverter circuit. This paper proposes a method to safeguard the circuitry by regulating the RMS current on the primary side. The control strategy is based on maintaining constant current (CC) mode by controlling the secondary side current, which in turn regulates the primary side current indirectly. This eliminates the need for an additional DC-DC converter and reduces the number of components on the secondary side, leading to a costeffective solution suitable for integration into electric vehicles.

title: An Image-Text Cross-Modal Retrieval Method Based on Multimodal Feature Fusion and Contrastive Quantization
authors: Xusheng Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: The cross-modal retrieval model proposed in this paper uses ViT and BERT as encoders, adopts bidirectional cross-attention and gating fusion to obtain 512-dimensional shared representations, and realizes efficient retrieval through 64-bit hashing. Meanwhile, the model jointly optimizes ITC, Triplet (with hard negative samples), and quantization losses, enabling stable convergence and good separability even during small-batch training. Additionally, optional CLIP distillation further stabilizes the representations. Moreover, the Tkinter-based interface supports one-click indexing and Top-K retrieval, and visualizes the training process in real time.

title: Design and Simulation of Voltage Oriented Controller Based Vienna Rectifier
authors: Surayya N, Manju A S, Aby Joseph
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The design and simulation of a voltage-oriented controller (VOC)-based vienna rectifier are presented in this paper. In the majority of industrial applications, such as electric vehicle charging stations, welding power supplies, and power quality enhancement, the Vienna rectifier has grown in popularity. The Vienna rectifier is a three-level unidirectional Active Rectifier topology that has low total harmonic distortion (THD), high power density, and high efficiency. The control strategy uses a synchronous reference frame-based voltage-oriented control to regulate the DC-link voltage with unity power factor and low total harmonic distortion (THD) on the AC side. The results of simulation of the 5kVA Vienna rectifier with MATLAB/Simulink are displayed.

title: FuILT-S: Full Chip ILT With Unified Boundary Healing and SRAF Co-Optimization
authors: Shuo Yin, ZIYANG YU, Wenqian Zhao, Lancheng Zou, Jiahao Xu, Su Zheng, Yuzhe Ma, Tsungyi Ho, Bei Yu
publish: ACM Transactions on Design Automation of Electronic Systems
abstract: None

title: STFO-Diff: A State Feedback Optimization-Based Diffusion Framework for Multimodal Medical Image Fusion
authors: Yu Cheng, Han Zhang, Yufang Dong, Mingjing Han, Xiongwen Quan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Diffusion models demonstrate strong potential for improving image quality and preserving fine-grained details in medical image fusion. However, the existing diffusion-based fusion methods lack modality-specific feature estimation during the iterative denoising process, resulting in the gradual entanglement of features. We propose a state-feedback optimization-based diffusion (STFO-Diff) framework, which can extract the generative state and enable accurate feedback learning, thereby achieving fine-grained modeling over modality-specific image generation. Specifically, we design a state measurement module (SMM) comprising two key components: a sparse basis decomposer (SBD) and a modality perception decomposer (MPD). The SBD provides a quantifiable physical representation of the fusion state by decomposing the intermediate fused image into distinct components. In parallel, the MPD provides a modality-aware evaluation by estimating the preservation and completeness of modality-specific information. In STFO-Diff, the estimated states are leveraged to optimize and supervise the reverse diffusion denoising process, where a state feedback mechanism adaptively maintains the modality integrity of modality information in the fused image. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, demonstrating its superior performance in medical image fusion tasks. The source code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/zhanglabNKU/STFO-DIff</uri>

title: TinyUSFM: Towards Compact and Efficient Ultrasound Foundation Models
authors: Chen Ma, Jing Jiao, Shuyu Liang, Junhu Fu, Qin Wang, Zeju Li, Yuanyuan Wang, Yi Guo
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Foundation models for medical imaging demonstrate superior generalization capabilities across diverse anatomical structures and clinical applications. Their outstanding performance relies on substantial computational resources, limiting deployment in resourceconstrained clinical environments. This paper presents TinyUSFM, the first lightweight ultrasound foundation model that maintains superior organ versatility and task adaptability of our large-scale Ultrasound Foundation Model (USFM) through knowledge distillation with strategically curated small datasets, delivering significant computational efficiency without sacrificing performance. Considering the limited capacity and representation ability of lightweight models, we propose a feature-gradient driven coreset selection strategy to curate high-quality compact training data, avoiding training degradation from lowquality redundant images. To preserve the essential spatial and frequency domain characteristics during knowledge transfer, we develop domain-separated masked image modeling assisted consistency-driven dynamic distillation. This novel framework adaptively transfers knowledge from large foundation models by leveraging teacher model consistency across different domain masks, specifically tailored for ultrasound interpretation. For evaluation, we establish the UniUS-Bench, the largest publicly available ultrasound benchmark comprising 8 classification and 10 segmentation datasets across 15 organs. Using only 200K images in distillation, TinyUSFM matches USFM's performance with just 6.36% of parameters and 6.40% of GFLOPs. TinyUSFM significantly outperforms the vanilla model by 9.45% in classification and 7.72% in segmentation, surpassing all state-of-the-art lightweight models, and achieving 84.91% average classification accuracy and 85.78% average segmentation Dice score across diverse medical devices and centers. This work successfully bridges the gap between high-performance foundation models and practical clinical deployment, winning the first place in MICCAI2025 IUGC Challenge.

title: Cooperative Localization of USV in Heterogeneous Sensor Networks: A Consensus-Based Estimation Approach
authors: Xingquan Fu, Guanghui Wen, Han Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Conventional localization of unmanned surface vehicle (USV) predominantly relies on a single integrated navigation system, typically comprising a global navigation satellite system (GNSS) and an inertial navigation system. However, the GNSS signals are vulnerable to external perturbations, which can substantially compromise the positioning accuracy. To achieve high-precision positioning of USV, this article investigates the cooperative localization problem for a class of USV in peer-to-peer heterogeneous sensor networks. A cooperative consensus localization (CCL) algorithm is proposed for the USV operating within heterogeneous sensor networks composed of ultra wideband modules and an integrated navigation system. The algorithm adopts a consensus-based state estimation approach founded on the principle of consensus on information. By integrating these methodologies, the proposed CCL algorithm enables high-precision localization of USVs, even in the presence of unknown inputs originating from the integrated navigation system. Furthermore, the stability of the proposed CCL algorithm is guaranteed under mild assumptions, including bounded system parameters, the doubly-stochastic property of the weight matrix, and connectivity in the heterogeneous sensor network. Specifically, the estimation error of each estimator remains uniformly bounded in the mean-square sense. Finally, the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed technique are validated through both numerical simulations and practical experiments.

title: Multi-time Scale Distributed Stochastic Economic Dispatch for Flexible Traction Power Supply System
authors: Minwu Chen, Yanlin He, Wei Wang, Gaoqiang Peng, Yinyu Chen, Zhichen Fan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: Flexible traction power supply system (FTPSS), integrated with renewable energy, energy storage, and power flow controllers (PFC), offers great potential for safe and economical railway operation. However, the uncertainty in traction load and photovoltaic (PV) output poses significant challenges to cost-effective dispatch. Therefore, a multi-time scale distributed stochastic economic dispatch method for FTPSS is proposed. The prediction module uses LSTM to produce point forecasts, while KDE models the forecast errors to construct predictive intervals and generate scenarios for PV output and traction load. A stochastic programming model is then formulated, where uncertainties in traction load and PV are represented by chance constraints and scenarios. In the first stage, the day-ahead forecast is used to solve the stochastic optimization model to minimize daily operational cost. In the second stage, the scheduler uses the day-ahead plan as a soft reference and reoptimizes at each rolling step with updated probabilistic forecasts, implementing only the first control move to maintain real-time responsiveness and economic efficiency. The optimization model is solved using an ADMM-based distributed algorithm, which accounts for limited inter-subsystem communication and reduces computational complexity. A case study based on a real electrified railway demonstrates that the proposed method improves economic performance by reducing operating costs by approximately 9% under uncertainty.

authors: N. Akhtar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: The ion acoustic wave (IAW) is explored in a two-component [electron-ion (EI)] magnetized plasma. The dynamic ions are warm, having pressure anisotropy, and electrons are (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$r,q$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) distributed. The anisotropic ion pressure is defined using the double adiabatic Chew–Goldberger–Low (CGL) theory. The Korteweg–de Vries–Burgers (KdVB) equation is obtained using the reductive perturbation method (RPM). <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$A$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$B$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$C$</tex-math> </inline-formula> are the different coefficients of the KdVB equation, which depends on the magnetic field intensity, direction cosines, nonthermal electrons spectral indices, nonthermal electrons coefficients, ion parallel and perpendicular temperatures, and viscosity. It is found that the phase velocity of the IAW flourishes with large ion temperature ratio in comparison with small ion temperature ratio in a magnetized plasma. The effects of different plasma parameters on the formation of shock structures have been discussed. Our numerical results forecast that the ratio of ion parallel temperature to electron temperature, ion perpendicular temperature to electron temperature ratio, spectral indices of the nonthermal electrons, direction cosines, and viscosity influences the amplitude of the nonlinear shock structure significantly. The (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$r,q$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) distribution, which generalizes the Maxwellian and kappa distributions, plays a crucial role in determining the properties of monotonic shock structures. Applications of these shock waves can be found in space plasmas, fusion research, and laboratory experiments.

title: Revisiting Flow Control in Node-Centric Datacenter Networks
authors: Peirui Cao, Rui Ning, Guangyu Zhao, Zhaochen Zhang, Chang Liu, Yunzhuo Liu, Rui Li, Chengyuan Huang, Tao Sun, Zhiqiang Li, Guihai Chen, Baochun Li, Chen Tian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: Node-centric Data Centers (NDCs) are highly flexible, cost-efficient, and failure-resilient, and have gained growing popularity in recent years. However, RDMA technology used in NDC still faces challenges, including high retransmission overhead, Head-of-Line Blocking (HoLB) and deadlock problems. Existing solutions for traditional data centers cannot simultaneously address these issues due to the unique topology and server transmission characteristics of NDC. In this paper, we propose a per-port flow control named PortFC for NDC. PortFC addresses the above problems through the designs of a Pause/Resume control signal, a per-port queue allocation method, an egress-detecting per-port flow control mechanism, and a server-aware queue scheduling method. Our evaluation shows that PortFC is free from retransmission, capable of eliminating HoLB and avoiding deadlocks. PortFC achieves 1.7-8.0 times higher throughput and reduces latency by 11.7%-87.7% compared to the state-of-the-art lossy RDMA based on IRN and the lossless RDMA method based on PFC. In particular, PortFC still demonstrates good performance in a Rail-only NDC with heterogeneous bandwidth domains.

title: 118 (1571216203): 2D-DOA Estimation Based Sparse Billboard and T-Shaped Arrays
authors: Saleh Ahmed Alawsh, Ali H Muqaibel, Mohammad Alhassoun
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This paper proposes two-dimensional (2D) sparse billboard and T-shaped arrays. The arrays have closed-form expressions for the sensor locations and have large uniform degrees of freedom (uDOF) based on the fourth-order difference coarray (FODC). The proposed configurations can estimate large number of sources greater than the total number of sensors. Simulation results validate the robustness of the proposed configurations.

title: A 2.4-GHz CMOS RF Energy Harvester With an Adaptive Power Management Unit Achieving 25-dB Input Power Range and 97.4% DC–DC Efficiency
authors: Jing-Ren Yan, Yao-Wei Huang, Zih-Ting Wang, Wei-Jen Lai, Jen-Hao Liao, Ching-Chun Lin, Yu-Te Liao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper presents a 2.4 GHz RF energy-harvesting CMOS chip with a co-designed dipole antenna, implemented in a standard 180 nm CMOS process and occupying an area of 2.56 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> 2.68 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The proposed design includes a reconfigurable rectifier, a maximum power harvesting efficiency optimization (MPHEO) controller, a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> switched-capacitor charge pump (SCCP<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula>), an ultra-low-power diode (ULPD), and several key analog building blocks. A discrete <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pi $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-matching network and dipole antenna are fabricated on an FR-4 substrate and a flexible polyimide printed circuit board, respectively. Measurement results demonstrate a peak RF front-end power harvesting efficiency (PHE) of 55.1% at −1 dBm input and a sensitivity of −16.2 dBm at 1.1-V output voltage. The SCCP<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> achieves a peak power conversion efficiency of 97.4%, while the ULPD exhibits sub-2 nA reverse leakage. The MPHEO algorithm extends the >20% PHE range across a 25 dB input power span from −12.2 dBm to 12.8 dBm, confirming the system’s high efficiency, robustness, and adaptability for low-power wireless energy harvesting applications.

title: Explainable CNN-GRU Model with Self-Attention for Human Activity Recognition Using Wearable Sensors through Data Augmentation
authors: Nadia Sultana, Sidratul Afrida, Bushra Akter, Tasnia Jahan, Afrin Ahmed, Mohammad Abu Yousuf, Md Zia Uddin
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Human activity recognition (HAR) has become an essential field of study, with applications in smart homes, sports training, human-computer interaction (HCI), healthcare, rehabilitation, and pervasive computing. Time-series data from wearable sensors, mobile devices, and Internet of Things (IoT) systems have been used to significantly increase the accuracy of HAR, owing to recent developments in deep learning. However, traditional models often struggle to balance accuracy and generalizability when applied to datasets with varying feature and timestep sizes, limiting their applicability across diverse real-world scenarios. In this study, we introduce a deep CNN-GRU-Self Attention model for HAR and employ SHapley Additive exPlanations for explainable AI (XAI) to identify influential features in HAR states. The CNN enhances feature extraction across various scales, whereas the GRU captures temporal connections to improve feature representation. The attention mechanism strategically focuses on the most relevant input features, enabling the extraction of time-dependent signals. This combination produces fewer parameters, leading to high-precision results with minimal processing time. Integrating XAI improves transparency, offering clear insights into feature contributions to DL-driven HAR models, which is crucial for validation and interpretability. Using the proposed sliding-window preprocessing strategy the model achieved accuracies of 99.70% and 99.14% on the UCI-HAR and WISDM datasets, respectively, and was further validated on PAMAP2 and WISDM-19 with accuracies of 96.80% and 96.04%, demonstrating its robust performance and strong generalization capability across a variety of activity recognition datasets. Its ability to capture spatial and temporal information simultaneously ensures resilience to variations in activity execution, making the proposed framework suitable for clinical, rehabilitation, and surveillance applications.

title: Sentinel Mode Using Sensor Fusion for Improving Security in Shared Vehicles
authors: Sandra Dixe, Cátia Loureiro, Beatriz Miranda, António Silva, Luís De Azevedo Cura, Nuno M.C. Da Costa, Ricardo Rodrigues, Tiago Aston, Diana Ferreira, Hugo Peixoto, José Machado, Duarte Fernandes
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a dual-mode sentinel surveillance architecture for shared vehicles that combines low-power inertial event triggering with low-rate fisheye vision and temporal decision logic. Unlike conventional sentry systems based on continuous vision or motion-only alarms, the proposed approach prioritizes energy efficiency while preserving contextual awareness. We introduce SentinelCAR, a real-world fisheye dataset comprising 18308 images annotated for activity-aware human presence around parked vehicles. A comprehensive benchmark of state-of-the-art detectors (YOLOv8-v12 and RT-DETR variants) shows that YOLOv11m provides the best accuracy-efficiency trade-off, achieving mAP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[.5:.95]</sub> ≈ 0.90. The selected model is deployed on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano and evaluated under multiple backends and precision formats (FP32, FP16, INT8). Experimental results demonstrate real-time performance of up to 70.4 FPS with (0.92-0.94) J per inference under TensorRT INT8 execution. In addition, an onboard post-processing pipeline based on spatial masking, motion consistency, false-positive suppression zones, and temporal hysteresis significantly improves state stability by reducing spurious activations caused by reflections and static background elements. These results provide a practical foundation for energy-aware, privacy-preserving sentinel surveillance in shared mobility scenarios.

title: Rolling Optimization Model for Power Systems Based on MultiScenario Probabilistic Assessment
authors: Mingchun Shao, Shao Xu Hao, Liang Lu, Lihua Zhang, Baixia Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: To address the scheduling challenges in power systems under high uncertainty, a rolling optimization model based on multi-scenario probabilistic evaluation is proposed. This model employs Copula functions to construct the joint distribution structure among disturbance variables. It combines Latin hypercube sampling with a boundary enhancement mechanism to generate representative time-series samples, and incorporates Benders decomposition with sample averaging approximation for multi-stage rolling optimization. Simulation results demonstrate that compared to the static baseline scheme, the rolling model reduces total system dispatch costs by 2.18% and decreases wind and solar curtailment rates by 31.60%. While ensuring operational safety boundaries, it significantly enhances renewable energy integration capacity and operational economics.

title: Study on Operational Strategy of Grid-Forming Energy Storage Participating in Multi-Level Joint Markets
authors: Zhiyi Hu, Ruichao Zhang, Sen Li, Zhongliang Sun
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: To address the technical bottlenecks of low renewable energy absorption, heavy thermal power peakshaving pressure, and grid stability risks under high-proportion renewable integration, this paper proposes an operational strategy for grid-forming energy storage (GFESS) in multi-level joint electricity markets (energy, auxiliary service, capacity). A bi-level optimization framework is constructed to balance the interests of GFESS investors and grid dispatch centers: the upper layer optimizes GFESS market participation strategies to enhance system flexibility while ensuring economic viability, and the lower layer develops a joint clearing model embedded with grid security constraints to coordinate renewable energy consumption and peak-shaving resources. The strategy is validated via mixed-integer linear programming, using technical parameters from Huawei string-type GFESS and Envision off-grid demonstration projects. Results show that the proposed mechanism increases regional renewable energy absorption rate by 4.7 percentage points, reduces thermal power peak-shaving costs by 12%, and narrows maximum frequency deviation to $\pm 0.2 \mathrm{~Hz}$. This work provides technical support for GFESS application in new power systems.

title: Multi-objective Optimization Framework for Controllable Circadian Rhythm LED Light Sources: A Comprehensive Spectral Design and Performance Evaluation System
authors: Rongkai Zhang, Lili Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Traditional LED lighting systems lack comprehensive circadian rhythm control capabilities, resulting in insufficient consideration of biological impacts and sub-optimal user wellbeing. The complexity of modern lighting requirements, coupled with the exponential growth of health-conscious design principles, necessitates advanced analytical approaches capable of processing multi-dimensional spectral data while providing actionable intelligence for lighting system optimization. This paper presents an intelligent LED spectral design system that integrates multi-objective optimization algorithms, distributed signal processing, and comprehensive evaluation techniques for circadian rhythm applications. The system combines Sequential Least Squares Programming (SLSQP) with Weighted Entropy Method (WEM) to create a robust A4 (Actor-Action-Asset-Attribute) scoring framework, incorporating Grid Search Random Forest (GSRF) algorithms for predictive modeling and spectral optimization. A comprehensive evaluation methodology enables real-time spectral analysis through statistical modeling and pattern detection using advanced optimization algorithms. The proposed framework addresses critical challenges in lighting design including spectral power distribution optimization, color rendering enhancement, and circadian effectiveness maximization. Experimental validation demonstrates superior performance with coefficient of determination R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.996, providing effective lighting solutions across diverse application contexts. The integrated approach achieves significant energy efficiency improvements compared to traditional lighting methods while providing automated circadian rhythm optimization for global lighting applications.

title: Performance Analysis of M-Type Barium Hexaferrite coated Artillery Shell for Radar Cross Section Reduction Applications
authors: Navdeep Singh, Sankarsan Padhy, Anil Kumar Chaubey
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This paper presents the Radar Cross Section (RCS) simulation for performance analysis of M-Type Barium Hexaferrite (BaFe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">19</inf>). Dielectric properties of BaFe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">19</inf> have been used to analyse the RCS in X-Band. CST Microwave Studio Suite has been used to simulate the RCS of uncoated artillery shell and BaFe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">19</inf> coated artillery shell. The maximum simulated RCS of -15.4dB has been obtained for uncoated artillery shell while the maximum simulated RCS of -24.7dB has been obtained for BaFe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">19</inf> coated artillery shell at 9.5GHz. BaFe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">19</inf> coated artillery shell has the maximum RCS reduction of 9.3dB at 9.5GHz. The results clearly show that the BaFe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">19</inf> coated artillery shell surpassed the uncoated artillery shell in terms of RCS.

title: Dynamic Modelling and Analysis of Adiabatic Compressed Air Energy Storage Discharging Process Under Multi-Mode Regulation
authors: Yu Zhou, Ningning Xie, Lailong Li, Linrui Ma, Yu'an Gong, Quanyuan Liu, Xiaodai Xue, Xuelin Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Adiabatic compressed air energy storage (A-CAES) has attracted considerable attention for its potential in providing frequency regulation services to the grid. The paper analyzes the dynamic behaviors of an A-CAES system operating under both automatic generation control (AGC) and primary frequency regulation (PFR) modes. First, a dynamic A-CAES discharging phase model incorporating the control valve (CV) pressure drop is proposed. Then, a control model combining PI and feedforward (FF) control is established for the A-CAES generation system. Finally, with reference to the assessment methods for thermal power plants, case studies are conducted to evaluate the A-CAES system's frequency regulation performance. The results indicate that A-CAES substantially outperforms thermal power plants in both AGC and PFR.

title: Research on Reconfiguration and Low-Carbon Optimization of Wind-Solar-Thermal Power Coupled Distribution Network Based on Improved IWOA Algorithm
authors: Shunjiang Wang, Shiyang Zhang, Yanli Liu, Zhen Yang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Aiming at the optimal operation of distribution networks with a high proportion of renewable energy, this paper investigates an economic dispatch model for an integrated electricity-heat system that primarily focuses on wind-to-heat conversion supplemented by solar thermal energy, combined with network reconfiguration technology. By collaboratively optimizing the switching states of the distribution network and the output ratio of electricity-to-heat conversion equipment, the model aims to deeply tap the potential of wind energy integration and enhance the comprehensive operational benefits of the system. By incorporating carbon-oriented economic costs, a comprehensive economic optimization objective is constructed, including power purchase costs, operation and maintenance costs, and environmental conversion costs. The study focuses on analyzing the regulation mechanism of network reconfiguration on the dynamic energy flow within the integrated electricity-heat physical network. To address the challenging large-scale nonlinear discrete optimization problem in the reconfiguration model, an Improved Whale Optimization Algorithm (IWOA) is employed. By optimizing the search operators and convergence strategies, the global search precision and convergence speed of the algorithm in handling complex topological combinatorial optimization are significantly improved. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed reconfiguration strategy effectively expands the physical boundary of wind-to-heat integration. While ensuring the balance of electricity and heat loads, it achieves optimal economic efficiency, providing a theoretical basis for the green dispatch and topological optimization of integrated electricity-heat systems.

title: AI and Yolo-Enabled Automated Traffic Management Framework for Emergency Vehicle Priority
authors: Rajkumar Pandiarajan, G. Logesh, M R. Goutham Balaji, M. Mohamed Sameer, N. Mohamed Najeeb
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Traffic congestion remains one of the critical challenges in smart city development, especially when emergency vehicles such as ambulances, fire engines, and police vans require immediate clearance. This research proposes an AI and Yolo-Enabled Automated Traffic Management Framework for Emergency Vehicle Priority that integrates computer vision, IoT, and mobile applications for automated and intelligent control. A Python-based vehicle detection model running on live video input was used to identify emergency vehicles in real time. Upon detection, the information is transmitted via Wi-Fi to a NodeMCU-based IoT controller, which activates an MP3 module and speaker for public voice alerts, while simultaneously overriding traffic lights to provide a green corridor. An Android mobile application further enhances coordination by notifying nearby traffic police with real-time alerts and displaying the vehicle's live GPS location. The proposed framework ensures rapid emergency response, reduces delays caused by traffic congestion, and improves public safety. Additionally, all detection data are logged in the cloud for performance monitoring and future AI-based traffic optimization. The system offers a low-cost, scalable, and automated smart city solution that contributes directly to sustainable urban mobility and life-saving healthcare access in smart cities.

title: A Rule-Based Real-Time Security Monitoring Framework for Cross-Slice Isolation in 5G Networks
authors: Pranish K, Suriya Kumar M, K Geetha
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The introduction of 5 G networks brings along the tech world with several advanced features, one of which has the highest impact is network slicing. It permits the segmentation of one physical network into several virtual networks, each catering to a different service type like enhanced mobile broadband (eMBB), massive machine-type communication (mMTC), and ultra-reliable low-latency communication (URLLC). This model not only boosts resource utilization but also comes with the drawback of new security challenges of having cross-slice attacks, resource contention, and data leakage because of partial isolation in virtualized environments. The typical security measures around the perimeter were unable to deal with the threats posed and consequently, their reaction was characterized by long delays and high operational costs. This paper presents an innovation: a rule-based, real-time security monitoring system that is designed to make it difficult to compromise strong cross-slice isolation in 5G networks. The model links the deterministic security policies to the leading 5G core functionalities such as Access and Mobility Management Function (AMF), Session Management Function (SMF), and Network Slice Selection Function (NSSF). The design consists of a Security Monitoring Layer whose sole job is to keep an eye on interslice communication and to do so, it employs packet capture tools (tcpdump), SDN flow rules, and NFV resource metrics. The isolation technique that is iptables and Open vSwitch to gain control at the allocation of resources between the slices. The system generates alerts and activates slice-level quarantine automatically upon detection of anomalies like unauthorized API access or breaches in resource sharing policies. The framework was tested in a containerized testbed using Open5GS and UERANSIM where it reached a detection accuracy of 98%, processing latency of 12 ms, and the result.

title: A Robust Motor Imagery EEG Classification Framework Using Adaptive Channel–Window Selection and Temporal Feature Pooling
authors: Marwan Hilali, Kais Belwafi, Fakhreddine Ghaffari
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Motor Imagery (MI)-based Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) rely on accurate decoding of Electroencephalography (EEG) activity to enable intuitive communication and control. Because EEG signals naturally exhibit temporal evolution and inter-subject variability, effective models must accommodate these intrinsic properties rather than view them as limitations. In this work, we propose a robust classification framework that integrates Adaptive Channel and Window Selection (ACWS), Common Spatial Pattern (CSP), and a lightweight temporal pooling strategy inspired by sequence-encoding models. The ACWS module automatically identifies subject-specific electrodes and temporal segments that maximize the discriminability of MI activity, while CSP extracts spatial patterns. Temporal dynamics are further summarized using channel-wise pooling and positional encoding, with features compacted through Principle Component Analysis (PCA). These representations are classified using an XGBoost model, with the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) applied to address class imbalance. The proposed framework was validated on a publicly available dataset comprising EEG recordings from 62 subjects performing four MI tasks. Performance was evaluated using both intra-subject 5-fold cross-validation and a leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) protocol to assess inter-subject generalization. The system achieved average accuracies of 85.03% for the binary case (left vs. right hand) and 80.10% for the four-class scenario, with an inter-subject standard deviation below 3.2%. These results surpass several CSP, Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-based baselines tested under the same conditions, while maintaining consistent performance across participants.

title: Innovations in data stream processing by combining temporal databases with edge intelligence
authors: Fuming Fu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the advancement of information technology, edge data volumes are growing at an astonishing rate, placing us in the era of big data with massive scale. By 2020, over 50 billion device data points had been uploaded to cloud services globally, covering various real-world edge scenarios where most applications involve AIoT technologies such as smart manufacturing, smart cities, and smart campuses. In recent years, AI4DB technology has emerged as a major research focus, attracting significant attention from database researchers. This technology aims to enhance database performance by integrating time-series databases with edge intelligence through machine learning and related AI techniques. This paper optimizes edge intelligence-based machine learning for time-series database storage and querying. We propose a lossy compression algorithm specifically designed for time-series databases. To achieve higher compression rates, we developed a parameterizable polynomial regression model to replace raw data. This model effectively fits periodic data patterns while reducing storage requirements. Experimental results demonstrate that our compression algorithm outperforms existing methods in time-series data compression, accurately identifying latent periodic values and achieving optimal fitting results in polynomial regression models trained on these periodic values. Compared to conventional approaches, our method significantly reduces storage consumption, improves data compression efficiency, and supports query processing, thereby substantially enhancing performance in large-scale data retrieval tasks. Current research on applying edge intelligence technology to database indexing primarily focuses on traditional relational database learning indexes, with no dedicated studies specifically targeting time series databases. This paper proposes an edge intelligence learning index optimized for time series databases, tailored to the unique data distribution characteristics during storage. The system employs a tree structure that adaptively selects different learning index schemes based on data types. Experimental results demonstrate that our index outperforms both traditional and existing learning index schemes in time series databases, showing superior performance in construction time, query efficiency, memory utilization, and error rate control.

title: Design of a Variation-Resilient 2 MHz PWM Circuit for 28 nm Buck Converters Using an Error-Budget Approach
authors: Over Amaya, Victor Muñoz, Javier Ardila
publish: 2026 Argentine Conference on Electronics (CAE)
abstract: This paper presents a design methodology for an analog pulse-width modulator circuit that accounts for errorbudget variations in a 28 nm CMOS node. The modulator is designed to operate at 2 MHz, aiming for DC-DC buck converter applications. The proposed design methodology is based on an error-budget approach for the switching frequency error contributions, including trimming implementation and providing high robustness against PVT and Monte Carlo simulations. Postlayout simulations show a nominal frequency of 2 MHz with a 5.3% variation due to PVT conditions, and a nominal current consumption of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$115 \mu \mathrm{A}$</tex> with a duty cycle range of [3.8%-98%]. Statistical analysis reveals a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \sigma$</tex> variation of 4.5% in the switching frequency.

title: Content-Path Optimization for TV News Programs
authors: Shoma Kaji, Risa Kaneko, Yuta Hagio, Masaru Miyazaki, Siya Bao, Nozomu Togawa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This paper formulates the construction of TV news program viewing routes that align with viewer interests while preserving topical continuity as an optimization problem that minimizes semantic distance. Two approaches are proposes: a content-level method that searches for a shortest path under a two-intermediate constraint using pairwise semantic distances computed by a large language model (LLM), and a genre-level method that induces inter-program distances from an LLM-derived inter-genre distance matrix. Evaluations on content sets of size 10 and 40 indicate that the content-level method yields the shortest route between specified endpoints, whereas the genre-level method promotes genre-consistent transitions for start–target pairs with large initial gaps. The results suggest reduced redundant viewing and a balanced trade-off between coverage and coherence for recent events.

title: A PUF-Based Security Framework for Fault and Intrusion Detection
authors: Ahmed Oun, Rishabh Das, Clay Hess, Aakriti Barat, Savas Kaya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Industrial Control Systems (ICS) rely on sensor feedback to keep safety-critical processes within operational limits. This research presents a hardware-root-of-trust that embeds a Physically Unclonable Function (PUF) at the measurement layer to authenticate sensor readings. The architecture combines voltage fingerprinting with a temporal authentication that integrates with standard industrial control system architecture. The research prototypes the PUF integration on a hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) water tank testbed using a Simulink-based PUF emulator. The system maintains 99.97% accuracy over a 5.18-hour period of normal operation and flags all injected anomalies, including spike faults, hard-over faults, and hardware trojan scenarios that push the system over to an unsafe operational state. The proposed architecture provides a process-aware, vendor-agnostic approach that can integrate with legacy plants to detect sensor signal degradation or sophisticated supply chain attacks.

title: Research and Implementation of Automated Testing for Vehicle Remote Diagnosis
authors: Liu Kaiquan, Wang Manna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: In view of the challenges faced by remote diagnosis function testing of intelligent networked vehicles, such as complex scenarios, low efficiency, and insufficient abnormal coverage, this paper proposes a set of automated testing technology solutions. By integrating model-driven and keyword-driven methods, combined with hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) simulation and cloud-vehicle communication simulation environment, an automated test framework covering protocol analysis, diagnostic services, exception injection and performance monitoring is constructed. Successfully applied in a vehicle project: 98.3% DTC management, data acquisition and remote control function coverage, 65% shortened the test cycle, 56 deep defects were found, and verify the performance metric of end-to-end latency being less than 1.2 seconds. It has been proved in practice that this solution significantly improves the test efficiency and system reliability, and provides an effective technical path for remote diagnostic testing of intelligent vehicles.

title: Hybrid Video Enhancement Framework Using Generative Convolutional Vision Transformer
authors: Balamurugan M, Narasimha Murthy G K, Rajesh Rao K, C. Balaji
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Enhancing video content is more challenging than improving still images because of greater computational demands and larger data volumes, along with the complexity of maintaining spatiotemporal consistency. These challenges are often exacerbated by the scarcity of sample pairs for the supervised methods. Therefore, we introduced a robust adversarial framework for video enhancement that learns from unpaired video samples. In this study, we investigated a classification method for detecting false positives in video enhancements using Computer Vision. Video enhancement with Gabor filters captures texture and edge features by emulating the sensitivity system in different orientations. PixStabNet is a deep learning model designed for feature extraction to stabilize video frames at the pixel level and ensure temporal consistency for reliable surveillance analysis. This model improves feature extraction by preserving crucial motion and appearance features in dynamic scenes. RVM + builds on the RVM design concept and offers a novel approach for advanced video segmentation. GenConViT combines convolutional and transformer techniques, where convolutional feature extraction is enhanced using attention-based transformers. Data training was extended until October 2023.This approach captures both local and global spatiotemporal patterns, resulting in an improved video quality. The Beluga Whale Optimization Algorithm (BWO) further enhances video quality by adjusting the enhancement parameters for optimal visual clarity, inspired by the echolocation and cooperative behavior of beluga whales. A highresolution multimodal dataset is ideal for developing and benchmarking video enhancement techniques for autonomous driving and deep learning research because it includes synchronized cameras and LiDAR streams across various geographical locations under different lighting conditions. In terms of F1-score, recall, accuracy, and precision, the experimental developments showed values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.81 \%, 98.84 \%, 98.88 \%, 98.85 \%$</tex>, and 99.02 % respectively.

title: Smart Street Lighting System Using AI, Computer Vision, and IoT for Energy Efficiency and Sustainability
authors: Suhan Krishna M G, Jossy George, Chanti S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: In pursuit of smart cities, optimum utilization of energy without any compromise on public security is a requirement. Traditional lighting systems work with minimal flexibility but result in enormous wastage of energy. The proposed model dynamically controls lighting intensity based on real-time pedestrian and environmental information. The model is capable of saving wasteful utilization of electricity by integrating innovative technologies without any compromise on public security and visibility levels. The system requires the deployment of IoT-enabled cameras at strategic points on the highways to monitor the movement and traffic of pedestrians and automobiles. The system is capable of detecting and recognizing moving objects in real-time through computer vision technologies. Based on movement and density patterns, the system determines the optimal level of brightness to be used over each street lighting segment. This adaptive lighting method ensures the utilization of energy only where it is required and minimizes the energy consumption substantially without any compromise on security. Overall, this AI-driven smart lighting solution is a cost-effective and scalable solution for cities to become intelligent and resource-efficient.

title: Secure Healthcare Framework Using Biometric Fingerprint Authentication and Generative AI for Electronic Health Record Management
authors: Pradeep S, Jareena Begam J, Rashika Vinola V, Sachin Prithiv K S, Sountharagopal R, Tamilmaran C S
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The healthcare landscape has fundamentally changed due to the large-scale digitalization of medical records; however, healthcare security, patient data privacy, and authentication of patient identity are still major issues to date. This paper presents a secure Healthcare 4.0 framework which integrates biometric fingerprint authentication and generative artificial intelligence (AI)-based anomaly detection for enhanced Electronic Health Record (EHR) security. In order to counter the danger of illegal exposure of data, the proposed system consists of fingerprint-based access controls, artificial intelligence based behavioural analysis and end-to-end encryption. Experimental evaluation conducted on a cloudbased platform yielded an identification accuracy of 98.6%, an authentication latency of 1.8s and a reduction of 34% in access errors compared to conventional methods. The false acceptance rate of the AI module was 1.2%, and the false rejection rate was 1.8%, so the AI module had high reliability. HIPAA and GDPR compliance ensure the strength of the system. As a result, the framework is successfully tested for strengthening patient identity verification, data confidentiality and scalability, paving the way for future integration to telemedicine and IoT-based healthcare systems.

title: An Improved LCL Filter Design with Passive Damping for Smartgrid and Microgrid Applications with Lower Losses
authors: Sourabh Jain, Manoj Modi, Monojit Seal
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: This paper presents an improved LCL filter design with passive damping for smartgrid and microgrid applications. Conventional LCL filters effectively suppress high-frequency harmonics from grid-connected converters but have increased power losses due to the presence of damping elements. The proposed topology addresses these challenges by incorporating a reconfigured passive damping strategy that minimises losses while ensuring stable resonance suppression. A detailed design methodology is developed, including filter parameter selection and damping optimisation to achieve both harmonic attenuation and efficiency. Theoretical analysis and simulation results confirm that the improved LCL filter delivers low-loss operation, enhanced stability, and reliable power quality in converterbased distributed generation systems. Owing to its simplicity and practicality, the proposed filter provides a robust solution for next-generation smartgrid and microgrid integration.

title: Research on Regional Electricity Consumption Forecasting Based on the Coupled LMDI-STIRPAT-System Dynamics Model
authors: Shiyao Hu, Zeya Zhang, Yunjia Wang, Chan Peng, Rui Liu, Ning Pang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To address electricity forecasting in the power system field, a coupled framework integrating LMDI decomposition, the STIRPAT model, and system dynamics is proposed. Drawing on the electricity identity, the influence mechanisms of core drivers (population, economy, industry) on electricity consumption are systematically explored. Empirical analysis of Province's 2005–2024 data identifies urbanization rate, per capita GDP, and secondary industry electricity intensity as key drivers. A system dynamics model encompassing four subsystems (population, economy, industry, residential electricity consumption) is developed, with three scenarios (baseline development, low-carbon policy, industrial upgrading) designed to forecast Province's electricity consumption for 2025–2035. Results indicate that under the baseline scenario, consumption will rise from 3013.58 TWh to 4498.63 TWh over the period. This coupled model provides scientific analytical support and decision-making reference for regional power planning and energy policy development.

title: Opinion Modeling of Social Media Comments Based on LSTM Sentiment Analysis
authors: Renwei Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This study focuses on the sentiment analysis and influence assessment of social media comments. It constructs a sentiment analysis model based on Long Short-Term Memory Network (LSTM) and proposes an "AE" model that integrates Attention and Event Energy. A large volume of comments from four platforms, including Weibo, is collected; after preprocessing, a deep learning architecture comprising a word-embedding layer, a bi-LSTM layer, and a fully connected layer is built. The model is trained with the Adam optimizer and cross-entropy loss function. Results show that the LSTM model achieves high accuracy and low loss in sentiment analysis, while the "AE" model offers a novel dimension for assessing content influence by quantitatively analyzing the dissemination mechanisms and public sentiment responses of social media posts. The findings provide a theoretically grounded and practically useful reference for public-opinion monitoring, policy making, and business decision making.

title: Perceptual and Structural Similarity-Driven Loss for Improving Unified Rain Removal
authors: Yusuke Yamamoto, Yinhao Li, Hiroshi Taga, Koki Iwasa, Ryuichi Shichikawa, Makoto Suganami, Kazuhiro Nakamoto, Yen-Wei Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Images captured in rainy scenes suffer from various non-uniform degradations—such as streaks, raindrops, and haze—that reduce visibility and impair downstream perception tasks. Most existing approaches train deep models with an L1 loss, which often fails to fully remove rain noise or inadvertently destroys structural details of background objects. In this work, we propose a composite objective that integrates structural and perceptual components, enabling deraining models to achieve both effective noise removal and high-quality visual results aligned with human perception. To validate the effectiveness of our method, we employ a unified Medical Image Restoration model adapted to natural images with rain-haze mixtures and systematically compare different loss functions. Experiments on a mixed rain/haze benchmark demonstrate that our loss consistently improves LPIPS scores and produces visually clearer restorations, while maintaining PSNR/SSIM comparable to strong baseline models.

title: Optimization Method for Static Voltage Stability in Distribution Systems Considering the Adjustable Capability of Distributed Resources
authors: Xiang Lin, Wei Cong, Yong Li, Bo Hu, Junzhe Wang, Qian Sun
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Rising distributed photovoltaic (PV) penetration requires harnessing distributed resources (DRs) to improve static voltage stability. This paper first models the active-reactive P-Q output characteristics and operational constraints of PVs, wind turbines, energy storage systems, and static var generators (SVGs). Leveraging a reformulated power-injection L-index, normalized active and reactive sensitivity indices <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex>-index and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex>-index are derived to quantitatively evaluate the regulation efficacy of various DRs. Subsequently, a Quadratic Programming (QP) optimization model is constructed to minimize the L -index of vulnerable nodes. An inverse-sensitivity weighting strategy is proposed for the objective function to guide the optimal allocation of DR outputs while strictly adhering to power flow, voltage safety, and capacity constraints. Finally, the method is validated using a 24 -hour typical high-load scenario on a modified IEEE 33-bus system. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy significantly reduces the L-index of weak nodes, eliminates voltage violations, and lowers network losses, showcasing superior performance in bolstering static voltage stability.

title: Energy-Efficient Wireless Charging Architecture Using LLC Resonant Converter and Synchronous Rectification for Portable Devices
authors: K Saranya, M. Deepak, Vasanth P, Gowtham C, Harish Ragavendar P
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: A high-efficiency LLC resonant wireless charging architecture for portable electronic devices is proposed, addressing the critical limitations of conventional converters, such as low efficiency, diode conduction losses, and restricted air-gap tolerance. The system is employing a high-frequency LLC resonant DC-DC converter with synchronous MOSFET rectification, utilizing planar spiral coils with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5-10 \text{mm}$</tex> separation and scaling output from 10 W to 25 W. Electromagnetic parameters were extracted in ANSYS Maxwell and integrated into MATLAB/Simulink for circuit-level simulation with adaptive frequency control, ensuring soft-switching (ZVS/ZCS) operation. The primary performance indicators include output voltage regulation, power transfer efficiency, mutual coupling, air-gap and misalignment tolerance, load adaptability, switching and conduction losses, and EMI characteristics. The Simulation results show a stable 10W output with a peak efficiency exceeding 90%, demonstrating a substantial improvement over conventional designs and confirming the system's scalability, reliability, and suitability for next-generation portable wireless charging applications.

title: Research on the Application of Big Data Deep Learning in Traceability and Risk Assessment in Clean Label Certified Food Supply Chains
authors: Ping Du, Zhuojun Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the challenges of low traceability efficiency and inaccurate risk assessment in clean label certified food supply chains, this paper proposes an original dual-path feature fusion deep learning algorithm (DPFF-DL) to construct a comprehensive traceability and risk assessment system. First, the characteristics of multi-source, heterogeneous supply chain data are analyzed to complete data preprocessing. The DPFF-DL algorithm is then designed, with dual paths processing time series and static data, respectively. It then incorporates an attention mechanism to dynamically fuse features and implement multi-task learning. An experimental platform is established based on 5.2TB of real-world data from three clean label food supply chains (dairy products, fruits and vegetables, and baked goods), and the algorithm is compared with traditional algorithms. Results show that DPFF-DL achieved a 96.8% traceability accuracy, representing improvements of 14.5, 16.3, and 6.7 percentage points over traditional LSTM (82.3%), CNN (80.5%), and CNN-LSTM concatenation (90.1%), respectively. Risk assessment achieved a MAE of 0.08 and an RMSE of 0.12, while risk level identification accuracy reached 94.5%, representing an error reduction of over 30% compared to the competing algorithms. Training for 100 epochs took 8.2 hours, and single-sample inference took 0.03 seconds. This algorithm effectively integrates multi-source data, improving traceability accuracy and risk assessment reliability.

title: Comparative Computational Study on Optimal Path Planning for an UAV
authors: Sumit Saha, Rahul Bhattacharya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Autonomous navigation within complex and dynamic environments remains a critical challenge for Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), necessitating precise and computationally efficient path planning methodologies. This study shows a comparative analysis of four proven algorithms namely the Rapidly-Exploring Random Tree (RRT), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Breadth-First Search (BFS) and A* that is developed and tested in simulation frameworks that are meant to represent both two and three-dimensional operating regions with stationary obstacles. All algorithms were evaluated based on convergence behavior, path optimality and computational robustness behavior towards different environmental constraints. The findings show that RRT exhibits a significant flexibility and performance whereas PSO and A* are able to display an improved convergence speed and trajectory smoothness respectively. The framework developed, therefore, offers a foundation of realistic, altitude-conscious UAV path planning on complicated aerial surfaces.

title: Heat Prediction and Control in Smart Phones Using Machine Learning
authors: Adhvaith H, Jenix Jebaraj F, Lathika Jenifer V, Krishnaraman K, M Venkateshkumar
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The use of mobile phones has increased enormously in recent years, which has awakened a brand-new issue, overheating. This issue makes the phone more vulnerable by reducing its lifespan and making it vulnerable to internal conditions (bugs, online viruses). This study uses machine learning to predict overheating conditions by taking inputs from the Antutu app. Machine learning was used for efficient prediction and control. Initial conditions such as battery status, Wi-Fi status, hotspot status, mobile usage, and overheating were considered. Based on the obtained dataset, various machine learning models were implemented, namely ANN, logistic regression, SVM and Decision tree. Among all the models mentioned above, ANN exhibited the best performance in predicting overheating, providing good accuracy.

title: Automated Detection of 3D Video Formats for Mixed Reality Headsets: A Feature Matching Approach Using Epipolar Geometry and Disparity Analysis
authors: Jeferson B. da Costa, Lucas G. M. de Castro, Rafael N. Cunha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: In the context of mixed reality technologies, the need for accurately detecting 3D video projection formats, such as side-by-side and top-bottom, is critical for providing seamless and immersive experiences. This paper presents an automated algorithm for identifying 3D video formats using feature-matching techniques, including ORB for keypoint detection and RANSAC for fundamental matrix estimation, combined with disparity analysis. Integrated into a video player, the tool processes video frames in real-time, classifying them as 3D SBS, 3D TB, or non-3D. The proposed method achieved an accuracy of 97.89% when tested on 3,474 frames across diverse video formats. The algorithm offers an efficient solution for enhancing user experiences in MR environments, particularly for users unfamiliar with 3D content. Future work may focus on expanding its applicability to live video streams and further improving classification precision.

title: MAR-MHNet: A Shipborne Crew State Monitoring Framework Integrating Cross-Modal Reliability- Aware Attention and Adaptive Threshold
authors: Pingzhi Zhao, Boning Di, Chaowei Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: In the long-haul maritime transportation, approximately 1.9 million seafarers work for extended periods in steel-enclosed environments, isolated from onshore facilities. They are vulnerable to disturbances from factors such as circadian rhythm disorders, glare, vibration, and noise, which lead to unstable conditions and potential safety risks. Statistics indicate that the incidence of symptoms related to impaired operational capabilities among seafarers is roughly four times that of onshore populations, and maritime accidents associated with human error account for approximately 13%. Addressing this practical scenario, this paper proposes MAR-MHNet: a shipborne multimodal monitoring framework integrating cross- modal reliability-aware attention (RA-CMA) and self-adaptive Bayesian threshold (SABT). RA-CMA achieves fine-grained alignment of visual and audio modalities through bidirectional Cross-Attention, measures modal credibility using attention entropy, and dynamically fuses them via gating. SABT derives thresholds online based on the minimum posterior risk, outputting interpretable "normal/alarm" labels and continuous risk scores. The model is pre-trained on DAIC-WOZ/CMU- MOSEI/MSP-IMPROV and adapted to real shipborne data (14,006 6-second segments) using MMD+GRL. It achieves Macro-F1=91.2% and AUC=0.946 on the main task (6.2% and 0.031 higher than strong baselines; p<0.01), with inference on Jetson Xavier at ≈110 ms/window (memory ≈1.3 GB). Ablation experiments show that removing the reliability gating or using fixed thresholds reduces F1 by ≈3.1% and ≈2.4%, respectively. The framework combines high accuracy, low latency, and deployability, providing an engineering path for active crew state monitoring and shipborne safety.

title: Design of Buck Converter Integrated Circuit in 180nm SCL Technology with CMOS Logic
authors: Surya Prakash JS, G Saravana Ilango
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a low-power, fully integrated current-programmed buck converter in 180 nm SCL Technology with CMOS Logic with an operating frequency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{7 4 K H z}$</tex> for stepping down <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1. 8 V}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1. 2 V}$</tex> with L of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 0} \mu \mathrm{H}$</tex> and C of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 0} \mu \mathrm{F}$</tex> with dual complementary switches (CMOS) with peak efficiency of 94.39 %. This design logic resists a maximum current of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 4 0 m A}$</tex> (built-in short-circuit protection) and uses the weak inversion bandgap reference as the voltage reference. The system requires an operational amplifier, which would act as a comparator, oscillator, in addition to a gate driver for driving gates, and an SR latch for a current programmed controller with appropriate design constraints. The simulation results validated the reliable working of the converter in various PVT-variations.

title: A Machine Learning and Explainable AI-Based Stroke Risk Prediction Approach With SHAP Based Advanced Interpretability
authors: K. M Mahabub Hossain, Mahfuzulhoq Chowdhury, Mehreen Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Stroke is a serious condition that needs to be diagnosed immediately with proper treatment and ML-based prediction. Previous literary works on stroke prediction mainly focus on binary prediction and ignore multiclass classification of stroke subtypes. The previous works experienced problems with class imbalance, poor feature selection, and decreased prediction accuracy. This work confers a ML based multiclass stroke classification framework that addresses these drawbacks by utilizing Bayesian Search-based hyper parameter tuning, SMOTE-NC-based class imbalance solving, feature selection through random forest, and sophisticated preprocessing. LightGBM is chosen as the best ML model for stroke prediction in this article due to its high F1 score (93.90%) and accuracy (93.94%). This paper also explains the interpretability of a global model based on SHAP. According to the comparative results, the proposed LightGBM model outperforms existing works by at least 3% higher accuracy value and F1 value.

title: TCN-KAN: A Parameter-Efficient and Causal Neural Network for Nonlinear Active Noise Control
authors: Canliang Li, Jiahao Zhuang, Geguo Du, Jiajun Yang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Active Noise Control (ANC) deployment is often hindered by hardware nonlinearity and strict memory constraints. While deep learning approaches offer superior noise suppression, they typically suffer from prohibitive parameter sizes unsuitable for embedded implementation. To address this, we propose TCN-KAN, a parameter-efficient and causal framework. By combining a Causal Dilated TCN with a Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) decoder utilizing learnable spline activations, the model efficiently captures complex secondary path nonlinearities. Experimental results demonstrate that TCN-KAN achieves state-of-the-art noise reduction and exceptional robustness against severe saturation distortion. Crucially, this performance is achieved with only 0.109 million parameters-a reduction of over two orders of magnitude compared to leading baselines-making TCN-KAN an ideal “compute-for-memory” solution for memory-critical ANC applications.

title: Research on Complex Time Series Prediction Algorithm Based on ENA-SARIMA-LSTM Combined Model
authors: Zeyu Xi, Zihao Yu, Zirui Zhang
