publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: In order to solve the problem that the traditional single model has insufficient prediction accuracy in dealing with complex time series with seasonal nonlinear features, this paper proposes a combined prediction algorithm that integrates elastic network regression and long short-term memory network algorithm. ENR elastic network regression is used to screen key characteristic variables, reduce data dimensions, and remove redundant information. Secondly, SARIMA is used to capture the seasonal and linear trends of the time series, the nonlinear dynamic features are mined with the help of LSTM, and the output of the two models is coupled through linear regression to form an ensemble prediction result. Experimental results show that the root mean square error of the proposed combined model is lower than that of LSTM, and the coefficient of determination R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> is increased from 0.35 to 0.876, which significantly improves the prediction accuracy and stability. The results show that the combined algorithm can effectively integrate the advantages of linear and nonlinear modeling, and provides an efficient solution for complex time series forecasting.

title: Evaluating Conformal Antenna Arrays for Microwave Head Imaging
authors: Anja Kovacevic, Darko Ninkovic, Branko Kolundzija, Marija Nikolic Stevanovic
publish: 2026 International Conference on Medical Microwave Imaging Applications (ICMWIA)
abstract: This paper aims to compare two different layouts of a helmet-like antenna system mounted onto a head phantom. The helmet surface is parameterized using a spherical harmonic approximation derived from the outer surface of the head. The position of each patch antenna is defined by the spherical coordinates of its mass center and a rotation angle within the plane tangential to the helmet surface. Two distinct, non-overlapping helmet layouts are presented. Their performance is evaluated in the presence of a brain stroke, whose position is varied along one axis. For each stroke position, the scattering matrix is computed, and the corresponding Fisher information is calculated for both layouts. The results indicate that, although the two layouts contain the same number of antennas, one exhibits greater sensitivity to changes in stroke position.

title: Computational Framework for Multi-Dimensional Parameter Prediction and Complex System Optimization Based on Weighted Ensemble Learning and NSGA-II Algorithm
authors: Jiaying Jin, Ruiyang Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper proposes a weighted ensemble learning model, an NSGA-II algorithm optimization model with an improved mechanism, a scheduling system model integrating multi-agent collaboration and cognitive computing, and a four-stage cognitive computing decision-making model. It focuses on exploring the application of each model in relevant prediction and system optimization. First, a weighted ensemble learning strategy employs linear regression, ridge regression, random forest, and gradient boosting trees as base learners. Weights are assigned based on validation set R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values to achieve target prediction, yielding an ensemble R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.8234 and RMSE of 0.0156, significantly enhancing prediction accuracy. Second, an improved NSGA-II algorithm with hierarchical initialization, tournament selection, and node crossover operations generates Pareto optimal solutions for the system, balancing multi-objective optimization including coverage and cost. Finally, a multi-agent collaborative model (incorporating perception, decision-making, and coordination modules) was constructed alongside a four-level cognitive computing framework (perception-memory-inference-learning). This integrated graph theory network flow optimization, Markov chain risk assessment, and Dynamic Programming to solve the cognitive model, thereby enhancing object survival rates and decision accuracy. This model enables precise prediction and optimized system scheduling, offering advantages such as high prediction accuracy, multi-objective optimization, support for growth requirements, and robust decision adaptability and stability.

title: Research on Intelligent AI-Based Telephone Billing System Architecture and Application in Utility Services
authors: Xueming Qiao, Xiaoshuai Lai, Xiangchen Fan, Weiwei Yu, Junwei Li, Ping Meng, Dong Han, Anqi Yu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of artificial intelligence technology, the integration of speech recognition, natural language processing, and speech synthesis has enabled the automation of traditional customer communication processes. This paper presents the design and implementation of an AI-driven telephone billing reminder system for utility services. The proposed system adopts a modular architecture based on cloud computing and intelligent dialogue models, achieving high-accuracy speech recognition, adaptive intent understanding, and natural human-computer interaction. Experimental results demonstrate that the system effectively improves the efficiency of billing reminders, significantly reduces human resource costs, and enhances customer interaction experience. Furthermore, this research discusses the optimization of AI models, data processing pipelines, and system scalability, providing a reference framework for the intelligent transformation of large-scale customer service systems in industrial scenarios.

title: Stabilization of an 8-DoF Wheeled Bipedal Robot via Whole-Body Control with Integrated LQR and MPC
authors: Maopu Chen, Hongjun Ma, Junyan Wang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Traditional wheeled-legged robots often employ simplified pitch-only legs, enabling balance control via LQR and wheeled inverted pendulum (WIP) models. When hip-roll joints are introduced, however, the resulting lateral redundancy and 3D coupling render such controllers insufficient for highDOF platforms. To overcome these limitations, we develop a hierarchical control framework that integrates LQR, Model Predictive Control (MPC), and Whole-Body Control (WBC). The LQR module provides baseline wheel torques using a 2D WIP model; an MPC based on a single-rigid-body formulation generates ground reaction force plans; and a WBC layer unifies all tasks-including posture, motion, and contact-force regulation-while applying a lightweight correction to the MPC force predictions. Simulations demonstrate stable standing, precise attitude regulation, validating the proposed approach for highDOF wheeled-legged robots.

title: Machine Learning-Based Fraud Detection in Healthcare Insurance with Optimized Storage in Blockchain Using Zero-Knowledge Proofs
authors: Santhi V, Simrithaa N V, Dhanaseelan V, Arul Arasu N, Lokajit G, Sudarshan S
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The prevalence of fraudulent activities in the insurance industry is alarmingly increasing and requires innovative solutions. The primary objective of the research is to identify instances of fraudulent insurance claims through machine learning algorithm and develop an efficient storage system of the insurance claims, which is secured, private and suitable for the industry. The XGBoost model with SMOTE oversampling is proven to be distinguished among other models. Hyperledger Fabric, a permissioned ledger is used to store and retrieve the insurance data, ensuring the reliability, immutability and authorization. To protect the data from public visibility, zero knowledge proof technology is utilized. This ensures the privacy and authenticity of the information. Overall, this research provides an efficient and automated claim validation system with higher accuracy and efficient storage in blockchain while preventing information leak.

title: Fault Diagnosis of Three-Phase T-Type Multilevel Inverter Based on Variance Analysis
authors: Jeevan N D, Niraj Kumar Dewangan, Karthik B M, Krishna Kumar Gupta, Anil Kumar Sharma, Pallavee Bhatnagar
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Multilevel inverters (MLIs) are extensively used in modern power conversion systems owing to their superior output quality and reduced harmonic distortion. However, the presence of a large number of power switches increases their susceptibility to faults, which can severely affect system reliability. This paper presents a fault detection and diagnosis framework for a three-phase, three-level T-type MLI (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \varphi-3\mathrm{L}-\mathrm{T}^{2}$</tex> MLI) based on Variance (Var) analysis of phase voltages and currents. The Var is first calculated from the raw signals and then normalized with respect to healthy conditions to minimize the influence of varying operating conditions. These normalized Var features are subsequently employed to detect faults and identify the corresponding faulty switch. Unlike computationally intensive artificial intelligence (AI) or model-based approaches, the proposed method offers a simple and efficient diagnostic scheme. The effectiveness of the technique is validated through MATLAB/Simulink simulations, demonstrating accurate and reliable fault detection and diagnosis.

title: Parameter Estimation of DC-DC Buck Converter Using Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
authors: Vedant Vyas, Aditya R. Gautam, Somesh Krishna Thanvi
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Power electronic converters (particularly DC-DC converters) play a critical role in modern energy conversion systems, and find a wide range of applications, such as in consumer electronics, industrial applications, distributed and renewable energy systems (solar PV systems, wind generation systems) and automotives. In such applications, the continuous and prolonged operation of DC-DC converters may lead to degradation of their elements, i.e. inductor, capacitor, transformer, or coupled inductor and semiconductor devices, not only due to ageing, but also due to the short-term operation at high current/voltage stress during a fault or overloading conditions. This poses adverse challenges to the performance of the entire system, including its control. In this paper, the concept of digital twin is used, to properly track and predict the performance of the system. The digital twin is the virtual copy of the physical system that mirrors its behaviour and performance. A discrete virtual model of a buck converter is developed using backward Euler method. Moreover, the Particle Swarm Optimization algorithm is used to estimate the system parameters. The proposed work is validated using simulation results.

title: Intelligent Analytical Framework for the Design of Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors Toward Renewable and Mobility Integration
authors: Om Jee Singh, Bikash Sah, Praveen Kumar
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The increasing demand for sustainable mobility solutions has intensified research on electric motor-driven vehicles. A major challenge in this domain lies in identifying machine design parameters that meet performance requirements with high accuracy, while avoiding the extensive iterations typically needed due to nonlinear dependencies among parameters. In particular, selecting appropriate limits for key nonlinear factors such as current density (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$J$</tex>) and air-gap flux density (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$B_{\delta}$</tex>) is essential to prevent deviations from an accurate design. This work introduces a knowledge-driven machine design methodology that systematically defines these limits, thereby enabling the prediction of near-optimal design parameters without iterative adjustments. The proposed framework is validated through comparison with a reference motor model, and its capability is further demonstrated by the complete development of a 30 kW PMSM using the platform. Results confirm the effectiveness of the approach in accelerating the design process while maintaining accuracy.

title: A Large-Scale Model Driven Framework for Automated Generation of Educational Video Courses: Application in the Tobacco Industry
authors: Wang Deji, Wang Cuilan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Traditional industrial training video production suffers from high manual costs, limited scalability, and inconsistent instructional quality due to extensive reliance on manual scripting and post-editing. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an end-to-end framework driven by Large Language Models (LLMs) for the automated synthesis of educational video courses from structured technical documents. The framework integrates document semantic parsing, instructional knowledge reconstruction, multimodal asset generation, and synchronized video composition. Experimental results in tobacco industry scenarios demonstrate that the system reduces human workload by approximately 85% while maintaining content fidelity above 92% and high multimodal alignment accuracy. This study provides a scalable, domain-adapted solution for intelligent industrial training.

title: A Low Cost Distributed Computing Cluster for Power Electronics
authors: Yogeshwar Nath, Henil Shah, Mukul Chandorkar
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and evaluation of a low-cost, energy-efficient distributed computing cluster built using BeagleBone Black (BBB) single-board computers, each featuring a 32-bit ARM Cortex-A8 processor with Linux and parallel processing support. Addressing the growing computational demands of edge computing and IoT applications, the proposed 4-node cluster offers an accessible alternative to conventional high-performance systems, which are often expensive and powerintensive. The cluster's performance was assessed through matrix multiplication, AES-128 encryption, and High-Performance Linpack (HPL) benchmarks, with particular focus on large matrix multiplication-an essential component in the simulation of large-scale power electronics systems. This paper presents the performance of large matrix multiplications on the developed cluster. In addition to educational and experimental use, the system demonstrates significant potential for power electronics applications, including simulation of large power electronics systems such as solar and wind power plants and large-scale battery storage system. The results validate the feasibility of using modest, open-source hardware for computationally intensive tasks in power electronics research and education.

title:
An Assessment of Teachers' TPACK Integration in Classroom Instruction: Implications for 21
                    <sup>St</sup>
                    Century Teaching Practices
authors: Carlito O. Loyola, Bruxelle Jazschwa L. De Leon, Kim Renzelberg A. Licerio, Michelle Louisse Therese Mercado
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: This study assessed the integration of Technological Pedagogical Content Knowledge (TPACK) among 500 senior high school science teachers in CALABARZON, employing a descriptive quantitative approach. Data were gathered using a validated and reliable survey instrument (overall Cronbach's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha = 0.908$</tex>) distributed through online and paper formats. Findings revealed that teachers demonstrated very high proficiency in Pedagogical Knowledge (PK), Content Knowledge (CK), and Pedagogical Content Knowledge (PCK), indicating strong instructional and subject mastery. However, Technological Knowledge (TK) scored comparatively lower, suggesting limited expertise in advanced or discipline-specific digital tools. The composite mean ratings across TPACK domains reflected teachers' readiness to integrate technology with pedagogy and content, though continuous professional development remains essential. Results emphasized that teachers with greater experience and prior training in educational technology achieved higher TPACK scores. The study concludes that sustained institutional and technological support is vital for enhancing teachers' digital competence and effective TPACK integration in science education.

title: Bayesian Echo State Networks for Uncertainty Estimation in Dynamic Systems
authors: Zhaozhao Zhang, Fangrui Zhang, Kang Li, Yingqin Zhu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: When facing complex, highly nonlinear, and noisy systems, conventional Echo State Networks (ESNs) with simple point-estimate outputs may produce unstable predictions and fail to characterize the predictive distribution in a probabilistic sense. To address this issue, we propose a Bayesian Echo State Network (BN-ESN) and its learning algorithm. The key idea is to augment the ESN readout with two Gaussian-sampling probabilistic layers to quantify uncertainty in dynamic systems. We employ variational Bayesian inference to approximate the intractable posterior, and train the network parameters by maximizing the evidence lower bound (ELBO) via gradient-based optimization. By computing the mean and standard deviation from Monte Carlo samples of the predictive distribution, we construct confidence intervals for time-series forecasts, enabling uncertainty-aware modeling of complex dynamics. Experimental results on a synthetic benchmark and a wind-farm wind-speed forecasting task demonstrate that BN-ESN delivers competitive predictive accuracy and effective uncertainty estimation, indicating its potential for practical deployment in complex high-noise environments.

title: Multimode Operation of Grid-Tied Voltage Source Converter with Synchronous Harmonic Decomposition and Regulation
authors: Korivi Yogananda Reddy, Praveen Vankadari, Rupesh Wandhare
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: In grid-connected systems with renewables and electric vehicles, the presence of nonlinear loads further deteriorates power quality, necessitating reliable compensation strategies. Conventional harmonic filters require additional hardware and employ extraction techniques that suffer from inherent delays and limited accuracy for dynamic harmonic variations. To address these issues, in this paper, a Feedback-Regulated Harmonic Detection System (FRHDS) based on Multiple Rotating Frame Transformation (MRFT) theory is integrated with the gridtied Voltage Source Converter (VSC) used in Electric Vehicle Charging Stations (EVCS). The extracted harmonic components are incorporated into the conventional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d q$</tex>-based VSC controller as reference signals, enabling active filtering without affecting existing charge dynamics. Moreover, the novelty of the proposed approach is that it offers flexibility for partial or full harmonic mitigation depending on the available VSC power capacity. Finally, a multimodal control strategy with active harmonic filtering for both grid-to-vehicle (G2V) and vehicle-to-grid (V2G) modes has been implemented in simulation to verify the efficacy of the proposed compensation technique.

title: Fuzzy Manifold Transfer Learning for Remaining Useful Life Prediction of Li-Ion Batteries
authors: Sun-Ho Kwon, Yong-Hyeon Kim, Hyeon-Jin Jang, Yeong-Jin Goh, Kyoung-Min Kim
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Autonomous Systems (ICoIAS)
abstract: Accurate Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction for Li-ion batteries is challenged by non-linear degradation and data scarcity. This paper proposes a Fuzzy Manifold Transfer Learning (FMTL) framework to address these issues. The proposed method utilizes t-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE) to learn a low-dimensional manifold from operational data, upon which Fuzzy C-Means (FCM) clustering defines soft degradation states. This fuzzy-state-aware representation is subsequently integrated into a Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) model to yield accurate RUL predictions with quantifiable uncertainty. To overcome data limitations, a transfer learning strategy is employed to fine-tune a pre-trained model using only sparse, early-life data from target batteries. Validation on the NASA dataset demonstrates the model's superior accuracy and robustness compared to Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) baselines. Furthermore, the study evaluates the framework's performance under varying data availability and computational constraints, providing a data-efficient solution for industrial battery prognostics.

title: Research on Power Allocation Strategy of Flywheel Energy Storage Array Based on Model Predictive Control
authors: Jianzhuang Song, You Rui
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: In this paper, a power allocation and coordinated control strategy of flywheel array based on dual vector model predictive control (Model Predictive Control, MPC) is proposed. Firstly, from the two levels of converter level and system level, the control methods of flywheel array in different operating modes are introduced in detail, and a power distribution method of flywheel array is designed to improve the energy balance and consistency within the array and accelerate the SOC balance speed of flywheel array.

title: FAC-Diff: A Feature-Augmented Conditional Diffusion Model for Spatio-Temporal Imputation
authors: Taoyuan Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Spatiotemporal data is essential in real-world systems like urban computing and climate monitoring, yet realworld observations often suffer from missing values due to sensor failures or transmission errors. Therefore, spatiotemporal data imputation is a key research problem, which aims to accurately impute missing values based on the available observed data. However, existing methods struggle with spatiotemporal dependency modeling as well as the construction and utilization of conditional information. To address these challenges, we propose a Feature-Augmented Conditional Diffusion model (FAC-Diff) that enhances spatiotemporal imputation. FAC-Diff introduces a conditional feature enhancement module, which integrates contextual information from adjacent time windows to improve the model's ability to infer missing values. Additionally, we propose a novel noise prediction module with spatiotemporal adaptive embeddings to better capture spatiotemporal relationships while avoiding the high computational overhead of complex architectures like GNNs. We conducted extensive experiments on real datasets, and the results show that FAC-Diff achieves state-of-the-art performance in various missing data scenarios, significantly improving interpolation accuracy and efficiency.

title: Compiler.next: A Search-Based Compiler to Power the AI-Native Future of Software Engineering
authors: Filipe R. Cogo, Gustavo A. Oliva, Ahmed E. Hassan
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: Unbalanced and balanced competition strategy-assisted dual-swarm optimizer for constrained multi-objective optimization
authors: Yubo Wang, Chengyu Hu, Xinyi Wu, Xuesong Yan, Wenyin Gong, Liang Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Constrained multi-objective optimization problems often exhibit complex and fragmented feasible regions, which makes global constrained Pareto front (CPF) search challenging. To address this, we present an unbalanced and balanced competitive strategy-assisted dual-swarm optimizer (UBCSO). UBCSO introduces a balanced swarm that uses random pairing and even grouping to accelerate convergence toward feasible regions, and an unbalanced, cluster-based swarm divides each cluster into three fixed groups for intra-cluster learning, promoting diverse exploration. Within clusters, we propose an unbalanced competition strategy to achieve more precise particle selection during learning, and a cluster-based environmental selection enables effective information exchange across clusters and swarms, enhancing CPF search. Additionally, the proposed cluster integration strategy enables efficient use of computing resources by reducing exploration in non-promising regions. Finally, across 37 instances, UBCSO attained the best overall ranking compared to ten popular methods (IGD+ 1.7838, HV 1.984) and achieved the best IGD+ metric in 25 out of 37 problems.

title: Joint Reconstruction of Building Layouts and Concealed Targets via Structural-Prior-Guided Compressive Sensing
authors: Chen Qiu, Zihan Xu, Jiahui Chen, Fengzhi Shao, Guobing Qian, Shisheng Guo, Guolong Cui, Lingjiang Kong
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Compressive sensing (CS) technology has proven highly effective in rapid data acquisition and super-resolution target imaging for through-the-wall radar imaging (TWRI) applications. However, most existing CS-based TWRI techniques focus primarily on high-quality imaging of behind-wall targets, often neglecting the reconstruction of building layouts, which is essential for determining the relative positions of targets in unknown environments. To address this limitation, a structural-prior-guided CS framework is proposed for the joint reconstruction of building layouts and behind-wall targets. Specifically, first, distinct imaging models are developed for layouts and targets, accounting for their unique structural properties: layouts, referring to wall structures, typically manifest as extended, piecewise-continuous line-like structures, whereas targets manifest as compact, point-like structures. Building on these models, a unified constrained optimization problem is formulated by integrating (i) the strong inter-channel correlation of layout echoes, enforced via a low-rank regularization on the layout component, and (ii) structured sparsity priors tailored to both the layout and target images. Then, the resulting composite problem is efficiently solved using proximal gradient algorithm, yielding simultaneous reconstruction of the unknown building layouts and behind-wall targets. Finally, simulations and experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: Extending the field of view in modulation-based X-ray phase microtomography
authors: Dominik John, Junan Chen, Christoph Gaßner, Sara Savatović, Lisa Marie Petzold, Sami Wirtensohn, Mirko Riedel, Jörg U. Hammel, Julian Moosmann, Felix Beckmann, Matthias Wieczorek, Julia Herzen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Recent advances in propagation-based phase-contrast imaging, such as hierarchical imaging, have enabled the visualization of internal structures in large biological specimens and material samples. However, modulation-based techniques, which provide quantitative electron density information, face challenges when imaging larger objects due to stringent beam stability requirements and detector distortions. Extending the field of view of these methods is crucial for obtaining comparable quantitative results across beamlines and adapting to the smaller beam profiles of fourth-generation synchrotron sources.We introduce a novel image processing technique combining an eigenflat optimization with deformable image registration to address the challenges and enable quantitative high-resolution scans of centimeter-sized objects with multiple-micrometer resolution. We demonstrate the potential of the method by obtaining an electron density map of a rat brain sample 15mm in diameter despite the limited horizontal field of view of 6mm of the beamline. This showcases the technique’s ability to significantly widen the range of applications of modulation-based techniques in both biological and materials science research.

title: An Improved Ion-Shuttling Approach for QCCD Architectures
authors: Tung-Yeh Wu, Ting-Chi Wang
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Symposium on Physical Design
abstract: None

title: National Advisory Committee: ICTCT 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)

title: Maximizing Revenue in Roadside Green Edge Server System
authors: Suvarthi Sarkar, Abinash Kumar Ray, Aryabartta Sahu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: The autonomous vehicle industry is growing rapidly and requires substantial computational resources for tasks such as perception and decision-making. Vehicular edge computing addresses this need by offloading computations to roadside edge servers. In alignment with green cloud computing trends, these servers are increasingly powered by solar energy and are equipped with batteries to maintain continuous operation during periods of low solar availability. We refer to such infrastructure as Green Edge Server System (GESS). In this work, we maximize the revenue of GESS by efficiently scheduling offloaded computational tasks where the tasks are characterized by arrival time, deadline, resource demand, and revenue. We consider two variants of the GESS: one with an infinite battery and another with a finite battery. For each cases, we propose customized heuristic for solutions for offline scenario by exploiting predicted solar power and incoming task patterns. After this, we present a online strategy which adjusts offline schedule dynamically based on real-time deviations in solar power and incoming task pattern. Our approach takes advantage of the nonlinear behavior of the cubic power model to minimize cost through efficient task batching. Experimental evaluations on real-life datasets demonstrate that our methods outperform state-of-the-art baselines by up to 40%. Additionally, the system remains robust under uncertainty, with revenue decreasing by only 4.68% when subjected to 10% spikes in task load and power shortages.

title: Development of Self-Compensated Thin-Film Strain Gauges Suitable for Applications From Room Temperature to 1200°C
authors: Haigang Wang, Mingyang Kong, Biling Wang, Ruliang Xu, Hanbin Wang, Fan Zhang, Yanfeng Zhan, Liangjie Gao, Qiang Li, Zhipeng Zhao, Zhe Yang, Xin Zhao, Zhichun Liu, Junsheng Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The strain measurement accuracy of aerospace hot end components is severely affected by the change of environmental temperature. How to reduce the apparent strain derived from temperature variation within a wide range still is of a great challenge hitherto. In this work, a self-compensated thin film strain gauge (SC-TFSG) with sensing resistor and suspended compensation resistor is developed. These two resistors are orthogonal to each other and form a half bridge measurement structure, which is used to reduce the apparent strain introduced by temperature changes. Results show that the temperature coefficient of resistance (TCR) of the SC-TFSG is only -136 ppm/°C within a wide temperature range from room temperature to 1200 °C. The thermal output is 89.52% lower than the strain gauges without compensation resistor. Furthermore, the results of the standard strain cyclic loading test at 600 °C, 800 °C, 1000 °C and 1200 °C indicate that the maximum error between the SC-TFSG measured values and the standard loading strain can be controlled within 6%. The work provides an effective method for strain measurement and accuracy compensation of hot end components in a wide temperature range.

title: Dust to Tower: Prior-Driven Coarse-to-Fine Photo-Realistic Scene Reconstruction from Sparse Uncalibrated Images_supp1-3677463.mp4
authors: Yongcai wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Photo-realistic scene reconstruction from sparseview, uncalibrated images is highly required in practice. Although some successes have been made, existing methods are either Sparse-View but require accurate camera parameters (i.e., intrinsic and extrinsic), or SfM-free but need densely captured images. This paper proposes Dust to Tower (D2T), a novel coarse-to-fine framework to address the coupled difficulty. The key idea is to explicitly narrow down the solution space and then introduce reliable supervision at novel viewpoints without resorting to expensive diffusion-based view synthesis. To do this, we first introduce a Coarse Construction Module (CCM) which exploits a fastMulti-View Stereo model to initialize a 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) and recover initial camera poses. To refine the 3D model at novel viewpoints, we introduce Confidence- Aware Depth Alignment (CADA), which aligns a monocular inverse-depth prior to the reliable regions of the coarse depth using DUSt3R confidence, producing sharp and scale-consistent depth maps for accurate warping. We further propose Warped Image-Guided Inpainting (WIGI), which converts the accurate warped views into multi-view-consistent pseudo supervision via elaborate warping and inpainting process. Experiments on three benchmark datasets show that D2T achieves superior novel view synthesis quality and pose accuracy over ten representative baselines, while keeping high efficiency. Code will be publicly available.

title: Seismic Data Deblended with Curvelet-Based Multi-level Texture Perception and Regularization
authors: Yifan Ma, Xiaotao Wen, Hao Wu, Bo Li, Huan Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Blended noise is coherent noise introduced during the simultaneous acquisition of multiple seismic sources. Due to its similar time-distance curve characteristics with effective waves in seismic records, suppressing blended noise poses great challenges. To address this problem, we propose a curvelet-transform-based method incorporating multi-level texture perception and regularization. Seismic data are first transformed into the curvelet domain to extract structural information and enhance the physical differences between similar signals. These features are then introduced as prior knowledge into a generative adversarial network (GAN) equipped with a multi-level texture perception mechanism for signal separation. In addition, curvelet-transform regularization is applied to the separated results as a soft constraint, guiding the model to reconstruct seismic signals with high fidelity across multiple scales and orientations. A hierarchical discriminator loss is further employed to impose high-dimensional constraints on the separated signals, improving the stability of the deblending process and the preservation of structural details. Experiments on both synthetic and field data demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior blended-noise suppression performance compared with DnCNN, GAN, and U-Net.

title: Double-Scale LLM Cooperation for Internet of Agents-Oriented Robot Scenarios
authors: Longyu Zhou, Wenjiao Feng, Yijing Liu, Supeng Leng, Tony Quek
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: The evaluation from the Internet of Things (IoT) to the Internet of Everything (IoE) has achieved the intelligent connection among massive IoT devices. In this context, the emerging Internet of Agents (IoA) is emerging to perform a fundamental shift in the digital paradigm for intelligent cognition. The IoA is expected to enable autonomous cooperation for intelligent applications, such as robots-based intelligent logistics. The IoA can envision a cognition-centric ecosystem in which intelligent robots perform cooperative sensing and reasoning. To achieve this goal, this article designs a terminal-edge cooperative Large Language Model (LLM) framework. It can achieve accurate service provisions by integrating cross-layer computing resources for IoA scenarios. In addition, our framework can enable robots to perform autonomous decision-making instead of requesting from the cloud or the edge servers for real-time service provisions. Based on our framework, we provide several potential IoA scenarios with detailed analysis. A case study of robots-based parcel sorting and handling is selected to validate the feasibility and effectiveness of our framework. The experiment results show that our framework achieves a high parcel sorting accuracy of up to 95% with a low delivery latency of under 1 second compared to traditional centralized manners on average, respectively.

title: Transforming Manufacturing Execution Systems with Large Language Models and Multi-Modal Processing
authors: Yi-Ling Cheng, Kung-Hsu Hou, Chung-Hsing Chao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This research introduces a framework integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) with Multi-modal Collaborative Processing (MCP) to enhance Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES) through automated report generation. Addressing the challenge of time-intensive manual report preparation, the LLM+MCP architecture achieves over 90% reduction in report generation time: daily reports from 60 to 5 minutes (91.67% reduction), weekly reports from 180 to 15 minutes (91.67% reduction), and monthly reports from 480 to 30 minutes (93.75% reduction). By leveraging advanced natural language processing and multi-modal data fusion, the framework automates data extraction, analysis, and synthesis into executive reports. Validated over three months in real manufacturing settings, the system demonstrates 99.9% availability, response times of 5-60 seconds, and improved decision-making efficiency. This study provides a scalable and replicable solution for MES digital transformation, highlighting key innovations in computational efficiency and multi-model data integration.

title: Deep Multi-Channel Signal Decoding with Cascaded Latent-space Fidelity Boosting for Precision Medicine
authors: Qingxue Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Remote health monitoring is of great potential to bring long-term continuous big data for precision medicine. The challenge arises since the noise and interferences usually contaminate the signal significantly. In this study, to decode the medical insights from the rapidly decayed signal captured remotely, we propose a deep multi-channel signal decoding system with cascaded latent-space fidelity boosting. The system is composed of the multi-channel acoustic sensing device and the deep multi-channel signal decoding algorithm. The device can be placed on the bedframe to capture the spatial dynamics of the acoustic information during breathing. The deep learning framework with cascaded latent-space fidelity boosting before inference, can suppress noise and interferences hidden in the signal, through multiple iterations of space transformation and signal reconstruction. We take the sleep apnea as the real-world study, to demonstrate the promise of the proposed system. Our experiments have demonstrated the effectiveness of the system, superior to state-of-the-art. This study will promisingly advance remote signal perception and mining for precision medicine.

title: A Hybrid Regenerative Braking Strategy in BLDC Motor Driven Electrical Vehicles
authors: Pandule Vishal Prakash, Abhaya Kumar Sahoo, V. Sankaranarayanan
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: In electric vehicles (EVs), maximizing the distance traveled per charge is a major challenge. This can be addressed by improving the efficiency of the motor, power converter, and battery, as well as using regenerative braking. Among various methods, single-stage regenerative braking offers notable advantages, where optimizing the duty cycle is key to maximizing energy recovery and minimizing stopping time. This study investigates four braking techniques: single-switch, twoswitch, three-switch, and plugging, the first three enabling energy recovery. Using MATLAB Simulation, the performance of each method is analyzed. Based on this, a new strategy is proposed that dynamically switches braking modes based on brake pedal input, effectively reducing stopping time while maximizing energy recovery. Simulation results and analysis confirm its effectiveness.

title: Snow Ablation Inspired Metaheuristic Algorithm for MPPT in Solar PV System
authors: Madhuraj, Sourav Mallick
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Solar photovoltaic (PV) systems are the prime choice because of their high energy density and low cost. Their efficiency, however, relies on precise Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT), which is still difficult under nonlinear I-V characteristics and changing environmental conditions like snow, shading, and temperature fluctuations. A Snow Ablation Optimization (SAO) algorithm is proposed to maximize power extraction from PV systems operating in changing weather conditions. In contrast to the conventional algorithms, which tend to fail in conditions of non-uniform irradiance, SAO draws motivation from the natural phenomenon of snow ablation, sequentially exposing the Global point of Maximum power generation on the panel of photo voltaic system design. To confirm the effectiveness of the adopted method, the algorithms were examined on a boost converter which is connected along with a photovoltaic (PV) energy system. The Matlab simulation results reflects that as compared to other metaheuristic algorithms like Flying Squirrel Search Optimization (FSSO), Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) the metaheuristic SAO algorithm is highly efficient and quickly reaches the maximum power point under varying climatic conditions. By optimizing adaptability and efficiency, SAO provides a feasible solution for real-time MPPT in Photovoltaic (PV) systems.

title: Modulation and Control Technique for a Reconfigurable Capacitor-based DAB Converter in EV Applications
authors: Thresia M. M., Josephine R. L.
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The rising trend in the applications of electric vehicles (EVs) has heightened the requirement of compact, efficient and dependable power conversion systems. One of the promising solutions is the Dual Active Bridge (DAB) converter because of its ability to transfer power in both directions at high frequency on an isolated basis. The modulation methods such as Single Phase Shift (SPS) and Dual Phase Shift (DPS) are supported in it because it can precisely govern power flow in contrast to traditional converters. These characteristics render it applicable in charging and discharging of EV. But still problems of instability of output voltage and high ripple of the inductor current persist and more sophisticated control measures are necessary to improve the results and provide stable and efficient energy transfer. Research has lately attempted to enhance the performance of the DAB converters with closed-loop and optimal modulation. This paper uses a reconfigurable capacitor-based DAB converter controlled by proportional integral (PI) controller to achieve a range of output voltage of 300 V to 900 V. MATLAB/ Simulink simulations examine how phase shift, inductance and controller gains affect operations. The comparison of the conventional openloop control with the modified closed-loop control is made in terms of voltage regulation, reduction of current ripple and efficiency factors. The aim is to find out the best modulation and control method to optimize performance of the DAB converters and facilitate effective and stable operation to viable use of electric vehicles (EV).

title: Mechanism and Modeling of Radiated EMI Measurement Using Current Transformers
authors: Jianxiang Pan, Qingbin Chen
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Measurement of radiated electromagnetic interference (EMI) must be carried out in anechoic chambers or open fields. The cost of such measurements is usually high, and the results only show the electric field strength emitted by the device under test. These results provide limited help for the mitigation of radiated EMI. Based on the relationship between common-mode (CM) current and electric field strength, this paper proposes a method to evaluate the radiated EMI of switching power supplies (SPS). This method measures the CM current on input and output cables using current transformers (CTs). Subsequently, the equivalent circuit of the CT is established, and a method for extracting its circuit parameters is given. Finally, experimental results verify the effectiveness and correctness of the proposed equivalent circuit of the CT, which lays a foundation for the design of CTs.

title: Guardian Angel - A Real Time Emotion-Aware Monitoring System
authors: Rekha R, S Mahadhevan, V Shalini, Vishal M, Syed Wasiq Hussain
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: GUARDIAN ANGEL is a novel, multi-modal real-time emotion-aware monitoring system for vulnerable populations. It utilizes an ESP32-based wearable to collect physiological biomarkers, specifically Heart Rate Variability (HRV) and Electrodermal Activity (EDA/GSR). Data is streamed via MQTT to a decoupled IoT architecture (ESP32 → AWS Lambda) where a machine learning model,validated against the WESAD dataset, classifies the user's state into one of four discrete emotion scores. The final score triggers the Ambient Feedback Engine (Raspberry Pi), which provides proactive, therapeutic intervention by adjusting an RGB LED (color) and playing prescriptive audio. The system shifts well-being support from reactive observation to proactive ambient guidance.

title: Enhancing Forest Fire Detection: Lightweight YOLOv8-AEC with EMA, CSPPC and Adown for Multi-Scale Targets
authors: Guoxiong Xue
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Forest fires pose a severe threat to global ecosystems and human society. Traditional methods (manual patrols, infrared monitoring) suffer from poor timeliness and high missed/false detection rates, while existing YOLO models still face challenges like small-target (e.g., tiny early fire spots) missed detection, complex forest background false detection, and unbalanced lightweight design-accuracy. To address these, this study proposes YOLOv8-AEC, a lightweight forest fire detection model improved from YOLOv8n, optimized via three core modules: 1) Efficient Multi-scale Attention (EMA) to enhance multi-scale fire feature extraction and suppress background interference; 2) Lightweight CSPPC module to reduce neck computation by 25% and strengthen feature fusion; 3) Adown downsampling to cut feature loss by 15% and improve small-target recall. Experiments were conducted on the FFD dataset (1,200 original images, expanded to 10,800 via data augmentation) in an Intel Core i9-13900K/NVIDIA RTX 4090 environment. Results show: YOLOv8-AEC has only 2.2MB parameters (26.7% less than YOLOv8), mAP50 of 0.803, F1-score of 0.789, and 13.8% higher recall for early small smoke; it outperforms mainstream models (SSD, YOLOv3-tiny, etc.) in complex scenarios (dense forest occlusion, light interference). YOLOv8-AEC balances high accuracy, lightweight design, and real-time performance, providing reliable support for automated forest fire early warning (UAV patrols, forest edge sensors).

title: Indigenous Design and Development of Digital Controller-Integrated SiC Inverter for High Performance Induction Motor Drives
authors: Urbesh Sarkar, Prasun Mishra
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The growing demand for high-efficiency and compact power conversion systems has accelerated the adoption of Wide Bandgap (WBG) semiconductor devices in electric vehicles, renewable energy systems, and industrial drives. In this context, Kelvin-source Silicon Carbide (SiC) MOSFETs have emerged as a promising choice, offering reduced common-source inductance, lower switching losses, and improved dynamic performance compared to conventional SiC devices. This paper presents the indigenous design and development of a three-phase, two-level voltage source inverter (VSI) based on Kelvin-source SiC MOSFETs, controlled by a TMS320F28379D dual-core real-time microcontroller. Experimental validation is performed on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.5 ~\text{kW}, 415 ~\mathrm{V}, 50 ~\text{Hz}$</tex> squirrel-cage induction motor (SCIM) under both open-loop <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$v / f$</tex> control and rotor flux-oriented control (RFOC). The proposed inverter employs optimized PCB layouts for the power stage, gate drivers, and controller, as well as sensor interface, while incorporating robust protection features against overvoltage, overcurrent, and overtemperature conditions. Experimental results demonstrate the stable operation with minimal overshoot in drain-to-source and gate-to-source waveforms, while achieving high-power density and efficiency of the inverter.

title: Intelligent Aerial Surveillance with Gesture Recognition and Alerts System
authors: Amarnath R, S. kayalvizhi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: This paper introduces a gesture recognition system that converts CCTV cameras into an intelligent communication device for physically disabled persons and emergency personnel. Through the use of OpenCV, NumPy, and the Math module, the system identifies pre-programmed hand gestures in real-time. The moment a critical gesture is detected, Twilio's API automatically sends notifications via SMS or messaging applications to emergency responders or caregivers. Lightweight, modular, and scalable in design, the system is capable of integrating more features, turning common CCTV into a sophisticated surveillance tool. The technology provides improved security, accessibility, and emergency response effectiveness across different environments.

title: Design of a 2 Port Multi-Band Antenna for Sub-6 GHz 5G Wireless Devices
authors: MONISHA P, D.JASMINE DAVID, J JOHN PAUL
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: A two port multi-input multi output MIMO antenna design for wireless 5 G devices operating below 6 GHz is presented. It covers the main sub- 6 GHz 5 G bands by operating at four resonance frequencies such as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.2 \text{GHz}, 2.3 \text{GHz}, 5.6 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and 5.885 GHz. The resonances have a voltage standing wave ratio (VSWR) of 1.06 and a minimum reflection coefficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{S}_{11}\right)$</tex> of -30 dB, brings compatibility of highest impedance. To provide required coverage to the entire frequency bands, broad impedance bandwidths displayed by antenna around each resonance as 300-500 MHz. The diversity performance of MIMO is reinforced by continuous mutual coupling below -20 dB and excellent port to port isolation. The antenna gains radiation efficacy above 88 % and standard radiation patterns with peak gains of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.2-5.8 \text{dBi}$</tex>. Diversity and isolation behaviors are confirmed by the envelope correlation coefficient (ECC), which stays below 0.01. Given its small size, high isolation, and multiband functionality, the two-port design is suitable for 5G smartphones, Internet of Things IoT modules, and small wireless systems.

title: Frequency-Aware Personalized Multi-Annotator Medical Image Segmentation with Mixture of Experts
authors: Soya Suzuki, Masaaki Ikehara
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Multi-annotator medical image segmentation involves modeling the annotations of multiple experts, each of whom interprets ambiguous image regions differently. Although recent approaches, such as generative or embedding-based personalization, have made progress, they often offer limited control over expert-specific behavior. In this study, we present a novel framework that enables explicit and flexible personalization by integrating a Mixture of Experts (MoE) structure into a U-Net decoder. We further extend this with a newly proposed MuGE-MoE Layer, inspired by the Multiple Granularity Edge detection method (MuGE), which enables the network to learn which high-frequency components (i.e., fine textures) each annotator focuses on. Specifically, we divide the input feature map into two channel-wise halves, apply a Fourier transform to the first half, and modulate its high-frequency components using annotator-specific learnable weights to encode individualized textural attention. Within the MuGE-MoE Layer, we incorporate a MoE block in which each annotator is assigned a dedicated gating network that adaptively combines shared expert decoders to capture annotator-specific segmentation styles. To ensure both personalization and consistency across annotators, this block is designed to include not only expert networks but also a parallel shared convolutional path. Moreover, we employ Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to minimize the computational burden of scaling up the number of experts. Experimental results on multi-annotator segmentation datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms existing baselines in terms of personalized prediction accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency.

title: Complex-Valued Orthogonal Superposition Encoding for Quantum Image Transmission
authors: Udara Jayasinghe, Thisarani Atulugama, Anil Fernando
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Reliable, high-fidelity image transmission in consumer electronics remains a challenge, particularly over noisy or bandwidth-limited channels. While quantum communication offers potential solutions, conventional single-qubit encoding schemes are limited by low state diversity and noise resilience, reducing their effectiveness for compressed image transmission. To address this, we propose Complex-Valued Orthogonal Unitary Superposition (COUS) encoding, which employs complex-valued superposition to generate highly noise-resilient quantum states within a single-qubit framework. The images are first source encoded using JPEG or HEIF and undergo classical channel coding. The resulting data are quantum encoded via COUS and transmitted over noisy channels. At the receiver, quantum states are sequentially COUS-decoded, channel-decoded, and source-decoded to reconstruct the images. System performance is evaluated using Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM), and Universal Quality Index (UQI). The results show that COUS encoding significantly improves image quality and noise resilience, achieving up to 3 dB channel SNR gain over advanced Quantum Fourier Transform (QFT)-based encodings, 4 dB gain over Hadamard encoding, and 6 dB gain relative to equivalent-bandwidth classical systems, demonstrating its potential for reliable, high-fidelity quantum image transmission regardless of image format.

title: Two-View Honeypot Framework for Behavioral Analysis of Consumer IoT Attack Campaigns
authors: Sara Afzal, Farrukh Aslam Khan, Abraham Gebrehiwot, Filippo Maria Lauria
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Internet-facing consumer Internet of Things (IoT) devices are probed and compromised at scale, yet converting raw honeypot logs into clear evidence about how attacks operate and which sessions belong to the same campaign remains difficult, especially without labels. In this work, we present a scalable two-view pipeline applied to one year of Cowrie SSH/Telnet logs that emulate vulnerable IoT endpoints (1.77M sessions; 3.67M command events). In the behavior view, we encode each session’s command sequence with an E5 encoder, reduce to 50 dimensions via Incremental PCA, and cluster the corpus with MiniBatchKMeans (k=250). On a 97k stratified sample, we observe high Silhouette and clear exemplars, and the full run completes in minutes on research hardware. As a robustness check, HDBSCAN on ~4×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> sessions discovers 44 dense groups with 13.94% labeled as noise. In the infrastructure view, we independently extract normalized URLs, domains, IPv4s, and SHA-256 hashes, build a 14-day time-windowed artifact-sharing graph with type-weighted edges and degree caps, and detect communities with Louvain. A representative graph has 250,043 nodes and 1,575,383 edges; its giant component has 18,938 nodes and 102,289 edges, revealing distinct, bursty communities that target IoT devices. Comparing views for overlap yields a low Adjusted Rand Index (0.019 at ≈ 1.07% coverage), indicating they capture complementary structure; similar workflows need not reuse the same infrastructure, and vice versa. We package outputs as a compact "findings pack" per large community (top artifacts, exemplar command snippets, and weekly activity curves). Overall, the pipeline offers a practical path from raw IoT honeypot logs to campaign-level insight without supervision.

title: Autonomous VTOL Drone Solution for Vegetation Overgrowth and Early Wildfire Detection
authors: Steeve Cantave, Dhruv Patel, Osmin Nolasco, James Sullivan, Daniel Burbano, Sasan Haghani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Wildfires increasingly threaten infrastructure, ecosystems, and public safety. In the United States alone, they cause billions of dollars in annual damages, disrupt critical transportation networks, and accelerate ecological degradation. Early detection and proactive risk management are therefore essential for mitigating these impacts. Unmanned aerial vehicles offer a flexible solution for environmental monitoring, yet conventional multirotor drones suffer from limited endurance and range due to battery constraints. Vertical Take-Off and Landing (VTOL) fixed-wing platforms aim to address this limitation by combining the hovering precision of quadcopters with the aerodynamic efficiency of airplanes. Building on this principle, this work presents a unified framework for autonomous, VTOL-based environmental monitoring that addresses both risk prevention and early hazard detection. We develop two complementary aerial systems featuring 3D-printed modular airframes, onboard AI inference, and adaptive flight control: (1) RailVision, a 4+1 VTOL quadplane designed to detect vegetation overgrowth along railways; and (2) an Early Wildfire Detection Drone that identifies emerging hotspots from low-resolution thermal imagery in real time.

title: Hybrid Cascade Delegation Vulnerability: A New Threat to Consumer Electronics
authors: Qian Wang, Bashan Zuo, Ming He, Sherali Zeadally
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: We identify and analyze the Hybrid Cascade Delegation (HCD) vulnerability. HCD is a complex delegation chain formed by combining NS and CNAME records. A carefully constructed HCD chain can exploit and bypass resolvers’ constraints on the number of outgoing queries per resolution. This vulnerability forces a resolver to issue a large volume of queries, resulting in significant amplification of DNS traffic. Currently employed defense technologies are unable to mitigate the threats posed by this vulnerability. In modern consumer electronic devices where service endpoints are embedded and vendor-controlled, the failure of DNS resolution to the core service infrastructure can easily cause high-intensity and large-scale impact. We investigate the HCD vulnerability and address this critical issue in consumer electronic environments. We also propose and discuss mitigation solutions for the HCD vulnerability.

title: Accurate Index-based Elemental Image Mapping for Light Field Display
authors: Wonseok Son, Chanyoung Shin, Sungwook Min
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: We propose a periodic index-based elemental-image mapping method that renders a light-field display with more usable viewpoints under arbitrary slant angles. By combining indexing with high-sampling subpixel modeling, the approach supports subpixel-level rendering for various pixel geometries. The experimental results on an S-stripe OLED panel verify the suppression of chromatic dispersion and improved 3D image quality.

title: Smart Quality Monitoring System for Edible Oils
authors: S. Deivanayagi, Palanivel P, Vignesh S R, Nithish P
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: This project is centered on adulteration detection and quality monitoring of edible oils with Machine Learning (ML) methods combined with a hardware sensing system. The model to be proposed is designed to assess the quality and purity of different edible oils like groundnut, mustard, sunflower, palm, coconut, soybean, and olive oils in terms of prominent physicochemical parameters like Free Fatty Acid (FFA), Peroxide Value (PV), Iodine Value (IV), Saponification Value (SV), Refractive Index (RI), and Density. The software module is designed to predict these parameters employing ML algorithms-specifically, the XGBoost classifier-to label oil samples adulterated or nonadulterated. In the hardware setup, the density parameter has been accurately measured employing an ultrasonic sensor with an Arduino Mega interface. The measured density values are incorporated into the software model for prediction. Experimental findings show that the system offers sound classification and forms a basis for an automated oil quality monitoring equipment in the future.

title: Parametric Analysis of Resonant Compensation Networks for Low-Power Wireless Charging in Urban Electric Vehicles
authors: Yuvaraja Shanmugam, P. Rathnavel, Rasolondrenibe Marius Christin, Sathyanarayanan TKS, Narayanamoorthi R
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) has emerged as a feasible alternative to conventional plug-in charging, particularly for urban electric vehicles (EVs) such as e-rickshaws that demand compact, user-friendly, and low-maintenance energy delivery solutions. In such systems, resonant compensation networks play a pivotal role in ensuring high-efficiency power transfer under varying coupling and load conditions. This work conducts a comparative parametric analysis of four widely adopted resonant topologies: Series– Series-Parallel (S-SP), Series–LCC (S-LCC), LCC–Series (LCC-S), and Double LCC (DLCC) with a focus on their voltage gain and current gain characteristics. Equivalent circuit models are developed for each configuration, and their performance is evaluated through NI Multisim-based AC and parametric sweeps across a wide range of magnetic coupling coefficients and load resistances. The analysis is grounded in a practical 3.3 kW, 220 V, 85 kHz stationary wireless charging system designed specifically for Level 1 e-rickshaw applications. Results reveal significant differences in gain behaviour across the topologies, with DLCC exhibiting the highest voltage stability and current control under misaligned and variable load conditions. Conversely, simpler topologies such as S-SP demonstrate poor gain regulation and thermal performance. The study offers critical insights into the impact of compensation topology on power delivery, system stress, and control requirements. These findings support the development of reliable and efficient WPT infrastructure tailored for light EV platforms operating in complex urban environments.

title: Harnessing Generative AI for L2 Written Corrective Feedback: A Systematic Review of Current Research and Future Direction
authors: Linqian Ding, Di Zou, Lucas Kohnke, Haoran Xie
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: Automated writing evaluation (AWE) has long been employed to assist second language (L2) learners in improving their writing through timely and consistent feedback. The emergence of generative artificial intelligence (GAI) introduces new possibilities for enhancing AWE systems. While a growing number of studies have explored using GAI for written corrective feedback (WCF), the findings remain mixed. This study systematically reviewed 37 empirical articles published between 2023 and 2025 that examine the application of GAI for L2 WCF. The review aimed to clarify research foci, examine implementation practices, and identify critical gaps to inform future research. Findings indicate that GAI tools show promise in providing accurate and helpful feedback, particularly at the surface level. However, limitations persist in addressing higherorder writing concerns. Hybrid feedback models that combine GAI with teacher input appear to yield the most comprehensive benefits. Additionally, issues related to student engagement, feedback uptake, prompt design, feedback sources, and individual learner differences require further investigation. This review highlights the need for more longitudinal, context-sensitive, and learner-centered research to fully harness the pedagogical potential of GAI in L2 writing instruction.

title: Research on Multi-Sensor Fusion-Based Gate Operational Status Monitoring and Early-Stage Disease Warning Technology
authors: Jianwei Tu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: As a key structure in water projects and hydropower projects, the operation status of gates directly affects engineering safety and economic scheduling. Traditional monitoring methods rely on single sensors, making it difficult to capture equipment degradation characteristics under complex operating conditions. This paper studies gate health monitoring technology based on multi-sensor fusion, through collaborative perception of multidimensional data such as vibration, stress, and acoustic emission, to construct a three-level fusion framework of "data-feature-decision". This article proposes an adaptive weighted fusion denoising method to suppress environmental interference. It uses PCA-LSTM network to extract deep temporal features and combines D-S evidence theory to complete state decision-making. Finally, based on the support vector data description (SVDD) model, early disease advanced warning was realized, providing technical support for the safe service of gates.

title: A Metric-Based Framework for Microwave Breast Imaging Array Design
authors: Eleonora Razzicchia, Carlos Ramos Flores, Yunxiao Zhang, Alan Poisot Palacios, Emily Porter, Lorenzo Crocco, Rosa Scapaticci
publish: 2026 International Conference on Medical Microwave Imaging Applications (ICMWIA)
abstract: Microwave imaging has shown promise for breast cancer detection and monitoring, yet the absence of standardized metrics for evaluating array configurations limits systematic design and comparison of prototypes. In this work, we propose the use of two quantitative metrics—spatial coverage and spectral coverage—that provide design-oriented insight into antenna placement. Their application is demonstrated on a simplified breast phantom model, with validation provided through an example of 3-D radar image reconstruction. Two array configurations were investigated: (i) a circular arrangement of eight uniformly spaced antennas in a single ring and (ii) a 16-element array composed of two rings of equally-spaced eight antennas. To further assess the role of spatial arrangement, two subsets of eight antennas were extracted from the 16-element array. The results show that the proposed metrics effectively capture the impact of geometry on image reconstruction, with the 16-element dual-ring configuration and the uniformly spaced 8-antenna ring both achieving broader and more uniform coverage than the other 8-antenna subsets. These findings lay the foundation for a reproducible framework to assess microwave imaging prototypes, representing an important step toward clinically practical imaging systems.

title: Hierarchical Inverse Kinematics for Seated Virtual Avatars: A Real-Time Motion Smoothing Algorithm Using FinalIK
authors: Jeferson B. da Costa, Lucas de M. Ferreira, Lucas G. M. Castro
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Achieving natural and comfortable motion in seated virtual avatars remains a significant challenge in Virtual Reality (VR), where noisy tracking signals and partial upper-body sensing often lead to jitter, discontinuities, and reduced embodiment. This paper presents a hierarchical inverse kinematics (IK) framework that enhances upper-body stability by combining FinalIK with coordinated head–pelvis coupling, bounded spatial smoothing, and a simplified finite-state machine (FSM) for arm control. The method runs asynchronously from the rendering loop, ensuring consistent update timing without introducing additional solver delay. Quantitative evaluation using real system telemetry shows that the proposed architecture preserves baseline solver latency (0.12 ms on average) while substantially improving motion quality, reducing wrist-trajectory jerk by 53.7% compared to a simplified baseline implementation. A user study with 12 participants further indicates increased comfort, realism, and perceived responsiveness, with 91.6% preferring the proposed approach for seated interaction tasks. These results validate the effectiveness of hierarchical IK for stabilizing upper-body motion under limited tracking and motivate future extensions toward adaptive filtering and hybrid IK–physics controllers.

title: Hybrid Compute-in-Memory Architecture for Edge AI: Navigating the Precision-Power-Latency Through Optimization Techniques
authors: K. Selva Sarmila, R. Shiva Reshma, R.P. Meenaakshi Sundhari, M. Sowmya, K. Logasoundharya
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The demand for energy-efficient edge AI hardware has shown the limitations of conventional digital architectures in achieving the desired performance per watt under tight power constraints. This paper examines the recent progress in hybrid analog-digital AI accelerator architectures developed to improve energy efficiency by retaining computational accuracy and scalability. The main advancement are analog compute-in-memory (AIMC) approaches, which minimize data movement and energy consumption by executing multiple accumulate operations directly within memory arrays. This review also discusses techniques such as DVFS and AI-driven runtime power management, which support adaptive workload-aware energy optimization across diverse application contexts. Additionally, the integration of ultralow-bit quantized neural networks, including 4-bit and binary models, improves computational throughput and energy efficiency in resource-constrained environments such as IoT nodes, wearables, and autonomous sensors. Comparative analyses shows that analog-digital codesigns continuously deliver significant energy and latency improvements over traditional digital accelerators.

title: Performance Enhancement of 3D Indoor Terahertz Communication System via Software-Aided Signal Processing and OTFS
authors: Bano Rashmi Shaik, Himansu Shekhar Pradhan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This work demonstrates the performance enhancement of 3-Dimensional (3D) Indoor Terahertz (THz) communication systems, which is operating at a higher frequency of 500 GHz. A significant challenge related to the signal degradation due to increased path loss at higher frequency is incorporated in this paper. The proposed communication system demonstrates high performance parameters such as coverage probability, SINR, and Bit Error Rate (BER) at a reduced transmit power of 4 dBm using Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation, Maximal Ratio Combining (MRC), and Minimum Mean Square Error (MMSE) equalization mechanisms. The simulation results under Multi-cluster Fluctuating Two-Ray (MFTR) fading conditions show that the proposed system achieved improved coverage and signal quality of 0.96 at 27 dB SINR threshold for the centre users compared to the existing system, which shows 0.91 for the same SINR threshold value. Furthermore, the proposed system achieved a BER of approximately 10⁻⁴ at a lower SNR of 15 dB, whereas the existing system requires nearly 20 dB SNR to achieve a similar BER, which confirms enhanced reliability under challenging indoor THz conditions.

title: Efficiency Comparison of Si and GaN-based External Power Supplies for Consumer Electronics in the Light of New Efficiency Regulations
authors: Manuel Burgsteiner, Shashank Panikkar, Markus Makoschitz
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The European Commission's recent regulation on the eco-design of external power supplies (EPS) marks a significant shift in the industry. Compared to the 2019 regulation, the 2025 version imposes stricter limits on no-load power consumption and average efficiency of the EPS. It also introduces a method for measuring and calculating the efficiency of multiport power supplies. This regulation is also timely, as new wide-bandgap semiconductor technology such as Gallium-Nitride (GaN) is rapidly evolving and becoming increasingly prominent in consumer-grade EPS, particularly in the 60 W power category. Thus, in this paper, the two Commission Regulations are compared, the specifics of the new requirements are examined and the efficiency improvements enabled by GaN-based designs with respect to conventional silicon solutions are analysed.

title: A Study of Reinforcement Learning Models for Computer Vision in Educational Scenarios
authors: Yan Tang, Lei Zheng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the problem of insufficient robustness of visual models due to dynamic disturbances such as illumination variations (fluctuations exceeding 3000 lux) and object occlusion (integrity loss exceeding 40%), this paper proposes a Dynamic Adaptive Visual Reinforcement Learning model (DAVRL). This model employs a modular hierarchical architecture, integrating a dynamic environment perception module (a multi-scale feature pyramid with spatiotemporal attention), a hybrid decision network, and a policy optimizer to create a bidirectional feedback mechanism between feature and decision flows. Experiments based on the EDU-Vis dataset (containing 12.5 hours of classroom videos, 3,200 self-study images, and 25,000 scanned assignments) show that DAVRL achieves an F1 score of 0.92 in classroom action recognition, a 6.98% and 13.58% improvement over DQN (0.86) and Q-learning (0.81), respectively. When the illumination changes from 500 lux to 3000 lux, the accuracy decays by only 8.3%, significantly lower than the 15.6% and 22.4% of the comparison models. DAVRL converges after 5,000 iterations, 33% faster than DQN. With 100,000 samples, the accuracy improves by 12.3%. The single-frame processing time of 35ms meets the real-time requirement of 30fps. Simulation results demonstrate DAVRL's high robustness, fast convergence, and sample efficiency in dynamic environments, providing an effective solution for computer vision tasks in educational scenarios such as smart classrooms and autonomous learning analysis.

title: Analysis of Traffic Flow Problem Based on K-Means Clustering Algorithm
authors: Juwen Li, Chenggang Tao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: In order to solve the problem of traffic light optimization and traffic flow control in small town scenic spots, this study focuses on the estimation of traffic flow at intersections. Firstly, the original vehicle data is preprocessed and rearranged according to the time series to obtain the traffic flow information of four phases in different periods. The K-means clustering algorithm is used to divide the day into multiple periods based on the characteristics of traffic flow changes. Then, in each period of time, the traffic flow in the four directions of west to east, east to west, north to south and south to north is estimated respectively. At the same time, according to the repeated license plate information of the four directions, the steering ratio of the vehicle to go straight, turn left and turn right at the intersection is calculated. Finally, by constructing the traffic flow model and combining the existing data to calculate the steering ratio of vehicles in all directions, the accurate estimation of three different steering traffic flows in four directions is realized, which provides basic theory and technical support for signal light optimization and traffic flow control.

title: A Novel Power Sources Planning Method under Extreme Weather Events with Constrained Renewable Energy Sources Outputs
authors: Guokang Yu, Peng Yao, Guiliang Gao, Zhiyun Hu, Junchen Wei, Qian Liang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: To balance the power supply and demand under extreme weather events with constrained renewable energy sources (RESs) outputs, this paper proposes a novel power sources planning method based on the worst scenario and integrated load demand. The extreme weather event is defined according to duration of RES low output. The worst scenario is identified based on the extreme weather event. Based on the worst scenario, a novel model of power sources planning is established to find a more accurate solution with integrated load demand. The particle swarm optimization (PSO) is adopted to find a global solution for power sources planning. Numerical simulations are conducted on an actual 34-bus power system to verify the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: BlockGuard: A PureChain Safety Framework for Agent Validation in Mission-Critical Systems
authors: Raneem Khafagy, Paul Angelo Oroceo, Jae Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
