publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Large Language Model agents show increasing potential for mission-critical edge environments, including autonomous drones, smart grid controllers, and industrial automation systems. However, the non-deterministic nature of LLMs poses significant safety risks when deployed in physical systems where erroneous decisions can lead to catastrophic failures. We present BlockGuard, a blockchain-anchored safety framework that binds agent actions to immutable, verifiable safety policies. The system employs a fast-path local validator for real-time deterministic enforcement, while PureChain smart contracts operate in a slow-path role to provide post-decision immutability, auditability, and policy provenance. PureChain does not sit on the real-time control loop; instead, it provides authoritative evidence, dispute resolution, and compliance guarantees without impacting control latency. Through simulation-based validation across 150 industrial control scenarios, BlockGuard blocked 100% of unsafe actions with a mean validation latency of 25.9 ms and 32.6 ms (with twin). Twin anchoring improved latency stability, bounding worst-case checks to ≤ 101 ms versus 272 ms spikes in baseline; explainability recall and audit completeness were both 1.0.

title: Understanding Body Shape from Numbers: A Sequential Attention Approach Using Anthropometric Data
authors: Shintami Chusnul Hidayati, Abdi Dewa Maha Rupawan, Yusuf Anfasya, Joko Lianto Buliali, Djoko Purwanto, Wen-Huang Cheng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Human body shape is an important indicator in fashion, health assessment, and personalized recommendation systems. Existing classification approaches often rely on imaging devices or 3D scanners, which limits their scalability and practical deployment. This study proposes a lightweight method for classifying female body shapes using only anthropometric measurements. This approach employs an optimized sequential attention–based deep learning architecture that adaptively selects informative body proportions and suppresses redundant features. A dataset collected from publicly available anthropometric records was labeled into five common body shape categories through visual inspection. Feature engineering and correlation-based selection were applied to enhance the representation quality. The experimental results show that the proposed model surpasses the three optimized baselines: SVM, Random Forest, and MLP. Ablation analysis highlights the importance of ratio-based features, whereas oversampling is detrimental to anthropometric data.

title: High Density Anomaly Collection Technology for Collaborative Offline Line Loss Data in Cloud Management Edge End
authors: Qi Rong, Zixuan Dui, Xinpeng Mao, Chuanren Ma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Accurately identify and locate abnormal line losses in the substation area, improve the efficiency of power grid operation and energy management level, and propose high-density abnormal data collection technology for substation line losses through cloud management edge end collaboration. Real time filtering and preliminary diagnosis are completed through the edge computing unit, and cross regional pattern mining is carried out in combination with the cloud based global analysis engine. Based on the power supply radius, maximum power supply length, and transformer load rate, electrical characteristic indicators are constructed, and an improved ant colony algorithm is used to complete the hierarchical clustering of characteristic parameters and screen for areas with similar power consumption properties. Design a CNN-LSTM hybrid network model, using convolutional neural networks to extract spatiotemporal features of line loss data, implementing feature screening and state memory through long short-term memory networks, and finally achieving automatic anomaly recognition through the Softmax classification layer. The test results show that the false alarm rate of the abnormal data collection method proposed in this paper is always below 2 %, and the average delay is always lower than the comparison method.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Low-Cost Microcontrollers for Real-Time Control on an Inverted Pendulum
authors: Miguel Angel Silva Plata, Laura Letizia Choque Mamani, Franco Milán Morales Albarracín, Gabriel Antonio Rojas Silva
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Autonomous Systems (ICoIAS)
abstract: Low-cost microcontrollers are widely used in control education and rapid prototyping. This work compares the realtime implementation of a linear quadratic Gaussian (LQG) controller on an inverted pendulum using four representative boards. The evaluation takes into account stabilization time, overshoot, output variance, memory usage, and power consumption. The results reveal the advantages and disadvantages: the Arduino Mega has a delay in response and stabilization related to temporal variability; the Raspberry Pi Pico W achieves low oscillation but a moderately delayed response; the ESP32 achieves the lowest RMS error and the STM32L432KC offers the most balanced overall performance with low actuator power consumption. The work shows the importance of choosing MCUs appropriately for the demands of the application, so that hardware limitations do not skew control performance in research, prototyping, or educational environments.

title: Application of 3D Scanning Technology in Automatic Inspection of Commodity Packaging
authors: Qing Yang, Rui Liu, Qiangsheng Xu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To address the environmental damage and resource waste caused by excessive commodity packaging, and overcome the defects of low accuracy, inefficiency and insufficient automation in traditional packaging metrological inspection relying on manual measurement and evaluation, this paper proposes a packaging metrological inspection method based on 3D scanning technology, which complies with the requirements of the national standard GB23350-2021 Restriction of Excessive Packaging for Food and Cosmetics. In this method, a handheld 3D scanner combined with point cloud slicing technology is adopted to scan commodities. After importing the scanned images into the software, the relevant algorithms are invoked to accurately calculate the packaging volume, so as to judge whether there is excessive packaging. The proposed method can accurately and quickly measure the initial packaging volume of commodities, and significantly improve the accuracy, efficiency and automation level of packaging metrological inspection.

title: Ensemble Learning Framework for Prosumer Energy Pattern Prediction: Integrating AI with Renewable Energy Systems for Power Dispatching
authors: Hongnian Meng
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The rapid growth of energy prosumers has introduced significant challenges in maintaining grid stability due to unpredictable energy consumption and production patterns. As renewable energy sources constitute an increasingly larger proportion of the power grid mix, their inherent output uncertainty poses critical threats to grid reliability and operational stability. This variability presents unprecedented challenges to traditional power dispatching systems, which were designed for predictable, centralized generation. Improving the prediction accuracy of renewable energy output is essential for formulating effective power dispatching strategies and achieving power grid load balancing under high renewable penetration scenarios. This paper presents a novel ensemble learning framework specifically designed for accurate prediction of prosumer energy behavior. Our approach integrates multiple machine learning models through a weighted ensemble architecture that combines the strengths of VotingRegressor systems based on LightGBM and CatBoost algorithms. The framework processes diverse data sources including weather forecasts, electricity prices, gas prices, and historical consumption patterns to generate comprehensive feature representations. We introduce specialized feature engineering techniques that capture temporal dependencies, spatial correlations, and external factors influencing energy behavior. Our comprehensive evaluation demonstrates that the proposed ensemble model achieves superior prediction accuracy compared to individual models, with a mean absolute error reduction of 12.8 % over the best single-model approach. The system has been validated on real-world data from Estonian solar customers, demonstrating practical applicability for reducing energy imbalance costs and improving grid reliability. This work represents a significant advancement in the integration of artificial intelligence with renewable energy systems, offering a scalable solution for energy providers managing growing prosumer populations while supporting enhanced power dispatching and load balancing capabilities.

title: Mono Instruction Set Computer design using a high-level hardware description language
authors: Nobuya Watanabe, Minoru Watanabe
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Reduced instruction set computer (RISC) processors are built on the philosophy of eliminating complex instructions and of limiting the instruction set to only simple operations, thereby emphasizing fast execution. This approach has been adopted in many modern processors. Extending the RISC concept even further, we propose mono instruction set computer (MISC) architecture, which constrains the number of instructions on a processor to a single instruction. Because a MISC processor contains only a single instruction, in order to execute a variety of instructions, MISC processors must be implemented onto a programmable architecture and its configuration must be done frequently. Existing FPGAs can not achieve a high-performance MISC processor due to their long reconfiguration time. Therefore, we have been developing an optically reconfigurable gate array (ORGA) that supports high-speed dynamic reconfiguration. The ORGA can realize an ideal platform for MISC processors. In this research, we designed MISC processors based on RISC-V processor using the high-level hardware description language FSL. The design has been implemented onto an FPGA. This paper presents the evaluated results.

title: Educational Concept Discovery Using Nonnegative Matrix Factorization in Curriculum Mining Systems
authors: Aziza Nishanbayeva, Zukhrobiddin Olimov, Dadaxon Abdullayev, Sodiqjon Ibragimov, Feruza Nizomova, Surayyo Matyakubova, Sobirjon Bazarov
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Curriculum mining has emerged as a process capable of uncovering implicit patterns, relationships, and educational concepts in substantial university datasets with respect to technology use and learning outcomes. As a result, recent reflection accounts have been more strongly oriented to the quantitative processes for automated qualitative analysis of course structure, learning resources, and student-related outcomes towards better curriculum design and delivery. Unfortunately, many of the commercially-based approaches have struggled to generate educationally interpretable concepts in high-dimensional curriculum data. Traditional clustering and associated methods have either overlooked or failed to discover latent educational structures and relationships to implicit discipline-specific learning outcomes. These obstacles have further complicated the legal to establish trustworthiness and usefulness of the concept discoveries in authentic settings. This presentation will present Non-Negative Matrix Factorization-Based Educational Concept Discovery (NMFECD) as a method to address these compromises. NMF-ECD involves the enchancement use of non-negative matrix factorization to identify latent concept structures from curricular data, while still maintaining at least one level of interpretability worth pursuing. NMF-ECD can include the use of semantic similarity methods with flexible weighting for better alignment with educational intentions, and record curricular gaps, redundancies and progression behaviour. The NMF-ECD will equally emphasize accuracy and interpretability to provide a more trustworthy method for discovering actionable knowledge regarding education. Evaluative data found that NMF-ECD improved concept coherence, correctly identified curriculum gaps, and systematically mapped knowledge in cultivate knowledge categories compared to the standard approaches. Yet, results showed the method delineated the capacity for large data analytics and provided fine-grain evaluative differentiating factors among curriculum approaches. The structure relates directly to personalized learning systems and adaptive pathways that include the discovery of concepts and the formation of curriculum.

title: A New Fault Detection Method of UAVs Based on Compressed Vibration Signal
authors: Yunfei Ma, Yihai Shi, Bo Xu, Xuan Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Aiming at the problem that the UAV system is prone to failure, this paper realizes real-time equipment monitoring by using a new fault detection method. Traditional fault detection method requires massive amounts of vibration data from mechanical components of equipment. The Compressed Sensing (CS) theory breaks through the limitation of Nyquist sampling theory which avoids wasting computing resources. However, it is suboptimal to recover the original signal and then perform fault diagnosis on the original signal. A novel harmonic detection method based on the shrinking Fourier basis has been proposed. Its main advantage is that fault harmonics can be detected directly from the measurement vector, while a significant reduction in the reconstruction time is achieved. Numerical simulations have been performed to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Nutrition-Aware and Climate-Responsive Crop Rotation Modeling in Indian Agriculture
authors: Vidhyarth S.E, Syed Faazil S, Nithes J.S, KiSyed Faazil T.A, Anju S Pillai
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Indian agriculture currently faces a dual challenge: adapting to unpredictable climate patterns while addressing widespread nutritional insecurity. We propose a machine learning framework which optimizes crop rotation by balancing yield maximization with specific nutritional targets. Unlike traditional models that focus solely on productivity, our system integrates Random Forest (RF) regression for rainfall forecasting with XGBoostbased crop yield prediction to optimize the output of five critical nutrients: protein, iron, calcium, zinc, and folate. We first model district-specific rainfall patterns using temporal and geographic data, achieving a Coefficient of Determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) of 0.816. These meteorological forecasts then feed into an XGBoost yield predictor (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.9388), which estimates production potential (tons/hectare). The final module generates three-crop rotation sequences that adhere to soil health protocols while maximizing nutrient density. Validated on agricultural data from West Bengal and Tamil Nadu, the framework successfully generates rotation plans that improve economic viability for farmers while simultaneously enhancing regional nutritional security.

title: A Method for Carbon Emission Quantification Data Correction in the Power Industry Chain with Artificial Intelligence Support
authors: Qiang Li, Feng Zhao, Wei Zhang, Linlin Zhao, Maokai Liu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: This paper presents a methodology for correcting carbon emission quantification data in the power industry chain, leveraging artificial intelligence (Artificial Intelligence, AI) support. The intricate nature of carbon emissions across various stages of the power system necessitates a robust correction method. The proposed approach employs statistical inference and time series analysis, particularly focusing on coal-fired power production as a case study. By considering the continuous and correlated nature of emissions data, this study introduces a covariance function estimation technique based on a stochastic process, facilitating the calculation of deviations through spline approximation. Additionally, the paper explores the application of Kernel Density Estimation (KDE) for modeling unobservable error distributions in autoregressive time series analysis. Under relatively relaxed assumptions, the proposed estimation demonstrates implicit validity, converging asymptotically with kernel estimates derived from the unobservable error distribution.

title: GEN AI Enabled Wearable for Early Detection of Sleep Apnea Using NXP I.MX RT1060 and GW1N- LV1LQ144C6/I5 FPGA
authors: Karthikeyan A, Balasrinivasan S A, Ashega S, Hamsa Ramyaa P, Harish S S
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Sleep apnea is a common respiratory disorder that is often left undiagnosed, however, has serious negative impacts on cardiovascular health as well as the quality of sleep in general. This manuscript presents a miniaturized and wearable non-invasive system for sleep apnea detection to realize real-time detection to monitor and intelligently analyze physiological signals continuously. The architecture consists of an NXP i.MX RT1060 microcontroller with a GOWIN GW1N-LV1LQ144C6/I5 field-programmable gate array [FPGA] in order to achieve the parallel acquisition and processing of biosignals. Respiration, snoring and photoplethysmography (PPG) are monitored by AD8233 analog front end, CMM-4030DB-26354-TR MEMS microphone and MAXM86161EFD+T PPG sensor, respectively. Signal conditioning (Switching Kalman filtering, Adaptive filtering Verilog HDL on the FPGA verification with Cadence SimVision in simulations and stable waveform tracking and accurate switching of models. Features extracted have been categorized using a Convolution Neural Network (CNN) which is lightweight and has been implemented on the microcontroller, and it achieved a detection accuracy of 94.6 %. On detecting aberrant respiratory patterns, the system provides realtime haptic feedback via an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 ~\text{mm} * 2 ~\text{mm}$</tex> ERM coin vibration motor as well as Generative AI assisted plain language summaries, along with actionable suggestions, including explanations for apnea episodes, guidance on sleeping positions, alcohol avoidance, and encouragement to consult a physician. Powered with 3.7V 500mAh Li-Po Battery (WLY752530), device works in an energy-efficient manner. Experimental results validate the low latency (approximately 1.2 milliseconds) and very precise results for apnea detection that provide valuable health feedback for actions, which has demonstrated a cost efficient and power efficient algorithmic optimized human health monitoring using signal processing on FPGAs and artificial intelligence embedded in the system.

title: Finite Element Analysis of a Human Body Communication Channel with a Wrist-Worn Transmitter and Grounded Receiver
authors: Adesh Ankush, Medha Boorla, Soumick Majumdar, Kurian Polachan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: A major challenge in designing systems that support Human Body Communication (HBC) is to determine the optimal size and placement of transceiver electrodes. Physical experiments to determine optimal configurations are possible but are often time-consuming, and costly. A more practical approach is to perform simulation studies. In this work, we present a Finite Element Method (FEM)-based simulation framework to study an HBC configuration comprising a wrist-worn transmitter and a grounded receiver, a configuration not well studied in the literature. We developed a custom HBC model for this purpose. The communication channel is modelled using geometries and materials representing the anatomical layers of the human body. Simulation results indicate that electrode geometry and positioning are critical factors in determining HBC performance. Channel gain was found to depend on several parameters: the width of the transmitter electrode (TxW), the gap between transmitter electrodes (TxG), the distance from the transmitter electrodes to the end of the arm (TxD), the alignment offset of the receiver ground electrode (RxA), and the vertical height of the receiver ground electrode (RxH). We observe that increasing TxW, TxG, RxH, and RxA leads to higher channel gain. Interestingly, increasing TxD initially increases the gain but, beyond a certain point, decreases it. We also propose a circuit model to explain these trends.

title: Variable Transformer Turns Dual Active Bridge Converter with Reduced Current Stress for Battery Charging Applications
authors: Harikesh V. Kurup, Shelas Sathyan
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Dual Active Bridge (DAB) converter topology is found relevant in many applications like electric vehicles, renewable energy and battery charging applications. For a conventional DAB, the voltage gain range is limited and only gives best performance when voltage ratio is equal to unity. To overcome such drawbacks a variable equivalent turns ratio transformer based DAB which delivers constant output voltage for wide change in input is presented in this article. When the equivalent turns ratio is changed the zero voltage switching (ZVS) range can be extended and explores new region of operation. To enhance the performance further, extended phase shift modulation is adopted in this work. Extended phase shift modulation gives minimum current stress operation with each operating point. Finally, the converter is tested on an experimental prototype rated for 500W and results are validated.

title: Enhanced Deep Learning Framework for Lung Disease Detection Using Optimized Segmentation and Classification of Chest X-Ray Images
authors: Thirunellai Annamalai, Parvathi Annamalai, A. Poongodi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: The automated detection of pulmonary diseases by Chest X-ray (CXR) images is crucial in modern medical diagnostics, enabling early identification and improved patient outcomes. Precise segmentation of pulmonary areas is crucial for improving classification accuracy, reducing false positives, and increasing interpretability in deep learning models. This research introduces an improved U-Net segmentation technique, U-Net-R50-ResB3-LReLU, which integrates a ResNet-50 encoder featuring three residual blocks and Leaky ReLU activation to enhance feature extraction and segmentation accuracy. The effectiveness of various U-Net architectures is evaluated using the Dice Coefficient, Jaccard Index, Sensitivity, and Specificity, demonstrating that the proposed model exceeds the performance of the baseline U-Net and its variants. The pulmonary regions are classified into four categories: Normal, COVID-19, Lung Opacity, and Viral Pneumonia, employing deep learning architectures including ResNet50, DenseNet201, VGG19, InceptionResNetV2, and EfficientNetB7. The experimental results indicate that the U-Net-R50-ResB3-LReLU segmentation model, combined with EfficientNetB7, achieves the highest specificity (97.50%) and F1-score <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(96.02 \%)$</tex>, exceeding conventional segmentation techniques. These findings emphasise the significance of accurate segmentation in improving the reliability of automated CXR analysis, hence enabling more precise and efficient detection of pulmonary illnesses in clinical settings.

title: The electromagnetic calculation and mechanical evaluation of CFETR CSMC under transient extreme operation
authors: Aihua Xu, Chao Yu, Yongsheng Wu, Fuhai Cai, Yan Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: As a crucial part of the Central Solenoid Model Coil (CSMC), the preload system is crucial to maintain the integrity of the coil modules and to prevent their offset and misalignment after energization. The purpose of this study is to ascertain the safety and dependability of the preload system components when subjected to extreme alternating currents, through a comprehensive analysis of electromagnetic-structural coupling and an assessment of stress levels. Initially, an eddy current calculation model for the preload system of the CSMC was established using ANSYS Maxwell, and the eddy current, magnetic density, and electromagnetic force density for the preload system were calculated, when subjected to alternating excitation current. Subsequently, an indirect coupling technique was utilized to examine the mechanical behaviour of the preload system, considering the combined effects of extreme electromagnetic loads and preload. Ultimately, the stress levels of the preload system were evaluated using stress linearization, with reference to analytical design principles. After stress assessment, it was found that all types of primary stresses in the preload components were below the corresponding stress thresholds, with sufficient safety margins. This study provides theoretical guidance for the energized testing of the CSMC and serves as an important theoretical basis for further optimizing the preload components.

title: 3GPP RedCap Non-Terrestrial Networks: Direct-to-Device Feasibility Study
authors: Muhammad Asad Ullah, Champaka Damuddara Gedara, Antti Anttonen, Alok Sethi, Muhammad Danyal Khattak, Marko Höyhtyä, Jeroen Wigard, Konstantin Mikhaylov
publish: IEEE Communications Standards Magazine
abstract: In the emerging New Space ecosystem, direct-to-device (D2D) communication enables direct transmission between a compact terrestrial user equipment (UE), including a handheld or a machine-type device, and satellites without using the terrestrial network. Unlike conventional satellite communication, modern D2D communication connects the satellites with UEs which are primarily designed for terrestrial use. Industry and academia researchers are investigating massive machine type communications (mMTC) technologies and their potential for D2D to support low data rate Internet of things (IoT) applications. This article extends the concept of D2D by advocating for 5G Reduced Capability (RedCap) and low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite integration. The RedCap D2D concept will support verticals, which require data rates higher than the existing mMTC technologies, but with lower rates and complexity than the technologies supporting enhanced mobile broadband (eMBB) applications. More specifically, we conduct a feasibility study in terms of the link budget and the random access scalability analysis to investigate the limitations of a direct connection from an unmodified terrestrial RedCap UE to an Earth orbiting LEO satellite. Despite the challenging link budget, our findings indicate that RedCap D2D communications is still feasible when using the high antenna gain at satellite. We also study the intra-satellite handover rate for two alternative cell size configurations. Finally, we highlight some open challenges and future research directions.

title: Decentralized Coordination of Household Energy Flexibility in Virtual Power Plants
authors: Yunqi Wang, Xinghuo Yu, Mahdi Jalili
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: The increasing penetration of distributed energy resources (DERs) and electric vehicles in residential sectors presents both challenges and opportunities for power system operators. Virtual power plants (VPPs) provide an effective mechanism to aggregate household flexibility and participate in electricity markets. This paper proposes a decentralized optimization framework for coordinating household flexibility within a VPP. Transformer-based non-intrusive load monitoring is applied to smart-meter data to recover appliance-level baselines, quantify flexibility, and estimate renewable generation. Building on these estimates, an alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM)-based algorithm is used to coordinate household scheduling with VPP-level participation in day-ahead, real-time, and ancillary service markets. The operator exchanges only aggregate signals and flexibility offers, while device-level decisions remain local, preserving privacy and enabling parallelism to improve computational efficiency and coordination responsiveness. Numerical studies on a real residential dataset under seasonal scenarios demonstrate the effectiveness and scalability of the proposed approach, showing competitive market performance with fast convergence at portfolio scale.

title: A New Active Capacitor-Embedded Pulsed Load Power Supply for Pulsed Power Suppression
authors: Xi Chen, Ping Yang, Shaocong Jin, Fei Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Since the periodic pulsed power is a strong disturbance to the power system, the pulsed power must be suppressed by the pulsed load power supply (PLPS). In general, an additional converter with a storage capacitor is formed as the active capacitor in the original PLPS to reduce storage capacitance and achieve pulsed power decoupling. However, since the introduction of additional converters, the existing PLPS still faces high power loss, large volume, more components, etc. In this article, an active capacitor unit is proposed to be embedded in the original converter to form a new pulsed power suppression topology, in which only two switches and one storage capacitor are introduced to establish an energy storage port with bidirectional power flow characteristics. By doing so, the active capacitor-embedded PLPS (ACE-PLPS) can be developed, which features a compact structure and fewer components. Furthermore, the process of pulsed power decoupling is integrated into the switching states, resulting in a significant reduction in power loss. The implementation topology and control scheme are presented. Design guidelines are also developed. The effectiveness and feasibility of the ACE-PLPS are validated by the experimental results.

title: A Novel Control Strategy for Grid-connected Cascaded Multilevel Inverters Improving Resilience with Flexible Neutral Shift
authors: Jinli Zhu, Yuan Li, Brad Lehman, Fang Z. Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: The asymmetric operation of a grid-connected three-phase cascaded multilevel inverter (CMI) system can significantly degrade grid current quality. Traditional solutions typically rely on hardware redundancy or complex zero-sequence injection to mitigate voltage imbalance, but these methods increase system cost or complexity. This paper proposes a flexible fault-tolerant control strategy based on adaptive shifting of the inverter’s output neutral point. After a fault, this strategy enables all phases to share the voltage deficit in the faulty phase, ensuring symmetric line-to-line voltages and balanced grid currents. A complete inner-loop current control is developed using internal modulation-based feedforward to improve steady-state tracking performance and reduce reliance on real-time PCC/grid voltage sensing. Theoretical modeling and closed-loop analysis are presented. Simulation and experimental results validate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy under fault-tolerant scenarios, demonstrating its suitability for resilient operation in CMI-based grid-connected applications.

title: Electrical Design and Application of Air-cooled Control System for Ultra-high Voltage Power Transformers Based on PLC
authors: Rong Liao, Weitong He, Huali Hu, Lingyan He
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: Ultra-high voltage power transformers are the core hub equipment of the power grid, and their operational reliability is directly related to the safety and stability of the grid. During operation, transformers generate a large amount of heat due to various losses, necessitating effective cooling through an air cooling control system to prevent overheating, which can lead to insulation material aging, reduced load capacity, and even equipment failure. Traditional relay control methods lack flexibility and pose difficulties in fault diagnosis, while relying solely on PLC control carries the risk of system failure due to program errors. Therefore, this paper proposes a hybrid control scheme combining “Relay + PLC”, which combines the advantages of high reliability and intelligence. The system adopts designs such as automatic dual power source switching, grouped and graded cooling fan control, dual criteria for start-stop based on temperature and load current, and automatic start of cooling fans after PLC failure. It features remote/local control, fault alarm, power monitoring, and other functions, ensuring continuous operation of the cooling system even in the event of PLC anomalies. Through the actual design and commissioning of a 500kV transformer, the reliability and stability of the system under various operating conditions have been verified. Currently, the system has been successfully applied in multiple 500 kV substations, significantly enhancing the automation level and operational safety of transformer air cooling control.

title: A Novel Coordinated Optimisation Control Method for Power System Generation, Transmission, Load and Storage Based on Multimodal Large Models
authors: Zhu Xiaoxiao, Li Hua, Xu Yaoting, Dong Jie, Jin Yaohua
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the continuous increase in renewable energy penetration and the widespread integration of flexible loads within new power systems, traditional source-grid-load-storage coordinated control methods face challenges including difficulties in fusing high-dimensional heterogeneous data, complex multi-timescale coupling, and acute conflicts of interest among multiple stakeholders. This paper proposes a collaborative optimisation control framework for source-grid-load-storage systems based on multimodal large models. By establishing cross-modal representation learning within a unified semantic space, it achieves deep integration of multi-source heterogeneous data encompassing grid topology, equipment status, meteorological conditions, and load characteristics. A collaborative decision-making mechanism for the four stakeholders (generation, grid, load, and storage) is designed. Regional grid control is modelled as a partially observable Markov decision process, solved using an improved multi-agent near-term policy optimisation algorithm. Experimental results on a provincial grid dataset demonstrate significant improvements over traditional methods in key metrics including renewable energy absorption rate, voltage quality, and control efficiency: Renewable energy curtailment rates decreased by 4.2 percentage points to 1.8%, voltage deviations reduced by 37.5%, control response times shortened to under 200 milliseconds, and false alarms decreased by 286,000 per annum. These findings validate the method's effectiveness and advanced capabilities within complex, uncertain environments.

title: Interpreting Subtle Facial Cues in Elephants: A Deep Learning Approach to Emotional State Recognition
authors: Helan Deva Priya, Vimal S, Vignesh M, Devaguru S
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Understanding emotional states in elephants is crucial for their conservation and welfare. This study presents a novel deep learning model designed to interpret subtle facial cues, such as eye positioning, trunk movements, and ear postures, to classify elephants' emotional states. Leveraging a dataset of annotated elephant facial images collected from wildlife reserves and conservation centers, we fine-tuned a convolutional neural network (CNN) model to detect and analyze these cues. The model incorporates transfer learning and attention mechanisms to enhance its sensitivity to fine-grained features. Our results demonstrate high accuracy in differentiating emotional states such as calmness, alertness, and distress, validating the approach's robustness. The model's interpretability is enhanced using Grad-CAM, providing insights into the facial regions critical for emotion classification. This research offers a non-invasive tool for monitoring elephant welfare and contributes to the broader understanding of emotional intelligence in non-human species. Future applications include conservation efforts and ethical wildlife management.

title: Comparing LSTM and ARIMA Approaches for Stock Price Forecasting with a Streamlit-Based Interactive Interface
authors: Roopa H M, Sahana S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Analysis of the stock market is crucial for the making of financial decisions and developing investment strategies. In this paper, we discuss the creation of an Interactive Stock Analysis Dashboard implemented using Python, Streamlit, and Yahoo Finance. Investors can visualize and make decisions based on real-time data. They can access live price data with moving averages, price change heatmaps, and comparative analysis of multiple companies. This paper discusses the methodologies employed, including data collection and preprocessing, visualization techniques, and the system's experimental setup. The system designed lets the user evaluate stock price movements, compare historical stock price data, and make investment decisions based on the data analysis. The focus of subsequent studies will be directed toward the use of forecasting techniques based on AI for greater accuracy in the analysis. This research has the following notable features: Fetching stock information in real-time: Setting up a real-time automated process for fetching stock prices and their corresponding traded volume. Processing of data and performance of analytical computations: Calculating moving averages, the prices, and their changes in a more advanced means identifying the trends. Display of stock trends in a line graph, bar chart, and heatmap for easy interpretation. Allowing easy comparison across multiple companies, enabling the user to identify trends in market. By integrating real-time data and visualization, and performing underlying statistical evaluations, the system targets to provide a straight-forward and practical solution for enthusiasts, traders, and financial analyst. Further refinements will focus on incorporating advanced algorithms aimed at predictive estimation for stock market movements, thus increasing the system's functionality beyond real-time data display.

title: Optimized Design of Planar High-Frequency Magnetics for DPS Modulated Dual Active Bridge Converter
authors: Tanzeela Mir, Ashiq Muhammed, Pallavi Bharadwaj
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: Fast electric vehicle (EV) chargers demand compact, high-power-density converters, where the design of high-frequency magnetic components is critical for achieving high efficiency and effective thermal performance. High-frequency operation allows the miniaturization of magnetic components. This paper presents a systematic, optimized step-by-step design methodology for planar high-frequency transformer and inductors for a 3 kW Dual Active Bridge (DAB) converter operating at 100 kHz under Dual Phase Shift (DPS) modulation. The proposed design explicitly accounts for key high-frequency effects, including skin effect, proximity effect, and fringing flux, all of which significantly impact the efficiency and thermal behavior. The designs are validated through finite element modelling (FEM) simulations and integration with DAB hardware prototype, demonstrating expected working.

title: hHand-Covered PIN Authentication for Virtual Reality Headsets with Tangible Touch Feedback
authors: Zeyu Deng, Jingwei Zhang, Chen Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Virtual Reality (VR) applications are rapidly expanding, yet user authentication methods have not kept pace. Existing systems often rely on handheld controllers or floating keypads, which provide little haptic feedback and easily expose hand motions to bystanders. This paper presents a hand-covered PIN authentication interface that leverages mixed reality to provide both privacy and usability on consumer VR headsets. Using built-in visual sensors, the system projects a virtual keypad onto the user’s palm, enabling natural two-hand PIN entry with real skin-touch feedback. Further, the interface simultaneously captures coordinated head and hand motions as behavioral biometrics for enhanced security. Implemented on the Meta Quest 2 using Unity, the prototype achieves 100% PIN-entry recognition and over 98.3% user authentication F1 score. Results demonstrate a low-cost, hardware-ready solution that transforms existing VR devices into secure, user-friendly authentication platforms for everyday consumer applications.

title: LeukoLite: A Lightweight and Robust CNN for Smartphone-Based Leukemia Diagnosis
authors: Saad Ahmed Syed, Humaira Nisar, Saeed Mian Qaisar, Lee Yu Jen, Hum Yan Chai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Accurate leukemia diagnosis often requires centralized laboratories and expert pathologists, resulting in barriers to rapid, accessible healthcare. To overcome this, we introduce LeukoLite (LL), a novel, application-driven Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) designed for point-of-care (POC) diagnostic systems. The key to LL's design is hybrid architecture that employs multi-scale kernels to effectively handle data variability, depthwise separable convolutions (DSCs) for computational efficiency, and skip connections to ensure robust feature learning. We validate our approach by first curating a large-scale, five-class (ALL, AML, CLL, CML, Healthy) heterogeneous dataset from nine public sources to simulate real-world conditions. On this challenging dataset, LL achieves a state-of-the-art accuracy of 91.24% and an F1-score of 91.25%. Furthermore, LL is highly optimized for resource-constrained environments. Through its reliance on DSCs, it operates at just 4.2 GFLOPs with a 40.44 MB footprint, making it ideal for deployment on smartphones or other low-cost consumer devices. These results demonstrate a significant step towards a generalizable and efficient automated tool, paving the way for accessible, user-oriented systems that can improve patient well-being and broaden access to leukemia screening.

title: Lightweight Infrared Defect Detection Network for Substation Equipment Based on Wavelet-Driven Structured Pruning
authors: Xin Tong, Cheng Zhao, Liwei Yang, Kai Liu, Shiqi Wang, Li Yin
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Infrared imaging plays a crucial role in substation equipment inspection by enabling non-contact identification of thermal anomalies that reveal potential defects. However, achieving high detection accuracy often requires large convolutional neural networks, which imposes significant computational and memory burdens and limits deployment on edge devices commonly used in power systems. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a lightweight and efficient infrared defect detection framework that incorporates wavelet-regularized soft channel pruning into a deep neural network. Specifically, a Wavelet-Based Channel Pruning (WCP) strategy is introduced to evaluate channel importance by analyzing the wavelet-domain coefficients of convolutional filters, allowing the model to preserve channels rich in defect-related edge information. Furthermore, to mitigate the risk of prematurely removing informative channels, a Soft Channel Reconstruction (SCR) mechanism is developed to dynamically restore selected pruned channels through cosineinterpolated parameter fusion during training. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior recognition accuracy while reducing the number of parameters and FLOPs by more than 50%.

title: An Attention guided Bilinear Convolution Neural Network using hybrid loss function for Efficient Skin Disease Classification
authors: Belal Ahmad, Yen-Lin Chen, Mohd Usama
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: accurate diagnosis of skin diseases, particularly melanoma is crucial for effective treatment. Traditional approaches for skin disease classification face challenges due to the diverse and subjective nature of skin lesion, therefore, it is difficult to distinguish between situations that have visual similarities. This paper presents a novel deep learning based model to address these challenges. The model integrates dual attention mechanisms with a bilinear Convolution Neural Network (CNN) to extract discriminative features from dermoscopic images. Generate an Attention Heat Maps (AHMs) for the crop regions of the input images to focus on significant regions and a novel loss function, which combined complementary entropy and cross-entropy for mitigating class imbalance issues and improve model performance. The proposed model achieving an accuracy of 94.24% on ISIC2019 and 92.86% on HAM10000.

title: Transformers for Vision-to-Language: A Comparative Evaluation of Image Captioning Architectures, Datasets, and Metrics
authors: Dharani R, Kavin Karthic M, Kanishka D, Rithanya K A, Subiksha MV, A Baskar
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Image Captioning (IC) is defined as automatic generation of semantically and contextually coherent descriptions of image. Early CNN-RNN approaches captured multimodality features but failed to capture context and longrange dependencies. This study explores Image Captioning (IC) and Visual Question Answering (VQA) tasks through implementations. We considered seven transformer based models for the study and their performance was assessed. BLEU, ROUGE, METEOR, F1 Score and VQA accuracy are the evaluation metrics used across the benchmarking datasets and custom datasets. We studied the response on two scenarios - with ground truth and without ground truth. For without ground truth scenario, we obtained user feedback to evaluate the generated captions. Initially, we discussed primary structural and architectural components like encoderdecoder, attention techniques and model parameters of the selected models, as they directly influence the performance of the model. Subsequently, we investigated the operational dynamics through IC and VQA as they impact the image-text grounding and reasoning ability of the models. Finally, performance metric along with error analysis offered insights on how architectural choices affect reasoning and visionlanguage alignment. This survey provides pathway for the development and analysis of transformer-based IC and VQA models across datasets.

title: Virtual Synchronous Machine-Controlled Inverter for Enhanced Stability in Microgrids: Design and Rapid Prototyping
authors: Ameen Akthar, Divya R. Nambiar, Gokul G, Kumaravel S
publish: 2025 12th National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources (RESs) into modern power systems is mainly through power electronic converters, which lack the natural inertia of synchronous generators. This reduction in system inertia increases vulnerability to frequency and voltage instabilities during load or generation fluctuations. To address this, virtual inertia control has emerged as a key solution. By regulating inverter power output in proportion to frequency deviations, the virtual synchronous machine (VSM) concept provides synthetic inertia and damping support. This approach effectively compensates for the loss of conventional generation, enhances system stability, and ensures reliable operation of renewabledominated grids. In microgrid applications, inverters must be capable of seamless operation in both grid-connected and autonomous (islanded) modes to ensure stability and reliability. This paper presents an evaluation of the Virtual Synchronous Machine (VSM) operating in stand-alone mode. The investigation combines detailed MATLAB/Simulink simulations with experimental validation, achieved through rapid prototyping of a VSM-based inverter implemented on the DSP TMS320F28379D controller.

title: Constraint-Parameterized Spin-Variable Reduction Method for QAP
authors: Raio Aoki, Takeru Ota, Tatsuhiko Shirai, Nozomu Togawa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Developing novel quadratic unconstrained binary optimization (QUBO) formulations is important to enhance the performance of the Ising machines. The spin-variable reduction (SVR) method formulates the QUBO with fewer binary variables than the penalty method. However, the SVR method can complicate the energy landscape. To address this issue, we propose a constraint-parameterized SVR method for the combinatorial optimization problems with a 2-way 1-hot constraint such as the quadratic assignment problem (QAP). Our proposed method introduces two key elements: (1) preliminary analysis using simulated annealing (SA) to statistically identify the variable that has a high probability of being the value of 1, and (2) parameterization of the SVR constraint term using this statistical information. Experiments on QAP benchmarks show that our method can find higher-precision solutions more efficiently than conventional SVR or the penalty method in certain cases.

title: IoT-Based Health and Pregnancy Monitoring System for Dairy Cattle
authors: Ankur Saxena, Mukesh Tiwari, Chandrmani Yadav, Bhagwat Kakde
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: The goal of this paper is to improve the usefulness and efficacy of traditional Cattle monitoring systems by introducing new architecture. This will allow for real-time monitoring of cow health and pregnancy status from any location and at any time. The farmer has a web app to monitor the system and receive messages if a cow's health or pregnancy status falls below a specified level. Farmers will no longer need to visit the cowshed regularly to check on their cows' health and pregnant status regular basis of their cows. This research will help to enhance livestock monitoring technology by addressing the limits and downsides of traditional livestock monitoring systems. The purpose of this study is to give a trustworthy and accessible source of information on cow health, which can lead to higher productivity and profitability for farmers.
